C:\language\mdmdview\crates\d2\src\ast.rs:
    1|       |//! AST node types for D2 diagram language.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! These types represent the parsed syntax tree of a D2 document.
    4|       |//! The AST closely mirrors D2's Go AST, adapted for Rust idioms.
    5|       |//! Key types:
    6|       |//! - `D2Map`: top-level container of all declarations
    7|       |//! - `MapNode`: individual declaration (key assignment or comment)
    8|       |//! - `Key`: shape/edge/property declaration with optional value
    9|       |//! - `Edge`: connection between two key paths
   10|       |//! - `KeyPath`: dotted key path (e.g., "server.style.fill")
   11|       |
   12|       |use crate::graph::Range;
   13|       |
   14|       |/// Top-level map containing all declarations.
   15|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
   16|       |pub struct D2Map {
   17|       |    pub nodes: Vec<MapNode>,
   18|       |    pub range: Range,
   19|       |}
   20|       |
   21|       |/// A node within a map.
   22|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
   23|       |pub enum MapNode {
   24|       |    Comment(Comment),
   25|       |    Key(Key),
   26|       |}
   27|       |
   28|       |/// A key declaration (shape, edge, or property assignment).
   29|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
   30|       |pub struct Key {
   31|       |    /// Dotted path: "a.b.c"
   32|       |    pub key: KeyPath,
   33|       |    /// Connection chain (empty if this is just a shape/property declaration)
   34|       |    pub edges: Vec<Edge>,
   35|       |    /// D2 syntax index for edge references: `(a -> b)[0]`
   36|       |    pub edge_index: Option<usize>,
   37|       |    /// Property on an edge reference
   38|       |    pub edge_key: Option<KeyPath>,
   39|       |    /// Label value (the primary scalar after `:`)
   40|       |    pub primary: Option<ScalarValue>,
   41|       |    /// Map, array, or scalar value
   42|       |    pub value: Option<Value>,
   43|       |    pub range: Range,
   44|       |}
   45|       |
   46|       |/// A string value with source position tracking.
   47|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
   48|       |pub struct StringValue {
   49|       |    /// The resolved string value (after unquoting).
   50|       |    pub value: String,
   51|       |    /// Original source text (before unquoting).
   52|       |    pub raw: String,
   53|       |    pub range: Range,
   54|       |}
   55|       |
   56|       |/// A comment line.
   57|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
   58|       |pub struct Comment {
   59|       |    pub value: String,
   60|       |    pub range: Range,
   61|       |}
   62|       |
   63|       |/// Dotted key path: "server.style.fill"
   64|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
   65|       |pub struct KeyPath {
   66|       |    pub segments: Vec<StringValue>,
   67|       |}
   68|       |
   69|       |impl std::fmt::Display for KeyPath {
   70|     32|    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
   71|     32|        let dotted = self
   72|     32|            .segments
   73|     32|            .iter()
   74|     32|            .map(|s| s.value.as_str())
   75|     32|            .collect::<Vec<_>>()
   76|     32|            .join(".");
   77|     32|        f.write_str(&dotted)
   78|     32|    }
   79|       |}
   80|       |
   81|       |/// An edge between two keys.
   82|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
   83|       |pub struct Edge {
   84|       |    pub src: KeyPath,
   85|       |    pub dst: KeyPath,
   86|       |    /// `<-` (source has arrowhead)
   87|       |    pub src_arrow: bool,
   88|       |    /// `->` (destination has arrowhead)
   89|       |    pub dst_arrow: bool,
   90|       |    pub range: Range,
   91|       |}
   92|       |
   93|       |/// Values that can appear on the right side of `:`.
   94|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
   95|       |pub enum Value {
   96|       |    Scalar(ScalarValue),
   97|       |    Map(D2Map),
   98|       |    Array(Vec<Value>),
   99|       |}
  100|       |
  101|       |/// Scalar value types.
  102|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
  103|       |pub enum ScalarValue {
  104|       |    Unquoted(String),
  105|       |    SingleQuoted(String),
  106|       |    DoubleQuoted(String),
  107|       |    Number(f64),
  108|       |    Boolean(bool),
  109|       |}
  110|       |
  111|       |impl ScalarValue {
  112|       |    /// Get the string representation of this scalar.
  113|    594|    pub fn as_str(&self) -> String {
  114|    594|        match self {
  115|    556|            ScalarValue::Unquoted(s)
  116|      0|            | ScalarValue::SingleQuoted(s)
  117|    592|            | ScalarValue::DoubleQuoted(s) => s.clone(),
                                                      ^36
  118|      0|            ScalarValue::Number(n) => n.to_string(),
  119|      2|            ScalarValue::Boolean(b) => b.to_string(),
  120|       |        }
  121|    594|    }
  122|       |}

C:\language\mdmdview\crates\d2\src\compiler.rs:
    1|       |//! AST → D2Graph compilation.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! The compiler walks the AST and builds the D2Graph:
    4|       |//! 1. Collect declarations: walk all Key nodes, build object/edge lists
    5|       |//! 2. Resolve hierarchy: dotted keys create parent-child relationships
    6|       |//! 3. Apply reserved keywords: shape, label, style.*, direction, width, height
    7|       |//! 4. Resolve named colors to hex during style processing
    8|       |//! 5. Validate shape types, style values, connection endpoints
    9|       |//! 6. Set defaults: Rectangle shape, Style fields left as None
   10|       |//! 7. Collect warnings for recognized but deferred keywords
   11|       |
   12|       |use std::collections::HashMap;
   13|       |
   14|       |use petgraph::stable_graph::NodeIndex;
   15|       |
   16|       |use crate::ast::*;
   17|       |use crate::graph::*;
   18|       |use crate::keywords;
   19|       |
   20|       |/// Compilation failure.
   21|       |#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]
   22|       |#[error("{message}")]
   23|       |pub struct CompileError {
   24|       |    pub message: String,
   25|       |    pub range: Option<Range>,
   26|       |}
   27|       |
   28|       |/// Compile a D2 AST into a graph IR.

   31|    360|    ctx.compile_map(ast, ctx.graph.root)?;
                                                      ^0
   32|    360|    Ok(ctx.graph)
   33|    360|}

  117|       |/// Compute a port point on the boundary of a shape.
  118|       |/// `direction` is the exit/entry side; `port_cross` is the desired cross-axis
  119|       |/// coordinate (y for horizontal exits, x for vertical exits).
  120|    872|fn compute_port(shape: ShapeType, rect: &Rect, direction: Direction, port_cross: f64) -> Point {
  121|    872|    match shape {
  122|       |        ShapeType::Diamond | ShapeType::Hexagon | ShapeType::Circle | ShapeType::Oval => {
  123|       |            // Clip from center toward the exit side at the desired cross position
  124|     32|            let target = match direction {
  125|      8|                Direction::Right => Point::new(rect.right() + 100.0, port_cross),
  126|      0|                Direction::Left => Point::new(rect.x - 100.0, port_cross),
  127|     12|                Direction::Down => Point::new(port_cross, rect.bottom() + 100.0),
  128|     12|                Direction::Up => Point::new(port_cross, rect.y - 100.0),
  129|       |            };
  130|     32|            clip_to_shape(shape, rect, rect.center(), target)
  131|       |        }
  132|       |        _ => {
  133|       |            // Place directly on bounding box edge
  134|    840|            match direction {
  135|    140|                Direction::Right => Point::new(rect.right(), port_cross),
  136|    148|                Direction::Left => Point::new(rect.x, port_cross),
  137|    276|                Direction::Down => Point::new(port_cross, rect.bottom()),
  138|    276|                Direction::Up => Point::new(port_cross, rect.y),
  139|       |            }
  140|       |        }
  141|       |    }

  703|      0|            Direction::Right => src_rect.right().max(dst_rect.right()) + SAME_RANK_JOG,
  704|      0|            Direction::Left => src_rect.x.min(dst_rect.x) - SAME_RANK_JOG,
  705|      0|            _ => unreachable!(),
  706|       |        };

  708|       |    } else {

  710|      0|        let jog = match ec.direction {
  711|      0|            Direction::Down => src_rect.bottom().max(dst_rect.bottom()) + SAME_RANK_JOG,

  713|      0|            _ => unreachable!(),
  714|       |        };

  890|       |/// Compute a label candidate position at a given percentage along the route.
  891|       |/// Returns the label center point, offset perpendicular to the segment.
  892|       |/// `side` controls which side of the edge the label is placed:
  893|       |///   -1.0 = up/left (default), +1.0 = down/right.
  894|       |/// If the natural offset exceeds `MAX_LABEL_OFFSET`, the label falls back to
  895|       |/// on-edge placement (offset = 0) to avoid drifting too far from the route.
  896|    964|fn label_position_at(
  897|    964|    route: &[Point],
  898|    964|    seg_lengths: &[f64],
  899|    964|    total_length: f64,
  900|    964|    percentage: f64,
  901|    964|    label_width: f64,
  902|    964|    label_height: f64,
  903|    964|    side: f64,
  904|    964|) -> Option<Point> {

  979|    964|}

 1234|       |/// Generate a self-loop path as two cubic Bézier segments.
 1235|       |/// Exits from the top of the shape, arcs outward to the upper-right,
 1236|       |/// and re-enters from the right side.
 1237|       |/// Returns 7 points: [start, c1, c2, mid, c3, c4, end] forming two
 1238|       |/// cubic Bézier segments that the SVG renderer consumes as:
 1239|       |///   M start C c1 c2 mid C c3 c4 end
 1240|     10|fn self_loop_route(rect: &crate::geo::Rect) -> Vec<Point> {
 1241|     10|    let cx = rect.x + rect.width / 2.0;
 1242|     10|    let top = rect.y;
 1243|     10|    let right = rect.x + rect.width;
 1244|     10|    let loop_size = rect.width.max(rect.height) * 0.4;
 1245|       |
 1246|       |    // Segment 1: exit from top, arc up and to the right
 1247|     10|    let start = Point::new(cx + rect.width * 0.15, top);
 1248|     10|    let ctrl1 = Point::new(cx + rect.width * 0.15, top - loop_size);
 1249|     10|    let ctrl2 = Point::new(right + loop_size * 0.5, top - loop_size);
 1250|     10|    let mid = Point::new(right + loop_size * 0.5, top + rect.height * 0.25);
 1251|       |
 1252|       |    // Segment 2: arc down and re-enter from the right side
 1253|     10|    let ctrl3 = Point::new(right + loop_size * 0.5, top + rect.height * 0.6);
 1254|     10|    let ctrl4 = Point::new(right + loop_size * 0.1, top + rect.height * 0.35);
 1255|     10|    let end = Point::new(right, top + rect.height * 0.35);
 1256|       |
 1257|     10|    vec![start, ctrl1, ctrl2, mid, ctrl3, ctrl4, end]
 1258|     10|}
 1259|       |
 1260|       |/// Compute arrowhead triangle at the endpoint of an edge.
 1261|       |/// `tip` is the point where the edge meets the shape.
 1262|       |/// `from` is the previous control point (gives direction).
 1263|       |/// Returns three polygon vertices: (tip, left, right).
 1264|    282|pub fn arrowhead_polygon(tip: Point, from: Point, size: f64) -> (Point, Point, Point) {
 1265|    282|    let dx = tip.x - from.x;
 1266|    282|    let dy = tip.y - from.y;
 1267|    282|    let len = (dx * dx + dy * dy).sqrt();
 1268|       |
 1269|    282|    if len < 1e-6 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (1269:8): [True: 0, False: 244]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (1269:8): [True: 2, False: 36]
  ------------------
 1270|       |        // Degenerate: tip ≈ from. Default to pointing downward.
 1271|      2|        return (
 1272|      2|            tip,
 1273|      2|            Point::new(tip.x - size * 0.4, tip.y - size),
 1274|      2|            Point::new(tip.x + size * 0.4, tip.y - size),
 1275|      2|        );
 1276|    280|    }
 1277|       |
 1278|    280|    let ux = dx / len;
 1279|    280|    let uy = dy / len;
 1280|    280|    let base_x = tip.x - ux * size;
 1281|    280|    let base_y = tip.y - uy * size;
 1282|    280|    let half_width = size * 0.4;
 1283|       |
 1284|    280|    let left = Point::new(base_x - uy * half_width, base_y + ux * half_width);
 1285|    280|    let right = Point::new(base_x + uy * half_width, base_y - ux * half_width);
 1286|       |
 1287|    280|    (tip, left, right)
 1288|    282|}

 1290|       |/// Compute diamond arrowhead at the endpoint of an edge.
 1291|      0|pub fn diamond_arrowhead(tip: Point, from: Point, size: f64) -> [Point; 4] {
 1292|      0|    let dx = tip.x - from.x;
 1293|      0|    let dy = tip.y - from.y;
 1294|      0|    let len = (dx * dx + dy * dy).sqrt();
 1295|       |
 1296|      0|    if len < 1e-6 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (1296:8): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (1296:8): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
 1297|      0|        return [tip; 4];
 1298|      0|    }
 1299|       |
 1300|      0|    let ux = dx / len;
 1301|      0|    let uy = dy / len;
 1302|      0|    let half = size * 0.5;
 1303|      0|    let quarter = size * 0.3;
 1304|       |
 1305|      0|    let mid = Point::new(tip.x - ux * half, tip.y - uy * half);
 1306|      0|    let back = Point::new(tip.x - ux * size, tip.y - uy * size);
 1307|      0|    let left = Point::new(mid.x - uy * quarter, mid.y + ux * quarter);
 1308|      0|    let right = Point::new(mid.x + uy * quarter, mid.y - ux * quarter);
 1309|       |
 1310|      0|    [tip, left, back, right]

 1345|       |/// Walk from `node` upward until we find the direct child of `ancestor`.
 1346|       |/// If `node == ancestor`, returns `node` (handles container-to-descendant edges).
 1347|    878|fn child_ancestor_of(graph: &D2Graph, node: NodeIndex, ancestor: NodeIndex) -> NodeIndex {
 1348|    878|    if node == ancestor {

  |  Branch (1348:8): [True: 4, False: 476]

  |  Branch (1352:15): [True: 516, False: 0]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (1352:15): [True: 450, False: 0]

 1353|    966|        if parent == ancestor {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (1353:12): [True: 476, False: 40]

 1361|       |
 1362|       |/// Walk from `container` upward through its parent chain and return the
 1363|       |/// first explicit `direction` override found. If none is set on any
 1364|       |/// ancestor, fall back to `graph.direction`.
 1365|    442|fn effective_direction(graph: &D2Graph, container: NodeIndex) -> Direction {
 1366|    442|    let mut current = container;
 1367|       |    loop {
 1368|    496|        if let Some(dir) = graph.graph[current].direction {
                                  ^2
  ------------------
  |  Branch (1368:16): [True: 0, False: 268]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (1368:16): [True: 2, False: 226]
  ------------------
 1369|      2|            return dir;
 1370|    494|        }
 1371|    494|        match graph.graph[current].parent {
 1372|     54|            Some(parent) => current = parent,
 1373|    440|            None => return graph.direction,
 1374|       |        }
 1375|       |    }

 1500|      2|    fn test_arrowhead_polygon() {
 1501|      2|        let tip = Point::new(100.0, 50.0);
 1502|      2|        let from = Point::new(50.0, 50.0);
 1503|      2|        let (t, l, r) = arrowhead_polygon(tip, from, 10.0);
 1504|      2|        assert!((t.x - 100.0).abs() < 0.01);
 1505|       |        // Left and right points should be offset perpendicular to the edge direction
 1506|      2|        assert!((l.y - r.y).abs() > 1.0); // left and right are on opposite sides
 1507|      2|        assert!(l.x < t.x); // both behind the tip
 1508|      2|        assert!(r.x < t.x);
 1509|      2|    }

 1696|       |            "dst.x ({}) should be left of src.x ({})",
 1697|      0|            edge.route[1].x,
 1698|      0|            edge.route[0].x,
 1699|       |        );
 1700|      2|    }
 1701|       |
 1702|       |    #[test]
 1703|      2|    fn test_ortho_straight_up() {
 1704|       |        // direction: up, a -> b — dst should be above src
 1705|      2|        let graph = layout_ok("direction: up\na -> b");
 1706|      2|        let edge = find_edge_between(&graph, "a", "b");
 1707|       |
 1708|      2|        assert_eq!(edge.route_type, RouteType::Orthogonal);
 1709|      2|        assert_eq!(
 1710|      2|            edge.route.len(),
 1711|       |            2,
 1712|       |            "straight up edge should have 2 route points, got {}",
 1713|      0|            edge.route.len()
 1714|       |        );
 1715|      2|        assert!(
 1716|      2|            edge.route[1].y < edge.route[0].y,
 1717|       |            "dst.y ({}) should be above src.y ({})",
 1718|      0|            edge.route[1].y,
 1719|      0|            edge.route[0].y,
 1720|       |        );
 1721|      2|    }

 1723|       |    #[test]
 1724|      2|    fn test_ortho_three_segment() {
 1725|       |        // direction: right, a -> c, b -> c — a and b are in different ranks,
 1726|       |        // the edge from the offset node should have >= 2 route points.
 1727|      2|        let graph = layout_ok("direction: right\na -> c\nb -> c");
 1728|      2|        let edge_ac = find_edge_between(&graph, "a", "c");
 1729|      2|        let edge_bc = find_edge_between(&graph, "b", "c");
 1730|       |
 1731|      2|        assert_eq!(edge_ac.route_type, RouteType::Orthogonal);
 1732|      2|        assert_eq!(edge_bc.route_type, RouteType::Orthogonal);
 1733|       |
 1734|       |        // At least one of them should have >= 2 points (both should)
 1735|      2|        assert!(
 1736|      2|            edge_ac.route.len() >= 2,
 1737|       |            "a->c route should have >= 2 points, got {}",
 1738|      0|            edge_ac.route.len()
 1739|       |        );
 1740|      2|        assert!(
 1741|      2|            edge_bc.route.len() >= 2,
 1742|       |            "b->c route should have >= 2 points, got {}",
 1743|      0|            edge_bc.route.len()
 1744|       |        );
 1745|      2|    }
 1746|       |
 1747|       |    #[test]
 1748|      2|    fn test_ortho_same_rank_jog() {
 1749|       |        // Same-rank edges need a jog (detour) since both nodes are at the
 1750|       |        // same primary coordinate. We use two nodes at the same rank by
 1751|       |        // having them both connect to a third node.
 1752|       |        // However, to directly test same-rank we need nodes at the same
 1753|       |        // position. A simpler approach: two nodes with edges to each other
 1754|       |        // creates a forward + backward pair.
 1755|       |        // For a true same-rank test we need parallel edges in the same rank.
 1756|       |        // Use a -> c, b -> c pattern where a and b end up on the same rank.
 1757|      2|        let graph = layout_ok("direction: right\na -> b\na -> c\nb -> c");
 1758|       |        // All edges should be non-empty and orthogonal
 1759|      6|        for &eidx in &graph.edges {
                                   ^2
 1760|      6|            let edata = &graph.graph[eidx];
 1761|      6|            assert_eq!(edata.route_type, RouteType::Orthogonal);
 1762|      6|            assert!(
 1763|      6|                !edata.route.is_empty(),
 1764|       |                "every edge should have route points"
 1765|       |            );
 1766|       |        }
 1767|      2|    }
 1768|       |
 1769|       |    #[test]
 1770|      2|    fn test_ortho_backward_edge() {
 1771|       |        // b -> a is a backward edge when direction=right and a is before b.
 1772|      2|        let graph = layout_ok("direction: right\na -> b\nb -> a");
 1773|       |
 1774|       |        // a -> b should be forward
 1775|      2|        let edge_ab = find_edge_between(&graph, "a", "b");
 1776|      2|        assert_eq!(edge_ab.route_type, RouteType::Orthogonal);
 1777|      2|        assert!(
 1778|      2|            !edge_ab.route.is_empty(),
 1779|       |            "forward edge should have route points"
 1780|       |        );
 1781|       |
 1782|       |        // b -> a should be backward
 1783|      2|        let edge_ba = find_edge_between(&graph, "b", "a");
 1784|      2|        assert_eq!(edge_ba.route_type, RouteType::Orthogonal);
 1785|      2|        assert!(
 1786|      2|            !edge_ba.route.is_empty(),
 1787|       |            "backward edge should have route points"
 1788|       |        );
 1789|      2|    }

 1791|       |    #[test]
 1792|      2|    fn test_ortho_self_loop_unchanged() {
 1793|       |        // Self-loops should still use Bézier routing with 7 points.
 1794|      2|        let graph = layout_ok("a -> a");
 1795|      2|        let a = find_node_by_label(&graph, "a");
 1796|      2|        for &eidx in &graph.edges {
 1797|      2|            if let Some((s, d)) = graph.graph.edge_endpoints(eidx) {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (1797:20): [True: 2, False: 0]
  ------------------
 1798|      2|                if s == a && d == a {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (1798:20): [True: 2, False: 0]
  |  Branch (1798:30): [True: 2, False: 0]
  ------------------
 1799|      2|                    let edata = &graph.graph[eidx];
 1800|      2|                    assert_eq!(
 1801|       |                        edata.route_type,
 1802|       |                        RouteType::Bezier,
 1803|       |                        "self-loop should use Bezier routing"
 1804|       |                    );
 1805|      2|                    assert_eq!(
 1806|      2|                        edata.route.len(),
 1807|       |                        7,
 1808|       |                        "self-loop should have 7 points, got {}",
 1809|      0|                        edata.route.len()
 1810|       |                    );
 1811|      2|                    return;
 1812|      0|                }
 1813|      0|            }
 1814|       |        }
 1815|      0|        panic!("no self-loop edge found");
 1816|      2|    }

 1835|       |    #[test]
 1836|      2|    fn test_ortho_architecture_integration() {
 1837|       |        // Full architecture example — all edges should be routed without panics.
 1838|      2|        let source = "\
 1839|      2|direction: right
 1840|      2|
 1841|      2|network: {
 1842|      2|  style.stroke: \"#4a90d9\"
 1843|      2|  load_balancer.shape: diamond
 1844|      2|  load_balancer.style.fill: \"#ffd700\"
 1845|      2|  server1
 1846|      2|  server2
 1847|      2|  load_balancer -> server1: route
 1848|      2|  load_balancer -> server2: route
 1849|      2|}
 1850|      2|
 1851|      2|database.shape: cylinder
 1852|      2|database.style.fill: \"#228b22\"
 1853|      2|
 1854|      2|network.server1 -> database: query
 1855|      2|network.server2 -> database: query
 1856|      2|";
 1857|      2|        let graph = layout_ok(source);
 1858|       |
 1859|      8|        for &eidx in &graph.edges {
                                   ^2
 1860|      8|            let edata = &graph.graph[eidx];
 1861|      8|            if let Some((s, d)) = graph.graph.edge_endpoints(eidx) {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (1861:20): [True: 8, False: 0]
  ------------------
 1862|      8|                if s == d {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (1862:20): [True: 0, False: 8]
  ------------------
 1863|      0|                    continue; // skip self-loops
 1864|      8|                }
 1865|      0|            }
 1866|      8|            assert!(
 1867|      8|                !edata.route.is_empty(),
 1868|       |                "every non-self-loop edge should have route points"
 1869|       |            );
 1870|      8|            assert_eq!(
 1871|       |                edata.route_type,
 1872|       |                RouteType::Orthogonal,
 1873|       |                "every non-self-loop edge should be Orthogonal"
 1874|       |            );
 1875|       |        }
 1876|      2|    }

 1895|       |    // --- Stage 4: label placement tests --------------------------------------
 1896|       |
 1897|       |    #[test]
 1898|      2|    fn test_ortho_label_horizontal() {
 1899|       |        // Horizontal layout with labeled edge: label should be between nodes
 1900|       |        // and offset above the line (negative Y offset)
 1901|      2|        let graph = layout_ok("direction: right\na -> b: hello");
 1902|      2|        let edge = find_edge_between(&graph, "a", "b");
 1903|      2|        let label_pos = edge
 1904|      2|            .label_position
 1905|      2|            .expect("labeled edge should have position");
 1906|       |
 1907|      2|        let a_rect = graph.graph[find_node_by_label(&graph, "a")].box_.unwrap();
 1908|      2|        let b_rect = graph.graph[find_node_by_label(&graph, "b")].box_.unwrap();
 1909|       |
 1910|       |        // Label x should be between the two nodes
 1911|      2|        assert!(
 1912|      2|            label_pos.x > a_rect.right() && label_pos.x < b_rect.x,
  ------------------
  |  Branch (1912:13): [True: 2, False: 0]
  |  Branch (1912:45): [True: 2, False: 0]
  ------------------
 1913|       |            "label x ({}) should be between a.right ({}) and b.left ({})",
 1914|       |            label_pos.x,
 1915|      0|            a_rect.right(),
 1916|       |            b_rect.x
 1917|       |        );
 1918|       |
 1919|       |        // For a 2-point straight horizontal route, the label should be offset
 1920|       |        // away from the line (either above or below).
 1921|       |        //
 1922|       |        // NOTE: The design doc (V4 §7.2) predicted side=-1 (above) would win
 1923|       |        // via the >= tie-breaker for symmetric layouts. In practice, the
 1924|       |        // overshoot scoring breaks the tie in favor of side=+1 (below) because
 1925|       |        // the edge route is not at the vertical center of the combined node
 1926|       |        // bounding box — the label overshoots less on the side with more room.
 1927|      2|        assert_eq!(edge.route.len(), 2, "straight edge should have 2 points");
 1928|      2|        let line_y = (edge.route[0].y + edge.route[1].y) / 2.0;
 1929|      2|        assert!(
 1930|      2|            (label_pos.y - line_y).abs() > 1.0,
 1931|       |            "label y ({}) should be offset from the line y ({})",
 1932|       |            label_pos.y,
 1933|       |            line_y
 1934|       |        );
 1935|      2|    }
 1936|       |
 1937|       |    #[test]
 1938|      2|    fn test_ortho_label_vertical() {
 1939|       |        // Vertical layout (default down) with labeled edge: label should be
 1940|       |        // offset away from the line (either left or right).
 1941|       |        //
 1942|       |        // NOTE: The design doc (V4 §7.2) predicted side=-1 (left) would win
 1943|       |        // via the >= tie-breaker for symmetric layouts. In practice, the
 1944|       |        // overshoot scoring breaks the tie in favor of side=+1 (right) because
 1945|       |        // the edge route is not at the horizontal center of the combined node
 1946|       |        // bounding box — the label overshoots less on the side with more room.
 1947|      2|        let graph = layout_ok("a -> b: hello");
 1948|      2|        let edge = find_edge_between(&graph, "a", "b");
 1949|      2|        let label_pos = edge
 1950|      2|            .label_position
 1951|      2|            .expect("labeled edge should have position");
 1952|       |
 1953|      2|        let a_rect = graph.graph[find_node_by_label(&graph, "a")].box_.unwrap();
 1954|      2|        let b_rect = graph.graph[find_node_by_label(&graph, "b")].box_.unwrap();
 1955|       |
 1956|       |        // Label y should be between the two nodes
 1957|      2|        assert!(
 1958|      2|            label_pos.y > a_rect.bottom() && label_pos.y < b_rect.y,
  ------------------
  |  Branch (1958:13): [True: 2, False: 0]
  |  Branch (1958:46): [True: 2, False: 0]
  ------------------
 1959|       |            "label y ({}) should be between a.bottom ({}) and b.top ({})",
 1960|       |            label_pos.y,
 1961|      0|            a_rect.bottom(),
 1962|       |            b_rect.y
 1963|       |        );
 1964|       |
 1965|       |        // For a 2-point straight vertical route, the label should be offset
 1966|       |        // away from the line (either left or right)
 1967|      2|        assert_eq!(edge.route.len(), 2, "straight edge should have 2 points");
 1968|      2|        let line_x = (edge.route[0].x + edge.route[1].x) / 2.0;
 1969|      2|        assert!(
 1970|      2|            (label_pos.x - line_x).abs() > 1.0,
 1971|       |            "label x ({}) should be offset from the line x ({})",
 1972|       |            label_pos.x,
 1973|       |            line_x
 1974|       |        );
 1975|      2|    }
 1976|       |
 1977|       |    #[test]
 1978|      2|    fn test_ortho_label_unlabeled() {
 1979|       |        // Unlabeled edge should have no label position
 1980|      2|        let graph = layout_ok("direction: right\na -> b");
 1981|      2|        let edge = find_edge_between(&graph, "a", "b");

 1983|      2|            edge.label_position.is_none(),
 1984|       |            "unlabeled edge should have no label position"
 1985|       |        );

 1988|       |    #[test]
 1989|      2|    fn test_ortho_labels_spread() {
 1990|       |        // Multiple labeled edges in the same gap should have different label positions
 1991|      2|        let graph = layout_ok("direction: right\na -> c: first\nb -> c: second");
 1992|      2|        let edge_ac = find_edge_between(&graph, "a", "c");
 1993|      2|        let edge_bc = find_edge_between(&graph, "b", "c");
 1994|       |
 1995|      2|        let pos_ac = edge_ac.label_position.expect("a->c should have label");
 1996|      2|        let pos_bc = edge_bc.label_position.expect("b->c should have label");
 1997|       |
 1998|       |        // Labels should not be at the exact same position
 1999|      2|        let dist = ((pos_ac.x - pos_bc.x).powi(2) + (pos_ac.y - pos_bc.y).powi(2)).sqrt();
 2000|      2|        assert!(
 2001|      2|            dist > 1.0,
 2002|       |            "labels should be spread apart, got distance {}",
 2003|       |            dist
 2004|       |        );
 2005|      2|    }
 2006|       |
 2007|       |    #[test]
 2008|      2|    fn test_ortho_channel_spread() {
 2009|       |        // Two edges from different sources to nodes at the same rank should
 2010|       |        // get different channel positions (spread in the same gap).
 2011|      2|        let graph = layout_ok("direction: right\na -> c\nb -> c");
 2012|      2|        let edge_ac = find_edge_between(&graph, "a", "c");
 2013|      2|        let edge_bc = find_edge_between(&graph, "b", "c");
 2014|       |
 2015|      2|        assert_eq!(edge_ac.route_type, RouteType::Orthogonal);
 2016|      2|        assert_eq!(edge_bc.route_type, RouteType::Orthogonal);
 2017|       |
 2018|       |        // Both edges should have been routed
 2019|      2|        assert!(!edge_ac.route.is_empty());
 2020|      2|        assert!(!edge_bc.route.is_empty());
 2021|       |
 2022|       |        // If both edges have 4 points (3-segment path), their channel positions
 2023|       |        // (the x coordinate of the middle segment) should differ.
 2024|      2|        if edge_ac.route.len() == 4 && edge_bc.route.len() == 4 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (2024:12): [True: 2, False: 0]
  |  Branch (2024:40): [True: 2, False: 0]
  ------------------
 2025|       |            // Channel segment is route[1].x to route[2].x for horizontal
 2026|      2|            let channel_ac = edge_ac.route[1].x;
 2027|      2|            let channel_bc = edge_bc.route[1].x;
 2028|       |            // They may be the same if both edges are straight lines
 2029|       |            // but if they use channels, the positions should be allocated
 2030|       |            // (this is a best-effort check)
 2031|       |            // At minimum, both should have valid positions
 2032|      2|            assert!(channel_ac.is_finite(), "channel_ac should be finite");
 2033|      2|            assert!(channel_bc.is_finite(), "channel_bc should be finite");
 2034|      0|        }
 2035|      2|    }

 2037|       |    #[test]
 2038|      2|    fn test_ortho_deep_nesting() {
 2039|       |        // 3-level nesting with cross-level edge.
 2040|       |        // outer -> inner -> leaf, with an edge from a different top-level node to the leaf.
 2041|      2|        let graph = layout_ok("outer: { inner: { leaf } }\npeer\npeer -> outer.inner.leaf");
 2042|       |
 2043|      2|        let edge = find_edge_between(&graph, "peer", "leaf");

 2047|       |            "deep cross-level edge should have route points"
 2048|       |        );

 2051|      2|            edge.route.len() >= 2,
 2052|       |            "deep nesting edge should have >= 2 points, got {}",
 2053|      0|            edge.route.len()
 2054|       |        );

 2057|       |    // --- label placement fix tests (V3 design doc, step 10) ------------------
 2058|       |
 2059|       |    #[test]
 2060|      2|    fn test_label_at_route_midpoint() {
 2061|       |        // For a 4-point route, verify the label is at ~50% of total arc length,
 2062|       |        // NOT at the enter segment midpoint. Use a multi-node horizontal layout
 2063|       |        // where at least one edge has a 4-point route.
 2064|      2|        let graph = layout_ok("direction: right\na -> c\nb -> c");
 2065|       |
 2066|       |        // Find an edge with a 4-point route (3-segment S-shape)
 2067|      2|        let edge_ac = find_edge_between(&graph, "a", "c");
 2068|      2|        let edge_bc = find_edge_between(&graph, "b", "c");
 2069|       |
 2070|       |        // Pick whichever edge has a 4-point route (the offset one)
 2071|      2|        let (edge, src_label, dst_label) =
 2072|      2|            if edge_ac.route.len() == 4 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (2072:16): [True: 2, False: 0]
  ------------------
 2073|      2|                (edge_ac, "a", "c")
 2074|      0|            } else if edge_bc.route.len() == 4 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (2074:23): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
 2075|      0|                (edge_bc, "b", "c")
 2076|       |            } else {
 2077|       |                // Both edges are 2-point straight lines; this test needs a 4-point route.
 2078|       |                // Add a label and re-layout to force the geometry check on the labeled version.
 2079|      0|                let graph2 = layout_ok("direction: right\na -> c: label\nb -> c");
 2080|      0|                let e = find_edge_between(&graph2, "a", "c");
 2081|      0|                if e.route.len() == 4 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (2081:20): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
 2082|      0|                    let src_rect = graph2.graph[find_node_by_label(&graph2, "a")].box_.unwrap();
 2083|      0|                    let dst_rect = graph2.graph[find_node_by_label(&graph2, "c")].box_.unwrap();
 2084|      0|                    let label_pos = e.label_position.expect("labeled edge should have position");
 2085|      0|                    let gap_mid_x = (src_rect.right() + dst_rect.x) / 2.0;
 2086|       |                    // Label x should be near the gap midpoint (within 40% of gap width)
 2087|      0|                    let gap_width = (dst_rect.x - src_rect.right()).abs();
 2088|      0|                    assert!(
 2089|      0|                    (label_pos.x - gap_mid_x).abs() < gap_width * 0.4,
 2090|       |                    "label x ({}) should be near gap midpoint ({}) within 40% of gap width ({})",
 2091|       |                    label_pos.x, gap_mid_x, gap_width
 2092|       |                );
 2093|      0|                    return;
 2094|      0|                }
 2095|       |                // If still no 4-point route, the test passes vacuously
 2096|      0|                return;
 2097|       |            };
 2098|       |
 2099|       |        // For the found 4-point edge, add a label and verify
 2100|      2|        let labeled_src = format!(
 2101|       |            "direction: right\n{} -> {}: lbl\nb -> c",
 2102|       |            src_label, dst_label
 2103|       |        );
 2104|      2|        let graph2 = layout_ok(&labeled_src);
 2105|      2|        let edge2 = find_edge_between(&graph2, src_label, dst_label);
 2106|       |
 2107|      2|        if edge2.route.len() >= 4 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (2107:12): [True: 2, False: 0]
  ------------------
 2108|      2|            let src_rect = graph2.graph[find_node_by_label(&graph2, src_label)]
 2109|      2|                .box_
 2110|      2|                .unwrap();
 2111|      2|            let dst_rect = graph2.graph[find_node_by_label(&graph2, dst_label)]
 2112|      2|                .box_
 2113|      2|                .unwrap();
 2114|      2|            let label_pos = edge2
 2115|      2|                .label_position
 2116|      2|                .expect("labeled edge should have position");
 2117|       |
 2118|       |            // The label x should be near the midpoint of the gap between source and dest
 2119|      2|            let gap_mid_x = (src_rect.right() + dst_rect.x) / 2.0;
 2120|      2|            let gap_width = (dst_rect.x - src_rect.right()).abs();
 2121|      2|            assert!(
 2122|      2|                (label_pos.x - gap_mid_x).abs() < gap_width * 0.4,
 2123|       |                "label x ({}) should be near gap midpoint ({}) within 40% of gap width ({})",
 2124|       |                label_pos.x,
 2125|       |                gap_mid_x,
 2126|       |                gap_width
 2127|       |            );
 2128|      0|        }
 2129|      2|        let _ = edge; // suppress unused warning
 2130|      2|    }
 2131|       |
 2132|       |    #[test]
 2133|      2|    fn test_label_offset_horizontal() {
 2134|       |        // For a horizontal 2-point route, verify the label is offset away from
 2135|       |        // the route line by at least label_height / 2 (on either side).
 2136|       |        //
 2137|       |        // NOTE: The design doc (V4 §7.2) predicted side=-1 (above) would win
 2138|       |        // for symmetric layouts. In practice, the overshoot scoring breaks the
 2139|       |        // tie in favor of the side with more room within the node bounding box.
 2140|      2|        let graph = layout_ok("direction: right\na -> b: hello");
 2141|      2|        let edge = find_edge_between(&graph, "a", "b");
 2142|      2|        let label_pos = edge
 2143|      2|            .label_position
 2144|      2|            .expect("labeled edge should have position");
 2145|       |
 2146|      2|        assert_eq!(
 2147|      2|            edge.route.len(),
 2148|       |            2,
 2149|       |            "straight horizontal edge should have 2 points"
 2150|       |        );
 2151|      2|        let line_y = (edge.route[0].y + edge.route[1].y) / 2.0;
 2152|       |
 2153|       |        // Label should be offset from the line (either above or below)
 2154|      2|        let offset = (label_pos.y - line_y).abs();
 2155|      2|        assert!(
 2156|      2|            offset >= edge.label_height / 2.0,
 2157|       |            "vertical offset ({}) should be >= label_height/2 ({})",
 2158|       |            offset,
 2159|      0|            edge.label_height / 2.0
 2160|       |        );
 2161|      2|    }
 2162|       |
 2163|       |    #[test]
 2164|      2|    fn test_label_offset_vertical() {
 2165|       |        // For a vertical 2-point route, verify the label is offset away from
 2166|       |        // the route line by at least label_width / 2 (on either side).
 2167|       |        //
 2168|       |        // NOTE: The design doc (V4 §7.2) predicted side=-1 (left) would win
 2169|       |        // for symmetric layouts. In practice, the overshoot scoring breaks the
 2170|       |        // tie in favor of the side with more room within the node bounding box.
 2171|      2|        let graph = layout_ok("a -> b: hello");
 2172|      2|        let edge = find_edge_between(&graph, "a", "b");
 2173|      2|        let label_pos = edge
 2174|      2|            .label_position
 2175|      2|            .expect("labeled edge should have position");
 2176|       |
 2177|      2|        assert_eq!(
 2178|      2|            edge.route.len(),
 2179|       |            2,
 2180|       |            "straight vertical edge should have 2 points"
 2181|       |        );
 2182|      2|        let line_x = (edge.route[0].x + edge.route[1].x) / 2.0;
 2183|       |
 2184|       |        // Label should be offset from the line (either left or right)
 2185|      2|        let offset = (label_pos.x - line_x).abs();
 2186|      2|        assert!(
 2187|      2|            offset >= edge.label_width / 2.0,
 2188|       |            "horizontal offset ({}) should be >= label_width/2 ({})",
 2189|       |            offset,
 2190|      0|            edge.label_width / 2.0
 2191|       |        );
 2192|      2|    }

 2194|       |    #[test]
 2195|      2|    fn test_label_no_node_overlap() {
 2196|       |        // The bug test. Architecture diagram where labels frequently overlapped
 2197|       |        // destination nodes. Verify that no label bounding rect overlaps any
 2198|       |        // non-endpoint leaf node's bounding box.
 2199|      2|        let source = "\
 2200|      2|direction: right
 2201|      2|lb: Load Balancer
 2202|      2|gw: API Gateway
 2203|      2|auth: Auth
 2204|      2|redis: Redis
 2205|      2|orders: Orders
 2206|      2|pg: PostgreSQL
 2207|      2|lb -> gw: HTTPS
 2208|      2|gw -> auth: verify
 2209|      2|auth -> redis: sessions
 2210|      2|gw -> orders: route
 2211|      2|orders -> pg: CRUD";
 2212|      2|        let graph = layout_ok(source);
 2213|       |
 2214|       |        // Collect all leaf node rects (non-container, non-root)
 2215|      2|        let leaf_rects: Vec<(NodeIndex, Rect)> = graph
 2216|      2|            .objects
 2217|      2|            .iter()
 2218|     12|            .filter(|&&idx| idx != graph.root && !graph.graph[idx].is_container)
                           ^2
  ------------------
  |  Branch (2218:29): [True: 12, False: 0]
  ------------------
 2219|     12|            .filter_map(|&idx| graph.graph[idx].box_.map(|r| (idx, r)))
                           ^2
 2220|      2|            .collect();
 2221|       |
 2222|       |        // For each labeled edge, check label bounding rect vs non-endpoint leaf nodes
 2223|      2|        let labeled_edges: Vec<(&str, &str)> = vec![
 2224|      2|            ("Load Balancer", "API Gateway"),
 2225|      2|            ("API Gateway", "Auth"),
 2226|      2|            ("Auth", "Redis"),
 2227|      2|            ("API Gateway", "Orders"),
 2228|      2|            ("Orders", "PostgreSQL"),
 2229|       |        ];
 2230|       |
 2231|     10|        for (src_label, dst_label) in &labeled_edges {
                                                    ^2
 2232|     10|            let src_idx = find_node_by_label(&graph, src_label);
 2233|     10|            let dst_idx = find_node_by_label(&graph, dst_label);
 2234|     10|            let edge = find_edge_between(&graph, src_label, dst_label);
 2235|     10|            let label_pos = edge.label_position.unwrap_or_else(|| {
                                                                                ^0
 2236|      0|                panic!(
 2237|       |                    "edge {} -> {} should have a label position",
 2238|       |                    src_label, dst_label
 2239|       |                )
 2240|       |            });
 2241|     10|            let lw = edge.label_width;
 2242|     10|            let lh = edge.label_height;
 2243|     10|            assert!(
 2244|     10|                lw > 0.0,
 2245|       |                "label_width should be > 0 for {} -> {}",
 2246|       |                src_label,
 2247|       |                dst_label
 2248|       |            );
 2249|     10|            assert!(
 2250|     10|                lh > 0.0,
 2251|       |                "label_height should be > 0 for {} -> {}",
 2252|       |                src_label,
 2253|       |                dst_label
 2254|       |            );
 2255|       |
 2256|       |            // Build label bounding rect with halo padding (matches nudge logic)
 2257|     10|            let halo = LABEL_HALO_PADDING;
 2258|     10|            let label_rect = Rect::new(
 2259|     10|                label_pos.x - lw / 2.0 - halo,
 2260|     10|                label_pos.y - lh / 2.0 - halo,
 2261|     10|                lw + halo * 2.0,
 2262|     10|                lh + halo * 2.0,
 2263|       |            );
 2264|       |
 2265|       |            // Check against all non-endpoint leaf nodes
 2266|     60|            for &(nidx, nrect) in &leaf_rects {
                                                ^10
 2267|     60|                if nidx == src_idx || nidx == dst_idx {
                                                    ^50
  ------------------
  |  Branch (2267:20): [True: 10, False: 50]
  |  Branch (2267:39): [True: 10, False: 40]
  ------------------
 2268|     20|                    continue; // skip endpoints — overlap with endpoints is acceptable
 2269|     40|                }
 2270|     40|                assert!(
 2271|     40|                    !label_rect.intersects(&nrect),
 2272|       |                    "label for edge {} -> {} overlaps non-endpoint node {:?} \
 2273|       |                     (label_rect: x={:.1} y={:.1} w={:.1} h={:.1}, \
 2274|       |                      node_rect: x={:.1} y={:.1} w={:.1} h={:.1})",
 2275|       |                    src_label,
 2276|       |                    dst_label,
 2277|      0|                    graph.graph[nidx].label,
 2278|       |                    label_rect.x,
 2279|       |                    label_rect.y,
 2280|       |                    label_rect.width,
 2281|       |                    label_rect.height,
 2282|       |                    nrect.x,
 2283|       |                    nrect.y,
 2284|       |                    nrect.width,
 2285|       |                    nrect.height,
 2286|       |                );
 2287|       |            }

 2351|       |    #[test]
 2352|      2|    fn test_nudge_finds_clear_position() {
 2353|       |        // Three-node chain where nodes are relatively close. The nudge mechanism
 2354|       |        // should ensure labels don't overlap any non-endpoint leaf node.
 2355|      2|        let graph = layout_ok("direction: right\na -> b: first\nb -> c: second\na -> c: direct");
 2356|       |
 2357|      2|        let node_rects: Vec<(String, crate::geo::Rect)> = graph
 2358|      2|            .objects
 2359|      2|            .iter()
 2360|      6|            .filter(|&&idx| idx != graph.root && !graph.graph[idx].is_container)
                           ^2
  ------------------
  |  Branch (2360:29): [True: 6, False: 0]
  ------------------
 2361|      6|            .filter_map(|&idx| {
                           ^2
 2362|      6|                graph.graph[idx]
 2363|      6|                    .box_
 2364|      6|                    .map(|r| (graph.graph[idx].label.clone(), r))
 2365|      6|            })
 2366|      2|            .collect();
 2367|       |
 2368|      6|        for &eidx in &graph.edges {
                                   ^2
 2369|      6|            let edge = &graph.graph[eidx];
 2370|      6|            let pos = match edge.label_position {
 2371|      6|                Some(p) => p,
 2372|      0|                None => continue,
 2373|       |            };
 2374|      6|            if edge.label_width < 1.0 || edge.label_height < 1.0 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (2374:16): [True: 0, False: 6]
  |  Branch (2374:42): [True: 0, False: 6]
  ------------------
 2375|      0|                continue;
 2376|      6|            }
 2377|       |
 2378|      6|            let (src, dst) = graph.graph.edge_endpoints(eidx).unwrap();
 2379|      6|            let pad = LABEL_HALO_PADDING;
 2380|      6|            let lr = crate::geo::Rect::new(
 2381|      6|                pos.x - edge.label_width / 2.0 - pad,
 2382|      6|                pos.y - edge.label_height / 2.0 - pad,
 2383|      6|                edge.label_width + pad * 2.0,
 2384|      6|                edge.label_height + pad * 2.0,
 2385|       |            );
 2386|       |
 2387|     18|            for &(ref name, rect) in &node_rects {
                                                   ^6
 2388|     18|                let node_idx = find_node_by_label(&graph, name);
 2389|     18|                if node_idx == src || node_idx == dst {
                                                    ^12
  ------------------
  |  Branch (2389:20): [True: 6, False: 12]
  |  Branch (2389:39): [True: 6, False: 6]
  ------------------
 2390|     12|                    continue;
 2391|      6|                }
 2392|      6|                assert!(
 2393|      6|                    !lr.intersects(&rect),
 2394|       |                    "label at ({:.1},{:.1}) overlaps non-endpoint node '{}'",
 2395|       |                    pos.x,
 2396|       |                    pos.y,
 2397|       |                    name
 2398|       |                );
 2399|       |            }
 2400|       |        }
 2401|      2|    }

 2403|       |    #[test]
 2404|      2|    fn test_parallel_labels_no_overlap() {
 2405|       |        // Two labeled edges sharing the same gap: their label rects should not overlap.
 2406|      2|        let graph = layout_ok("direction: right\na -> c: alpha\nb -> c: beta");
 2407|       |
 2408|      2|        let edge_ac = find_edge_between(&graph, "a", "c");
 2409|      2|        let edge_bc = find_edge_between(&graph, "b", "c");
 2410|       |
 2411|      2|        let pos_ac = edge_ac.label_position.expect("a->c label");
 2412|      2|        let pos_bc = edge_bc.label_position.expect("b->c label");
 2413|       |
 2414|      2|        let pad = LABEL_HALO_PADDING;
 2415|      2|        let lr_ac = crate::geo::Rect::new(
 2416|      2|            pos_ac.x - edge_ac.label_width / 2.0 - pad,
 2417|      2|            pos_ac.y - edge_ac.label_height / 2.0 - pad,
 2418|      2|            edge_ac.label_width + pad * 2.0,
 2419|      2|            edge_ac.label_height + pad * 2.0,
 2420|       |        );
 2421|      2|        let lr_bc = crate::geo::Rect::new(
 2422|      2|            pos_bc.x - edge_bc.label_width / 2.0 - pad,
 2423|      2|            pos_bc.y - edge_bc.label_height / 2.0 - pad,
 2424|      2|            edge_bc.label_width + pad * 2.0,
 2425|      2|            edge_bc.label_height + pad * 2.0,
 2426|       |        );
 2427|       |
 2428|      2|        assert!(!lr_ac.intersects(&lr_bc),
 2429|       |            "parallel labels should not overlap: ac at ({:.1},{:.1}) size {:.1}x{:.1}, bc at ({:.1},{:.1}) size {:.1}x{:.1}",
 2430|       |            pos_ac.x, pos_ac.y, edge_ac.label_width, edge_ac.label_height,
 2431|       |            pos_bc.x, pos_bc.y, edge_bc.label_width, edge_bc.label_height);
 2432|      2|    }

 2436|       |    #[test]
 2437|      2|    fn test_detour_avoids_intermediate_node() {
 2438|       |        // 3-node vertical chain: a -> b, a -> c, b -> c
 2439|       |        // The a -> c edge should not pass through b's rect
 2440|      2|        let graph = layout_ok("a -> b\na -> c\nb -> c");
 2441|       |
 2442|      2|        let b_rect = graph.graph[find_node_by_label(&graph, "b")].box_.unwrap();
 2443|      2|        let edge_ac = find_edge_between(&graph, "a", "c");
 2444|       |
 2445|       |        // Verify route has more than 2 points (detour was applied)
 2446|      2|        assert!(
 2447|      2|            edge_ac.route.len() > 2,
 2448|       |            "a->c should have a detour route (>2 points), got {}",
 2449|      0|            edge_ac.route.len()
 2450|       |        );
 2451|       |
 2452|       |        // Check that no segment of a->c route intersects b's rect
 2453|     10|        for i in 0..edge_ac.route.len().saturating_sub(1) {
                               ^2 ^2
 2454|     10|            assert!(
 2455|     10|                !ortho_segment_hits_rect(&edge_ac.route[i], &edge_ac.route[i + 1], &b_rect, 2.0),
 2456|       |                "a->c route segment {}-{} at ({:.1},{:.1})->({:.1},{:.1}) should not pass through b's rect \
 2457|       |                 (x={:.1} y={:.1} w={:.1} h={:.1})",
 2458|      0|                i, i + 1,
 2459|      0|                edge_ac.route[i].x, edge_ac.route[i].y,
 2460|      0|                edge_ac.route[i + 1].x, edge_ac.route[i + 1].y,
 2461|       |                b_rect.x, b_rect.y, b_rect.width, b_rect.height,
 2462|       |            );
 2463|       |        }
 2464|      2|    }

 2493|      2|    fn test_detour_no_false_positive() {

 2505|       |
 2506|       |    // --- V4 design doc tests (Section 7.1) -----------------------------------
 2507|       |
 2508|       |    /// Parse the viewBox attribute from an SVG string.
 2509|       |    /// Returns (x, y, width, height).
 2510|      4|    fn parse_viewbox(svg: &str) -> (f64, f64, f64, f64) {
 2511|      4|        let start = svg.find("viewBox=\"").expect("SVG should have viewBox") + 9;
 2512|      4|        let end = svg[start..].find('"').expect("viewBox should close") + start;
 2513|      4|        let parts: Vec<f64> = svg[start..end]
 2514|      4|            .split_whitespace()
 2515|     16|            .map(|s| {
                           ^4
 2516|     16|                s.parse::<f64>()
 2517|     16|                    .expect("viewBox component should be numeric")
 2518|     16|            })
 2519|      4|            .collect();
 2520|      4|        assert_eq!(parts.len(), 4, "viewBox should have 4 components");
 2521|      4|        (parts[0], parts[1], parts[2], parts[3])
 2522|      4|    }
 2523|       |
 2524|       |    /// Build a viewBox Rect from parsed (x, y, w, h) tuple.
 2525|      4|    fn viewbox_rect(vb: (f64, f64, f64, f64)) -> crate::geo::Rect {
 2526|      4|        crate::geo::Rect::new(vb.0, vb.1, vb.2, vb.3)
 2527|      4|    }
 2528|       |
 2529|       |    /// Build a label bounding rect (with halo padding) for an edge.
 2530|     12|    fn edge_label_rect(edge: &crate::graph::D2EdgeData) -> crate::geo::Rect {
 2531|     12|        let pos = edge
 2532|     12|            .label_position
 2533|     12|            .expect("edge should have label_position");
 2534|     12|        let pad = LABEL_HALO_PADDING;
 2535|     12|        crate::geo::Rect::new(
 2536|     12|            pos.x - edge.label_width / 2.0 - pad,
 2537|     12|            pos.y - edge.label_height / 2.0 - pad,
 2538|     12|            edge.label_width + pad * 2.0,
 2539|     12|            edge.label_height + pad * 2.0,
 2540|       |        )
 2541|     12|    }
 2542|       |
 2543|       |    /// Check that rect `inner` is fully contained within rect `outer`.
 2544|      8|    fn rect_contains(outer: &crate::geo::Rect, inner: &crate::geo::Rect) -> bool {
 2545|      8|        inner.x >= outer.x
  ------------------
  |  Branch (2545:9): [True: 8, False: 0]
  ------------------
 2546|      8|            && inner.y >= outer.y
  ------------------
  |  Branch (2546:16): [True: 8, False: 0]
  ------------------
 2547|      8|            && inner.right() <= outer.right()
  ------------------
  |  Branch (2547:16): [True: 8, False: 0]
  ------------------
 2548|      8|            && inner.bottom() <= outer.bottom()
 2549|      8|    }
 2550|       |
 2551|       |    #[test]
 2552|      2|    fn test_viewbox_includes_labels() {
 2553|       |        // Parse SVG output from a simple labeled-edge diagram, verify the viewBox
 2554|       |        // contains all edge label bounding rects.
 2555|      2|        let graph = layout_ok("a -> b: hello");
 2556|      2|        let svg = crate::svg_render::render(&graph, &RenderOptions::default());
 2557|       |
 2558|      2|        let vb = parse_viewbox(&svg);
 2559|      2|        let vb_rect = viewbox_rect(vb);
 2560|       |
 2561|       |        // Every labeled edge's bounding rect must fit within the viewBox
 2562|      2|        for &eidx in &graph.edges {
 2563|      2|            let edge = &graph.graph[eidx];
 2564|      2|            if edge.label_position.is_none() || edge.label_width <= 0.0 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (2564:16): [True: 0, False: 2]
  |  Branch (2564:49): [True: 0, False: 2]
  ------------------
 2565|      0|                continue;
 2566|      2|            }
 2567|      2|            let lr = edge_label_rect(edge);
 2568|      2|            assert!(
 2569|      2|                rect_contains(&vb_rect, &lr),
 2570|       |                "label rect (x={:.1} y={:.1} w={:.1} h={:.1}) should be contained in \
 2571|       |                 viewBox (x={:.1} y={:.1} w={:.1} h={:.1})",
 2572|       |                lr.x,
 2573|       |                lr.y,
 2574|       |                lr.width,
 2575|       |                lr.height,
 2576|       |                vb_rect.x,
 2577|       |                vb_rect.y,

 2584|       |    #[test]
 2585|      2|    fn test_label_not_clipped() {
 2586|       |        // The motivating example: 4-node vertical chain with labels on each edge.
 2587|       |        // Verify no label bounding rect extends beyond the SVG viewBox.
 2588|      2|        let source = "\
 2589|      2|client -> api: REST/JSON
 2590|      2|api -> cache: get/set
 2591|      2|cache -> db: SQL queries";
 2592|      2|        let graph = layout_ok(source);
 2593|      2|        let svg = crate::svg_render::render(&graph, &RenderOptions::default());
 2594|       |
 2595|      2|        let vb = parse_viewbox(&svg);
 2596|      2|        let vb_rect = viewbox_rect(vb);
 2597|       |
 2598|      2|        let labeled_edges: Vec<(&str, &str)> =
 2599|      2|            vec![("client", "api"), ("api", "cache"), ("cache", "db")];
 2600|       |
 2601|      6|        for (src_label, dst_label) in &labeled_edges {
                                                    ^2
 2602|      6|            let edge = find_edge_between(&graph, src_label, dst_label);
 2603|      6|            assert!(
 2604|      6|                edge.label_position.is_some(),
 2605|       |                "edge {} -> {} should have a label position",
 2606|       |                src_label,
 2607|       |                dst_label
 2608|       |            );
 2609|      6|            let lr = edge_label_rect(edge);
 2610|      6|            assert!(
 2611|      6|                rect_contains(&vb_rect, &lr),
 2612|       |                "label for {} -> {} (x={:.1} y={:.1} w={:.1} h={:.1}) extends beyond \
 2613|       |                 viewBox (x={:.1} y={:.1} w={:.1} h={:.1})",
 2614|       |                src_label,
 2615|       |                dst_label,
 2616|       |                lr.x,

 2618|       |                lr.width,

 2628|       |    /// Test that Fix B places labels on the side with better clearance when
 2629|       |    /// the layout is asymmetric (one side blocked by a node).
 2630|       |    #[test]
 2631|      2|    fn test_label_side_asymmetric() {
 2632|       |        // Vertical layout: a at top, b at bottom, c to the right of the a->b edge.
 2633|       |        // The label on a->b should avoid c by going left (or at least not overlapping c).
 2634|      2|        let source = "\
 2635|      2|direction: down
 2636|      2|a -> b: hi
 2637|      2|a -> c
 2638|      2|";
 2639|      2|        let graph = layout_ok(source);
 2640|       |
 2641|      2|        let edge = find_edge_between(&graph, "a", "b");
 2642|      2|        let label_pos = edge.label_position.expect("label position should exist");
 2643|       |
 2644|       |        // c is to the right of the a->b edge. The label should not overlap c.
 2645|      2|        let c_rect = graph.graph[find_node_by_label(&graph, "c")]
 2646|      2|            .box_
 2647|      2|            .expect("c should have a box");
 2648|      2|        let lr = edge_label_rect(edge);
 2649|      2|        assert!(
 2650|      2|            !lr.intersects(&c_rect),
 2651|       |            "label rect {:?} should not overlap node c rect {:?}",
 2652|       |            lr,
 2653|       |            c_rect
 2654|       |        );
 2655|       |
 2656|       |        // Verify the label is offset from the edge line (not zero — "hi" is 2 chars,
 2657|       |        // well below the 72px Fix C threshold)
 2658|      2|        assert!(
 2659|      2|            edge.label_width < 72.0,
 2660|       |            "label should be below Fix C threshold"
 2661|       |        );
 2662|      2|        let line_x = (edge.route[0].x + edge.route.last().unwrap().x) / 2.0;
 2663|      2|        let x_offset = (label_pos.x - line_x).abs();
 2664|      2|        assert!(
 2665|      2|            x_offset > 1.0,
 2666|       |            "label should be offset from the edge line, got offset {}",
 2667|       |            x_offset
 2668|       |        );
 2669|      2|    }
 2670|       |
 2671|       |    #[test]
 2672|      2|    fn test_wide_label_on_edge() {
 2673|       |        // "SQL queries" is 11 chars at default font (7.7px/char) = ~84.7px width.
 2674|       |        // Natural offset = 84.7/2 + 4 = 46.35 > MAX_LABEL_OFFSET (40).
 2675|       |        // Fix C should center it on the edge (offset = 0).
 2676|      2|        let graph = layout_ok("a -> b: SQL queries");
 2677|      2|        let edge = find_edge_between(&graph, "a", "b");
 2678|      2|        let label_pos = edge
 2679|      2|            .label_position
 2680|      2|            .expect("labeled edge should have label_position");
 2681|       |
 2682|       |        // Default layout is vertical. For a vertical edge, the label is offset
 2683|       |        // in the x-direction. The route line x is at the midpoint of the route.
 2684|      2|        assert_eq!(
 2685|      2|            edge.route.len(),
 2686|       |            2,
 2687|       |            "straight vertical edge should have 2 points"
 2688|       |        );
 2689|      2|        let line_x = (edge.route[0].x + edge.route[1].x) / 2.0;
 2690|       |
 2691|       |        // With Fix C, a wide label should have offset = 0 (centered on line).
 2692|       |        // Allow a small tolerance for floating-point and nudge adjustments.
 2693|      2|        let x_offset = (label_pos.x - line_x).abs();
 2694|      2|        assert!(
 2695|      2|            x_offset < 5.0,
 2696|       |            "wide label ('SQL queries', width={:.1}) should be centered on edge \
 2697|       |             (line_x={:.1}, label_x={:.1}, offset={:.1}), expected offset < 5.0",
 2698|       |            edge.label_width,
 2699|       |            line_x,
 2700|       |            label_pos.x,
 2701|       |            x_offset,
 2702|       |        );
 2703|       |
 2704|       |        // Verify the label width is indeed > 72px (the threshold)
 2705|      2|        assert!(
 2706|      2|            edge.label_width > 72.0,
 2707|       |            "label width ({:.1}) should exceed the 72px threshold for on-edge placement",
 2708|       |            edge.label_width,
 2709|       |        );
 2710|      2|    }
 2711|       |
 2712|       |    /// Test that Fix B's overshoot tie-breaking prefers placing labels toward
 2713|       |    /// the interior of the diagram (less overshoot beyond node bounding box).
 2714|       |    #[test]
 2715|      2|    fn test_overshoot_prefers_inward() {
 2716|       |        // Horizontal layout: a -> b -> c -> d, with a->b at the left boundary.
 2717|       |        // The label on a->b should prefer the side closer to diagram center.
 2718|      2|        let source = "\
 2719|      2|direction: right
 2720|      2|a -> b: tag
 2721|      2|b -> c
 2722|      2|c -> d
 2723|      2|";
 2724|      2|        let graph = layout_ok(source);
 2725|       |
 2726|      2|        let edge = find_edge_between(&graph, "a", "b");
 2727|      2|        let _label_pos = edge.label_position.expect("label position");
 2728|       |
 2729|       |        // Compute the node bounding box
 2730|      2|        let node_bounds = {
 2731|      2|            let mut min_x = f64::INFINITY;
 2732|      2|            let mut min_y = f64::INFINITY;
 2733|      2|            let mut max_x = f64::NEG_INFINITY;
 2734|      2|            let mut max_y = f64::NEG_INFINITY;
 2735|      8|            for &idx in &graph.objects {
                                      ^2
 2736|      8|                if idx == graph.root || graph.graph[idx].is_container {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (2736:20): [True: 0, False: 8]
  |  Branch (2736:41): [True: 0, False: 8]
  ------------------
 2737|      0|                    continue;
 2738|      8|                }
 2739|      8|                if let Some(r) = graph.graph[idx].box_ {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (2739:24): [True: 8, False: 0]
  ------------------
 2740|      8|                    min_x = min_x.min(r.x);
 2741|      8|                    min_y = min_y.min(r.y);
 2742|      8|                    max_x = max_x.max(r.x + r.width);
 2743|      8|                    max_y = max_y.max(r.y + r.height);
 2744|      8|                }
                              ^0
 2745|       |            }
 2746|      2|            (min_x, min_y, max_x, max_y)
 2747|       |        };
 2748|       |
 2749|       |        // The a->b edge is horizontal, so the label offset is vertical (up or down).
 2750|       |        // Compute the label rect's overshoot in each vertical direction.
 2751|      2|        let lr = edge_label_rect(edge);
 2752|      2|        let top_overshoot = (node_bounds.1 - lr.y).max(0.0);
 2753|      2|        let bottom_overshoot = (lr.y + lr.height - node_bounds.3).max(0.0);
 2754|       |
 2755|       |        // The total overshoot should be small — the label should stay near the
 2756|       |        // diagram's vertical extent. With 4 nodes in a horizontal row, the
 2757|       |        // vertical extent is just the node height, and the label should be
 2758|       |        // placed to minimize vertical overshoot.
 2759|      2|        let total_overshoot = top_overshoot + bottom_overshoot;
 2760|      2|        assert!(
 2761|      2|            total_overshoot < edge.label_height + LABEL_HALO_PADDING * 2.0,
 2762|       |            "total vertical overshoot ({:.1}) should be less than one label height ({:.1})",
 2763|       |            total_overshoot,
 2764|      0|            edge.label_height + LABEL_HALO_PADDING * 2.0
 2765|       |        );
 2766|      2|    }
 2767|       |

    1|       |//! Geometry primitives for D2 layout and rendering.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! All coordinates use top-left origin convention:
    4|       |//! - x increases rightward
    5|       |//! - y increases downward
    6|       |//! - Rect.x/y is the top-left corner of the rectangle
    7|       |//!
    8|       |//! This matches SVG's coordinate system, so no conversion is needed
    9|       |//! when generating SVG output.
   10|       |
   11|       |/// A 2D point.
   12|       |#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq)]
   13|       |pub struct Point {
   14|       |    pub x: f64,
   15|       |    pub y: f64,
   16|       |}
   17|       |
   18|       |impl Point {
   19|  3.92k|    pub fn new(x: f64, y: f64) -> Self {
   20|  3.92k|        Self { x, y }
   21|  3.92k|    }
   22|       |
   23|       |    /// Euclidean distance to another point.
   24|    428|    pub fn distance_to(&self, other: &Point) -> f64 {
   25|    428|        let dx = self.x - other.x;
   26|    428|        let dy = self.y - other.y;
   27|    428|        (dx * dx + dy * dy).sqrt()
   28|    428|    }
   29|       |}
   30|       |
   31|       |/// An axis-aligned rectangle (top-left origin).
   32|       |#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq)]
   33|       |pub struct Rect {
   34|       |    pub x: f64,
   35|       |    pub y: f64,
   36|       |    pub width: f64,
   37|       |    pub height: f64,
   38|       |}
   39|       |
   40|       |impl Rect {
   41|  4.14k|    pub fn new(x: f64, y: f64, width: f64, height: f64) -> Self {
   42|  4.14k|        Self {
   43|  4.14k|            x,
   44|  4.14k|            y,
   45|  4.14k|            width,
   46|  4.14k|            height,
   47|  4.14k|        }
   48|  4.14k|    }
   49|       |
   50|       |    /// Center point of the rectangle.
   51|  1.80k|    pub fn center(&self) -> Point {
   52|  1.80k|        Point {
   53|  1.80k|            x: self.x + self.width / 2.0,
   54|  1.80k|            y: self.y + self.height / 2.0,
   55|  1.80k|        }
   56|  1.80k|    }
   57|       |
   58|       |    /// Right edge x-coordinate.
   59|  21.7k|    pub fn right(&self) -> f64 {
   60|  21.7k|        self.x + self.width
   61|  21.7k|    }
   62|       |
   63|       |    /// Bottom edge y-coordinate.
   64|  22.8k|    pub fn bottom(&self) -> f64 {
   65|  22.8k|        self.y + self.height
   66|  22.8k|    }
   67|       |

   70|    294|        self.x < other.right()

   74|    294|    }
   75|       |
   76|       |    /// Minimum axis-aligned separation between two rects.
   77|       |    /// Returns positive when separated, negative when overlapping.
   78|  5.37k|    pub fn min_separation(&self, other: &Rect) -> f64 {
   79|  5.37k|        let sep_x = if self.right() <= other.x {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (79:24): [True: 672, False: 1.52k]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (79:24): [True: 1.31k, False: 1.86k]
  ------------------

  ------------------

  ------------------
  |  Branch (81:19): [True: 867, False: 1.00k]
  ------------------
   82|  1.46k|            self.x - other.right()
   83|       |        } else {
   84|  1.92k|            -(self.right().min(other.right()) - self.x.max(other.x))
   85|       |        };

  ------------------
  |  Branch (87:24): [True: 836, False: 1.35k]

  |  Branch (87:24): [True: 646, False: 2.53k]

   89|  3.88k|        } else if other.bottom() <= self.y {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (89:19): [True: 600, False: 756]
  ------------------

   97|  1.29k|            sep_x.min(sep_y)
   98|       |        } else {
   99|       |            // At least one axis overlaps: take the larger (least negative or positive)
  100|  4.07k|            sep_x.max(sep_y)
  101|       |        }
  102|  5.37k|    }

  131|       |    #[test]
  132|      2|    fn test_rect_intersects() {
  133|       |        // Overlapping rects
  134|      2|        let a = Rect::new(0.0, 0.0, 10.0, 10.0);
  135|      2|        let b = Rect::new(5.0, 5.0, 10.0, 10.0);
  136|      2|        assert!(a.intersects(&b));
  137|      2|        assert!(b.intersects(&a));
  138|       |
  139|       |        // Touching (shared edge) — not intersecting (strict)
  140|      2|        let c = Rect::new(10.0, 0.0, 10.0, 10.0);
  141|      2|        assert!(!a.intersects(&c), "touching rects should not intersect");
  142|      2|        assert!(!c.intersects(&a));
  143|       |
  144|       |        // Fully separated
  145|      2|        let d = Rect::new(20.0, 20.0, 5.0, 5.0);
  146|      2|        assert!(!a.intersects(&d));
  147|      2|        assert!(!d.intersects(&a));
  148|       |
  149|       |        // Nested (one inside the other)
  150|      2|        let outer = Rect::new(0.0, 0.0, 100.0, 100.0);
  151|      2|        let inner = Rect::new(10.0, 10.0, 20.0, 20.0);
  152|      2|        assert!(outer.intersects(&inner));
  153|      2|        assert!(inner.intersects(&outer));
  154|      2|    }
  155|       |
  156|       |    #[test]
  157|      2|    fn test_rect_min_separation() {
  158|       |        // Positive: separated horizontally
  159|      2|        let a = Rect::new(0.0, 0.0, 10.0, 10.0);
  160|      2|        let b = Rect::new(15.0, 0.0, 10.0, 10.0);
  161|      2|        let sep = a.min_separation(&b);
  162|      2|        assert!((sep - 5.0).abs() < 1e-10, "expected 5.0, got {}", sep);
  163|       |
  164|       |        // Zero: touching
  165|      2|        let c = Rect::new(10.0, 0.0, 10.0, 10.0);
  166|      2|        let sep = a.min_separation(&c);
  167|      2|        assert!(sep.abs() < 1e-10, "expected 0.0, got {}", sep);
  168|       |
  169|       |        // Negative: overlapping
  170|      2|        let d = Rect::new(5.0, 5.0, 10.0, 10.0);
  171|      2|        let sep = a.min_separation(&d);
  172|      2|        assert!(sep < 0.0, "expected negative, got {}", sep);
  173|       |        // Overlap on x: min(10,15) - max(0,5) = 10-5 = 5, so sep_x = -5
  174|       |        // Overlap on y: min(10,15) - max(0,5) = 10-5 = 5, so sep_y = -5
  175|       |        // Both negative → max(-5, -5) = -5
  176|      2|        assert!((sep - (-5.0)).abs() < 1e-10, "expected -5.0, got {}", sep);
  177|       |
  178|       |        // Both axes separated: returns the tighter gap (min)
  179|      2|        let e = Rect::new(15.0, 20.0, 10.0, 10.0);
  180|       |        // e is 5px right of a and 10px below a
  181|       |        // sep_x = 5.0, sep_y = 10.0 → min_separation = 5.0
  182|      2|        let sep = a.min_separation(&e);
  183|      2|        assert!(
  184|      2|            (sep - 5.0).abs() < 1e-10,
  185|       |            "both-axes-separated should return min gap, got {}",
  186|       |            sep
  187|       |        );
  188|      2|    }
  189|       |}

C:\language\mdmdview\crates\d2\src\graph.rs:
    1|       |//! Graph IR types for D2 diagram compilation and layout.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! The graph IR is the output of the compiler (Phase 2) and the input
    4|       |//! to the layout engine (Phase 4). It uses petgraph's `StableDiGraph`
    5|       |//! as the backbone, with `D2Object` for nodes and `D2EdgeData` for edges.
    6|       |//!
    7|       |//! Key types:
    8|       |//! - `D2Graph`: the complete diagram graph with hierarchy
    9|       |//! - `D2Object`: a shape or container
   10|       |//! - `D2EdgeData`: a connection/edge with routing data
   11|       |//! - `Style`: visual properties (fill, stroke, etc.)
   12|       |//! - `ShapeType`, `Direction`, `ArrowheadType`: enums
   13|       |
   14|       |use crate::geo::{Point, Rect};
   15|       |use petgraph::stable_graph::{EdgeIndex, NodeIndex, StableDiGraph};
   16|       |
   17|       |// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   18|       |// Source position tracking
   19|       |// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   20|       |
   21|       |/// Source position (0-indexed).
   22|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Default, PartialEq, Eq)]
   23|       |pub struct Position {
   24|       |    pub line: usize,
   25|       |    pub column: usize,
   26|       |}
   27|       |
   28|       |/// Source range.
   29|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Default, PartialEq, Eq)]
   30|       |pub struct Range {
   31|       |    pub start: Position,
   32|       |    pub end: Position,
   33|       |}
   34|       |
   35|       |// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   36|       |// D2Graph
   37|       |// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   38|       |
   39|       |/// The complete diagram graph.
   40|       |pub struct D2Graph {
   41|       |    /// petgraph backing store.
   42|       |    pub graph: StableDiGraph<D2Object, D2EdgeData>,
   43|       |
   44|       |    /// Root object (implicit top-level container).
   45|       |    /// The root is special: it has no shape, no label, no border.
   46|       |    /// It exists only as the parent of top-level objects.
   47|       |    /// Its `box_` is set to the bounding box of all children after layout.
   48|       |    /// The SVG renderer must NOT draw a shape or label for the root.
   49|       |    pub root: NodeIndex,
   50|       |
   51|       |    /// All objects in declaration order (preserved for deterministic iteration).
   52|       |    pub objects: Vec<NodeIndex>,
   53|       |
   54|       |    /// All edges in declaration order (preserved for deterministic rendering).
   55|       |    pub edges: Vec<EdgeIndex>,
   56|       |
   57|       |    /// Top-level layout direction (default: Down).
   58|       |    pub direction: Direction,
   59|       |
   60|       |    /// Non-fatal warnings accumulated during compilation and layout.
   61|       |    pub warnings: Vec<D2Warning>,
   62|       |}
   63|       |

  527|       |    /// D2 requires the `#` prefix for hex colors; bare hex strings are not accepted.

  536|       |    /// Relative luminance per WCAG 2.0 (range 0.0–1.0).
  537|       |    /// Used to determine whether text should be dark or light on a given background.
  538|     22|    pub fn luminance(&self) -> f64 {
  539|     22|        let (r, g, b) = match *self {
  540|     22|            Color::Hex(r, g, b) | Color::HexAlpha(r, g, b, _) => (r, g, b),
                                                                ^0 ^0 ^0
  541|       |        };
  542|       |        // sRGB linearization
  543|     66|        fn lin(c: u8) -> f64 {
  544|     66|            let s = c as f64 / 255.0;
  545|     66|            if s <= 0.03928 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (545:16): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (545:16): [True: 16, False: 50]
  ------------------
  546|     16|                s / 12.92
  547|       |            } else {
  548|     50|                ((s + 0.055) / 1.055).powf(2.4)
  549|       |            }
  550|     66|        }
  551|     22|        0.2126 * lin(r) + 0.7152 * lin(g) + 0.0722 * lin(b)
  552|     22|    }
  553|       |
  554|       |    /// Return dark or light text color to contrast against this color as a background.
  555|       |    /// Uses WCAG luminance threshold of 0.179.
  556|     14|    pub fn contrasting_text_color(&self) -> Color {
  557|     14|        if self.luminance() > 0.179 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (557:12): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (557:12): [True: 10, False: 4]
  ------------------
  558|     10|            Color::Hex(0x17, 0x17, 0x17) // dark text on light bg
  559|       |        } else {
  560|      4|            Color::Hex(0xE0, 0xE0, 0xE0) // light text on dark bg
  561|       |        }
  562|     14|    }

C:\language\mdmdview\crates\d2\src\layout.rs:
    1|       |//! Layout orchestration for D2 diagrams.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! Coordinates the four layout sub-phases:
    4|       |//! 1. Text measurement (compute node dimensions)
    5|       |//! 2. Sugiyama node positioning (recursive, bottom-up)
    6|       |//! 3. Container fitting
    7|       |//! 4. Edge routing (last, after all positions finalized)
    8|       |
    9|       |use petgraph::stable_graph::NodeIndex;
   10|       |
