   28|       |/// Perform layout on the graph.
   29|       |/// Modifies graph in-place: sets node positions, edge routes,
   30|       |/// and may append to graph.warnings.
   31|    310|pub fn layout(graph: &mut D2Graph, options: &RenderOptions) -> Result<(), LayoutError> {
   32|    310|    if graph.objects.is_empty() {

  |  Branch (32:8): [True: 2, False: 116]

   37|    304|    let font_size = theme.font_size;

   39|       |
   40|       |    // Phase 3: Text measurement — compute node dimensions
   41|    304|    measure_all_nodes(graph, font_size, font_family);

   58|       |/// Measure all nodes and set their dimensions.

   61|    864|    for &idx in &objects {

  124|    116|fn fit_container(graph: &mut D2Graph, container: NodeIndex) {

  135|       |    };

  144|    116|    let mut max_y = f64::NEG_INFINITY;

  263|       |    /// Helper: parse+compile+layout a D2 source string, return the graph.
  264|     24|    fn layout_ok(source: &str) -> crate::graph::D2Graph {
  265|     24|        let ast = parse(source).ast;
  266|     24|        let mut graph = compile(&ast).expect("compile should succeed");
  267|     24|        layout(&mut graph, &opts()).expect("layout should succeed");
  268|     24|        graph

  285|       |    // --- test_layout_simple_graph --------------------------------------------
  286|       |
  287|       |    #[test]
  288|      2|    fn test_layout_simple_graph() {
  289|       |        // a -> b: both should have non-zero positions, b below a (direction=down).
  290|      2|        let graph = layout_ok("a -> b");
  291|       |
  292|      2|        let a = find_by_label(&graph, "a");
  293|      2|        let b = find_by_label(&graph, "b");
  294|       |
  295|      2|        let a_rect = a.box_.expect("a should have a box after layout");
  296|      2|        let b_rect = b.box_.expect("b should have a box after layout");
  297|       |
  298|       |        // Both should have non-zero dimensions
  299|      2|        assert!(a_rect.width > 0.0, "a should have non-zero width");
  300|      2|        assert!(b_rect.width > 0.0, "b should have non-zero width");
  301|       |
  302|       |        // Default direction is Down, so b should be below a
  303|      2|        assert!(
  304|      2|            b_rect.y > a_rect.y,
  305|       |            "b (y={}) should be below a (y={}) in direction=down",
  306|       |            b_rect.y,
  307|       |            a_rect.y
  308|       |        );
  309|      2|    }

  311|       |    // --- test_layout_container -----------------------------------------------
  312|       |
  313|       |    #[test]
  314|      2|    fn test_layout_container() {
  315|       |        // Container with children: container bounds should enclose children.
  316|      2|        let graph = layout_ok("group: {\n  child1\n  child2\n}");
  317|       |
  318|      2|        let group = find_by_label(&graph, "group");
  319|      2|        let child1 = find_by_label(&graph, "child1");
  320|      2|        let child2 = find_by_label(&graph, "child2");
  321|       |
  322|      2|        let g_rect = group.box_.expect("group should have a box");
  323|      2|        let c1_rect = child1.box_.expect("child1 should have a box");
  324|      2|        let c2_rect = child2.box_.expect("child2 should have a box");
  325|       |
  326|       |        // Container should enclose both children
  327|      2|        assert!(
  328|      2|            g_rect.x <= c1_rect.x,
  329|       |            "container left ({}) should be <= child1 left ({})",
  330|       |            g_rect.x,
  331|       |            c1_rect.x
  332|       |        );
  333|      2|        assert!(
  334|      2|            g_rect.y <= c1_rect.y,
  335|       |            "container top ({}) should be <= child1 top ({})",
  336|       |            g_rect.y,
  337|       |            c1_rect.y
  338|       |        );
  339|      2|        assert!(
  340|      2|            g_rect.x + g_rect.width >= c2_rect.x + c2_rect.width,
  341|       |            "container right ({}) should be >= child2 right ({})",
  342|      0|            g_rect.x + g_rect.width,
  343|      0|            c2_rect.x + c2_rect.width
  344|       |        );
  345|      2|        assert!(
  346|      2|            g_rect.y + g_rect.height >= c2_rect.y + c2_rect.height,
  347|       |            "container bottom ({}) should be >= child2 bottom ({})",
  348|      0|            g_rect.y + g_rect.height,
  349|      0|            c2_rect.y + c2_rect.height
  350|       |        );
  351|      2|    }

  359|       |

  362|      2|        let c = find_by_label(&graph, "c");
  363|       |

  365|      2|        let b_rect = b.box_.expect("b should have a box");
  366|      2|        let c_rect = c.box_.expect("c should have a box");
  367|       |
  368|      2|        assert!(
  369|      2|            b_rect.x > a_rect.x,

  372|       |            a_rect.x
  373|       |        );

  375|      2|            c_rect.x > b_rect.x,

  378|       |            b_rect.x
  379|       |        );
  380|      2|    }

  409|       |    // --- helpers for container tests -----------------------------------------
  410|       |
  411|       |    /// Assert that a leaf rect is fully enclosed by a parent rect.
  412|     36|    fn assert_inside(
  413|     36|        leaf_label: &str,
  414|     36|        leaf: crate::geo::Rect,
  415|     36|        parent_label: &str,
  416|     36|        parent: crate::geo::Rect,
  417|     36|    ) {
  418|     36|        assert!(
  419|     36|            parent.x <= leaf.x,
  420|       |            "{leaf_label} left ({}) escapes {parent_label} left ({})",
  421|       |            leaf.x,
  422|       |            parent.x,
  423|       |        );
  424|     36|        assert!(
  425|     36|            parent.y <= leaf.y,
  426|       |            "{leaf_label} top ({}) escapes {parent_label} top ({})",
  427|       |            leaf.y,
  428|       |            parent.y,
  429|       |        );

    1|       |//! Sugiyama algorithm implementation for D2 layout.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! Implements the four classic Sugiyama phases with node-size awareness:
    4|       |//! 1. Cycle removal (reverse back-edges)
    5|       |//! 2. Rank assignment (longest-path)
    6|       |//! 3. Crossing reduction (barycenter heuristic)
    7|       |//! 4. Coordinate assignment (simple center-aligned placement)
    8|       |//!
    9|       |//! This operates on a subset of the graph: the direct children of a
   10|       |//! container node, treating child containers as opaque nodes with
   11|       |//! pre-computed dimensions.
   12|       |
   13|       |use std::collections::{HashMap, HashSet, VecDeque};
   14|       |
   15|       |use petgraph::stable_graph::{EdgeIndex, NodeIndex};
   16|       |
   17|       |use crate::graph::{D2Graph, Direction};
   18|       |use crate::layout::LayoutError;
   19|       |
   20|       |/// Default spacing between nodes (horizontal).
   21|       |const NODE_SPACING_H: f64 = 40.0;
   22|       |/// Default spacing between ranks (vertical).
   23|       |const NODE_SPACING_V: f64 = 60.0;
   24|       |
   25|       |/// Gap between disconnected components (pixels).
   26|       |const COMPONENT_GAP: f64 = 60.0;
   27|       |
   28|       |/// Minimum spacing between container nodes in the same rank.
   29|       |const CONTAINER_CROSS_SPACING: f64 = 60.0;
   30|       |/// Minimum spacing between ranks when either rank has a container.
   31|       |const CONTAINER_RANK_SPACING: f64 = 80.0;
   32|       |
   33|       |/// Number of barycenter crossing-reduction sweeps.
   34|       |/// Empirically sufficient for diagrams under ~200 nodes.
   35|       |const CROSSING_REDUCTION_ITERATIONS: usize = 12;
   36|       |

  151|  2.01k|        while parent_map[&x] != x {

  156|  1.48k|        x
  157|  1.48k|    }

  184|    232|    children: &[NodeIndex],

  186|    232|    direction: Direction,
  187|    232|) {
  188|       |    // Phase 1: Cycle removal
  189|    232|    let (forward_edges, _back_edges) = remove_cycles(children, edges);
  190|       |
  191|       |    // Phase 2: Rank assignment
  192|    232|    let ranks = assign_ranks(children, &forward_edges);
  193|       |
  194|       |    // Phase 3: Crossing reduction
  195|    232|    let ordered_ranks = reduce_crossings(&ranks, &forward_edges);
  196|       |
  197|       |    // Phase 4: Coordinate assignment
  198|    232|    assign_coordinates(graph, &ordered_ranks, direction);
  199|    232|}

  304|       |/// Longest-path rank assignment.
  305|    238|fn assign_ranks(
  306|    238|    children: &[NodeIndex],
  307|    238|    edges: &[(NodeIndex, NodeIndex, EdgeIndex)],
  308|    238|) -> HashMap<usize, Vec<NodeIndex>> {

  320|    436|        adj.entry(src).or_default().push(dst);
  321|    436|    }

                                 ^644
  ------------------

  384|       |/// Crossing reduction using barycenter heuristic.
  385|       |#[allow(clippy::needless_range_loop)]
  386|    234|fn reduce_crossings(
  387|    234|    ranks: &HashMap<usize, Vec<NodeIndex>>,
  388|    234|    edges: &[(NodeIndex, NodeIndex, EdgeIndex)],
  389|    234|) -> Vec<Vec<NodeIndex>> {
  390|    234|    let max_rank = ranks.keys().copied().max().unwrap_or(0);
  391|    234|    let mut ordered: Vec<Vec<NodeIndex>> = (0..=max_rank)
  392|    556|        .map(|r| ranks.get(&r).cloned().unwrap_or_default())
                       ^234
  393|    234|        .collect();
  394|       |
  395|       |    // Build position maps
  396|    234|    let mut node_positions: HashMap<NodeIndex, usize> = HashMap::new();
  397|    556|    for rank_nodes in &ordered {
                                    ^234
  398|    632|        for (i, &node) in rank_nodes.iter().enumerate() {
                                        ^556              ^556
  399|    632|            node_positions.insert(node, i);
  400|    632|        }
  401|       |    }
  402|       |
  403|  2.80k|    for _iter in 0..CROSSING_REDUCTION_ITERATIONS {
                               ^234
  404|       |        // Downward sweep
  405|  3.86k|        for r in 1..=max_rank {
                               ^2.80k
  406|  3.86k|            let curr_rank = &ordered[r];
  407|       |
  408|  3.86k|            let mut barycenters: Vec<(NodeIndex, f64)> = curr_rank
  409|  3.86k|                .iter()
  410|  4.56k|                .map(|&node| {
                               ^3.86k
  411|  4.56k|                    let incoming: Vec<f64> = edges
  412|  4.56k|                        .iter()
  413|  12.0k|                        .filter(|(_, dst, _)| *dst == node)
                                       ^4.56k
  414|  5.13k|                        .filter_map(|(src, _, _)| node_positions.get(src))
                                       ^4.56k
  415|  5.13k|                        .map(|&pos| pos as f64)
                                       ^4.56k
  416|  4.56k|                        .collect();
  417|       |
  418|  4.56k|                    let bc = if incoming.is_empty() {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (418:33): [True: 0, False: 2.59k]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (418:33): [True: 0, False: 1.96k]
  ------------------
  419|      0|                        node_positions.get(&node).map(|&p| p as f64).unwrap_or(0.0)
  420|       |                    } else {
  421|  4.56k|                        incoming.iter().sum::<f64>() / incoming.len() as f64
  422|       |                    };
  423|  4.56k|                    (node, bc)
  424|  4.56k|                })
  425|  3.86k|                .collect();
  426|       |
  427|  3.86k|            barycenters.sort_by(|a, b| a.1.partial_cmp(&b.1).unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal));
                                                     ^696^696        ^696  ^696      ^696
  428|  3.86k|            ordered[r] = barycenters.iter().map(|(node, _)| *node).collect();
  429|       |
  430|       |            // Update positions
  431|  4.56k|            for (i, &node) in ordered[r].iter().enumerate() {
                                            ^3.86k            ^3.86k
  432|  4.56k|                node_positions.insert(node, i);
  433|  4.56k|            }
  434|       |        }
  435|       |
  436|       |        // Upward sweep
  437|  3.86k|        for r in (0..max_rank).rev() {
                                ^2.80k       ^2.80k
  438|  3.86k|            let curr_rank = &ordered[r];
  439|       |
  440|  3.86k|            let mut barycenters: Vec<(NodeIndex, f64)> = curr_rank
  441|  3.86k|                .iter()
  442|  4.17k|                .map(|&node| {
                               ^3.86k
  443|  4.17k|                    let outgoing: Vec<f64> = edges
  444|  4.17k|                        .iter()
  445|  9.91k|                        .filter(|(src, _, _)| *src == node)
                                       ^4.17k
  446|  5.13k|                        .filter_map(|(_, dst, _)| node_positions.get(dst))
                                       ^4.17k
  447|  5.13k|                        .map(|&pos| pos as f64)
                                       ^4.17k
  448|  4.17k|                        .collect();
  449|       |
  450|  4.17k|                    let bc = if outgoing.is_empty() {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (450:33): [True: 0, False: 2.11k]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (450:33): [True: 24, False: 2.04k]
  ------------------
  451|     24|                        node_positions.get(&node).map(|&p| p as f64).unwrap_or(0.0)
  452|       |                    } else {
  453|  4.15k|                        outgoing.iter().sum::<f64>() / outgoing.len() as f64
  454|       |                    };
  455|  4.17k|                    (node, bc)
  456|  4.17k|                })

  458|       |

  534|       |    // --- test_linear_chain_ranking -------------------------------------------
  535|       |
  536|       |    #[test]
  537|      2|    fn test_linear_chain_ranking() {
  538|       |        // a -> b -> c should produce 3 distinct ranks: a=0, b=1, c=2.
  539|      2|        let (mut graph, children) = make_graph(&["a", "b", "c"]);
  540|      2|        let a = children[0];
  541|      2|        let b = children[1];
  542|      2|        let c = children[2];
  543|       |
  544|      2|        let e1 = add_edge(&mut graph, a, b);
  545|      2|        let e2 = add_edge(&mut graph, b, c);
  546|       |
  547|      2|        let edges = vec![(a, b, e1), (b, c, e2)];
  548|      2|        let (forward, _back) = remove_cycles(&children, &edges);
  549|      2|        let ranks = assign_ranks(&children, &forward);
  550|       |
  551|       |        // There should be 3 distinct ranks: 0, 1, 2
  552|      2|        assert_eq!(ranks.len(), 3, "expected 3 ranks, got {}", ranks.len());
                                                                             ^0    ^0
  553|       |
  554|       |        // Build a node-to-rank lookup
  555|      2|        let mut node_rank: HashMap<NodeIndex, usize> = HashMap::new();
  556|      6|        for (&rank, nodes) in &ranks {
                                            ^2
  557|      6|            for &node in nodes {
  558|      6|                node_rank.insert(node, rank);
  559|      6|            }
  560|       |        }
  561|       |
  562|      2|        let rank_a = node_rank[&a];
  563|      2|        let rank_b = node_rank[&b];
  564|      2|        let rank_c = node_rank[&c];
  565|       |
  566|      2|        assert!(
  567|      2|            rank_a < rank_b,
  568|       |            "a (rank {rank_a}) should be before b (rank {rank_b})"
  569|       |        );
  570|      2|        assert!(
  571|      2|            rank_b < rank_c,
  572|       |            "b (rank {rank_b}) should be before c (rank {rank_c})"
  573|       |        );
  574|      2|    }
  575|       |
  576|       |    // --- test_cycle_removal --------------------------------------------------
  577|       |
  578|       |    #[test]
  579|      2|    fn test_cycle_removal() {
  580|       |        // a -> b -> c -> a (cycle). Should not panic or infinite loop.
  581|       |        // Back-edges list should be non-empty (at least one edge reversed).
  582|      2|        let (mut graph, children) = make_graph(&["a", "b", "c"]);
  583|      2|        let a = children[0];
  584|      2|        let b = children[1];
  585|      2|        let c = children[2];
  586|       |
  587|      2|        let e1 = add_edge(&mut graph, a, b);
  588|      2|        let e2 = add_edge(&mut graph, b, c);
  589|      2|        let e3 = add_edge(&mut graph, c, a);
  590|       |
  591|      2|        let edges = vec![(a, b, e1), (b, c, e2), (c, a, e3)];
  592|      2|        let (forward, back) = remove_cycles(&children, &edges);
  593|       |
  594|       |        // At least one edge should be classified as a back-edge
  595|      2|        assert!(
  596|      2|            !back.is_empty(),
  597|       |            "expected at least one back-edge in a cycle"
  598|       |        );
  599|       |
  600|       |        // All edges should appear in either forward or back (none lost)
  601|      2|        assert_eq!(
  602|      2|            forward.len() + back.len(),
  603|      2|            edges.len(),
  604|       |            "forward + back should account for all edges"
  605|       |        );
  606|       |
  607|       |        // The forward edges should be usable for ranking without panic
  608|      2|        let ranks = assign_ranks(&children, &forward);
  609|      2|        assert!(
  610|      2|            !ranks.is_empty(),
  611|       |            "ranking should succeed after cycle removal"
  612|       |        );
  613|      2|    }

  617|       |    #[test]
  618|      2|    fn test_crossing_reduction() {
  619|       |        // Minimal case: a -> c, b -> d. Two independent edges, no crossings.
  620|       |        // After crossing reduction, the order should not introduce crossings.
  621|      2|        let (mut graph, children) = make_graph(&["a", "b", "c", "d"]);
  622|      2|        let a = children[0];
  623|      2|        let b = children[1];
  624|      2|        let c = children[2];
  625|      2|        let d = children[3];
  626|       |
  627|      2|        let e1 = add_edge(&mut graph, a, c);
  628|      2|        let e2 = add_edge(&mut graph, b, d);
  629|       |
  630|      2|        let edges = vec![(a, c, e1), (b, d, e2)];
  631|      2|        let (forward, _) = remove_cycles(&children, &edges);
  632|      2|        let ranks = assign_ranks(&children, &forward);
  633|      2|        let ordered = reduce_crossings(&ranks, &forward);
  634|       |
  635|       |        // Should produce 2 ranks
  636|      2|        assert_eq!(ordered.len(), 2, "expected 2 ranks, got {}", ordered.len());
                                                                               ^0      ^0
  637|       |
  638|       |        // Each rank should have 2 nodes
  639|      2|        assert_eq!(ordered[0].len(), 2);
  640|      2|        assert_eq!(ordered[1].len(), 2);
  641|       |
  642|       |        // Verify no crossing: if a is before b in rank 0, then c should be
  643|       |        // before d in rank 1 (or vice versa — the relative order should match).
  644|      4|        let a_pos = ordered[0].iter().position(|&n| n == a).unwrap();
                          ^2      ^2                ^2                    ^2
  645|      2|        let b_pos = ordered[0].iter().position(|&n| n == b).unwrap();

  647|      2|        let d_pos = ordered[1].iter().position(|&n| n == d).unwrap();
  648|       |
  649|       |        // If a < b then c < d (no crossing), or if b < a then d < c
  650|      2|        let same_order = (a_pos < b_pos && c_pos < d_pos) || (b_pos < a_pos && d_pos < c_pos);
                                                         ^0
  ------------------
  |  Branch (650:27): [True: 0, False: 2]

  652|      2|            same_order,
  653|       |            "crossing reduction should not introduce crossings: \
  654|       |             a@{a_pos}, b@{b_pos}, c@{c_pos}, d@{d_pos}"
  655|       |        );
  656|      2|    }

C:\language\mdmdview\crates\d2\src\lib.rs:
    1|       |//! mdmdview-d2: Native D2 diagram renderer.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! This crate implements a subset of the D2 diagram language
    4|       |//! (https://d2lang.com/) as a pure-Rust library. It parses D2 source
    5|       |//! text, compiles it to an internal graph representation, performs
    6|       |//! automatic hierarchical layout via the Sugiyama algorithm, and
    7|       |//! renders the result to SVG.
    8|       |//!
    9|       |//! The primary entry point is [`render_d2_to_svg()`], which takes D2
   10|       |//! source text and rendering options and returns an SVG string.
   11|       |//! Individual pipeline phases ([`parse`], [`compile`], [`layout`],
   12|       |//! [`render_svg`]) are also exposed for testing and debugging.
   13|       |
   14|       |pub mod ast;
   15|       |pub mod compiler;
   16|       |pub mod edge_routing;
   17|       |pub mod geo;
   18|       |pub mod graph;
   19|       |pub mod keywords;
   20|       |pub mod layout;
   21|       |pub mod layout_sugiyama;
   22|       |pub mod parser;
   23|       |pub mod shapes;
   24|       |pub mod svg_render;
   25|       |pub mod text;
   26|       |pub mod theme;
   27|       |
   28|       |pub use geo::{Point, Rect};
   29|       |pub use graph::{
   30|       |    ArrowheadType, Color, D2EdgeData, D2Graph, D2Object, D2Warning, Direction, RouteType,
   31|       |    ShapeType, Style,
   32|       |};
   33|       |pub use theme::Theme;
   34|       |

    1|       |//! Recursive-descent parser for D2 diagram language.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! Parses D2 source text into an AST (`D2Map`). The parser handles
    4|       |//! all v1 syntax features: shape declarations, connections, containers,
    5|       |//! styles, comments, semicolons, and quoted strings.
    6|       |//!
    7|       |//! The parser collects multiple errors and continues, returning a
    8|       |//! partial AST alongside diagnostics. This matches D2's behavior
    9|       |//! and supports future LSP integration.
   10|       |
   11|       |use crate::ast::*;
   12|       |use crate::graph::{Position, Range};
   13|       |
   14|       |/// A single parse diagnostic (position + message).
   15|       |#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]
   16|       |#[error("{range:?}: {message}")]
   17|       |pub struct ParseError {
   18|       |    pub message: String,
   19|       |    pub range: Range,
   20|       |}
   21|       |
   22|       |/// Result of parsing: always contains the AST, plus any errors.
   23|       |pub struct ParseResult {
   24|       |    pub ast: D2Map,
   25|       |    pub errors: Vec<ParseError>,
   26|       |}
   27|       |
   28|       |/// Parse D2 source text into an AST.

  716|       |#[cfg(test)]
  717|       |mod tests {
  718|       |    use super::*;
  719|       |
  720|     52|    fn parse_ok(source: &str) -> D2Map {
  721|     52|        let result = parse(source);
  722|     52|        assert!(
  723|     52|            result.errors.is_empty(),
  724|       |            "unexpected parse errors: {:?}",
  725|       |            result.errors
  726|       |        );

  728|     52|    }
  729|       |
  730|       |    #[test]
  731|      2|    fn test_empty_source() {
  732|      2|        let ast = parse_ok("");
  733|      2|        assert!(ast.nodes.is_empty());
  734|      2|    }
  735|       |
  736|       |    #[test]
  737|      2|    fn test_simple_shape() {
  738|      2|        let ast = parse_ok("server");
  739|      2|        assert_eq!(ast.nodes.len(), 1);
  740|      2|        if let MapNode::Key(key) = &ast.nodes[0] {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (740:16): [True: 2, False: 0]
  ------------------
  741|      2|            assert_eq!(key.key.to_string(), "server");
  742|      2|            assert!(key.edges.is_empty());
  743|       |        } else {
  744|      0|            panic!("expected Key node");
  745|       |        }

 1037|       |    #[test]
 1038|      2|    fn test_complex_diagram() {
 1039|      2|        let source = r#"
 1040|      2|direction: right
 1041|      2|
 1042|      2|# Services
 1043|      2|api: "API Gateway" {
 1044|      2|  shape: hexagon
 1045|      2|}
 1046|      2|db: "PostgreSQL" {
 1047|      2|  shape: cylinder
 1048|      2|}
 1049|      2|cache: "Redis Cache" {
 1050|      2|  shape: cylinder
 1051|      2|}
 1052|      2|
 1053|      2|api -> db: queries
 1054|      2|api -> cache: reads
 1055|      2|cache -> db: cache miss
 1056|      2|"#;
 1057|      2|        let ast = parse_ok(source);
 1058|       |        // Should have: direction, comment, api, db, cache, 3 edges
 1059|      2|        let key_count = ast
 1060|      2|            .nodes
 1061|      2|            .iter()
 1062|     16|            .filter(|n| matches!(n, MapNode::Key(_)))
                           ^2
 1063|      2|            .count();
 1064|      2|        assert!(
 1065|      2|            key_count >= 6,
 1066|       |            "expected at least 6 key nodes, got {}",
 1067|       |            key_count
 1068|       |        );
 1069|      2|    }
 1070|       |
 1071|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
 1072|       |    // Error recovery tests
 1073|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
 1074|       |
 1075|       |    /// Parse input and return the full ParseResult (AST + errors).
 1076|     10|    fn parse_with_errors(source: &str) -> ParseResult {
 1077|     10|        parse(source)
 1078|     10|    }
 1079|       |
 1080|       |    #[test]
 1081|      2|    fn test_stray_closing_brace() {
 1082|       |        // A stray `}` at the file level is not valid syntax. The parser
 1083|       |        // should emit an error for the unexpected `}` but still parse the
 1084|       |        // surrounding declarations `a` and `b`.
 1085|      2|        let result = parse_with_errors("a\n}\nb");
 1086|      2|        assert!(
 1087|      2|            !result.errors.is_empty(),
 1088|       |            "expected errors for stray `}}`, got none"
 1089|       |        );
 1090|       |        // Both `a` and `b` should still be parsed as key nodes.
 1091|      2|        let key_count = result
 1092|      2|            .ast
 1093|      2|            .nodes
 1094|      2|            .iter()
 1095|      4|            .filter(|n| matches!(n, MapNode::Key(_)))
                           ^2
 1096|      2|            .count();
 1097|      2|        assert!(
 1098|      2|            key_count >= 2,
 1099|       |            "expected at least 2 key nodes after recovery, got {}",
 1100|       |            key_count
 1101|       |        );
 1102|      2|    }
 1103|       |
 1104|       |    #[test]
 1105|      2|    fn test_unterminated_map() {
 1106|       |        // Opening a map with `{` but never closing it should produce an
 1107|       |        // error about the missing `}`.
 1108|      2|        let result = parse_with_errors("a: { b");
 1109|      2|        assert!(
 1110|      2|            !result.errors.is_empty(),
 1111|       |            "expected error for missing `}}`, got none"
 1112|       |        );
 1113|      2|        let has_brace_error = result.errors.iter().any(|e| e.message.contains('}'));
 1114|      2|        assert!(
 1115|      2|            has_brace_error,
 1116|       |            "expected an error mentioning `}}`, got: {:?}",
 1117|       |            result.errors
 1118|       |        );
 1119|      2|    }
 1120|       |
 1121|       |    #[test]
 1122|      2|    fn test_edge_missing_destination() {
 1123|       |        // `a -> ` has an edge operator but no destination. The parser
 1124|       |        // should handle this gracefully without panicking.
 1125|      2|        let result = parse_with_errors("a -> ");
 1126|       |        // The parser consumed the edge operator but failed to find a
 1127|       |        // destination key. This may result in the entire key being
 1128|       |        // dropped (no nodes) or an error being emitted. Either outcome
 1129|       |        // is acceptable as long as the parser doesn't panic.
 1130|      2|        let _ = result.ast.nodes.len();
 1131|      2|        let _ = result.errors.len();
 1132|      2|    }
 1133|       |
 1134|       |    #[test]
 1135|      2|    fn test_unterminated_string() {
 1136|       |        // A double-quoted string that is never closed should produce a
 1137|       |        // parse error rather than hanging or panicking.
 1138|      2|        let result = parse_with_errors("a: \"unterminated");
 1139|      2|        assert!(
 1140|      2|            !result.errors.is_empty(),
 1141|       |            "expected error for unterminated string, got none"
 1142|       |        );
 1143|      2|        let has_unterminated = result
 1144|      2|            .errors
 1145|      2|            .iter()
 1146|      2|            .any(|e| e.message.contains("unterminated"));
 1147|      2|        assert!(
 1148|      2|            has_unterminated,
 1149|       |            "expected 'unterminated' in error message, got: {:?}",
 1150|       |            result.errors
 1151|       |        );
 1152|      2|    }
 1153|       |
 1154|       |    #[test]
 1155|      2|    fn test_multiple_errors_collected() {
 1156|       |        // Several syntax problems in one input. The parser should collect
 1157|       |        // all of them rather than bailing out after the first.
 1158|       |        //
 1159|       |        // Line 1: `}`       — stray closing brace at file level
 1160|       |        // Line 2: `a`       — valid key (should still parse)
 1161|       |        // Line 3: `}`       — another stray closing brace
 1162|       |        // Line 4: `b`       — valid key (should still parse)
 1163|       |        //
 1164|       |        // Each stray `}` is an independent error that the parser can
 1165|       |        // recover from by skipping the unrecognized character.
 1166|      2|        let result = parse_with_errors("}\na\n}\nb");
 1167|      2|        assert!(
 1168|      2|            result.errors.len() >= 2,
 1169|       |            "expected at least 2 errors for multiple syntax problems, got {}: {:?}",
 1170|      0|            result.errors.len(),
 1171|       |            result.errors,
 1172|       |        );
 1173|       |        // Despite multiple errors, the parser should still produce some
 1174|       |        // AST nodes — both `a` and `b` should survive recovery.
 1175|      2|        let key_count = result
 1176|      2|            .ast
 1177|      2|            .nodes
 1178|      2|            .iter()
 1179|      4|            .filter(|n| matches!(n, MapNode::Key(_)))
                           ^2
 1180|      2|            .count();
 1181|      2|        assert!(
 1182|      2|            key_count >= 2,

  425|       |/// Clip to ellipse boundary.
  426|      6|fn clip_to_ellipse(rect: &Rect, _from: Point, toward: Point) -> Point {
  427|      6|    let cx = rect.x + rect.width / 2.0;
  428|      6|    let cy = rect.y + rect.height / 2.0;
  429|      6|    let rx = rect.width / 2.0;
  430|      6|    let ry = rect.height / 2.0;
  431|       |
  432|      6|    let dx = toward.x - cx;
  433|      6|    let dy = toward.y - cy;
  434|      6|    let len = (dx * dx + dy * dy).sqrt();
  435|       |
  436|      6|    if len < 1e-10 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (436:8): [True: 0, False: 4]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (436:8): [True: 0, False: 2]
  ------------------
  437|      0|        return Point::new(cx, cy - ry);
  438|      6|    }
  439|       |
  440|      6|    let ux = dx / len;
  441|      6|    let uy = dy / len;
  442|       |
  443|       |    // Parametric intersection with ellipse
  444|      6|    let denom = ((ux * ux) / (rx * rx) + (uy * uy) / (ry * ry)).sqrt();

  446|       |
  447|      6|    Point::new(cx + ux * t, cy + uy * t)

  450|       |/// Clip to diamond boundary.

  469|       |/// Clip to hexagon boundary.
  470|       |/// Hexagon has 6 edges: top-left, top-right, right, bottom-right, bottom-left, left.
  471|      0|fn clip_to_hexagon(rect: &Rect, _from: Point, toward: Point) -> Point {
  472|      0|    let cx = rect.x + rect.width / 2.0;
  473|      0|    let cy = rect.y + rect.height / 2.0;
  474|      0|    let inset = rect.width * 0.25;
  475|       |
  476|       |    // Hexagon vertices (matching the SVG generator)
  477|      0|    let vertices = [
  478|      0|        Point::new(rect.x + inset, rect.y),              // top-left
  479|      0|        Point::new(rect.x + rect.width - inset, rect.y), // top-right
  480|      0|        Point::new(rect.x + rect.width, cy),             // right
  481|      0|        Point::new(rect.x + rect.width - inset, rect.y + rect.height), // bottom-right
  482|      0|        Point::new(rect.x + inset, rect.y + rect.height), // bottom-left
  483|      0|        Point::new(rect.x, cy),                          // left
  484|      0|    ];
  485|       |
  486|      0|    let dx = toward.x - cx;
  487|      0|    let dy = toward.y - cy;
  488|       |
  489|      0|    if dx.abs() < 1e-10 && dy.abs() < 1e-10 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (489:8): [True: 0, False: 0]
  |  Branch (489:28): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (489:8): [True: 0, False: 0]
  |  Branch (489:28): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  490|      0|        return vertices[0]; // default: top-left vertex
  491|      0|    }
  492|       |
  493|       |    // Test intersection with each of the 6 edges
  494|      0|    let mut best_t = f64::INFINITY;
  495|      0|    let mut best_point = Point::new(cx, cy);
  496|       |
  497|      0|    for i in 0..6 {
  498|      0|        let v0 = vertices[i];
  499|      0|        let v1 = vertices[(i + 1) % 6];
  500|       |
  501|       |        // Ray from center in direction (dx, dy): P = center + t * dir
  502|       |        // Edge from v0 to v1: Q = v0 + s * (v1 - v0)
  503|      0|        let ex = v1.x - v0.x;
  504|      0|        let ey = v1.y - v0.y;
  505|      0|        let det = dx * ey - dy * ex;
  506|       |

  ------------------
  |  Branch (507:12): [True: 0, False: 0]

  509|      0|        }
  510|       |
  511|      0|        let ox = v0.x - cx;
  512|      0|        let oy = v0.y - cy;
  513|      0|        let t = (ox * ey - oy * ex) / det;
  514|      0|        let s = (ox * dy - oy * dx) / det;

  518|      0|            best_point = Point::new(cx + dx * t, cy + dy * t);
  519|      0|        }
  520|       |    }

    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! Generates SVG strings from positioned D2Graph. The SVG uses a
    4|       |//! computed viewBox based on the bounding box of all elements plus padding.
    5|       |//!
    6|       |//! Z-ordering (back to front):
    7|       |//! 1. Background rectangle
    8|       |//! 2. Container fills (outermost first)
    9|       |//! 3. Shape fills + strokes
   10|       |//! 4. Edges + arrowheads
   11|       |//! 5. All labels (topmost — always readable)

   32|       |///
   33|       |/// Each internal vertex gets a quadratic Bézier arc that rounds the corner.
   34|       |/// The radius is clamped so it never exceeds half the length of either adjacent
   35|       |/// segment, which handles very short segments gracefully.
   36|    282|fn polyline_with_rounded_corners(points: &[Point], radius: f64) -> String {

  140|       |/// Render a positioned D2Graph to SVG string.
  141|    198|pub fn render(graph: &D2Graph, options: &RenderOptions) -> String {
  142|    198|    let theme = Theme::for_mode(options.dark_mode);
  143|    198|    let mut svg = String::with_capacity(4096);
  144|       |
  145|       |    // Compute viewBox
  146|    198|    let (vx, vy, vw, vh) = compute_viewbox(graph);
  147|       |
  148|       |    // SVG header
  149|    198|    svg.push_str(&format!(
  150|    198|        "<svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" viewBox=\"{} {} {} {}\">\n",
  151|    198|        c(vx),
  152|    198|        c(vy),
  153|    198|        c(vw),
  154|    198|        c(vh)
  155|    198|    ));
  156|       |
  157|       |    // Layer 1: Background
  158|    198|    let bg = theme.background.to_svg_string();
  159|    198|    svg.push_str(&format!(
  160|    198|        "  <rect x=\"{}\" y=\"{}\" width=\"{}\" height=\"{}\" fill=\"{}\"/>\n",
  161|    198|        c(vx),
  162|    198|        c(vy),
  163|    198|        c(vw),
  164|    198|        c(vh),
  165|    198|        bg
  166|    198|    ));
  167|       |
  168|       |    // Layer 2: Container fills (outermost first via depth-first)
  169|    198|    render_container_fills(graph, &theme, &mut svg, graph.root, 0);
  170|       |
  171|       |    // Layer 3: Shape fills + strokes (non-container nodes)
  172|    198|    render_shapes(graph, &theme, options, &mut svg);
  173|       |
  174|       |    // Layer 4: Edges + arrowheads
  175|    198|    render_edges(graph, &theme, options, &mut svg);
  176|       |
  177|       |    // Layer 5: All labels (topmost)
  178|    198|    render_labels(graph, &theme, options, &mut svg);
  179|       |
  180|    198|    svg.push_str("</svg>\n");
  181|    198|    svg
  182|    198|}

  472|       |/// Render a single arrowhead.
  473|    278|fn render_arrowhead(
  474|    278|    svg: &mut String,
  475|    278|    arrow_type: &ArrowheadType,
  476|    278|    tip: Point,
  477|    278|    from: Point,
  478|    278|    color: &str,
  479|    278|    stroke_width: f64,
  480|    278|) {
  481|    278|    let size = stroke_width * 5.0;
  482|       |
  483|    278|    match arrow_type {
  484|    278|        ArrowheadType::Arrow | ArrowheadType::Triangle => {
  485|    278|            let (t, l, r) = arrowhead_polygon(tip, from, size);
  486|    278|            svg.push_str(&format!(
  487|    278|                "  <polygon points=\"{},{} {},{} {},{}\" fill=\"{}\"/>\n",
  488|    278|                t.x, t.y, l.x, l.y, r.x, r.y, color,
  489|    278|            ));
  490|    278|        }
  491|       |        ArrowheadType::Diamond | ArrowheadType::FilledDiamond => {
  492|      0|            let pts = diamond_arrowhead(tip, from, size);
  493|      0|            let fill = if *arrow_type == ArrowheadType::FilledDiamond {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (493:27): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (493:27): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  494|      0|                color
  495|       |            } else {
  496|      0|                "none"
  497|       |            };
  498|      0|            svg.push_str(&format!(
  499|      0|                "  <polygon points=\"{},{} {},{} {},{} {},{}\" fill=\"{}\" stroke=\"{}\" stroke-width=\"1\"/>\n",
  500|      0|                pts[0].x, pts[0].y, pts[1].x, pts[1].y, pts[2].x, pts[2].y, pts[3].x, pts[3].y,
  501|      0|                fill, color,
  502|      0|            ));
  503|       |        }
  504|       |        ArrowheadType::Circle | ArrowheadType::FilledCircle => {
  505|      0|            let r = size * 0.4;
  506|      0|            let dx = tip.x - from.x;
  507|      0|            let dy = tip.y - from.y;
  508|      0|            let len = (dx * dx + dy * dy).sqrt();
  509|      0|            let (cx, cy) = if len > 1e-6 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (509:31): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (509:31): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  510|      0|                let ux = dx / len;
  511|      0|                let uy = dy / len;
  512|      0|                (tip.x - ux * r, tip.y - uy * r)
  513|       |            } else {
  514|      0|                (tip.x, tip.y - r)
  515|       |            };
  516|      0|            let fill = if *arrow_type == ArrowheadType::FilledCircle {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (516:27): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (516:27): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  517|      0|                color
  518|       |            } else {
  519|      0|                "none"
  520|       |            };
  521|      0|            svg.push_str(&format!(
  522|      0|                "  <circle cx=\"{}\" cy=\"{}\" r=\"{}\" fill=\"{}\" stroke=\"{}\" stroke-width=\"1\"/>\n",
  523|      0|                cx, cy, r, fill, color,
  524|      0|            ));
  525|       |        }
  526|       |        ArrowheadType::CfOne | ArrowheadType::CfOneRequired => {
  527|       |            // Crow's foot: single line perpendicular at tip
  528|      0|            let dx = tip.x - from.x;
  529|      0|            let dy = tip.y - from.y;
  530|      0|            let len = (dx * dx + dy * dy).sqrt();
  531|      0|            if len > 1e-6 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (531:16): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------

  791|       |/// XML-escape a string for safe inclusion in SVG.
  792|    640|fn xml_escape(s: &str) -> String {
  793|    640|    s.replace('&', "&amp;")
  794|    640|        .replace('<', "&lt;")
  795|    640|        .replace('>', "&gt;")
  796|    640|        .replace('"', "&quot;")
  797|    640|        .replace('\'', "&apos;")

  828|       |    /// Helper: parse+compile+layout a D2 source string, return the graph.
  829|     12|    fn layout_ok(source: &str) -> D2Graph {
  830|     12|        let ast = crate::parse(source).ast;
  831|     12|        let mut graph = crate::compile(&ast).expect("compile should succeed");
  832|     12|        crate::layout(&mut graph, &RenderOptions::default()).expect("layout should succeed");
  833|     12|        graph
  834|     12|    }

  838|       |        // Node with light fill (#d4edda) should get dark text, not the dark-mode default (#e0e0e0).
  839|      2|        let graph = layout_ok("success: OK {\n  style.fill: \"#d4edda\"\n}");
  840|      2|        let mut options = RenderOptions::default();
  841|      2|        options.dark_mode = true;

  846|      2|            svg.contains("fill=\"#171717\""),

  871|      2|    fn test_explicit_font_color_overrides_auto_contrast() {
  872|       |        // When font_color is explicitly set, it should be used regardless of fill.
  873|      2|        let graph =
  874|      2|            layout_ok("a: Hello {\n  style.fill: \"#d4edda\"\n  style.font-color: \"#ff0000\"\n}");
  875|      2|        let mut options = RenderOptions::default();
  876|      2|        options.dark_mode = true;
  877|      2|        let svg = render(&graph, &options);
  878|       |
  879|      2|        assert!(
  880|      2|            svg.contains("fill=\"#ff0000\""),
  881|       |            "explicit font-color should override auto-contrast, SVG:\n{svg}"
  882|       |        );
  883|      2|    }

  904|       |    #[test]
  905|      2|    fn test_polyline_two_points() {
  906|       |        // Straight line, no Q commands
  907|      2|        let points = vec![Point::new(0.0, 0.0), Point::new(100.0, 0.0)];
  908|      2|        let path = polyline_with_rounded_corners(&points, 5.0);
  909|      2|        assert!(path.starts_with("M"));
  910|      2|        assert!(path.contains("L"));
  911|      2|        assert!(!path.contains("Q"));
  912|      2|    }
  913|       |
  914|       |    #[test]
  915|      2|    fn test_polyline_three_points_has_curve() {
  916|       |        // Right-angle bend should have Q command
  917|      2|        let points = vec![
  918|      2|            Point::new(0.0, 0.0),
  919|      2|            Point::new(50.0, 0.0),
  920|      2|            Point::new(50.0, 50.0),
  921|       |        ];
  922|      2|        let path = polyline_with_rounded_corners(&points, 5.0);

    1|       |//! Text measurement and wrapping for D2 label sizing.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! Uses heuristic measurement based on average character widths for
    4|       |//! proportional fonts, with word-wrapping for long text. This doesn't
    5|       |//! need to be pixel-perfect — the layout just needs reasonable width/height
    6|       |//! estimates. The final text rendering is done by resvg with real font metrics.
    7|       |
    8|       |use crate::graph::ShapeType;
    9|       |
   10|       |/// Maximum label width before wrapping (pixels at default font size of 14px).
   11|       |const MAX_LABEL_WIDTH: f64 = 200.0;
   12|       |
   13|       |/// Average character width multiplier for proportional fonts.
   14|       |/// Arial at 14px: average ~7.5px per character → 0.55 * font_size.
   15|       |const CHAR_WIDTH_FACTOR: f64 = 0.55;
   16|       |
   17|       |/// Line height multiplier.
   18|       |const LINE_HEIGHT_FACTOR: f64 = 1.4;
   19|       |
   20|       |/// Result of text measurement.
   21|       |pub struct TextMeasurement {
   22|       |    /// Wrapped lines of text.
   23|       |    pub lines: Vec<String>,
   24|       |    /// Total text width in pixels.
   25|       |    pub width: f64,
   26|       |    /// Total text height in pixels.
   27|       |    pub height: f64,
   28|       |}
   29|       |

 1014|       |    /// Calculate readability statistics for the given text
 1015|    532|    fn calculate_readability(text: &str) -> ReadabilityStats {
 1016|    532|        let words: Vec<&str> = text.split_whitespace().collect();
 1017|    532|        let word_count = words.len();
 1018|       |
 1019|    532|        if word_count == 0 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (1019:12): [True: 4, False: 528]
  ------------------
 1020|      4|            return ReadabilityStats::default();
 1021|    528|        }
 1022|       |
 1023|       |        // Count sentences (periods, exclamation marks, question marks)
 1024|    528|        let sentence_count = text
 1025|    528|            .chars()
 1026|  8.88M|            .filter(|&c| c == '.' || c == '!' || c == '?')
                           ^528                    ^8.88M      ^8.88M
  ------------------
  |  Branch (1026:26): [True: 2.98k, False: 8.88M]
  |  Branch (1026:38): [True: 484, False: 8.88M]
  ------------------
 1027|    528|            .count()
 1028|    528|            .max(1); // At least 1 sentence
 1029|       |
 1030|       |        // Count syllables
 1031|  74.3k|        let syllable_count: usize = words.iter().map(|w| Self::count_syllables(w)).sum();
                          ^528            ^528    ^528         ^528                              ^528
 1032|       |
 1033|    528|        let words_f = word_count as f32;
 1034|    528|        let sentences_f = sentence_count as f32;
 1035|    528|        let syllables_f = syllable_count as f32;
 1036|       |
 1037|       |        // Flesch Reading Ease: 206.835 - 1.015*(words/sentences) - 84.6*(syllables/words)
 1038|    528|        let flesch_ease =
 1039|    528|            206.835 - 1.015 * (words_f / sentences_f) - 84.6 * (syllables_f / words_f);
 1040|       |
 1041|       |        // Flesch-Kincaid Grade Level: 0.39*(words/sentences) + 11.8*(syllables/words) - 15.59
 1042|    528|        let grade_level = 0.39 * (words_f / sentences_f) + 11.8 * (syllables_f / words_f) - 15.59;
 1043|       |
 1044|    528|        ReadabilityStats {
 1045|    528|            flesch_ease: flesch_ease.clamp(0.0, 100.0),
 1046|    528|            grade_level: grade_level.max(0.0),
 1047|    528|            word_count,
 1048|    528|            sentences: sentence_count,
 1049|    528|            syllables: syllable_count,
 1050|    528|        }
 1051|    532|    }

 2328|     14|        text.chars().take(count).collect()
 2329|     14|    }

 2331|      2|    fn take_last_chars(text: &str, count: usize) -> String {
 2332|      2|        let mut chars: Vec<char> = text.chars().rev().take(count).collect();
 2333|      2|        chars.reverse();

 2337|     14|    fn middle_ellipsize(text: &str, max_chars: usize) -> String {
 2338|     14|        let total_chars = text.chars().count();
 2339|     14|        if total_chars <= max_chars {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (2339:12): [True: 0, False: 14]
  ------------------
 2340|      0|            return text.to_string();
 2341|     14|        }
 2342|       |
 2343|     14|        if max_chars <= 3 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (2343:12): [True: 0, False: 14]
  ------------------
 2344|      0|            return ".".repeat(max_chars);
 2345|     14|        }
 2346|       |
 2347|     14|        let file_name = text
 2348|     14|            .rsplit(['\\', '/'])
 2349|     14|            .next()
 2350|     14|            .filter(|segment| !segment.is_empty());
 2351|       |
 2352|     14|        if let Some(file_name) = file_name {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (2352:16): [True: 14, False: 0]
  ------------------
 2353|     14|            let file_name_chars = file_name.chars().count();
 2354|     14|            if file_name_chars + 3 < max_chars {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (2354:16): [True: 12, False: 2]
  ------------------
 2355|     12|                let prefix_chars = max_chars - file_name_chars - 3;
 2356|     12|                return format!("{}...{}", Self::take_chars(text, prefix_chars), file_name);
 2357|      2|            }
 2358|      0|        }
 2359|       |
 2360|      2|        let head_chars = (max_chars - 3) / 2;
 2361|      2|        let tail_chars = max_chars - 3 - head_chars;
 2362|      2|        format!(
 2363|       |            "{}...{}",
 2364|      2|            Self::take_chars(text, head_chars),
 2365|      2|            Self::take_last_chars(text, tail_chars)
 2366|       |        )
 2367|     14|    }

 2369|      6|    fn fit_middle_ellipsized_label(
 2370|      6|        ui: &egui::Ui,
 2371|      6|        prefix: &str,
 2372|      6|        value: &str,
 2373|      6|        max_width: f32,

  ------------------
  |  Branch (2376:12): [True: 0, False: 6]

 2377|      4|            return full_label;
 2378|      2|        }
 2379|       |

 2382|      2|        let mut low = 3;
 2383|      2|        let mut high = value_chars.max(3);
 2384|       |
 2385|     14|        while low <= high {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (2385:15): [True: 12, False: 2]

  ------------------

 2389|      4|                best = candidate;
 2390|      4|                low = mid + 1;
 2391|      8|            } else {
 2392|      8|                high = mid.saturating_sub(1);
 2393|      8|            }
 2394|       |        }
 2395|       |
 2396|      2|        best
 2397|      6|    }

 3525|  1.33k|        for ch in value.chars() {
                                ^28   ^28

 3527|    172|                '\\' => out.push_str("\\\\"),
 3528|      2|                '"' => out.push_str("\\\""),

 3532|  1.15k|                _ => out.push(ch),
 3533|       |            }
 3534|       |        }

 3579|       |    fn env_lock() -> std::sync::MutexGuard<'static, ()> {
 3580|       |        static ENV_LOCK: OnceLock<Mutex<()>> = OnceLock::new();
 3581|       |        ENV_LOCK
 3582|       |            .get_or_init(|| Mutex::new(()))
 3583|       |            .lock()
 3584|       |            .expect("env lock")
 3585|       |    }
 3586|       |
 3587|       |    struct EnvGuard {
 3588|       |        key: &'static str,
 3589|       |        original: Option<String>,
 3590|       |    }
 3591|       |
 3592|       |    impl EnvGuard {
 3593|       |        fn set(key: &'static str, value: &str) -> Self {
 3594|       |            let original = std::env::var(key).ok();
 3595|       |            std::env::set_var(key, value);
 3596|       |            Self { key, original }
 3597|       |        }
 3598|       |    }
 3599|       |
 3600|       |    impl Drop for EnvGuard {
 3601|       |        fn drop(&mut self) {
 3602|       |            if let Some(value) = &self.original {
 3603|       |                std::env::set_var(self.key, value);
 3604|       |            } else {
 3605|       |                std::env::remove_var(self.key);
 3606|       |            }
 3607|       |        }
 3608|       |    }
 3609|       |
 3610|       |    struct CurrentDirGuard {
 3611|       |        _lock: std::sync::MutexGuard<'static, ()>,
 3612|       |        original: PathBuf,
 3613|       |    }
 3614|       |
 3615|       |    impl CurrentDirGuard {
 3616|       |        fn set(path: &Path) -> Result<Self> {
 3617|       |            let lock = env_lock();
 3618|       |            let original = std::env::current_dir()?;
 3619|       |            std::env::set_current_dir(path)?;
 3620|       |            Ok(Self {
 3621|       |                _lock: lock,
 3622|       |                original,
 3623|       |            })
 3624|       |        }
 3625|       |    }
 3626|       |
 3627|       |    impl Drop for CurrentDirGuard {
 3628|       |        fn drop(&mut self) {
 3629|       |            let _ = std::env::set_current_dir(&self.original);
 3630|       |        }
 3631|       |    }

 3633|       |    /// Helper to set platform-appropriate config env var and return expected config subdir.
 3634|       |    fn set_config_env(temp_path: &std::path::Path) -> (EnvGuard, std::path::PathBuf) {
 3635|       |        #[cfg(target_os = "windows")]
 3636|       |        {
 3637|       |            let guard = EnvGuard::set("APPDATA", temp_path.to_string_lossy().as_ref());
 3638|       |            let subdir = temp_path.join("MarkdownView");
 3639|       |            (guard, subdir)
 3640|       |        }
 3641|       |        #[cfg(target_os = "macos")]
 3642|       |        {
 3643|       |            let guard = EnvGuard::set("HOME", temp_path.to_string_lossy().as_ref());
 3644|       |            let subdir = temp_path.join("Library/Application Support/MarkdownView");
 3645|       |            (guard, subdir)
 3646|       |        }
 3647|       |        #[cfg(not(any(target_os = "windows", target_os = "macos")))]
 3648|       |        {
 3649|       |            let guard = EnvGuard::set("XDG_CONFIG_HOME", temp_path.to_string_lossy().as_ref());
 3650|       |            let subdir = temp_path.join("mdmdview");
 3651|       |            (guard, subdir)
 3652|       |        }
 3653|       |    }

 3683|       |    fn test_app_update_calls_update_impl() {
 3684|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 3685|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 3686|       |        let _ = ctx.run(default_input(), |_| {});
 3687|       |        // SAFETY: eframe::Frame is unused by update(), so a dummy value is sufficient here.
 3688|       |        let mut frame = std::mem::MaybeUninit::<eframe::Frame>::zeroed();
 3689|       |        let frame = unsafe { frame.assume_init_mut() };
 3690|       |        app.update(&ctx, frame);
 3691|       |    }
 3692|       |
 3693|       |    struct ForcedAppActions {
 3694|       |        actions: Vec<&'static str>,
 3695|       |    }
 3696|       |
 3697|       |    impl ForcedAppActions {
 3698|       |        fn new(actions: &[&'static str]) -> Self {
 3699|       |            FORCED_APP_ACTIONS.with(|set| {
 3700|       |                let mut set = set.borrow_mut();
 3701|       |                for action in actions {
 3702|       |                    set.insert(*action);
 3703|       |                }
 3704|       |            });
 3705|       |            Self {
 3706|       |                actions: actions.to_vec(),
 3707|       |            }
 3708|       |        }
 3709|       |    }
 3710|       |
 3711|       |    impl Drop for ForcedAppActions {
 3712|       |        fn drop(&mut self) {
 3713|       |            FORCED_APP_ACTIONS.with(|set| {
 3714|       |                let mut set = set.borrow_mut();
 3715|       |                for action in &self.actions {
 3716|       |                    set.remove(action);
 3717|       |                }
 3718|       |            });
 3719|       |        }
 3720|       |    }
 3721|       |
 3722|       |    struct ForcedDialogPaths;
 3723|       |
 3724|       |    impl ForcedDialogPaths {
 3725|       |        fn new(open: Option<PathBuf>, save: Option<PathBuf>) -> Self {
 3726|       |            FORCED_OPEN_PATH.with(|slot| {
 3727|       |                *slot.borrow_mut() = open;
 3728|       |            });
 3729|       |            FORCED_SAVE_PATH.with(|slot| {
 3730|       |                *slot.borrow_mut() = save;
 3731|       |            });
 3732|       |            Self
 3733|       |        }
 3734|       |    }
 3735|       |
 3736|       |    impl Drop for ForcedDialogPaths {
 3737|       |        fn drop(&mut self) {
 3738|       |            FORCED_OPEN_PATH.with(|slot| {
 3739|       |                slot.borrow_mut().take();
 3740|       |            });
 3741|       |            FORCED_SAVE_PATH.with(|slot| {
 3742|       |                slot.borrow_mut().take();
 3743|       |            });
 3744|       |        }
 3745|       |    }
 3746|       |
 3747|       |    /// RAII guard that sets a thread-local `RefCell<bool>` flag to `true` on creation
 3748|       |    /// and restores it to `false` on drop. Used to inject errors in test paths.
 3749|       |    struct ForcedFlag {
 3750|       |        flag: &'static std::thread::LocalKey<RefCell<bool>>,
 3751|       |    }
 3752|       |
 3753|       |    impl ForcedFlag {
 3754|       |        fn new(flag: &'static std::thread::LocalKey<RefCell<bool>>) -> Self {
 3755|       |            flag.with(|f| *f.borrow_mut() = true);
 3756|       |            Self { flag }
 3757|       |        }
 3758|       |    }
 3759|       |
 3760|       |    impl Drop for ForcedFlag {
 3761|       |        fn drop(&mut self) {
 3762|       |            self.flag.with(|f| *f.borrow_mut() = false);
 3763|       |        }
 3764|       |    }
 3765|       |
 3766|       |    fn force_load_error() -> ForcedFlag {
 3767|       |        ForcedFlag::new(&FORCED_LOAD_ERROR)
 3768|       |    }
 3769|       |
 3770|       |    fn force_scan_error() -> ForcedFlag {
 3771|       |        ForcedFlag::new(&FORCED_SCAN_ERROR)
 3772|       |    }
 3773|       |
 3774|       |    fn force_scan_entry_error() -> ForcedFlag {
 3775|       |        ForcedFlag::new(&FORCED_SCAN_ENTRY_ERROR)
 3776|       |    }
 3777|       |
 3778|       |    fn force_lossy_read_error() -> ForcedFlag {
 3779|       |        ForcedFlag::new(&FORCED_READ_LOSSY_ERROR)
 3780|       |    }

 3813|       |    /// Helper to construct a key event for shortcut testing.

 3815|       |        egui::Event::Key {
 3816|       |            key,
 3817|       |            physical_key: None,

 3823|       |
 3824|       |    /// Helper to store a TextEditState with a given cursor position into the raw editor.
 3825|       |    fn store_raw_editor_cursor(ctx: &egui::Context, cursor_pos: usize) {
 3826|       |        let editor_id = egui::Id::new("raw_editor");
 3827|       |        let mut state = egui::text_edit::TextEditState::default();
 3828|       |        let cr = egui::text::CCursorRange::one(egui::text::CCursor::new(cursor_pos));
 3829|       |        state.cursor.set_char_range(Some(cr));
 3830|       |        state.store(ctx, editor_id);
 3831|       |    }
 3832|       |
 3833|       |    /// Helper to store a TextEditState with no cursor range into the raw editor.
 3834|       |    fn store_raw_editor_no_cursor(ctx: &egui::Context) {
 3835|       |        let editor_id = egui::Id::new("raw_editor");
 3836|       |        let mut state = egui::text_edit::TextEditState::default();
 3837|       |        state.cursor.set_char_range(None);
 3838|       |        state.store(ctx, editor_id);
 3839|       |    }

 3841|       |    /// Helper to set up app in raw write mode with the given buffer content.
 3842|       |    fn setup_raw_write_mode(app: &mut MarkdownViewerApp, content: &str) {
 3843|       |        app.view_mode = ViewMode::Raw;
 3844|       |        app.write_enabled = true;
 3845|       |        app.raw_buffer = content.to_string();
 3846|       |        app.current_content = app.raw_buffer.clone();
 3847|       |    }

 3855|       |
 3856|       |    #[test]
 3857|       |    fn test_normalize_line_endings() {
 3858|       |        // Windows style (\r\n)
 3859|       |        assert_eq!(
 3860|       |            MarkdownViewerApp::normalize_line_endings("Hello\r\nWorld"),
 3861|       |            "Hello\nWorld"
 3862|       |        );
 3863|       |
 3864|       |        // Unix style (\n) - no change
 3865|       |        assert_eq!(
 3866|       |            MarkdownViewerApp::normalize_line_endings("Hello\nWorld"),
 3867|       |            "Hello\nWorld"
 3868|       |        );
 3869|       |
 3870|       |        // Old Mac style (\r)
 3871|       |        assert_eq!(
 3872|       |            MarkdownViewerApp::normalize_line_endings("Hello\rWorld"),
 3873|       |            "Hello\nWorld"
 3874|       |        );
 3875|       |
 3876|       |        // Mixed line endings
 3877|       |        assert_eq!(
 3878|       |            MarkdownViewerApp::normalize_line_endings("A\r\nB\nC\rD"),
 3879|       |            "A\nB\nC\nD"
 3880|       |        );
 3881|       |
 3882|       |        // Multiple blank lines with Windows endings
 3883|       |        assert_eq!(
 3884|       |            MarkdownViewerApp::normalize_line_endings("A\r\n\r\nB"),
 3885|       |            "A\n\nB"
 3886|       |        );
 3887|       |
 3888|       |        // Empty string
 3889|       |        assert_eq!(MarkdownViewerApp::normalize_line_endings(""), "");
 3890|       |
 3891|       |        // No line endings
 3892|       |        assert_eq!(
 3893|       |            MarkdownViewerApp::normalize_line_endings("Single line"),
 3894|       |            "Single line"
 3895|       |        );
 3896|       |    }
 3897|       |
 3898|       |    #[test]
 3899|       |    fn test_load_file_with_windows_line_endings() -> Result<()> {
 3900|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 3901|       |        let mut temp_file = NamedTempFile::new()?;
 3902|       |
 3903|       |        // Write content with explicit Windows line endings
 3904|       |        temp_file.write_all(b"Line 1\r\nLine 2\r\n\r\nParagraph text")?;
 3905|       |        temp_file.flush()?;
 3906|       |
 3907|       |        app.load_file(temp_file.path().to_path_buf(), true)?;
 3908|       |
 3909|       |        // Should not contain any \r characters after normalization
 3910|       |        assert!(!app.current_content.contains('\r'));
 3911|       |        assert!(app.current_content.contains("Line 1\nLine 2"));
 3912|       |        assert!(app.current_content.contains("\n\nParagraph"));
 3913|       |        Ok(())
 3914|       |    }
 3915|       |
 3916|       |    #[test]
 3917|       |    fn test_load_file_with_mixed_line_endings() -> Result<()> {
 3918|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 3919|       |        let mut temp_file = NamedTempFile::new()?;
 3920|       |
 3921|       |        // Mix of \r\n (Windows), \n (Unix), and \r (old Mac)
 3922|       |        temp_file.write_all(b"Line 1\r\nLine 2\nLine 3\rLine 4")?;
 3923|       |        temp_file.flush()?;
 3924|       |
 3925|       |        app.load_file(temp_file.path().to_path_buf(), true)?;
 3926|       |
 3927|       |        // All should be normalized to \n
 3928|       |        assert!(!app.current_content.contains('\r'));
 3929|       |        assert_eq!(app.current_content.lines().count(), 4);
 3930|       |        let lines: Vec<&str> = app.current_content.lines().collect();
 3931|       |        assert_eq!(lines[0], "Line 1");
 3932|       |        assert_eq!(lines[1], "Line 2");
 3933|       |        assert_eq!(lines[2], "Line 3");
 3934|       |        assert_eq!(lines[3], "Line 4");
 3935|       |        Ok(())
 3936|       |    }

 3939|       |    fn test_load_file_strips_bom() -> Result<()> {
 3940|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 3941|       |        let mut temp_file = NamedTempFile::new()?;
 3942|       |
 3943|       |        temp_file.write_all(b"\xEF\xBB\xBF# Heading\nContent")?;
 3944|       |        temp_file.flush()?;
 3945|       |
 3946|       |        app.load_file(temp_file.path().to_path_buf(), true)?;
 3947|       |
 3948|       |        assert!(!app.current_content.starts_with('\u{FEFF}'));

 3951|       |    }

 4085|       |    #[test]
 4086|       |    fn test_spawn_file_loader_processes_request() -> Result<()> {
 4087|       |        let temp = NamedTempFile::new()?;
 4088|       |        writeln!(&temp, "hello")?;
 4089|       |
 4090|       |        let (request_tx, result_rx) = MarkdownViewerApp::spawn_file_loader();
 4091|       |        request_tx
 4092|       |            .send(FileLoadRequest {
 4093|       |                id: 99,
 4094|       |                path: temp.path().to_path_buf(),
 4095|       |            })
 4096|       |            .expect("send");
 4097|       |        let result = result_rx
 4098|       |            .recv_timeout(std::time::Duration::from_secs(1))
 4099|       |            .expect("result");
 4100|       |        let (content, lossy) = result.content.expect("content");
 4101|       |        assert!(content.contains("hello"));
 4102|       |        assert!(!lossy);
 4103|       |        drop(request_tx);
 4104|       |        Ok(())
 4105|       |    }

 4161|       |    #[test]
 4162|       |    fn test_load_file_async_sends_request() -> Result<()> {
 4163|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 4164|       |        let mut temp_file = NamedTempFile::new()?;
 4165|       |        let payload = vec![b'a'; ASYNC_LOAD_THRESHOLD_BYTES as usize];
 4166|       |        temp_file.write_all(&payload)?;
 4167|       |        temp_file.flush()?;
 4168|       |
 4169|       |        let path = temp_file.path().to_path_buf();
 4170|       |        app.load_file(path.clone(), false)?;

 4172|       |        assert_eq!(app.pending_file_load.as_ref().expect("pending").path, path);
 4173|       |        Ok(())
 4174|       |    }

 4214|       |    #[test]
 4215|       |    fn test_poll_file_loads_handles_mismatch_and_lossy() {
 4216|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 4217|       |        let (result_tx, result_rx) = unbounded::<FileLoadResult>();
 4218|       |        app.file_load_rx = result_rx;
 4219|       |
 4220|       |        let temp = NamedTempFile::new().expect("temp");
 4221|       |        let path = temp.path().to_path_buf();
 4222|       |        app.pending_file_load = Some(PendingFileLoad {
 4223|       |            id: 1,
 4224|       |            path: path.clone(),
 4225|       |        });
 4226|       |
 4227|       |        result_tx
 4228|       |            .send(FileLoadResult {
 4229|       |                id: 2,
 4230|       |                content: Ok(("ignored".to_string(), false)),
 4231|       |            })
 4232|       |            .expect("send mismatch");
 4233|       |        app.poll_file_loads();
 4234|       |        assert!(app.pending_file_load.is_some());
 4235|       |
 4236|       |        result_tx
 4237|       |            .send(FileLoadResult {
 4238|       |                id: 1,
 4239|       |                content: Ok(("loaded".to_string(), true)),
 4240|       |            })
 4241|       |            .expect("send match");
 4242|       |        app.poll_file_loads();
 4243|       |        assert!(app.pending_file_load.is_none());
 4244|       |        assert_eq!(app.current_file, Some(path));
 4245|       |    }

 4272|       |    fn test_poll_file_loads_sets_error_on_failure() {
 4273|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 4274|       |        let (result_tx, result_rx) = unbounded::<FileLoadResult>();
 4275|       |        app.file_load_rx = result_rx;
 4276|       |
 4277|       |        let temp = NamedTempFile::new().expect("temp");
 4278|       |        let path = temp.path().to_path_buf();
 4279|       |        app.pending_file_load = Some(PendingFileLoad { id: 1, path });
 4280|       |
 4281|       |        result_tx
 4282|       |            .send(FileLoadResult {
 4283|       |                id: 1,
 4284|       |                content: Err("boom".to_string()),
 4285|       |            })
 4286|       |            .expect("send match");
 4287|       |        app.poll_file_loads();
 4288|       |        assert!(app.pending_file_load.is_none());
 4289|       |        assert!(app.error_message.as_deref().unwrap_or("").contains("boom"));

 4339|       |    #[test]
 4340|       |    fn test_reload_current_file() -> Result<()> {
 4341|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 4342|       |
 4343|       |        // Create a temporary markdown file
 4344|       |        let mut temp_file = NamedTempFile::new()?;
 4345|       |        let content1 = "# Title\n\nVersion 1";
 4346|       |        temp_file.write_all(content1.as_bytes())?;
 4347|       |        temp_file.flush()?;
 4348|       |
 4349|       |        let path = temp_file.path().to_path_buf();
 4350|       |        app.load_file(path.clone(), true)?;
 4351|       |        assert!(app.current_content.contains("Version 1"));
 4352|       |
 4353|       |        // Update file content
 4354|       |        let content2 = "# Title\n\nVersion 2";
 4355|       |        temp_file.as_file_mut().set_len(0)?; // clear
 4356|       |        temp_file.write_all(content2.as_bytes())?;
 4357|       |        temp_file.flush()?;
 4358|       |
 4359|       |        // Reload and verify
 4360|       |        app.reload_current_file()?;
 4361|       |        assert!(app.current_content.contains("Version 2"));
 4362|       |        Ok(())
 4363|       |    }

 4411|       |    #[test]
 4412|       |    fn test_window_state_change_detection() {
 4413|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 4414|       |        app.last_window_pos = Some([10.0, 10.0]);
 4415|       |        app.last_window_size = Some([800.0, 600.0]);
 4416|       |        app.last_window_maximized = false;
 4417|       |
 4418|       |        let state = app.current_window_state().expect("state");
 4419|       |        assert!(app.window_state_changed(&state));
 4420|       |        app.last_persisted_state = Some(state);
 4421|       |        assert!(!app.window_state_changed(&state));
 4422|       |
 4423|       |        let mut moved = state;
 4424|       |        moved.pos = [12.0, 10.0];
 4425|       |        assert!(app.window_state_changed(&moved));
 4426|       |
 4427|       |        let mut resized = state;
 4428|       |        resized.size = [820.0, 610.0];
 4429|       |        assert!(app.window_state_changed(&resized));
 4430|       |
 4431|       |        let mut maximized = state;
 4432|       |        maximized.maximized = true;
 4433|       |        assert!(app.window_state_changed(&maximized));
 4434|       |    }

 4619|       |    #[test]
 4620|       |    fn test_page_navigation_calculations() {
 4621|       |        // Test the page size calculation logic used in navigation
 4622|       |        let viewport_height = 800.0f32;
 4623|       |        let page_size = viewport_height * 0.8;
 4624|       |        assert_eq!(page_size, 640.0);
 4625|       |
 4626|       |        // Test boundary conditions for Page Up
 4627|       |        let current_offset = 100.0f32;
 4628|       |        let new_offset_up = (current_offset - page_size).max(0.0);
 4629|       |        assert_eq!(new_offset_up, 0.0); // Should clamp to 0
 4630|       |
 4631|       |        // Test Page Down calculation
 4632|       |        let new_offset_down = current_offset + page_size;
 4633|       |        assert_eq!(new_offset_down, 740.0);
 4634|       |
 4635|       |        // Test that Page Up from near top goes to 0
 4636|       |        let near_top = 300.0f32;
 4637|       |        let from_near_top = (near_top - page_size).max(0.0);
 4638|       |        assert_eq!(from_near_top, 0.0);
 4639|       |    }
 4640|       |
 4641|       |    #[test]
 4642|       |    fn test_compute_window_adjustment_clamps_offscreen_window() {
 4643|       |        let outer = egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(1200.0, 900.0), egui::vec2(800.0, 600.0));
 4644|       |        let monitor = egui::vec2(1024.0, 768.0);
 4645|       |        let adjustment =
 4646|       |            MarkdownViewerApp::compute_window_adjustment(Some(outer), Some(outer), Some(monitor))
 4647|       |                .expect("should adjust window geometry");
 4648|       |        let pos = adjustment.pos.expect("expected position adjustment");
 4649|       |        let size = adjustment.size.unwrap_or_else(|| outer.size());
 4650|       |        assert!(pos.x <= monitor.x - size.x + 1.0);
 4651|       |        assert!(pos.y <= monitor.y - size.y + 1.0);
 4652|       |        assert!(adjustment.size.is_none());
 4653|       |    }
 4654|       |
 4655|       |    #[test]
 4656|       |    fn test_compute_window_adjustment_respects_min_size() {
 4657|       |        let outer = egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(-200.0, -100.0), egui::vec2(200.0, 100.0));
 4658|       |        let monitor = egui::vec2(1920.0, 1080.0);
 4659|       |        let adjustment =
 4660|       |            MarkdownViewerApp::compute_window_adjustment(Some(outer), Some(outer), Some(monitor))
 4661|       |                .expect("should enforce minimum window size");
 4662|       |        let pos = adjustment.pos.unwrap_or(outer.min);
 4663|       |        let size = adjustment.size.expect("expected size adjustment");
 4664|       |        assert!(size.x >= 600.0);
 4665|       |        assert!(size.y >= 400.0);
 4666|       |        assert!(pos.x >= 0.0);
 4667|       |        assert!(pos.y >= 0.0);
 4668|       |    }
 4669|       |
 4670|       |    #[test]
 4671|       |    fn test_is_valid_markdown_file() {
 4672|       |        let app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 4673|       |
 4674|       |        // Valid markdown extensions
 4675|       |        assert!(app.is_valid_markdown_file(Path::new("test.md")));
 4676|       |        assert!(app.is_valid_markdown_file(Path::new("test.markdown")));
 4677|       |        assert!(app.is_valid_markdown_file(Path::new("test.mdown")));
 4678|       |        assert!(app.is_valid_markdown_file(Path::new("test.mkd")));
 4679|       |        assert!(app.is_valid_markdown_file(Path::new("test.txt")));
 4680|       |
 4681|       |        // Case insensitive
 4682|       |        assert!(app.is_valid_markdown_file(Path::new("test.MD")));
 4683|       |        assert!(app.is_valid_markdown_file(Path::new("test.Markdown")));
 4684|       |
 4685|       |        // Invalid extensions
 4686|       |        assert!(!app.is_valid_markdown_file(Path::new("test.pdf")));
 4687|       |        assert!(!app.is_valid_markdown_file(Path::new("test.docx")));
 4688|       |        assert!(!app.is_valid_markdown_file(Path::new("test.html")));
 4689|       |
 4690|       |        // No extension
 4691|       |        assert!(!app.is_valid_markdown_file(Path::new("test")));
 4692|       |    }
 4693|       |
 4694|       |    #[test]
 4695|       |    fn test_scan_directory() -> Result<()> {
 4696|       |        let temp_dir = tempfile::TempDir::new()?;
 4697|       |        let dir_path = temp_dir.path();
 4698|       |
 4699|       |        // Create test files
 4700|       |        std::fs::write(dir_path.join("zebra.md"), "# Z")?;
 4701|       |        std::fs::write(dir_path.join("alpha.md"), "# A")?;
 4702|       |        std::fs::write(dir_path.join("image.png"), "fake")?;
 4703|       |        std::fs::write(dir_path.join("beta.markdown"), "# B")?;
 4704|       |
 4705|       |        // Create subdirectory (should be ignored)
 4706|       |        std::fs::create_dir(dir_path.join("subdir"))?;
 4707|       |        std::fs::write(dir_path.join("subdir/nested.md"), "# N")?;
 4708|       |
 4709|       |        let app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 4710|       |        let files = app.scan_directory(dir_path)?;
 4711|       |
 4712|       |        // Should find 3 markdown files (alpha, beta, zebra)
 4713|       |        // Should NOT find image.png or nested.md
 4714|       |        assert_eq!(files.len(), 3);
 4715|       |
 4716|       |        // Should be sorted alphabetically
 4717|       |        assert!(files[0].ends_with("alpha.md"));
 4718|       |        assert!(files[1].ends_with("beta.markdown"));
 4719|       |        assert!(files[2].ends_with("zebra.md"));
 4720|       |
 4721|       |        Ok(())
 4722|       |    }

 4777|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 4778|       |        let temp_dir = tempfile::TempDir::new()?;
 4779|       |
 4780|       |        let files: Vec<PathBuf> = (0..5)
 4781|       |            .map(|i| {
 4782|       |                let path = temp_dir.path().join(format!("file{}.md", i));
 4783|       |                std::fs::write(&path, format!("# File {}", i)).unwrap();
 4784|       |                path
 4785|       |            })

 4789|       |

 4853|       |    #[test]
 4854|       |    fn test_queue_navigation() -> Result<()> {
 4855|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 4856|       |        let temp_dir = tempfile::TempDir::new()?;
 4857|       |
 4858|       |        let file1 = temp_dir.path().join("file1.md");
 4859|       |        let file2 = temp_dir.path().join("file2.md");
 4860|       |
 4861|       |        std::fs::write(&file1, "# File 1")?;
 4862|       |        std::fs::write(&file2, "# File 2")?;
 4863|       |
 4864|       |        // Load first file and queue second
 4865|       |        app.load_file(file1.clone(), true)?;
 4866|       |        app.pending_files.push_back(file2.clone());
 4867|       |
 4868|       |        assert!(app.can_navigate_forward());
 4869|       |        app.navigate_forward();
 4870|       |        assert_eq!(app.current_file, Some(file2));
 4871|       |        assert!(app.pending_files.is_empty());
 4872|       |
 4873|       |        Ok(())
 4874|       |    }

 4878|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 4879|       |        let temp_dir = tempfile::TempDir::new()?;
 4880|       |
 4881|       |        let md_file = temp_dir.path().join("test.md");
 4882|       |        let pdf_file = temp_dir.path().join("test.pdf");
 4883|       |
 4884|       |        std::fs::write(&md_file, "# Test")?;
 4885|       |        std::fs::write(&pdf_file, "fake pdf")?;
 4886|       |
 4887|       |        app.handle_file_drop(vec![md_file.clone(), pdf_file]);
 4888|       |
 4889|       |        // Should open the valid markdown file
 4890|       |        assert_eq!(app.current_file, Some(md_file));
 4891|       |        // Should show error about the invalid file
 4892|       |        assert!(app.error_message.is_some());
 4893|       |        Ok(())
 4894|       |    }
