C:\language\mdmdview\src\d2_renderer.rs:
    1|       |//! D2 diagram renderer.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! Renders D2 diagram markup to SVG using the embedded mdmdview-d2 crate,
    4|       |//! then rasterizes SVG to egui textures via usvg/resvg. Heavy rendering
    5|       |//! work (D2 compilation + SVG rasterization) runs on a dedicated worker
    6|       |//! thread; the main thread only handles the cheap GPU texture upload.
    7|       |//!
    8|       |//! Key components:
    9|       |//! - `D2Renderer` struct: holds font database, caches, and worker channels
   10|       |//! - `LruCache<K, V>`: generic LRU cache (shared module in lru_cache.rs)
   11|       |//! - `render_d2_to_svg()`: static method calling into the mdmdview-d2 crate
   12|       |//! - `rasterize_svg()`: pure function: SVG string -> RGBA bytes (runs on worker)
   13|       |//! - `poll_results()`: drains worker results, uploads textures to GPU (main thread)
   14|       |//!
   15|       |//! This module is conditionally compiled behind `#[cfg(feature = "d2")]`.
   16|       |//! It integrates with `MarkdownRenderer` following the same public API pattern
   17|       |//! as `PikchrRenderer` (render_block, begin_frame, has_pending, release_gpu_textures).
   18|       |
   19|       |use std::cell::RefCell;
   20|       |use std::collections::{HashMap, HashSet};
   21|       |use std::sync::Arc;
   22|       |use std::time::{Duration, Instant};
   23|       |
   24|       |use crossbeam_channel::{Receiver, Sender, TrySendError};
   25|       |
   26|       |use crate::lru_cache::{hash_str, LruCache};
   27|       |use crate::ThemeColors;
   28|       |
   29|       |// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   30|       |// Cache capacity constants
   31|       |// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   32|       |
   33|       |const D2_TEXTURE_CACHE_CAPACITY: usize = 64;
   34|       |const D2_SVG_CACHE_CAPACITY: usize = 64;
   35|       |const D2_ERROR_CACHE_CAPACITY: usize = 32;
   36|       |/// Trailing-edge debounce cooldown for resize. During window resize, suppress
   37|       |/// rasterization enqueue and show the last rendered texture scaled to fit.
   38|       |/// Only enqueue a new rasterization job once the width stabilizes for this
   39|       |/// duration.
   40|       |const RESIZE_DEBOUNCE_MS: u64 = 150;
   41|       |/// Maximum rasterized dimension (width or height) in pixels.
   42|       |/// Matches the cap used by pikchr_renderer and mermaid_renderer.
   43|       |const MAX_RASTER_SIDE: u32 = 4096;
   44|       |/// Rasterization supersample factor. D2 diagrams benefit from higher pixel
   45|       |/// density for crisp text and clean lines, same as pikchr_renderer.
   46|       |const D2_SUPERSAMPLE: f32 = 2.0;
   47|       |/// Bounded channel capacity for job and result channels. With debounce
   48|       |/// suppressing enqueue during resize, at most one job per diagram is
   49|       |/// enqueued after the debounce expires. A document with ~6-8 diagrams
   50|       |/// fits within 8 slots. The bounded channel provides natural backpressure
   51|       |/// if the worker falls behind.
   52|       |const CHANNEL_CAPACITY: usize = 8;
   53|       |
   54|       |// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   55|       |// Channel type aliases
   56|       |// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   57|       |
   58|       |type JobSender = Sender<RasterJob>;
   59|       |type JobReceiver = Receiver<RasterJob>;
   60|       |type ResultSender = Sender<RasterResult>;
   61|       |type ResultReceiver = Receiver<RasterResult>;
   62|       |
   63|       |// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   64|       |// RasterJob / RasterResult
   65|       |// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   66|       |
   67|       |/// Job sent from main thread to worker.
   68|       |struct RasterJob {
   69|       |    /// Hash of the source code (for cache keying).
   70|       |    code_hash: u64,
   71|       |    /// Composite texture cache key (includes width, scale, theme).
   72|       |    texture_key: String,
   73|       |    /// Source code to render. None if SVG is provided directly.
   74|       |    code: Option<String>,
   75|       |    /// Pre-cached SVG string. If present, skip source->SVG step.
   76|       |    svg: Option<String>,
   77|       |    /// Dark mode flag (affects D2 source->SVG output).
   78|       |    dark_mode: bool,
   79|       |    /// Width bucket (32px quantized) for rasterization scaling.
   80|       |    width_bucket: u32,
   81|       |    /// Scale bucket (integer %, e.g., 100 = 1.0x zoom).
   82|       |    scale_bucket: u32,
   83|       |}
   84|       |
   85|       |/// Result sent from worker back to main thread.
   86|       |struct RasterResult {
   87|       |    /// Hash of the source code (for cache keying).
   88|       |    code_hash: u64,
   89|       |    /// Composite texture cache key.
   90|       |    texture_key: String,
   91|       |    /// Dark mode flag (copied from job, for SVG cache key computation).
   92|       |    dark_mode: bool,
   93|       |    /// SVG string (always set on success, for caching).
   94|       |    svg: Option<String>,
   95|       |    /// RGBA pixel data from rasterization.
   96|       |    rgba: Option<Vec<u8>>,
   97|       |    /// Raster dimensions in actual pixels (for GPU upload).
   98|       |    raster_size: Option<[usize; 2]>,
   99|       |    /// Display dimensions in logical pixels (for UI layout).
  100|       |    display_size: Option<[u32; 2]>,
  101|       |    /// Error message (set on failure, mutually exclusive with rgba).
  102|       |    error: Option<String>,
  103|       |}
  104|       |
  105|       |/// Intermediate output from `rasterize_svg`, before GPU upload.
  106|       |struct RasterOutput {
  107|       |    rgba: Vec<u8>,
  108|       |    raster_size: [usize; 2],
  109|       |    display_size: [u32; 2],
  110|       |}
  111|       |
  112|       |// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  113|       |// D2TextureEntry
  114|       |// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  115|       |
  116|       |/// A cached rasterized D2 diagram texture with its display dimensions.
  117|       |#[derive(Clone)]
  118|       |struct D2TextureEntry {
  119|       |    /// GPU texture handle (Clone wraps Arc internally).
  120|       |    texture: egui::TextureHandle,
  121|       |    /// Display dimensions in logical pixels [width, height].
  122|       |    /// The actual texture is larger by `D2_SUPERSAMPLE` for crisp rendering.
  123|       |    display_size: [u32; 2],
  124|       |}
  125|       |
  126|       |// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  127|       |// Helper functions
  128|       |// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  129|       |
  130|       |/// Composite SVG cache key: embeds both the source code hash and dark_mode flag.
  131|       |/// Dark mode produces different SVG output for the same source code.
  132|     16|fn svg_cache_key(code_hash: u64, dark_mode: bool) -> u128 {
  133|     16|    ((code_hash as u128) << 1) | (dark_mode as u128)
  134|     16|}
  135|       |
  136|       |/// Quantize a width value to 32-pixel buckets to avoid cache thrashing
  137|       |/// when the panel width changes by a few pixels (e.g., during resize).
  138|       |/// Returns the bucket midpoint as u32.
  139|     16|fn bucket_width(width: f32) -> u32 {
  140|     16|    let bucket = (width / 32.0).round() as u32;
  141|     16|    bucket * 32
  142|     16|}
  143|       |
  144|       |/// Quantize a UI scale factor to integer percentage buckets.
  145|       |/// e.g., 1.0 -> 100, 1.25 -> 125, 1.5 -> 150.
  146|      8|fn bucket_scale(scale: f32) -> u32 {
  147|      8|    (scale * 100.0).round() as u32
  148|      8|}
  149|       |
  150|       |// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  151|       |// D2Renderer
  152|       |// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  153|       |
  154|       |/// Per-diagram resize debounce state.
  155|       |struct DiagramDebounce {
  156|       |    /// Width bucket at which we last rasterized (and cached a texture).
  157|       |    last_rasterized_bucket: u32,
  158|       |    /// Width bucket seen on the most recent frame (for trailing-edge reset).
  159|       |    last_seen_bucket: u32,
  160|       |    /// When `last_seen_bucket` last changed. `None` when no resize is active.
  161|       |    bucket_changed_at: Option<Instant>,
  162|       |}
  163|       |
  164|       |/// D2 diagram renderer.
  165|       |///
  166|       |/// Renders D2 diagram markup to SVG using the embedded mdmdview-d2 crate,
  167|       |/// then rasterizes SVG to egui textures via usvg/resvg. Heavy rendering
  168|       |/// runs on a dedicated worker thread; the main thread only uploads the
  169|       |/// resulting RGBA data to the GPU (~0.1-1ms).
  170|       |pub(crate) struct D2Renderer {
  171|       |    /// LRU cache: texture_key (String) -> (TextureHandle, [width, height])
  172|       |    textures: RefCell<LruCache<String, D2TextureEntry>>,
  173|       |    /// SVG cache: svg_cache_key (u128: code_hash << 1 | dark_mode) -> SVG string
  174|       |    svg_cache: RefCell<LruCache<u128, String>>,
  175|       |    /// Error cache: code_hash (u64) -> error message.
  176|       |    /// Errors don't depend on dark_mode, only on source syntax.
  177|       |    errors: RefCell<LruCache<u64, String>>,
  178|       |    /// Per-diagram debounce state for resize suppression.
  179|       |    debounce: RefCell<HashMap<u64, DiagramDebounce>>,
  180|       |    /// Maps code_hash -> texture_key of the most recently rasterized texture.
  181|       |    /// Used to display a stale texture scaled to fit during debounce cooldown.
  182|       |    latest_texture_key: RefCell<HashMap<u64, String>>,
  183|       |    /// Job sender. Wrapped in Option so Drop can close the channel
  184|       |    /// before joining the worker thread (same pattern as Pikchr/Mermaid).
  185|       |    job_tx: Option<JobSender>,
  186|       |    /// Result receiver. Polled each frame in poll_results().
  187|       |    result_rx: ResultReceiver,
  188|       |    /// Worker thread handle. Joined on drop for clean shutdown.
  189|       |    worker_handle: Option<std::thread::JoinHandle<()>>,
  190|       |    /// Set of code_hashes with in-flight jobs. Keyed by code_hash (not
  191|       |    /// texture_key) to prevent redundant enqueues when the width changes
  192|       |    /// while a job is already pending for the same diagram.
  193|       |    pending: RefCell<HashSet<u64>>,
  194|       |    /// Tracks the currently desired width bucket per diagram (for stale discard).
  195|       |    wanted_bucket: RefCell<HashMap<u64, u32>>,
  196|       |}
  197|       |
  198|       |impl D2Renderer {
  199|       |    /// Create a new D2Renderer, loading system fonts once at startup.
  200|       |    /// Without system fonts, usvg's default empty fontdb silently drops all
  201|       |    /// text labels in rasterized SVGs. Takes ~50-200ms (one-time cost).
  202|       |    /// Spawns a dedicated worker thread for background rasterization.
  203|       |    /// Same pattern as pikchr_renderer.rs and mermaid_renderer.rs.
  204|    880|    pub(crate) fn new() -> Self {
  205|    880|        let mut db = usvg::fontdb::Database::new();
  206|    880|        db.load_system_fonts();
  207|    880|        let fontdb = Arc::new(db);
  208|       |
  209|    880|        let (job_tx, job_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(CHANNEL_CAPACITY);
  210|    880|        let (result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(CHANNEL_CAPACITY);
  211|       |
  212|       |        // Clone fontdb for the worker thread
  213|    880|        let worker_fontdb = Arc::clone(&fontdb);
  214|    880|        let worker_handle = std::thread::Builder::new()
  215|    880|            .name("d2-raster".into())
  216|    880|            .spawn(move || {
  217|    880|                Self::worker_loop(worker_fontdb, job_rx, result_tx);
  218|    880|            })
  219|    880|            .expect("failed to spawn d2 raster worker");
  220|       |
  221|    880|        Self {
  222|    880|            textures: RefCell::new(LruCache::new(D2_TEXTURE_CACHE_CAPACITY)),
  223|    880|            svg_cache: RefCell::new(LruCache::new(D2_SVG_CACHE_CAPACITY)),
  224|    880|            errors: RefCell::new(LruCache::new(D2_ERROR_CACHE_CAPACITY)),
  225|    880|            debounce: RefCell::new(HashMap::new()),
  226|    880|            latest_texture_key: RefCell::new(HashMap::new()),
  227|    880|            job_tx: Some(job_tx),
  228|    880|            result_rx,
  229|    880|            worker_handle: Some(worker_handle),
  230|    880|            pending: RefCell::new(HashSet::new()),
  231|    880|            wanted_bucket: RefCell::new(HashMap::new()),
  232|    880|        }
  233|    880|    }
  234|       |
  235|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  236|       |    // Worker thread
  237|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  238|       |
  239|       |    /// Main loop for the worker thread. Receives jobs, processes them, and
  240|       |    /// sends results back. Uses `try_send` to avoid blocking if the result
  241|       |    /// channel is full (prevents shutdown deadlock). Same pattern as Pikchr
  242|       |    /// and Mermaid renderers.
  243|    888|    fn worker_loop(
  244|    888|        fontdb: Arc<usvg::fontdb::Database>,
  245|    888|        job_rx: JobReceiver,
  246|    888|        result_tx: ResultSender,
  247|    888|    ) {
  248|    896|        while let Ok(job) = job_rx.recv() {
                                   ^8
  ------------------
  |  Branch (248:19): [True: 8, False: 888]
  ------------------
  249|      8|            let result = Self::process_job(&fontdb, job);
  250|      8|            // Use try_send to avoid blocking if result channel is full.
  251|      8|            // This prevents deadlock during shutdown: if Drop joins workers
  252|      8|            // while they're blocked on send (because result_rx isn't being
  253|      8|            // polled), we'd hang forever. Dropping results is acceptable --
  254|      8|            // the main thread still has the code_hash in the pending set, so
  255|      8|            // render_block will show the stale texture or placeholder and the
  256|      8|            // pending entry will be leaked (harmless, cleared on next
  257|      8|            // release_gpu_textures or app restart).
  258|      8|            let _ = result_tx.try_send(result);
  259|      8|        }
  260|       |        // job_rx disconnected (main thread dropped job_tx) -- exit
  261|    888|    }
  262|       |
  263|       |    /// Process a single rasterization job: source->SVG (if needed), then
  264|       |    /// SVG->RGBA. Returns a result with either RGBA data or an error.
  265|     10|    fn process_job(fontdb: &Arc<usvg::fontdb::Database>, job: RasterJob) -> RasterResult {
  266|       |        // Step 1: Get SVG (from cache or render fresh)
  267|     10|        let svg = match job.svg {
  268|      2|            Some(cached_svg) => Ok(cached_svg),
  269|       |            None => {
  270|      8|                let code = job.code.as_deref().unwrap_or("");
  271|      8|                Self::render_d2_to_svg(code, job.dark_mode)
  272|       |            }
  273|       |        };
  274|       |
  275|     10|        let svg = match svg {
                          ^8
  276|      8|            Ok(svg) => svg,
  277|      2|            Err(err) => {
  278|      2|                return RasterResult {
  279|      2|                    code_hash: job.code_hash,
  280|      2|                    texture_key: job.texture_key,
  281|      2|                    dark_mode: job.dark_mode,
  282|      2|                    svg: None,
  283|      2|                    rgba: None,
  284|      2|                    raster_size: None,
  285|      2|                    display_size: None,
  286|      2|                    error: Some(err),
  287|      2|                };
  288|       |            }
  289|       |        };
  290|       |
  291|       |        // Step 2: Rasterize SVG to RGBA
  292|      8|        match Self::rasterize_svg(fontdb, &svg, job.width_bucket, job.scale_bucket) {
  293|      8|            Ok(output) => RasterResult {
  294|      8|                code_hash: job.code_hash,
  295|      8|                texture_key: job.texture_key,
  296|      8|                dark_mode: job.dark_mode,
  297|      8|                svg: Some(svg),
  298|      8|                rgba: Some(output.rgba),
  299|      8|                raster_size: Some(output.raster_size),
  300|      8|                display_size: Some(output.display_size),
  301|      8|                error: None,
  302|      8|            },
  303|      0|            Err(err) => RasterResult {
  304|      0|                code_hash: job.code_hash,
  305|      0|                texture_key: job.texture_key,
  306|      0|                dark_mode: job.dark_mode,
  307|      0|                svg: Some(svg), // SVG succeeded -- cache it even if rasterize failed
  308|      0|                rgba: None,
  309|      0|                raster_size: None,
  310|      0|                display_size: None,
  311|      0|                error: Some(err),
  312|      0|            },
  313|       |        }
  314|     10|    }
  315|       |
  316|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  317|       |    // Rasterization (runs on worker thread)
  318|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  319|       |
  320|       |    /// Rasterize an SVG string to RGBA pixels. Pure function -- no GPU access,
  321|       |    /// no egui context needed. Runs on the worker thread.
  322|       |    ///
  323|       |    /// Parses via usvg (using the shared fontdb with system fonts), and
  324|       |    /// rasterizes via resvg/tiny-skia. D2 SVGs include proper font references,
  325|       |    /// so no font-family injection is needed (unlike Pikchr).
  326|       |    /// Rasterizes at `D2_SUPERSAMPLE` x the logical display resolution
  327|       |    /// for crisp text and lines. Returns display dimensions in logical pixels
  328|       |    /// and raster dimensions in actual pixels.
  329|      8|    fn rasterize_svg(
  330|      8|        fontdb: &Arc<usvg::fontdb::Database>,
  331|      8|        svg: &str,
  332|      8|        width_bucket: u32,
  333|      8|        scale_bucket: u32,
  334|      8|    ) -> Result<RasterOutput, String> {
  335|       |        // Parse SVG using the shared fontdb with system fonts loaded.
  336|      8|        let opt = usvg::Options {
  337|      8|            fontdb: Arc::clone(fontdb),
  338|      8|            ..Default::default()
  339|      8|        };
  340|      8|        let tree = usvg::Tree::from_data(svg.as_bytes(), &opt)
  341|      8|            .map_err(|e| format!("SVG parse error: {}", e))?;
                                       ^0                                ^0
  342|       |
  343|       |        // Compute dimensions from the parsed SVG tree
  344|      8|        let size = tree.size().to_int_size();
  345|      8|        let (w, h) = (size.width().max(1), size.height().max(1));
  346|       |
  347|       |        // Compute logical scale factor (user zoom, constrained by available width)
  348|      8|        let base_scale = scale_bucket as f32 / 100.0;
  349|      8|        let width_scale = if width_bucket > 0 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (349:30): [True: 8, False: 0]
  ------------------
  350|      8|            width_bucket as f32 / w.max(1) as f32
  351|       |        } else {
  352|      0|            base_scale
  353|       |        };
  354|      8|        let scale = base_scale.min(width_scale).clamp(0.1, 4.0);
  355|       |
  356|       |        // Display dimensions in logical pixels (what render_texture uses)
  357|      8|        let display_w = (w as f32 * scale).round().max(1.0) as u32;
  358|      8|        let display_h = (h as f32 * scale).round().max(1.0) as u32;
  359|       |
  360|       |        // Raster dimensions: supersample for crisp text and thin lines
  361|      8|        let raster_scale = scale * D2_SUPERSAMPLE;
  362|      8|        let mut raster_w = (w as f32 * raster_scale).round().max(1.0) as u32;
  363|      8|        let mut raster_h = (h as f32 * raster_scale).round().max(1.0) as u32;
  364|       |
  365|       |        // Clamp to reasonable maximum
  366|      8|        if raster_w > MAX_RASTER_SIDE || raster_h > MAX_RASTER_SIDE {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (366:12): [True: 0, False: 8]
  |  Branch (366:42): [True: 0, False: 8]
  ------------------
  367|      0|            let clamp_scale = (MAX_RASTER_SIDE as f32 / raster_w as f32)
  368|      0|                .min(MAX_RASTER_SIDE as f32 / raster_h as f32);
  369|      0|            raster_w = (raster_w as f32 * clamp_scale).round().max(1.0) as u32;
  370|      0|            raster_h = (raster_h as f32 * clamp_scale).round().max(1.0) as u32;
  371|      8|        }
  372|       |
  373|       |        // Rasterize: D2 SVGs include background fills, so no separate fill needed.
  374|      8|        let mut pixmap = tiny_skia::Pixmap::new(raster_w, raster_h)
  375|      8|            .ok_or_else(|| "Pixmap allocation failed".to_string())?;
                                         ^0                         ^0          ^0
  376|      8|        let transform = tiny_skia::Transform::from_scale(raster_scale, raster_scale);
  377|      8|        let mut pmut = pixmap.as_mut();
  378|      8|        resvg::render(&tree, transform, &mut pmut);
  379|       |
  380|      8|        Ok(RasterOutput {
  381|      8|            rgba: pixmap.data().to_vec(),
  382|      8|            raster_size: [raster_w as usize, raster_h as usize],
  383|      8|            display_size: [display_w, display_h],
  384|      8|        })
  385|      8|    }
  386|       |
  387|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  388|       |    // Main-thread polling
  389|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  390|       |
  391|       |    /// Drain completed results from the worker thread. Called once per frame
  392|       |    /// from `begin_frame`, NOT from `render_block`. This is critical:
  393|       |    /// `render_block` only runs for visible diagrams, so if diagrams scroll
  394|       |    /// off-screen while jobs are in-flight, results would pile up in the
  395|       |    /// bounded channel and eventually block the worker thread (deadlock).
  396|       |    /// Polling from `begin_frame` ensures results are always drained.
  397|     62|    fn poll_results(&self, ctx: &egui::Context) -> bool {
  398|     62|        let mut any_processed = false;
  399|       |
  400|       |        loop {
  401|     62|            match self.result_rx.try_recv() {
  402|      0|                Ok(result) => {
  403|      0|                    any_processed = true;
  404|       |
  405|       |                    // Remove from pending set
  406|      0|                    self.pending.borrow_mut().remove(&result.code_hash);
  407|       |
  408|       |                    // Always cache SVG (content-addressed, width-independent)
  409|      0|                    if let Some(ref svg) = result.svg {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (409:28): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  410|      0|                        let sk = svg_cache_key(result.code_hash, result.dark_mode);
  411|      0|                        self.svg_cache.borrow_mut().insert(sk, svg.clone());
  412|      0|                    }
  413|       |
  414|       |                    // Always cache errors (prevents infinite retry loops)
  415|      0|                    if let Some(ref err) = result.error {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (415:28): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  416|      0|                        self.errors
  417|      0|                            .borrow_mut()
  418|      0|                            .insert(result.code_hash, err.clone());
  419|      0|                        continue; // No RGBA to upload
  420|      0|                    }
  421|       |
  422|       |                    // Stale result check: skip GPU upload if width bucket changed since enqueue
  423|      0|                    if self.is_stale_result(&result.texture_key, result.code_hash) {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (423:24): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  424|      0|                        continue;
  425|      0|                    }
  426|       |
  427|       |                    // Upload RGBA to GPU texture
  428|      0|                    if let (Some(rgba), Some(raster_size), Some(display_size)) =
  ------------------
  |  Branch (428:28): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  429|      0|                        (result.rgba, result.raster_size, result.display_size)
  430|       |                    {
  431|      0|                        let image = egui::ColorImage::from_rgba_unmultiplied(raster_size, &rgba);
  432|      0|                        let texture = ctx.load_texture(
  433|      0|                            result.texture_key.clone(),
  434|      0|                            image,
  435|       |                            egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR,
  436|       |                        );
  437|      0|                        let entry = D2TextureEntry {
  438|      0|                            texture,
  439|      0|                            display_size,
  440|      0|                        };
  441|      0|                        self.textures
  442|      0|                            .borrow_mut()
  443|      0|                            .insert(result.texture_key.clone(), entry);
  444|      0|                        self.latest_texture_key
  445|      0|                            .borrow_mut()
  446|      0|                            .insert(result.code_hash, result.texture_key.clone());
  447|       |                        // Update debounce state
  448|      0|                        if let Some(width_bucket) =
  ------------------
  |  Branch (448:32): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  449|      0|                            Self::parse_width_from_texture_key(&result.texture_key)
  450|      0|                        {
  451|      0|                            self.debounce.borrow_mut().insert(
  452|      0|                                result.code_hash,
  453|      0|                                DiagramDebounce {
  454|      0|                                    last_rasterized_bucket: width_bucket,
  455|      0|                                    last_seen_bucket: width_bucket,
  456|      0|                                    bucket_changed_at: None,
  457|      0|                                },
  458|      0|                            );
  459|      0|                        }
  460|      0|                    }
  461|       |                }
  462|     62|                Err(crossbeam_channel::TryRecvError::Empty) => break,
  463|       |                Err(crossbeam_channel::TryRecvError::Disconnected) => {
  464|       |                    // Worker thread has died (panic or unexpected exit).
  465|       |                    // Clear all pending entries so render_block can detect the dead
  466|       |                    // worker via try_send(Disconnected) and show an error instead of
  467|       |                    // showing "Rendering..." forever.
  468|      0|                    self.pending.borrow_mut().clear();
  469|      0|                    break;
  470|       |                }
  471|       |            }
  472|       |        }
  473|       |
  474|     62|        if any_processed {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (474:12): [True: 0, False: 62]
  ------------------
  475|      0|            ctx.request_repaint();
  476|     62|        }
  477|     62|        any_processed
  478|     62|    }
  479|       |
  480|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  481|       |    // Public API
  482|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  483|       |
  484|       |    /// Called once at the start of each egui frame. Drains completed results
  485|       |    /// from the worker thread and requests repaint if jobs are still pending.
  486|     62|    pub(crate) fn begin_frame(&self, ctx: &egui::Context) {
  487|       |        // Drain completed results from worker thread. Must happen here (not in
  488|       |        // render_block) because render_block only runs for visible diagrams --
  489|       |        // off-screen results would pile up and block the worker.
  490|     62|        self.poll_results(ctx);
  491|       |
  492|       |        // If jobs are still in-flight, ensure we get another frame to poll again.
  493|     62|        if self.has_pending() {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (493:12): [True: 0, False: 62]
  ------------------
  494|      0|            ctx.request_repaint_after(Duration::from_millis(16));
  495|     62|        }
  496|     62|    }
  497|       |
  498|       |    /// Returns true if any rasterization jobs are in-flight on the worker thread.
  499|     90|    pub(crate) fn has_pending(&self) -> bool {
  500|     90|        !self.pending.borrow().is_empty()
  501|     90|    }
  502|       |
  503|       |    /// Release GPU textures to reduce idle GPU usage (e.g., when minimized).
  504|       |    /// SVG and error caches are retained for fast texture rebuilds on next render.
  505|       |    /// Also clears pending, wanted_bucket, and debounce to allow clean re-enqueue
  506|       |    /// on restore. In-flight results will be detected as stale (wanted_bucket is
  507|       |    /// empty) and discarded in poll_results.
  508|      4|    pub(crate) fn release_gpu_textures(&self) {
  509|      4|        self.textures.borrow_mut().clear();
  510|      4|        self.debounce.borrow_mut().clear();
  511|      4|        self.latest_texture_key.borrow_mut().clear();
  512|      4|        self.wanted_bucket.borrow_mut().clear();
  513|      4|        self.pending.borrow_mut().clear();
  514|      4|    }
  515|       |
  516|       |    /// Main entry point for rendering a D2 code block.
  517|       |    ///
  518|       |    /// Checks caches (error, texture, SVG) before enqueuing. On cache miss,
  519|       |    /// enqueues a job to the worker thread and shows a stale texture or
  520|       |    /// placeholder while waiting.
  521|       |    ///
  522|       |    /// Returns `true` when the block has produced UI output (always true).
  523|      0|    pub(crate) fn render_block(
  524|      0|        &self,
  525|      0|        ui: &mut egui::Ui,
  526|      0|        code: &str,
  527|      0|        ui_scale: f32,
  528|      0|        code_font_size: f32,
  529|      0|    ) -> bool {
  530|      0|        let code_hash = hash_str(code);
  531|      0|        let dark_mode = ui.visuals().dark_mode;
  532|      0|        let svg_key = svg_cache_key(code_hash, dark_mode);
  533|       |
  534|       |        // 1. Check error cache (don't re-render known failures).
  535|       |        //    Errors depend only on syntax, not dark_mode, so keyed by code_hash.
  536|       |        //    Clone the error and drop the RefMut before calling render_error_block,
  537|       |        //    to avoid holding a borrow on self.errors across a &self method call.
  538|      0|        let cached_err = self.errors.borrow_mut().get(&code_hash);
  539|      0|        if let Some(err) = cached_err {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (539:16): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  540|      0|            self.render_error_block(ui, &err, code, code_font_size);
  541|      0|            return true;
  542|      0|        }
  543|       |
  544|       |        // 2. Compute texture cache key (incorporates width bucket + scale + theme)
  545|      0|        let available_width = ui.available_width().max(1.0);
  546|      0|        let width_bucket = bucket_width(available_width);
  547|      0|        let scale_bucket = bucket_scale(ui_scale);
  548|      0|        let texture_key = format!(
  549|       |            "d2:{:016x}:w{}:s{}:dm{}",
  550|      0|            code_hash, width_bucket, scale_bucket, dark_mode as u8
  551|       |        );
  552|       |
  553|       |        // 2b. Update wanted_bucket (for stale discard in poll_results)
  554|      0|        self.wanted_bucket
  555|      0|            .borrow_mut()
  556|      0|            .insert(code_hash, width_bucket);
  557|       |
  558|       |        // 3. Check texture cache.
  559|       |        {
  560|      0|            let mut cache = self.textures.borrow_mut();
  561|      0|            if let Some(entry) = cache.get(&texture_key) {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (561:20): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  562|      0|                Self::render_texture(ui, &entry, available_width);
  563|      0|                return true;
  564|      0|            }
  565|       |        }
  566|       |
  567|       |        // 3b. Debounce gate: suppress rasterization enqueue during resize.
  568|       |        //     Show the last rendered texture scaled to fit. Only enqueue
  569|       |        //     once the width stabilizes for RESIZE_DEBOUNCE_MS.
  570|      0|        let db_entry = self.debounce.borrow().get(&code_hash).map(|d| {
  571|      0|            (
  572|      0|                d.last_rasterized_bucket,
  573|      0|                d.last_seen_bucket,
  574|      0|                d.bucket_changed_at,
  575|      0|            )
  576|      0|        });
  577|      0|        if let Some((last_rasterized, last_seen, _changed_at)) = db_entry {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (577:16): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  578|      0|            if width_bucket != last_rasterized {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (578:16): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  579|       |                // Width changed since last rasterize -- apply trailing-edge debounce
  580|      0|                if width_bucket != last_seen {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (580:20): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  581|       |                    // New bucket since last frame -- reset timer
  582|      0|                    if let Some(d) = self.debounce.borrow_mut().get_mut(&code_hash) {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (582:28): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  583|      0|                        d.last_seen_bucket = width_bucket;
  584|      0|                        d.bucket_changed_at = Some(Instant::now());
  585|      0|                    }
  586|      0|                }
  587|      0|                let changed_at = self
  588|      0|                    .debounce
  589|      0|                    .borrow()
  590|      0|                    .get(&code_hash)
  591|      0|                    .and_then(|d| d.bucket_changed_at)
  592|      0|                    .unwrap_or_else(Instant::now);
  593|      0|                let elapsed = changed_at.elapsed();
  594|      0|                let debounce_dur = Duration::from_millis(RESIZE_DEBOUNCE_MS);
  595|      0|                if elapsed < debounce_dur {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (595:20): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  596|       |                    // Still in cooldown -- show stale texture scaled to fit
  597|      0|                    if let Some(stale_key) =
  ------------------
  |  Branch (597:28): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  598|      0|                        self.latest_texture_key.borrow().get(&code_hash).cloned()
  599|       |                    {
  600|      0|                        if let Some(entry) = self.textures.borrow_mut().get(&stale_key) {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (600:32): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  601|      0|                            Self::render_texture(ui, &entry, available_width);
  602|      0|                            ui.ctx().request_repaint_after(debounce_dur - elapsed);
  603|      0|                            return true;
  604|      0|                        }
  605|      0|                    }
  606|       |                    // No stale texture available (first render) -- fall through to enqueue
  607|      0|                }
  608|       |                // Debounce expired -- fall through to enqueue
  609|      0|            }
  610|       |            // width == last_rasterized: texture was evicted -- fall through to enqueue
  611|      0|        }
  612|       |        // No debounce entry: first render -- fall through to enqueue
  613|       |
  614|       |        // 4. Check if already pending for this diagram (by code_hash, not texture_key,
  615|       |        //    to prevent redundant enqueues when the width changes mid-flight)
  616|      0|        if self.pending.borrow().contains(&code_hash) {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (616:12): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  617|      0|            return self.show_stale_or_placeholder(ui, code_hash, available_width);
  618|      0|        }
  619|       |
  620|       |        // 5. Enqueue job
  621|      0|        let cached_svg = self.svg_cache.borrow_mut().get(&svg_key);
  622|      0|        let job = RasterJob {
  623|      0|            code_hash,
  624|      0|            texture_key: texture_key.clone(),
  625|      0|            code: if cached_svg.is_none() {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (625:22): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  626|      0|                Some(code.to_string())
  627|       |            } else {
  628|      0|                None
  629|       |            },
  630|      0|            svg: cached_svg,
  631|      0|            dark_mode,
  632|      0|            width_bucket,
  633|      0|            scale_bucket,
  634|       |        };
  635|       |
  636|      0|        match self.job_tx.as_ref().unwrap().try_send(job) {
  637|      0|            Ok(()) => {
  638|      0|                self.pending.borrow_mut().insert(code_hash);
  639|      0|            }
  640|      0|            Err(TrySendError::Full(_)) => {
  641|      0|                // Queue full -- will retry next frame
  642|      0|            }
  643|       |            Err(TrySendError::Disconnected(_)) => {
  644|       |                // Worker died -- show error
  645|      0|                self.errors
  646|      0|                    .borrow_mut()
  647|      0|                    .insert(code_hash, "Raster worker unavailable".to_string());
  648|      0|                self.render_error_block(ui, "Raster worker unavailable", code, code_font_size);
  649|      0|                return true;
  650|       |            }
  651|       |        }
  652|       |
  653|       |        // 6. Show stale texture or placeholder while waiting
  654|      0|        self.show_stale_or_placeholder(ui, code_hash, available_width)
  655|      0|    }
  656|       |
  657|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  658|       |    // Helpers
  659|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  660|       |
  661|       |    /// Show a stale texture (from a previous width) scaled to fit, or a
  662|       |    /// minimal-height placeholder if no stale texture is available.
  663|       |    /// Returns true (block always produces UI output).
  664|      0|    fn show_stale_or_placeholder(
  665|      0|        &self,
  666|      0|        ui: &mut egui::Ui,
  667|      0|        code_hash: u64,
  668|      0|        available_width: f32,
  669|      0|    ) -> bool {
  670|       |        // Try stale texture first (gives a good visual approximation)
  671|      0|        if let Some(stale_key) = self.latest_texture_key.borrow().get(&code_hash).cloned() {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (671:16): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  672|      0|            if let Some(entry) = self.textures.borrow_mut().get(&stale_key) {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (672:20): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  673|      0|                Self::render_texture(ui, &entry, available_width);
  674|      0|                return true;
  675|      0|            }
  676|      0|        }
  677|       |        // No stale texture -- show minimal placeholder (first render).
  678|       |        // Use a small fixed height to reduce layout shift when the real texture arrives.
  679|      0|        let placeholder_height = 60.0;
  680|      0|        let (rect, _) = ui.allocate_exact_size(
  681|      0|            egui::vec2(available_width, placeholder_height),
  682|      0|            egui::Sense::hover(),
  683|      0|        );
  684|      0|        ui.painter().text(
  685|      0|            rect.center(),
  686|       |            egui::Align2::CENTER_CENTER,
  687|       |            "Rendering...",
  688|      0|            egui::FontId::proportional(12.0),
  689|      0|            ui.visuals().weak_text_color(),
  690|       |        );
  691|      0|        true
  692|      0|    }
  693|       |
  694|       |    /// Parse the width bucket value from a texture cache key.
  695|       |    /// Key format: `d2:<hex>:w<width>:s<scale>:dm<0|1>`
  696|     14|    fn parse_width_from_texture_key(key: &str) -> Option<u32> {
  697|     14|        let w_start = key.find(":w")? + 2;
                          ^12                     ^2
  698|     12|        let w_end = key[w_start..].find(':')? + w_start;
                                                          ^0
  699|     12|        key[w_start..w_end].parse().ok()
  700|     14|    }
  701|       |
  702|       |    /// Check whether a result's width bucket no longer matches what the main
  703|       |    /// thread currently wants for this diagram. Stale results have their SVG
  704|       |    /// cached but skip the expensive GPU upload.
  705|      6|    fn is_stale_result(&self, texture_key: &str, code_hash: u64) -> bool {
  706|      6|        let result_bucket = Self::parse_width_from_texture_key(texture_key);
  707|      6|        let wanted = self.wanted_bucket.borrow().get(&code_hash).copied();
  708|      6|        match (result_bucket, wanted) {
  709|      4|            (Some(rb), Some(wb)) => rb != wb, // Width mismatch -> stale
  710|      2|            (Some(_), None) => true,          // No wanted bucket (e.g. after
  711|       |            //                                   release_gpu_textures) -> stale
  712|      0|            _ => false, // Can't parse -> assume fresh
  713|       |        }
  714|      6|    }
  715|       |
  716|       |    /// Render D2 source code to an SVG string.
  717|       |    ///
  718|       |    /// Static associated function (no `&self`) -- pure input/output.
  719|       |    /// Calls into the mdmdview-d2 crate pipeline.
  720|     18|    fn render_d2_to_svg(code: &str, dark_mode: bool) -> Result<String, String> {
  721|     18|        let options = mdmdview_d2::RenderOptions {
  722|     18|            dark_mode,
  723|     18|            ..Default::default()
  724|     18|        };
  725|       |
  726|     18|        mdmdview_d2::render_d2_to_svg(code, &options)
  727|     18|            .map(|result| result.svg)
  728|     18|            .map_err(|e| e.to_string())
                                       ^4^4
  729|     18|    }
  730|       |
  731|       |    /// Display a cached texture in the UI, scaling down if it exceeds available width.
  732|      0|    fn render_texture(ui: &mut egui::Ui, entry: &D2TextureEntry, available_width: f32) {
  733|      0|        let (tw, th) = (entry.display_size[0] as f32, entry.display_size[1] as f32);
  734|      0|        let scale = if tw > available_width {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (734:24): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  735|      0|            (available_width / tw).clamp(0.01, 4.0)
  736|       |        } else {
  737|      0|            1.0
  738|       |        };
  739|      0|        let size = egui::vec2((tw * scale).round(), (th * scale).round());
  740|      0|        ui.add(egui::Image::new(&entry.texture).fit_to_exact_size(size));
  741|      0|    }
  742|       |
  743|       |    /// Display a D2 error block with the error message and original source code.
  744|      0|    fn render_error_block(&self, ui: &mut egui::Ui, error: &str, code: &str, code_font_size: f32) {
  745|      0|        let tc = ThemeColors::current(ui.visuals().dark_mode);
  746|      0|        egui::Frame::none()
  747|      0|            .fill(tc.box_bg)
  748|      0|            .stroke(egui::Stroke::new(1.0, egui::Color32::from_rgb(200, 80, 80)))
  749|      0|            .inner_margin(8.0)
  750|      0|            .show(ui, |ui| {
  751|      0|                ui.label(
  752|      0|                    egui::RichText::new("D2 rendering error:")
  753|      0|                        .color(egui::Color32::from_rgb(200, 80, 80))
  754|      0|                        .size(code_font_size),
  755|       |                );
  756|      0|                ui.label(
  757|      0|                    egui::RichText::new(error)
  758|      0|                        .color(egui::Color32::LIGHT_GRAY)
  759|      0|                        .size(code_font_size)
  760|      0|                        .family(egui::FontFamily::Monospace),
  761|       |                );
  762|      0|                ui.add_space(4.0);
  763|      0|                ui.label(
  764|      0|                    egui::RichText::new(code)
  765|      0|                        .size(code_font_size)
  766|      0|                        .color(tc.code_fallback_text)
  767|      0|                        .family(egui::FontFamily::Monospace),
  768|       |                );
  769|      0|            });
  770|      0|    }
  771|       |}
  772|       |
  773|       |impl Drop for D2Renderer {
  774|    880|    fn drop(&mut self) {
  775|       |        // Close job channel -- worker will see Disconnected on recv() and exit.
  776|       |        // The worker uses try_send for results (never blocks), so it will
  777|       |        // drain remaining jobs and exit promptly once job_rx disconnects.
  778|    880|        self.job_tx.take();
  779|       |        // Join worker thread for clean shutdown
  780|    880|        if let Some(handle) = self.worker_handle.take() {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (780:16): [True: 880, False: 0]
  ------------------
  781|    880|            let _ = handle.join();
  782|    880|        }
                      ^0
  783|    880|    }
  784|       |}

  914|       |    /// Helper: create a fontdb with system fonts loaded (shared across worker tests).
  915|     10|    fn make_fontdb() -> Arc<usvg::fontdb::Database> {
  916|     10|        let mut db = usvg::fontdb::Database::new();
  917|     10|        db.load_system_fonts();
  918|     10|        Arc::new(db)
  919|     10|    }
  920|       |
  921|       |    #[test]
  922|      2|    fn test_worker_processes_job() {
  923|      2|        let fontdb = make_fontdb();
  924|      2|        let (job_tx, job_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded::<RasterJob>(4);
  925|      2|        let (result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded::<RasterResult>(4);
  926|       |
  927|      2|        let code = "a -> b";
  928|      2|        let code_hash = hash_str(code);
  929|      2|        let texture_key = format!("d2:{:016x}:w640:s100:dm0", code_hash);
  930|      2|        let job = RasterJob {
  931|      2|            code_hash,
  932|      2|            texture_key: texture_key.clone(),
  933|      2|            code: Some(code.to_string()),
  934|      2|            svg: None,
  935|      2|            dark_mode: false,
  936|      2|            width_bucket: 640,
  937|      2|            scale_bucket: 100,
  938|      2|        };
  939|       |
  940|      2|        job_tx.send(job).unwrap();
  941|       |        // Drop sender so worker_loop exits after processing the one job.
  942|      2|        drop(job_tx);
  943|       |
  944|      2|        D2Renderer::worker_loop(fontdb, job_rx, result_tx);
  945|       |
  946|      2|        let result = result_rx.recv_timeout(Duration::from_secs(10)).unwrap();
  947|      2|        assert!(result.rgba.is_some(), "Worker should produce RGBA data");
  948|      2|        assert!(
  949|      2|            result.raster_size.is_some(),
  950|       |            "Worker should produce raster_size"
  951|       |        );
  952|      2|        assert!(
  953|      2|            result.display_size.is_some(),
  954|       |            "Worker should produce display_size"
  955|       |        );
  956|      2|        assert!(result.error.is_none(), "Worker should not produce an error");
  957|      2|        assert!(result.svg.is_some(), "Worker should produce SVG on success");
  958|      2|    }
  959|       |

 1033|       |    #[test]
 1034|      2|    fn test_is_stale_result_width_mismatch() {
 1035|      2|        let renderer = D2Renderer::new();
 1036|      2|        let code_hash = 12345u64;
 1037|       |

 1040|       |

 1044|      2|            renderer.is_stale_result(&stale_key, code_hash),
 1045|       |            "Width 1024 vs wanted 640 should be stale"
 1046|       |        );
 1047|       |
 1048|       |        // Texture key with w640 should be fresh (match)
 1049|      2|        let fresh_key = format!("d2:{:016x}:w640:s100:dm0", code_hash);
 1050|      2|        assert!(
 1051|      2|            !renderer.is_stale_result(&fresh_key, code_hash),
 1052|       |            "Width 640 vs wanted 640 should be fresh"
 1053|       |        );
 1054|      2|    }

 1129|       |    #[test]
 1130|      2|    fn test_queue_full_does_not_block() {
 1131|      2|        let (tx, _rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded::<RasterJob>(2);
 1132|       |
 1133|       |        // Fill the channel with 2 dummy jobs
 1134|      4|        for i in 0..2 {
                               ^2
 1135|      4|            tx.send(RasterJob {
 1136|      4|                code_hash: i,
 1137|      4|                texture_key: format!("d2:dummy:w640:s100:dm0:{}", i),
 1138|      4|                code: Some("a -> b".to_string()),
 1139|      4|                svg: None,
 1140|      4|                dark_mode: false,
 1141|      4|                width_bucket: 640,
 1142|      4|                scale_bucket: 100,
 1143|      4|            })

 1203|      2|    fn test_worker_try_send_full_does_not_block() {
 1204|      2|        let fontdb = make_fontdb();
 1205|      2|        let (job_tx, job_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded::<RasterJob>(4);
 1206|       |        // Result channel with capacity 1

 1208|       |

 1210|      2|        result_tx
 1211|      2|            .send(RasterResult {
 1212|      2|                code_hash: 0,

 1219|      2|                error: None,
 1220|      2|            })
 1221|      2|            .unwrap();
 1222|       |

 1233|      2|                width_bucket: 640,

 1235|      2|            })

 1238|      2|        drop(job_tx);

 1241|      2|        let handle = std::thread::spawn(move || {
 1242|      2|            D2Renderer::worker_loop(fontdb, job_rx, result_tx);
 1243|      2|        });
 1244|       |
 1245|       |        // Wait up to 10 seconds for the worker to finish (D2 can be slower than pikchr)
 1246|      2|        let start = Instant::now();
 1247|       |        loop {
 1248|      7|            if handle.is_finished() {
  ------------------

  ------------------
  |  Branch (1251:16): [True: 0, False: 5]
  ------------------

 1255|       |        }

 1261|       |            dummy.code_hash, 0,
 1262|       |            "Original dummy result should still be in the channel"

    1|       |// emoji_assets.rs -- Procedural vector fallback icons for a small subset of emoji.
    2|       |//
    3|       |// When a Twemoji PNG is unavailable (see emoji_catalog.rs), this module can
    4|       |// generate simple rasterised shapes for a handful of commonly used emoji.
    5|       |// The fallback set is intentionally small; most emoji are served as embedded
    6|       |// PNGs from the catalog.
    7|       |//
    8|       |// Public API:
    9|       |//   make_image(emoji, size) -> Option<ColorImage>  -- render a fallback icon
   10|       |//   has_fallback(emoji)     -> bool                 -- cheap membership check
   11|       |
   12|       |use egui::Color32 as C;
   13|       |
   14|       |/// Emoji codepoints that have procedural vector fallbacks.
   15|       |/// Both `make_image` and `has_fallback` derive from this list, keeping them
   16|       |/// automatically in sync.
   17|       |const FALLBACK_EMOJI: &[&str] = &[
   18|       |    "\u{2705}",  // white check mark
   19|       |    "\u{1f389}", // tada / party popper
   20|       |    "\u{1f680}", // rocket
   21|       |    "\u{2764}",  // red heart
   22|       |    "\u{1f496}", // sparkling heart (shares heart renderer)
   23|       |    "\u{2b50}",  // star
   24|       |    "\u{1f525}", // fire
   25|       |];
   26|       |
   27|       |/// Draw a simple vector fallback icon for a supported emoji.
   28|       |///
   29|       |/// Returns `None` for emoji not in `FALLBACK_EMOJI`.
   30|     25|pub fn make_image(emoji: &str, size: usize) -> Option<egui::ColorImage> {
   31|     25|    let mut img = egui::ColorImage::new([size, size], C::TRANSPARENT);
   32|     25|    match emoji {
   33|     25|        "\u{2705}" => {
   34|      2|            draw_circle(&mut img, size, C::from_rgb(34, 139, 34));
   35|      2|            draw_check(&mut img, size, C::WHITE);
   36|      2|        }
   37|     23|        "\u{1f389}" => {
   38|      2|            draw_circle(&mut img, size, C::from_rgb(255, 215, 0));
   39|      2|            confetti(&mut img, size);
   40|      2|        }
   41|     21|        "\u{1f680}" => {
   42|      3|            draw_circle(&mut img, size, C::from_rgb(60, 60, 170));
   43|      3|            rocket(
   44|      3|                &mut img,
   45|      3|                size,
   46|      3|                C::from_rgb(230, 230, 230),
   47|      3|                C::from_rgb(255, 110, 60),
   48|      3|            );
   49|      3|        }
   50|     18|        "\u{2764}" | "\u{1f496}" => {
                                   ^16
   51|      4|            heart(&mut img, size, C::from_rgb(220, 20, 60));
   52|      4|        }
   53|     14|        "\u{2b50}" => {
   54|      2|            star(&mut img, size, C::from_rgb(255, 215, 0));
   55|      2|        }
   56|     12|        "\u{1f525}" => {
   57|      2|            flame(&mut img, size);
   58|      2|        }
   59|     10|        _ => return None,
   60|       |    }
   61|     15|    Some(img)
   62|     25|}
   63|       |
   64|       |/// Returns true if the emoji has a procedural vector fallback in `make_image`.
   65|       |///

   67|     22|pub fn has_fallback(emoji: &str) -> bool {
   68|     22|    FALLBACK_EMOJI.contains(&emoji)
   69|     22|}

   73|      7|    let cy = cx;

C:\language\mdmdview\src\emoji_catalog.rs:
    1|       |// emoji_catalog.rs -- Embedded Twemoji PNG lookup and shortcode-to-emoji mapping.
    2|       |//
    3|       |// The catalog embeds 80 Twemoji PNG files at compile time via `include_bytes!`.
    4|       |// Two lookup paths are provided:
    5|       |//   image_bytes_for(emoji)       -> Option<&[u8]>   -- codepoint to PNG bytes
    6|       |//   emoji_for_shortcode(code)    -> Option<&str>     -- shortcode to codepoint
    7|       |//   shortcode_map()              -> &HashMap         -- full shortcode table
    8|       |//
    9|       |// Adding a new emoji requires:
   10|       |//   1. Place the PNG in assets/emoji/<hex>.png
   11|       |//   2. Add the codepoint to the `emoji_png!` invocation in `image_bytes_for`
   12|       |//   3. Add the shortcode mapping in `shortcode_map`
   13|       |//   4. Add the codepoint to `ALL_EMOJI_CODEPOINTS` in tests
   14|       |
   15|       |use std::collections::HashMap;
   16|       |
   17|       |/// Generates a match expression that maps Unicode literals to embedded PNG bytes.
   18|       |/// Eliminates the repetitive `Some(include_bytes!(concat!(...)))` boilerplate.
   19|       |macro_rules! emoji_png {
   20|       |    ( $input:expr; $( $unicode:literal => $file:literal ),+ $(,)? ) => {
   21|       |        match $input {
   22|       |            $( $unicode => Some(include_bytes!(concat!(env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR"), "/assets/emoji/", $file))), )+
   23|       |            _ => None,
   24|       |        }
   25|       |    };
   26|       |}
   27|       |
   28|       |/// Returns the embedded PNG bytes for a known emoji character.
   29|       |///
   30|       |/// Uses pattern matching only -- cannot panic regardless of input.
   31|       |/// Unknown emoji characters return `None`.
   32|  12.0k|pub fn image_bytes_for(emoji: &str) -> Option<&'static [u8]> {
   33|  12.0k|    emoji_png! { emoji;
   34|       |        // --- Faces (4) ---
   35|       |        "\u{1f642}" => "1f642.png",
   36|       |        "\u{1f600}" => "1f600.png",
   37|       |        "\u{1f609}" => "1f609.png",

  237|       |/// Looks up an emoji by its shortcode (e.g., ":rocket:").
  238|       |///
  239|       |/// Returns `Some(emoji_str)` if the shortcode is known, `None` otherwise.
  240|       |#[inline]
  241|     18|pub fn emoji_for_shortcode(shortcode: &str) -> Option<&'static str> {
  242|     18|    shortcode_map().get(shortcode).copied()
  243|     18|}

  344|       |    #[test]
  345|       |    fn test_image_bytes_for_all_known_emojis() {
  346|       |        for emoji in ALL_EMOJI_CODEPOINTS {
  347|       |            let bytes = image_bytes_for(emoji)
  348|       |                .unwrap_or_else(|| panic!("missing embedded bytes for {emoji:?}"));
  349|       |            assert!(!bytes.is_empty(), "empty bytes for {emoji:?}");
  350|       |        }
  351|       |    }
  352|       |
  353|       |    #[test]
  354|       |    fn test_image_bytes_for_unknown_emoji() {
  355|       |        assert!(image_bytes_for("\u{1f47d}").is_none());
  356|       |    }
  357|       |
  358|       |    #[test]
  359|       |    fn test_shortcode_map_contains_expected_entries() {
  360|       |        let map = shortcode_map();
  361|       |        // Spot-check representative entries from each category
  362|       |        assert_eq!(map.get(":tada:"), Some(&"\u{1f389}"));
  363|       |        assert_eq!(map.get(":white_check_mark:"), Some(&"\u{2705}"));
  364|       |        assert_eq!(map.get(":rocket:"), Some(&"\u{1f680}"));
  365|       |        assert_eq!(map.get(":fire:"), Some(&"\u{1f525}"));
  366|       |        assert_eq!(map.get(":wrench:"), Some(&"\u{1f527}"));
  367|       |        assert_eq!(map.get(":warning:"), Some(&"\u{26a0}"));
  368|       |        assert_eq!(map.get(":bug:"), Some(&"\u{1f41b}"));
  369|       |        assert_eq!(map.get(":heart:"), Some(&"\u{2764}"));
  370|       |        assert_eq!(map.get(":eyes:"), Some(&"\u{1f440}"));
  371|       |        assert_eq!(map.get(":trophy:"), Some(&"\u{1f3c6}"));
  372|       |        // Aliases
  373|       |        assert_eq!(map.get(":+1:"), Some(&"\u{1f44d}"));
  374|       |        assert_eq!(map.get(":-1:"), Some(&"\u{1f44e}"));
  375|       |        assert_eq!(map.get(":target:"), Some(&"\u{1f3af}"));
  376|       |        assert_eq!(map.get(":open_book:"), Some(&"\u{1f4d6}"));
  377|       |        // Unknown
  378|       |        assert!(map.get(":does_not_exist:").is_none());
  379|       |    }
  380|       |
  381|       |    #[test]
  382|       |    fn test_shortcode_count() {
  383|       |        let map = shortcode_map();
  384|       |        assert_eq!(
  385|       |            map.len(),
  386|       |            85,
  387|       |            "expected exactly 85 shortcodes (80 primary + 5 aliases), got {}",
  388|       |            map.len()
  389|       |        );

  393|       |    fn test_all_shortcodes_map_to_catalogued_emojis() {
  394|       |        let map = shortcode_map();
  395|       |        for (shortcode, emoji) in map.iter() {
  396|       |            assert!(
  397|       |                image_bytes_for(emoji).is_some(),
  398|       |                "shortcode {shortcode} maps to {emoji:?} which has no embedded PNG"
  399|       |            );
  400|       |        }
  401|       |    }
  402|       |
  403|       |    #[test]
  404|       |    fn test_emoji_for_shortcode() {
  405|       |        // Known shortcodes return Some
  406|       |        assert_eq!(emoji_for_shortcode(":rocket:"), Some("\u{1f680}"));
  407|       |        assert_eq!(emoji_for_shortcode(":tada:"), Some("\u{1f389}"));
  408|       |        assert_eq!(emoji_for_shortcode(":fire:"), Some("\u{1f525}"));
  409|       |        assert_eq!(emoji_for_shortcode(":bug:"), Some("\u{1f41b}"));
  410|       |        assert_eq!(emoji_for_shortcode(":zap:"), Some("\u{26a1}"));
  411|       |
  412|       |        // Unknown shortcodes return None
  413|       |        assert_eq!(emoji_for_shortcode(":unknown:"), None);
  414|       |        assert_eq!(emoji_for_shortcode(""), None);
  415|       |        assert_eq!(emoji_for_shortcode("no_colons"), None);
  416|       |        assert_eq!(emoji_for_shortcode(":partial"), None);
  417|       |    }

C:\language\mdmdview\src\image_decode.rs:
    1|       |// Image decoding module for raster (PNG, JPEG, etc.) and SVG images.
    2|       |//
    3|       |// Provides functions to decode image bytes into egui `ColorImage` values
    4|       |// with optional background blending. SVG images are rendered via usvg/resvg
    5|       |// and automatically scaled to fit within MAX_IMAGE_SIDE limits. Raster images
    6|       |// are validated against size and pixel count limits before decoding.
    7|       |//
    8|       |// The primary entry point is `bytes_to_color_image_guess`, which tries raster
    9|       |// decoding first and falls back to SVG.
   10|       |
   11|       |use egui::ColorImage;
   12|       |#[cfg(test)]
   13|       |use std::cell::RefCell;
   14|       |use std::io::Cursor;
   15|       |
   16|       |#[cfg(test)]
   17|       |thread_local! {
   18|       |    static FORCED_ZERO_SVG_DIMENSIONS: RefCell<bool> = const { RefCell::new(false) };
   19|       |    static FORCED_RASTER_DIMENSIONS: RefCell<Option<(u32, u32)>> = const { RefCell::new(None) };
   20|       |    static FORCED_SVG_DIMENSIONS: RefCell<Option<(u32, u32)>> = const { RefCell::new(None) };
   21|       |    static FORCED_PIXMAP_ALLOC_FAIL: RefCell<bool> = const { RefCell::new(false) };
   22|       |    static FORCED_GUESS_FORMAT_ERROR: RefCell<bool> = const { RefCell::new(false) };
   23|       |}
   24|       |
   25|       |#[cfg(test)]

   96|     56|pub(crate) fn raster_bytes_to_color_image_with_bg(
   97|     56|    bytes: &[u8],
   98|     56|    bg: Option<[u8; 4]>,
   99|     56|) -> Option<(ColorImage, u32, u32)> {
  100|     56|    if bytes.len() > MAX_IMAGE_BYTES {

  102|     54|    }

                               ^38^38
  ------------------
  |  Branch (103:12): [True: 38, False: 16]

  ------------------
  |  Branch (104:12): [True: 2, False: 36]

  106|     36|        }
  107|     16|    }

                      ^36                                      ^16

  111|       |    #[cfg(test)]

                                            ^2        ^2
  ------------------
  |  Branch (112:25): [True: 2, False: 34]
  ------------------
  113|      2|        (forced_w, forced_h)
  114|       |    } else {
  115|     34|        (w, h)
  116|       |    };
  117|     36|    if raster_exceeds_limits(w, h) {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (117:8): [True: 2, False: 34]
  ------------------
  118|      2|        return None;
  119|     34|    }
  120|     34|    let mut ci = ColorImage::from_rgba_unmultiplied([w as usize, h as usize], &rgba);
  121|     34|    if let Some([br, bgc, bb, ba]) = bg {
                               ^2  ^2   ^2  ^2

  124|      2|            let inv = 1.0 - a;
  125|      2|            p[0] = (a * p[0] as f32 + inv * br as f32) as u8;
  126|      2|            p[1] = (a * p[1] as f32 + inv * bgc as f32) as u8;
  127|      2|            p[2] = (a * p[2] as f32 + inv * bb as f32) as u8;
  128|      2|            p[3] = ba;
  129|      2|        }
  130|     32|    }

  134|     30|pub(crate) fn svg_bytes_to_color_image_with_bg(
  135|     30|    bytes: &[u8],
  136|     30|    bg: Option<[u8; 4]>,
  137|     30|) -> Option<(ColorImage, u32, u32)> {
  138|     30|    let mut opt = usvg::Options::default();
  139|     30|    let mut db = usvg::fontdb::Database::new();
  140|     30|    db.load_system_fonts();
  141|     30|    opt.fontdb = std::sync::Arc::new(db);
  142|     30|    let tree = usvg::Tree::from_data(bytes, &opt).ok()?;
                      ^18                                           ^12
  143|     18|    let sz = tree.size();
  144|     18|    let pix = sz.to_int_size();
  145|     18|    let (mut w, mut h) = (pix.width(), pix.height());
  146|       |    #[cfg(test)]
  147|     18|    if take_forced_zero_svg_dimensions() {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (147:8): [True: 2, False: 16]
  ------------------
  148|      2|        w = 0;
  149|      2|        h = 0;
  150|     16|    }

  152|     18|    if let Some((forced_w, forced_h)) = take_forced_svg_dimensions() {
                               ^2        ^2
  ------------------
  |  Branch (152:12): [True: 2, False: 16]
  ------------------
  153|      2|        w = forced_w;
  154|      2|        h = forced_h;
  155|     16|    }
  156|     18|    if w == 0 || h == 0 {
                               ^16
  ------------------
  |  Branch (156:8): [True: 2, False: 16]
  |  Branch (156:18): [True: 2, False: 14]

  157|      4|        w = 256;
  158|      4|        h = 256;
  159|     14|    }

  163|      6|        w = (w as f32 * scale) as u32;
  164|      6|        h = (h as f32 * scale) as u32;
  165|     12|    }

                      ^16                                   ^2

  169|      2|        pixmap.fill(color);
  170|     14|    }

  173|     16|    let data = pixmap.data();
  174|     16|    let img = ColorImage::from_rgba_unmultiplied([w as usize, h as usize], data);
  175|     16|    Some((img, w, h))

  272|       |    fn encode_png(rgba: &RgbaImage) -> Vec<u8> {
  273|       |        let mut bytes = Vec::new();
  274|       |        let mut cursor = Cursor::new(&mut bytes);
  275|       |        image::DynamicImage::ImageRgba8(rgba.clone())
  276|       |            .write_to(&mut cursor, ImageOutputFormat::Png)
  277|       |            .expect("encode png");
  278|       |        bytes
  279|       |    }
  280|       |
  281|       |    #[test]
  282|       |    fn test_raster_dimensions_invalid_bytes_returns_none() {
  283|       |        assert!(raster_dimensions(&[]).is_none());
  284|       |    }
  285|       |
  286|       |    #[test]
  287|       |    fn test_raster_dimensions_valid_png_returns_some() {
  288|       |        let mut rgba = RgbaImage::new(2, 1);
  289|       |        rgba.put_pixel(0, 0, image::Rgba([0, 0, 0, 255]));
  290|       |        rgba.put_pixel(1, 0, image::Rgba([255, 255, 255, 255]));
  291|       |        let bytes = encode_png(&rgba);
  292|       |        assert_eq!(raster_dimensions(&bytes), Some((2, 1)));
  293|       |    }
  294|       |

  299|       |        rgba.put_pixel(0, 0, image::Rgba([0, 0, 0, 255]));

  305|       |    fn test_bytes_to_color_image_guess_png_blends_background() {
  306|       |        let mut rgba = RgbaImage::new(1, 1);
  307|       |        rgba.put_pixel(0, 0, image::Rgba([255, 0, 0, 255]));
  308|       |        let bytes = encode_png(&rgba);
  309|       |
  310|       |        let bg = [0, 0, 255, 200];
  311|       |        let (img, w, h) = bytes_to_color_image_guess(&bytes, Some(bg)).expect("png decode");
  312|       |        assert_eq!((w, h), (1, 1));
  313|       |
  314|       |        let px = img.pixels[0];
  315|       |        assert_eq!(px[0], 255);
  316|       |        assert_eq!(px[1], 0);
  317|       |        assert_eq!(px[2], 0);
  318|       |        assert_eq!(px[3], bg[3]);
  319|       |    }

   26|       |// Automatically lower the test binary's process priority on Windows.
   27|       |// The full test suite (~986 tests, ~8 minutes) saturates all CPU cores,
   28|       |// making remote desktop sessions unresponsive. Setting BELOW_NORMAL priority
   29|       |// lets tests use idle CPU without starving interactive applications.
   30|       |// This runs via the C runtime .CRT$XCU initializer, before main().
   31|       |#[cfg(all(test, windows))]
   32|       |mod test_priority {
   33|       |    extern "system" {
   34|       |        fn GetCurrentProcess() -> isize;
   35|       |        fn SetPriorityClass(hProcess: isize, dwPriorityClass: u32) -> i32;
   36|       |        fn GetPriorityClass(hProcess: isize) -> u32;
   37|       |    }
   38|       |    const BELOW_NORMAL_PRIORITY_CLASS: u32 = 0x00004000;
   39|       |
   40|       |    #[used]
   41|       |    #[link_section = ".CRT$XCU"]
   42|       |    static SET_BELOW_NORMAL: unsafe extern "C" fn() = {
   43|      2|        unsafe extern "C" fn init() {
   44|      2|            SetPriorityClass(GetCurrentProcess(), BELOW_NORMAL_PRIORITY_CLASS);
   45|      2|        }
   46|       |        init
   47|       |    };
   48|       |
   49|       |    #[test]
   50|      2|    fn test_process_priority_is_below_normal() {
   51|      2|        let priority = unsafe { GetPriorityClass(GetCurrentProcess()) };
   52|      2|        assert_eq!(
   53|       |            priority, BELOW_NORMAL_PRIORITY_CLASS,
   54|       |            "Test process should be running at BELOW_NORMAL priority, got 0x{:08X}",
   55|       |            priority
   56|       |        );
   57|      2|    }
   58|       |}
   59|       |
   60|       |pub use app::{MarkdownViewerApp, APP_TITLE_PREFIX};
   61|       |pub use markdown_renderer::{MarkdownElement, MarkdownRenderer};
