  139|     38|    value
  140|     38|        .parse::<T>()
  141|     38|        .map_err(|_| format!("Invalid {flag} value: {value}"))
                                   ^18
  142|     38|}
  143|       |
  144|       |#[cfg(not(test))]
  145|       |fn load_fonts_from_dir(ctx: &egui::Context, dir: &Path) -> Result<(), String> {
  146|       |    let mut font_paths: Vec<PathBuf> = std::fs::read_dir(dir)
  147|       |        .map_err(|e| format!("Failed to read font dir {dir:?}: {e}"))?
  148|       |        .filter_map(|entry| entry.ok().map(|e| e.path()))
  149|       |        .filter(|path| {
  150|       |            path.extension()
  151|       |                .and_then(|ext| ext.to_str())
  152|       |                .map(|ext| matches!(ext.to_ascii_lowercase().as_str(), "ttf" | "otf"))
  153|       |                .unwrap_or(false)
  154|       |        })
  155|       |        .collect();
  156|       |
  157|       |    if font_paths.is_empty() {
  158|       |        return Err(format!("No font files found in {dir:?}"));
  159|       |    }
  160|       |
  161|       |    font_paths.sort_by(|a, b| a.to_string_lossy().cmp(&b.to_string_lossy()));
  162|       |
  163|       |    let mut fonts = egui::FontDefinitions::default();
  164|       |    let mut prop_names = Vec::new();
  165|       |    let mut mono_names = Vec::new();
  166|       |    let mut used_names = std::collections::HashSet::new();
  167|       |
  168|       |    for path in font_paths {
  169|       |        let bytes =
  170|       |            std::fs::read(&path).map_err(|e| format!("Failed to read font {path:?}: {e}"))?;
  171|       |        let name = path
  172|       |            .file_stem()
  173|       |            .and_then(|stem| stem.to_str())
  174|       |            .map(|stem| stem.to_string())
  175|       |            .unwrap_or_else(|| format!("font-{}", prop_names.len() + mono_names.len()));
  176|       |        let base = format!("test-{name}");
  177|       |        let mut name = base.clone();
  178|       |        let mut counter = 1usize;
  179|       |        while used_names.contains(&name) {
  180|       |            name = format!("{base}-{counter}");
  181|       |            counter += 1;
  182|       |        }
  183|       |        used_names.insert(name.clone());
  184|       |
  185|       |        fonts
  186|       |            .font_data
  187|       |            .insert(name.clone(), egui::FontData::from_owned(bytes));
  188|       |
  189|       |        let lower = name.to_ascii_lowercase();
  190|       |        if lower.contains("mono") || lower.contains("code") {
  191|       |            mono_names.push(name);
  192|       |        } else {
  193|       |            prop_names.push(name);
  194|       |        }
  195|       |    }
  196|       |
  197|       |    if prop_names.is_empty() {
  198|       |        prop_names = mono_names.clone();
  199|       |    }
  200|       |    if mono_names.is_empty() {
  201|       |        mono_names = prop_names.clone();
  202|       |    }
  203|       |
  204|       |    if let Some(family) = fonts.families.get_mut(&egui::FontFamily::Proportional) {
  205|       |        for name in prop_names.iter().rev() {
  206|       |            family.insert(0, name.clone());
  207|       |        }
  208|       |    }
  209|       |    if let Some(family) = fonts.families.get_mut(&egui::FontFamily::Monospace) {
  210|       |        for name in mono_names.iter().rev() {
  211|       |            family.insert(0, name.clone());
  212|       |        }
  213|       |    }
  214|       |
  215|       |    ctx.set_fonts(fonts);
  216|       |    Ok(())
  217|       |}
  218|       |
  219|       |/// Application entry point
  220|       |#[cfg(not(test))]
  221|       |fn main() -> Result<(), eframe::Error> {
  222|       |    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]
  223|       |    env_logger::init();
  224|       |
  225|       |    let cli = match parse_cli_args() {
  226|       |        Ok(opts) => opts,
  227|       |        Err(err) => {
  228|       |            eprintln!("{err}");
  229|       |            return Ok(());
  230|       |        }
  231|       |    };
  232|       |
  233|       |    let screenshot_enabled = cli.screenshot;
  234|       |    let resolved_theme = if screenshot_enabled {
  235|       |        Some(cli.theme.unwrap_or(ThemeChoice::Light))
  236|       |    } else {
  237|       |        cli.theme
  238|       |    };
  239|       |    let screenshot_zoom = cli.zoom.unwrap_or(1.0);
  240|       |
  241|       |    let default_width = if screenshot_enabled { 1280.0 } else { 1000.0 };
  242|       |    let default_height = if screenshot_enabled { 720.0 } else { 700.0 };
  243|       |    let window_width = cli.width.unwrap_or(default_width);
  244|       |    let window_height = cli.height.unwrap_or(default_height);
  245|       |
  246|       |    let mut viewport = egui::ViewportBuilder::default()
  247|       |        .with_title(APP_TITLE_PREFIX.to_string())
  248|       |        .with_inner_size(egui::Vec2::new(window_width, window_height))
  249|       |        .with_min_inner_size(egui::Vec2::new(
  250|       |            if screenshot_enabled { 100.0 } else { 600.0 },
  251|       |            if screenshot_enabled { 100.0 } else { 400.0 },
  252|       |        ))
  253|       |        .with_icon(create_app_icon())
  254|       |        .with_resizable(!screenshot_enabled)
  255|       |        .with_maximize_button(!screenshot_enabled)
  256|       |        .with_minimize_button(!screenshot_enabled)
  257|       |        .with_drag_and_drop(!screenshot_enabled)
  258|       |        // Start hidden to avoid white flash before dark theme is applied
  259|       |        .with_visible(screenshot_enabled);
  260|       |
  261|       |    if !screenshot_enabled {
  262|       |        if let Some(ws) = load_window_state() {
  263|       |            if let Some(ws) = sanitize_window_state(ws) {
  264|       |                viewport = viewport
  265|       |                    .with_inner_size(egui::Vec2::new(ws.size[0], ws.size[1]))
  266|       |                    .with_position(egui::pos2(ws.pos[0], ws.pos[1]))
  267|       |                    .with_maximized(ws.maximized);
  268|       |            }
  269|       |        }
  270|       |    }
  271|       |
  272|       |    let native_options = eframe::NativeOptions {
  273|       |        viewport,
  274|       |        // Disable eframe's built-in window persistence - we handle it ourselves via
  275|       |        // load_window_state/save_window_state to avoid conflicts
  276|       |        persist_window: false,
  277|       |        ..Default::default()
  278|       |    };
  279|       |
  280|       |    let initial_file = cli.initial_file.clone();
  281|       |    let test_fonts = cli.test_fonts.clone();
  282|       |    let screenshot_config = if screenshot_enabled {
  283|       |        let theme = match resolved_theme.unwrap_or(ThemeChoice::Light) {
  284|       |            ThemeChoice::Light => ScreenshotTheme::Light,
  285|       |            ThemeChoice::Dark => ScreenshotTheme::Dark,
  286|       |        };
  287|       |        Some(ScreenshotConfig {
  288|       |            output_path: cli
  289|       |                .screenshot_output
  290|       |                .clone()
  291|       |                .expect("screenshot output required"),
  292|       |            viewport_width: window_width,
  293|       |            viewport_height: window_height,
  294|       |            content_only: cli.content_only,
  295|       |            scroll_ratio: cli.scroll,
  296|       |            wait_ms: cli.wait_ms.unwrap_or(2000),
  297|       |            settle_frames: cli.settle_frames.unwrap_or(3),
  298|       |            zoom: screenshot_zoom,
  299|       |            theme,
  300|       |            font_source: test_fonts
  301|       |                .as_ref()
  302|       |                .map(|path| path.to_string_lossy().to_string()),
  303|       |        })
  304|       |    } else {
  305|       |        None
  306|       |    };
  307|       |
  308|       |    eframe::run_native(
  309|       |        APP_TITLE_PREFIX,
  310|       |        native_options,
  311|       |        Box::new(move |cc| {
  312|       |            if let Some(theme) = resolved_theme {
  313|       |                let visuals = match theme {
  314|       |                    ThemeChoice::Light => egui::Visuals::light(),
  315|       |                    ThemeChoice::Dark => egui::Visuals::dark(),
  316|       |                };
  317|       |                cc.egui_ctx.set_visuals(visuals);
  318|       |            }
  319|       |
  320|       |            configure_egui_style(&cc.egui_ctx);
  321|       |            if let Some(font_dir) = test_fonts.as_ref() {
  322|       |                if let Err(err) = load_fonts_from_dir(&cc.egui_ctx, font_dir) {
  323|       |                    eprintln!("{err}");
  324|       |                }
  325|       |            }
  326|       |
  327|       |            let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
  328|       |            // Apply saved theme ONLY when no explicit --theme flag was passed
  329|       |            if resolved_theme.is_none() {
  330|       |                app.apply_saved_theme(&cc.egui_ctx);
  331|       |            }
  332|       |            if screenshot_zoom != 1.0 {
  333|       |                app.set_zoom_scale(screenshot_zoom);
  334|       |            }
  335|       |            if let Some(config) = screenshot_config {
  336|       |                app.set_screenshot_mode(config);
  337|       |            }
  338|       |
  339|       |            if let Some(file_path) = initial_file {
  340|       |                if file_path.exists() && file_path.is_file() {
  341|       |                    if let Err(e) = app.load_file(file_path, true) {
  342|       |                        eprintln!("Failed to load file: {e}");
  343|       |                    }
  344|       |                } else {
  345|       |                    eprintln!("File not found: {}", file_path.display());
  346|       |                }
  347|       |            }
  348|       |
  349|       |            Box::new(app)
  350|       |        }),
  351|       |    )
  352|       |}

  428|       |/// Configure egui styling for optimal markdown display

  454|       |/// Configure font fallbacks for better symbol coverage.
  455|       |/// Adds platform-specific symbol fonts as fallbacks so characters like ✓ ✗ ⚠
  456|       |/// render instead of showing tofu boxes.
  457|      4|fn configure_font_fallbacks(ctx: &egui::Context) {
  458|      4|    let mut fonts = egui::FontDefinitions::default();
  459|       |
  460|       |    // Platform-specific symbol font paths
  461|       |    #[cfg(target_os = "windows")]
  462|      4|    let symbol_font_paths = &[
  463|      4|        "C:\\Windows\\Fonts\\seguisym.ttf", // Segoe UI Symbol
  464|      4|        "C:\\Windows\\Fonts\\segoeui.ttf",  // Segoe UI (backup)
  465|      4|    ];
  466|       |
  467|       |    #[cfg(target_os = "macos")]
  468|       |    let symbol_font_paths = &[
  469|       |        "/System/Library/Fonts/Apple Symbols.ttf",
  470|       |        "/System/Library/Fonts/Supplemental/Arial Unicode.ttf",
  471|       |    ];
  472|       |
  473|       |    #[cfg(target_os = "linux")]
  474|       |    let symbol_font_paths = &[
  475|       |        "/usr/share/fonts/truetype/dejavu/DejaVuSans.ttf",
  476|       |        "/usr/share/fonts/truetype/freefont/FreeSans.ttf",
  477|       |    ];
  478|       |
  479|       |    #[cfg(not(any(target_os = "windows", target_os = "macos", target_os = "linux")))]
  480|       |    let symbol_font_paths: &[&str] = &[];
  481|       |
  482|       |    // Load the first available symbol font as fallback
  483|      4|    let font_data = symbol_font_paths.iter().find_map(|p| std::fs::read(p).ok());
  484|      4|    if let Some(font_data) = font_data {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (484:12): [True: 4, False: 0]
  ------------------
  485|      4|        let font_name = "symbol_fallback".to_string();
  486|      4|        fonts
  487|      4|            .font_data
  488|      4|            .insert(font_name.clone(), egui::FontData::from_owned(font_data));
  489|       |
  490|      4|        if let Some(family) = fonts.families.get_mut(&egui::FontFamily::Proportional) {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (490:16): [True: 4, False: 0]
  ------------------
  491|      4|            family.push(font_name.clone());
  492|      4|        }
                      ^0
  493|      4|        if let Some(family) = fonts.families.get_mut(&egui::FontFamily::Monospace) {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (493:16): [True: 4, False: 0]
  ------------------
  494|      4|            family.push(font_name);
  495|      4|        }
                      ^0
  496|      0|    }
  497|       |
  498|      4|    ctx.set_fonts(fonts);

  500|       |
  501|       |#[cfg(test)]
  502|       |#[cfg_attr(coverage_nightly, coverage(off))]
  503|       |mod tests {
  504|       |    use super::*;
  505|       |    use std::sync::{Mutex, OnceLock};
  506|       |
  507|       |    fn env_lock() -> std::sync::MutexGuard<'static, ()> {
  508|       |        static ENV_LOCK: OnceLock<Mutex<()>> = OnceLock::new();
  509|       |        ENV_LOCK
  510|       |            .get_or_init(|| Mutex::new(()))
  511|       |            .lock()
  512|       |            .expect("env lock")
  513|       |    }
  514|       |
  515|       |    struct EnvGuard {
  516|       |        key: &'static str,
  517|       |        original: Option<String>,
  518|       |        _lock: std::sync::MutexGuard<'static, ()>,
  519|       |    }
  520|       |
  521|       |    impl EnvGuard {
  522|       |        fn unset(key: &'static str) -> Self {
  523|       |            let lock = env_lock();
  524|       |            let original = std::env::var(key).ok();
  525|       |            std::env::remove_var(key);
  526|       |            Self {
  527|       |                key,
  528|       |                original,
  529|       |                _lock: lock,
  530|       |            }
  531|       |        }
  532|       |    }
  533|       |
  534|       |    impl Drop for EnvGuard {
  535|       |        fn drop(&mut self) {
  536|       |            if let Some(prev) = &self.original {
  537|       |                std::env::set_var(self.key, prev);
  538|       |            } else {
  539|       |                std::env::remove_var(self.key);
  540|       |            }
  541|       |        }
  542|       |    }
  543|       |
  544|       |    #[test]
  545|       |    fn test_app_icon_creation() {
  546|       |        let icon = create_app_icon();
  547|       |        assert_eq!(icon.width, 32);
  548|       |        assert_eq!(icon.height, 32);
  549|       |        assert_eq!(icon.rgba.len(), 32 * 32 * 4); // RGBA format
  550|       |    }
  551|       |
  552|       |    #[test]
  553|       |    fn test_configure_egui_style_dark_mode() {
  554|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
  555|       |        let mut style = (*ctx.style()).clone();
  556|       |        style.visuals.dark_mode = true;
  557|       |        ctx.set_style(style);
  558|       |
  559|       |        configure_egui_style(&ctx);
  560|       |
  561|       |        let style = ctx.style();
  562|       |        assert_eq!(style.spacing.item_spacing, egui::Vec2::new(8.0, 8.0));
  563|       |        assert_eq!(style.spacing.window_margin, egui::Margin::same(8.0));
  564|       |        assert_eq!(style.spacing.menu_margin, egui::Margin::same(6.0));

  567|       |        assert_eq!(style.visuals.window_fill, egui::Color32::BLACK);
  568|       |        assert_eq!(style.visuals.panel_fill, egui::Color32::BLACK);

  789|       |    fn test_env_guard_restores_previous_value() {
  790|       |        let key = "MDMDVIEW_TEST_GUARD_RESTORE";
  791|       |        let _lock = env_lock();
  792|       |        {
  793|       |            std::env::set_var(key, "orig");
  794|       |            let guard = EnvGuard {
  795|       |                key,
  796|       |                original: Some("orig".to_string()),
  797|       |                _lock,
  798|       |            };
  799|       |            std::env::set_var(key, "overwritten");
  800|       |            drop(guard);
  801|       |        }
  802|       |        assert_eq!(std::env::var(key).ok().as_deref(), Some("orig"));
  803|       |        // Clean up
  804|       |        let _lock = env_lock();
  805|       |        std::env::remove_var(key);
  806|       |    }
  807|       |
  808|       |    #[test]
  809|       |    fn test_env_guard_drop_removes_unset_key() {
  810|       |        let key = "MDMDVIEW_TEST_GUARD_UNSET";
  811|       |        std::env::remove_var(key);
  812|       |        {
  813|       |            let _guard = EnvGuard::unset(key);
  814|       |            std::env::set_var(key, "temp");
  815|       |        }
  816|       |        assert!(std::env::var(key).is_err());
  817|       |    }

  136|       |    /// to its field-specific bounds. The closure receives (current_value,
  137|       |    /// default_value, min, max) and returns the new value.
  138|     36|    fn transform_all(&mut self, f: impl Fn(f32, f32, f32, f32) -> f32) {
  139|     36|        let d = FontSizes::default();
  140|     36|        self.body = f(self.body, d.body, 8.0, 32.0);

  217|       |/// Generic LRU (Least Recently Used) cache with configurable capacity.
  218|       |///
  219|       |/// This cache evicts the least recently accessed entries when capacity is exceeded.
  220|       |/// Used for `emoji_textures` and `image_failures` to prevent unbounded memory growth.
  221|       |///
  222|       |/// # Performance Note
  223|       |///
  224|       |/// The `touch()`, `insert()` (for existing keys), and `remove()` operations use
  225|       |/// `VecDeque::retain()` which is O(n) where n is the cache capacity. This is acceptable
  226|       |/// because:
  227|       |/// - Cache capacities are small and bounded (256-512 entries max)
  228|       |/// - These operations are infrequent compared to `get()` which is O(1)
  229|       |/// - For these cache sizes, the constant factors of a more complex O(1) data structure
  230|       |///   (e.g., intrusive linked list with HashMap) would likely outweigh the O(n) cost
  231|       |struct LruCache<K, V> {
  232|       |    entries: HashMap<K, V>,
  233|       |    order: VecDeque<K>,
  234|       |    capacity: usize,
  235|       |}
  236|       |

  448|       |impl ImageCacheEntry {
  449|       |    /// Estimate texture memory size: width * height * 4 bytes (RGBA).
  450|    136|    fn estimate_bytes(size: [u32; 2]) -> usize {
  451|    136|        (size[0] as usize) * (size[1] as usize) * 4
  452|    136|    }
  453|       |}
  454|       |
  455|       |/// Cache for image textures with entry count and memory size limits.
  456|       |///
  457|       |/// Entry count is capped at `capacity` (safety valve).  Memory budget is
  458|       |/// enforced separately by `enforce_budget()`, which degrades far-from-viewport
  459|       |/// entries to tiny placeholders instead of evicting them.
  460|       |///
  461|       |/// # Performance Note
  462|       |///
  463|       |/// The `remove()` operation uses `VecDeque::retain()` which is O(n).
  464|       |/// This is acceptable because the cache capacity is bounded (256 entries max)
  465|       |/// and these operations are infrequent compared to `get()` which is O(1).
  466|       |struct ImageCache {
  467|       |    entries: HashMap<String, ImageCacheEntry>,
  468|       |    order: VecDeque<String>,
  469|       |    capacity: usize,
  470|       |    /// Total approximate bytes of all cached textures.
  471|       |    total_bytes: usize,
  472|       |    /// Maximum total bytes before degradation triggers.
  473|       |    max_bytes: usize,
  474|       |    /// Distance of each cached image from the viewport center (in pixels).
  475|       |    /// Updated each frame by the renderer.  When non-empty, enforce_budget()
  476|       |    /// degrades the entry with the greatest distance first.
  477|       |    viewport_distances: HashMap<String, f32>,
  478|       |    /// Estimated bytes of all in-flight restore reloads (enqueued but not yet
  479|       |    /// completed).  Used by `would_restore_fit` to prevent over-committing the
  480|       |    /// budget when multiple degraded images are enqueued in the same frame.
  481|       |    pending_restore_bytes: usize,
  482|       |}
  483|       |
  484|       |impl ImageCache {
  485|    914|    fn new(capacity: usize) -> Self {
  486|    914|        Self {
  487|    914|            entries: HashMap::new(),
  488|    914|            order: VecDeque::new(),
  489|    914|            capacity: capacity.max(1),
  490|    914|            total_bytes: 0,
  491|    914|            max_bytes: MAX_IMAGE_TEXTURE_CACHE_BYTES,
  492|    914|            viewport_distances: HashMap::new(),
  493|    914|            pending_restore_bytes: 0,
  494|    914|        }
  495|    914|    }

  575|       |
  576|       |    /// Degrade far-from-viewport entries to keep memory within budget and
  577|       |    /// create headroom for near-viewport degraded entries to restore.
  578|       |    ///
  579|       |    /// Phase 1: Degrade orphan entries (no viewport distance, from old documents).
  580|       |    /// Phase 2: Degrade current-document entries furthest-first until under budget.
  581|       |    /// Phase 3: Proactively degrade far non-degraded entries to make room for
  582|       |    ///   near-viewport degraded entries. Without this, enforce_budget and
  583|       |    ///   would_restore_fit deadlock: enforce_budget stops because under budget,
  584|       |    ///   but would_restore_fit blocks restores because there's no headroom.
  585|     14|    fn enforce_budget(&mut self, ctx: &egui::Context, viewport_height: f32) {
  586|       |        // Phase 1: Degrade orphan entries (no viewport distance) first.
  587|       |        // These are leftovers from a previous document — safe to degrade before
  588|       |        // touching current-document entries.

  628|       |
  629|       |        // Phase 3: Proactive headroom — degrade far non-degraded entries to make
  630|       |        // room for near-viewport degraded entries that want to restore.
  631|       |        // Compute how many bytes near-viewport degraded entries would need.
  632|     14|        let reload_zone = viewport_height * 2.0;
  633|     14|        let needed: usize = self
  634|     14|            .entries
  635|     14|            .iter()
  636|     14|            .filter(|(_, e)| e.degraded)
  637|     20|            .filter(|(k, _)| {
                           ^14
  638|     20|                self.viewport_distances
  639|     20|                    .get(k.as_str())
  640|     20|                    .is_some_and(|&d| d < reload_zone)
                                                    ^16 ^16
  641|     20|            })
  642|     14|            .map(|(_, e)| ImageCacheEntry::estimate_bytes(e.size).saturating_sub(e.byte_size))
                                        ^2                              ^2      ^2             ^2
  643|     14|            .sum();
  644|       |
  645|     14|        if needed > 0 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (645:12): [True: 2, False: 12]
  ------------------
  646|      2|            let target = self.max_bytes.saturating_sub(needed);
  647|       |            // Re-filter candidates (some may have been degraded in Phase 2).
  648|      2|            let far_candidates: Vec<(String, f32)> = candidates
  649|      2|                .iter()
  650|      4|                .filter(|(k, _)| self.entries.get(k).is_some_and(|e| !e.degraded))
                               ^2
  651|      4|                .filter(|(_, d)| *d >= reload_zone)
                               ^2
  652|      2|                .cloned()
  653|      2|                .collect();
  654|       |
  655|      4|            for (key, _) in &far_candidates {
                                          ^2
  656|      4|                if self.total_bytes <= target {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (656:20): [True: 2, False: 2]
  ------------------
  657|      2|                    break;
  658|      2|                }
  659|      6|                let non_degraded_count = self.entries.values().filter(|e| !e.degraded).count();
                                  ^2                   ^2           ^2       ^2                      ^2
  660|      2|                if non_degraded_count <= 1 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (660:20): [True: 0, False: 2]
  ------------------
  661|      0|                    break;
  662|      2|                }
  663|      2|                self.degrade(key, ctx);
  664|       |            }
  665|     12|        }
  666|     14|    }
  667|       |
  668|       |    /// Returns true if restoring a degraded entry (replacing its 2x2 texture
  669|       |    /// with full-resolution) would keep `total_bytes` within `max_bytes`.
  670|     22|    fn would_restore_fit(&self, key: &str) -> bool {
  671|     22|        let entry = match self.entries.get(key) {
                          ^20
  672|     20|            Some(e) => e,
  673|      2|            None => return false,
  674|       |        };
  675|     20|        if !entry.degraded {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (675:12): [True: 2, False: 18]
  ------------------
  676|      2|            return false;
  677|     18|        }
  678|     18|        let full_bytes = ImageCacheEntry::estimate_bytes(entry.size);
  679|     18|        self.total_bytes.saturating_sub(entry.byte_size) + full_bytes + self.pending_restore_bytes
  680|     18|            <= self.max_bytes
  681|     22|    }
  682|       |
  683|    534|    fn clear(&mut self) {
  684|    534|        self.entries.clear();
  685|    534|        self.order.clear();
  686|    534|        self.total_bytes = 0;
  687|    534|        self.viewport_distances.clear();
  688|    534|        self.pending_restore_bytes = 0;
  689|    534|    }

  702|       |
  703|       |struct ImageLoadRequest {

  707|       |    /// Results from older generations are discarded in poll_image_results.

  725|       |/// Markdown renderer with proper inline element handling
  726|       |pub struct MarkdownRenderer {
  727|       |    font_sizes: FontSizes,
  728|       |    syntax_set: SyntaxSet,
  729|       |    theme_set: ThemeSet,
  730|       |    emoji_textures: RefCell<LruCache<String, egui::TextureHandle>>,
  731|       |    image_textures: RefCell<ImageCache>,
  732|       |    image_pending: RefCell<HashSet<String>>,
  733|       |    image_failures: RefCell<LruCache<String, ImageFailure>>,
  734|       |    image_job_tx: Sender<ImageLoadRequest>,
  735|       |    image_result_rx: Receiver<ImageLoadResult>,
  736|       |    /// Generation counter incremented on each document load.  Image load results
  737|       |    /// from a previous generation are silently discarded so that stale background
  738|       |    /// loads do not pollute the cache after a document switch.
  739|       |    image_generation: Cell<u64>,
  740|       |    // Per-frame Y position of each image (keyed by resolved path) for viewport
  741|       |    // distance calculation.  Populated during rendering, consumed at frame end
  742|       |    // to update the cache's degradation distances.
  743|       |    image_doc_y: RefCell<HashMap<String, f32>>,
  744|       |    // Mapping of header id -> last rendered rect (for in-document navigation)
  745|       |    header_rects: RefCell<HashMap<String, egui::Rect>>,
  746|       |    // Last clicked internal anchor (e.g., "getting-started") for the app to consume
  747|       |    pending_anchor: RefCell<Option<String>>,
  748|       |    // Unique counter to avoid egui Id collisions for repeated links
  749|       |    link_counter: RefCell<u64>,
  750|       |    // Per-frame table counter to build stable table ids in render order
  751|       |    table_counter: RefCell<u64>,
  752|       |    // Per-frame rect for each top-level element in render order
  753|       |    element_rects: RefCell<Vec<egui::Rect>>,
  754|       |    // Optional highlight phrase (lowercased) for in-text highlighting
  755|       |    highlight_phrase: RefCell<Option<String>>,
  756|       |    // Cache for image/diagram textures
  757|       |    // Base directory used to resolve relative image paths
  758|       |    base_dir: RefCell<Option<PathBuf>>,
  759|       |    mermaid: MermaidRenderer,
  760|       |    /// Current resolved Mermaid theme (Auto resolved at set time).
  761|       |    mermaid_theme: Cell<MermaidTheme>,
  762|       |    #[cfg(feature = "pikchr")]
  763|       |    pikchr: pikchr_renderer::PikchrRenderer,
  764|       |    #[cfg(feature = "d2")]
  765|       |    d2: d2_renderer::D2Renderer,
  766|       |    table_layout_cache: RefCell<CellLayoutCache>,
  767|       |    table_metrics: RefCell<TableMetrics>,
  768|       |    column_stats_cache: RefCell<HashMap<u64, ColumnStatsCacheEntry>>,
  769|       |    /// Allow loading images from remote URLs (http/https)
  770|       |    allow_remote_images: Cell<bool>,
  771|       |    /// Current theme mode — true for dark, false for light.
  772|       |    /// Used by syntect highlighting at parse time.
  773|       |    dark_mode: bool,
  774|       |    /// Last observed viewport height (pixels).  Updated each frame during
  775|       |    /// viewport distance calculation in `render_to_ui`.  Used by
  776|       |    /// `get_or_load_image_texture` to decide the reload zone for degraded
  777|       |    /// entries (2× viewport height per the LOD HLD).
  778|       |    last_viewport_height: Cell<f32>,
  779|       |}

 2938|       |    /// Render parsed markdown elements to egui UI
 2939|     62|    pub fn render_to_ui(&self, ui: &mut egui::Ui, elements: &[MarkdownElement]) {
 2940|     62|        self.poll_image_results(ui.ctx());
 2941|     62|        self.mermaid.begin_frame();
 2942|       |        #[cfg(feature = "pikchr")]
 2943|     62|        self.pikchr.begin_frame(ui.ctx());
 2944|       |        #[cfg(feature = "d2")]
 2945|     62|        self.d2.begin_frame(ui.ctx());
 2946|       |        // Clear header rects before rendering a new frame
 2947|     62|        self.header_rects.borrow_mut().clear();
 2948|       |        // Reset per-frame link counter to ensure link IDs are stable across frames
 2949|     62|        *self.link_counter.borrow_mut() = 0;
 2950|       |        // Reset per-frame table counter
 2951|     62|        *self.table_counter.borrow_mut() = 0;
 2952|       |        // Reset per-frame element rects
 2953|     62|        self.element_rects.borrow_mut().clear();
 2954|       |        // Clear per-frame image Y positions
 2955|     62|        self.image_doc_y.borrow_mut().clear();
 2956|    206|        for element in elements.iter() {
                                     ^62      ^62
 2957|       |            // Wrap each element in a no-op frame to capture its rect
 2958|    206|            let ir = egui::Frame::none().show(ui, |ui| {
 2959|    206|                self.render_element_body(ui, element);
 2960|    206|            });
 2961|    206|            self.element_rects.borrow_mut().push(ir.response.rect);
 2962|       |        }
 2963|       |
 2964|       |        // Update viewport distances for image cache degradation.
 2965|       |        // `clip_rect()` gives the visible portion of the scroll area.
 2966|     62|        let viewport_height = ui.clip_rect().height();
 2967|     62|        self.last_viewport_height.set(viewport_height);
 2968|     62|        let viewport_center_y = ui.clip_rect().center().y;
 2969|     62|        let distances: HashMap<String, f32> = self
 2970|     62|            .image_doc_y
 2971|     62|            .borrow()

                                            ^8  ^8       ^8                      ^8

 2975|     62|        if !distances.is_empty() {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (2975:12): [True: 2, False: 60]
  ------------------
 2976|      2|            let mut cache = self.image_textures.borrow_mut();
 2977|      2|            cache.update_viewport_distances(distances);
 2978|      2|            cache.enforce_budget(ui.ctx(), self.last_viewport_height.get());
 2979|     60|        }
 2980|       |
 2981|       |        // Add a little extra breathing room at the end so
 2982|       |        // the final line doesn't sit flush under the status bar.
 2983|     62|        ui.add_space(16.0);
 2984|     62|    }

 3047|       |    #[cfg(test)]

 3782|       |    // Expand ^...^ segments into Unicode superscript characters when available.
 3783|       |    // Example: "5^th^" -> "5??"
 3784|  1.18k|    fn expand_superscripts(s: &str) -> String {
 3785|  1.18k|        if !s.contains('^') {

 4483|     40|    fn render_table_tablebuilder(
 4484|     40|        &self,
 4485|     40|        ui: &mut egui::Ui,
 4486|     40|        headers: &[Vec<InlineSpan>],
 4487|     40|        rows: &[Vec<Vec<InlineSpan>>],
 4488|     40|        alignments: &[Alignment],
 4489|     40|        table_id: u64,
 4490|     40|    ) {
 4491|     40|        let column_stats = self.column_stats_for_table(table_id, headers, rows, alignments);
 4492|     40|        let ctx =
 4493|     40|            TableColumnContext::new(headers, rows, &column_stats, self.font_sizes.body, table_id);
 4494|     40|        let mut column_specs = derive_column_specs(&ctx);

 4498|     30|                spec.set_policy(policy);

                           ^40
 4504|     40|            .collect();

 4507|     40|        let viewport_width = ui.available_width().max(1.0);

 4509|     40|        let resolved_widths = self.resolve_table_column_widths(table_id, &column_specs, min_floor);
 4510|       |

 4512|       |        // and policy, then determine if the table fits within the viewport.

 4514|     40|            &column_stats,

 4522|       |

 4528|     38|                ColumnPolicy::Remainder { .. } | ColumnPolicy::Auto => min_floor,
 4529|    112|            })

 4940|     52|    ) -> (egui::Rect, egui::Rect) {
 4941|       |        // Combine header and body clip rects to ensure dividers respect scroll bounds.
 4942|     52|        let clip_rect = match (header_clip_rect, body_clip) {
 4943|     38|            (Some(h), Some(b)) => Some(h.union(b)),
 4944|     14|            (h, b) => h.or(b),
 4945|       |        };
 4946|       |
 4947|       |        // Calculate accurate table width from column widths.
 4948|       |        // This is more accurate than union of cell min_rects which may be smaller
 4949|       |        // than the allocated column space.
 4950|     52|        let calculated_width: f32 =
 4951|     52|            widths.iter().sum::<f32>() + column_spacing * widths.len().saturating_sub(1) as f32;
 4952|       |
 4953|       |        // Combine header/body bounds with layout bounds for accurate borders,
 4954|       |        // especially when the first column is centered or right-aligned.
 4955|     52|        let left = layout_rect
 4956|     52|            .map(|rect| rect.left())
                                      ^44  ^44
 4957|     52|            .or_else(|| header_rect.map(|rect| rect.left()))
                                      ^8          ^8         ^2   ^2
 4958|     52|            .or_else(|| body_rect.map(|rect| rect.left()));
                                      ^6        ^6         ^2   ^2
 4959|     52|        let right = left.map(|value| value + calculated_width);
                                                   ^48     ^48
 4960|     52|        let top = header_rect
 4961|     52|            .map(|rect| rect.top())
                                      ^44  ^44
 4962|     52|            .or_else(|| body_rect.map(|rect| rect.top()))
                                      ^8        ^8         ^2   ^2
 4963|     52|            .or_else(|| layout_rect.map(|rect| rect.top()));
                                      ^6          ^6         ^2   ^2
 4964|     52|        let bottom = body_rect
 4965|     52|            .map(|rect| rect.bottom())
                                      ^42  ^42
 4966|     52|            .or_else(|| header_rect.map(|rect| rect.bottom()))
                                      ^10         ^10        ^4   ^4
 4967|     52|            .or_else(|| layout_rect.map(|rect| rect.bottom()));
                                      ^6          ^6         ^2   ^2
 4968|     52|        let table_rect = match (left, right, top, bottom) {
 4969|     48|            (Some(left), Some(right), Some(top), Some(bottom)) => Some(egui::Rect::from_min_max(
 4970|     48|                egui::pos2(left, top),
 4971|     48|                egui::pos2(right, bottom),
 4972|     48|            )),
 4973|      4|            _ => None,
 4974|       |        };
 4975|       |
 4976|     52|        let zero_rect = egui::Rect::from_min_max(egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0), egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0));
 4977|     52|        let rect = table_rect.or(layout_rect).unwrap_or(zero_rect);
 4978|     52|        let clip_rect = clip_rect.unwrap_or(rect);
 4979|     52|        (rect, clip_rect)
 4980|     52|    }

 4989|     46|        column_specs: &[ColumnSpec],

 5125|       |
 5126|       |    /// Estimate per-column natural widths from content statistics and
 5127|       |    /// policy-resolved widths (which include any user-persisted widths).
 5128|       |    ///
 5129|       |    /// For each column, takes the maximum of:
 5130|       |    /// - Content-based estimate (grapheme count * char_width_factor + cell_padding)
 5131|       |    /// - Policy-resolved width (from resolve_table_column_widths)
 5132|       |    ///
 5133|       |    /// Each column width is clamped to [MIN_COL_WIDTH, MAX_COL_WIDTH].
 5134|     58|    fn estimate_natural_column_widths(
 5135|     58|        &self,
 5136|     58|        column_stats: &[ColumnStat],
 5137|     58|        headers: &[Vec<InlineSpan>],
 5138|     58|        resolved_widths: &[f32],
 5139|     58|        column_count: usize,
 5140|     58|    ) -> Vec<f32> {
 5141|     58|        let body = self.font_sizes.body;
 5142|     58|        let char_width_factor = body * 0.62;
 5143|     58|        let cell_padding = body * 1.8;
 5144|     58|        let min_col_width = body * 4.0;
 5145|     58|        let max_col_width = body * 35.0;
 5146|       |
 5147|     58|        (0..column_count)
 5148|    154|            .map(|i| {
                           ^58
 5149|       |                // Extract header text and compute its display width
 5150|    154|                let header_width = headers
 5151|    154|                    .get(i)
 5152|    154|                    .map(|spans| {
                                               ^144
 5153|    144|                        let text = spans_to_text(spans);
 5154|    144|                        UnicodeWidthStr::width(text.as_str())
 5155|    144|                    })
 5156|    154|                    .unwrap_or(0);
 5157|       |
 5158|       |                // Content width from stats (already unicode-width-based)
 5159|    154|                let content_graphemes = column_stats.get(i).map(|s| s.max_graphemes).unwrap_or(0);
 5160|       |
 5161|    154|                let display_graphemes = header_width.max(content_graphemes);
 5162|    154|                let content_width = display_graphemes as f32 * char_width_factor + cell_padding;
 5163|    154|                let policy_width = resolved_widths.get(i).copied().unwrap_or(0.0);
 5164|    154|                let natural = content_width.max(policy_width);
 5165|    154|                natural.clamp(min_col_width, max_col_width)
 5166|    154|            })
 5167|     58|            .collect()
 5168|     58|    }
 5169|       |
 5170|       |    /// Compute the total natural table width from per-column widths and inter-column spacing.
 5171|     50|    fn natural_table_width(natural_widths: &[f32], column_spacing: f32) -> f32 {
 5172|     50|        let spacing = column_spacing * natural_widths.len().saturating_sub(1) as f32;
 5173|     50|        natural_widths.iter().sum::<f32>() + spacing
 5174|     50|    }

 5355|       |    /// Paint vertical dividers between table columns, a horizontal header separator,
 5356|       |    /// and an outer border.
 5357|       |    ///
 5358|       |    /// # Arguments
 5359|       |    /// * `widths` - The actual allocated column widths from `TableBody::widths()`.
 5360|       |    ///   IMPORTANT: These must NOT be cell content widths from `ui.min_rect()`,
 5361|       |    ///   which would cause dividers to be misaligned.
 5362|       |    /// * `header_height` - The height of the header row. A horizontal separator is
 5363|       |    ///   drawn at this y-offset to visually separate header from body rows.
 5364|       |    #[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]
 5365|     52|    fn paint_table_dividers(
 5366|     52|        &self,
 5367|     52|        painter: &Painter,
 5368|     52|        visuals: &Visuals,
 5369|     52|        rect: egui::Rect,
 5370|     52|        clip_rect: egui::Rect,
 5371|     52|        widths: &[f32],
 5372|     52|        header_height: f32,
 5373|     52|        column_spacing: f32,
 5374|     52|        row_spacing: f32,
 5375|     52|        draw_dividers: bool,
 5376|     52|    ) {
 5377|     52|        if widths.is_empty() {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (5377:12): [True: 2, False: 50]
  ------------------
 5378|      2|            return;
 5379|     50|        }
 5380|     50|        let separator_color = visuals
 5381|     50|            .widgets
 5382|     50|            .noninteractive
 5383|     50|            .bg_stroke
 5384|     50|            .color
 5385|     50|            .gamma_multiply(0.9);
 5386|     50|        let separator_stroke = Stroke::new(1.0, separator_color);
 5387|     50|        let border_stroke = visuals.window_stroke();
 5388|       |        // Expand border rect by half the column/row spacing to match the visual
 5389|       |        // extent of egui's striped row backgrounds (which expand by
 5390|       |        // item_spacing/2 on each side). This eliminates the double-line effect
 5391|       |        // at table edges and provides padding between the border and cell text.
 5392|     50|        let half_col_spacing = column_spacing * 0.5;
 5393|     50|        let half_row_spacing = row_spacing * 0.5;
 5394|     50|        let border_rect = egui::Rect::from_min_max(
 5395|     50|            egui::pos2(
 5396|     50|                rect.left() - half_col_spacing,
 5397|     50|                rect.top() - half_row_spacing,
 5398|       |            ),
 5399|     50|            egui::pos2(
 5400|     50|                rect.right() + half_col_spacing,
 5401|     50|                rect.bottom() + half_row_spacing,
 5402|       |            ),
 5403|       |        );
 5404|       |        // Expand clip_rect to include the full border rect so borders aren't clipped.
 5405|       |        // The cell clip_rect may not include the outer border area.
 5406|     50|        let expanded_clip = clip_rect.union(border_rect);
 5407|     50|        let painter = painter.with_clip_rect(expanded_clip);
 5408|       |
 5409|       |        // Draw vertical dividers between columns
 5410|     50|        if draw_dividers && widths.len() > 1 {
                                          ^22
  ------------------
  |  Branch (5410:12): [True: 22, False: 28]
  |  Branch (5410:29): [True: 20, False: 2]
  ------------------
 5411|     20|            let mut x = rect.left();
 5412|     38|            for (idx, width) in widths
                                              ^20
 5413|     20|                .iter()
 5414|     20|                .take(widths.len().saturating_sub(1))
 5415|     20|                .enumerate()
 5416|     38|            {
 5417|     38|                x += *width;
 5418|     38|                let divider_x = x + column_spacing * (idx as f32 + 0.5);
 5419|     38|                let x_pos = (divider_x.round() + 0.5).clamp(rect.left(), rect.right());
 5420|     38|                painter.vline(x_pos, border_rect.y_range(), separator_stroke);
 5421|     38|            }
 5422|     30|        }
 5423|       |
 5424|       |        // Draw horizontal separator below header row
 5425|     50|        if header_height > 0.0 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (5425:12): [True: 48, False: 2]

 5426|     48|            let header_y = rect.top() + header_height;
 5427|     48|            if header_y < rect.bottom() {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (5427:16): [True: 46, False: 2]

 5428|     46|                painter.hline(
 5429|     46|                    border_rect.x_range(),
 5430|     46|                    header_y.round() + 0.5,
 5431|     46|                    separator_stroke,
 5432|     46|                );
 5433|     46|            }
                          ^2
 5434|      2|        }
 5435|       |
 5436|       |        // Draw outer border
 5437|     50|        painter.rect_stroke(border_rect, 0.0, border_stroke);
 5438|     52|    }

 5611|     16|    fn resolve_table_widths(available: f32, mins: &[f32], desired: &[f32]) -> Vec<f32> {
 5612|     16|        debug_assert_eq!(mins.len(), desired.len());
 5613|     16|        if mins.is_empty() {

  |  Branch (5613:12): [True: 2, False: 14]
  ------------------
 5614|      2|            return Vec::new();
 5615|     14|        }

 5617|     14|        let desired_total: f32 = desired.iter().sum();

 5619|       |

  ------------------
  |  Branch (5620:25): [True: 4, False: 10]

 5631|       |            }

 6252|     48|    fn image_source_stale_with_timestamp(
 6253|     48|        cached_modified: Option<SystemTime>,
 6254|     48|        current: Option<SystemTime>,
 6255|     48|    ) -> bool {
 6256|     48|        match (cached_modified, current) {
 6257|     28|            (Some(a), Some(b)) => {
 6258|       |                // Treat timestamps within the tolerance window as identical.
 6259|     28|                let delta = if a > b {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (6259:32): [True: 4, False: 24]
  ------------------
 6260|      4|                    a.duration_since(b)
 6261|       |                } else {
 6262|     24|                    b.duration_since(a)
 6263|       |                };
 6264|     28|                match delta {
 6265|     28|                    Ok(d) => d > IMAGE_TIMESTAMP_TOLERANCE,
 6266|      0|                    Err(_) => true, // clock went backwards — treat as stale
 6267|       |                }
 6268|       |            }
 6269|       |            // Had a valid cache but metadata now fails (file locked by AV,
 6270|       |            // transient 9P error, etc.) — keep the cached texture rather than
 6271|       |            // flashing "Loading image…" every frame.
 6272|      4|            (Some(_), None) => false,
 6273|     12|            (None, None) => false,
 6274|       |            // File appeared after being missing → stale, pick it up.
 6275|      4|            (None, Some(_)) => true,
 6276|       |        }
 6277|     48|    }

 6506|       |/// Compute a fixed-size cache key for a data URI string.
 6507|       |///
 6508|       |/// Uses `DefaultHasher` (SipHash-1-3) to produce a 16-hex-digit hash,
 6509|       |/// prefixed with `data-uri:`. The resulting key is always 25 characters
 6510|       |/// regardless of payload size, preventing megabyte-sized URI strings from
 6511|       |/// bloating the `ImageCache` key storage.
 6512|     42|fn data_uri_cache_key(uri: &str) -> String {
 6513|     42|    let mut hasher = DefaultHasher::new();
 6514|     42|    uri.hash(&mut hasher);
 6515|     42|    format!("data-uri:{:016x}", hasher.finish())
 6516|     42|}
 6517|       |
 6518|       |/// Downscale a `ColorImage` if it exceeds `max_w` or `max_h`, preserving
 6519|       |/// aspect ratio.  Returns the (possibly new) image and its pixel dimensions.
 6520|       |///
 6521|       |/// Uses nearest-neighbor sampling directly on the `Color32` pixel buffer so
 6522|       |/// there is **no intermediate byte-copy** — peak memory is just the original
 6523|       |/// plus the (smaller) destination.
 6524|     18|fn downscale_color_image(
 6525|     18|    image: egui::ColorImage,
 6526|     18|    max_w: u32,
 6527|     18|    max_h: u32,
 6528|     18|) -> (egui::ColorImage, u32, u32) {
 6529|     18|    let (src_w, src_h) = (image.size[0] as u32, image.size[1] as u32);
 6530|     18|    if src_w <= max_w && src_h <= max_h {
                                       ^14
  ------------------
  |  Branch (6530:8): [True: 14, False: 4]
  |  Branch (6530:26): [True: 14, False: 0]
  ------------------
 6531|     14|        return (image, src_w, src_h);
 6532|      4|    }
 6533|       |    // Determine uniform scale factor
 6534|      4|    let scale_x = max_w as f64 / src_w as f64;
 6535|      4|    let scale_y = max_h as f64 / src_h as f64;
 6536|      4|    let scale = scale_x.min(scale_y);
 6537|      4|    let dst_w = ((src_w as f64 * scale).round() as u32).max(1);
 6538|      4|    let dst_h = ((src_h as f64 * scale).round() as u32).max(1);
 6539|       |
 6540|       |    // Nearest-neighbor downscale — reads directly from the source pixel
 6541|       |    // buffer with no intermediate Vec<u8> copy.
 6542|      4|    let src_stride = src_w as usize;
 6543|      4|    let mut dst_pixels = Vec::with_capacity((dst_w * dst_h) as usize);
 6544|    400|    for y in 0..dst_h {
                           ^4
 6545|    400|        let src_y = ((y as f64 / scale).min((src_h - 1) as f64)) as usize;
 6546|   120k|        for x in 0..dst_w {
                               ^400
 6547|   120k|            let src_x = ((x as f64 / scale).min((src_w - 1) as f64)) as usize;
 6548|   120k|            dst_pixels.push(image.pixels[src_y * src_stride + src_x]);
 6549|   120k|        }
 6550|       |    }
 6551|      4|    let out = egui::ColorImage {
 6552|      4|        size: [dst_w as usize, dst_h as usize],
 6553|      4|        pixels: dst_pixels,
 6554|      4|    };
 6555|      4|    (out, dst_w, dst_h)
 6556|     18|}
 6557|       |

 6575|       |    static MERMAID_ENV_LOCK: Mutex<()> = Mutex::new(());
 6576|       |
 6577|       |    struct EnvVarGuard {
 6578|       |        key: &'static str,
 6579|       |        previous: Option<String>,
 6580|       |        _lock: std::sync::MutexGuard<'static, ()>,
 6581|       |    }
 6582|       |
 6583|       |    impl EnvVarGuard {
 6584|       |        fn set(key: &'static str, value: &str) -> Self {
 6585|       |            let lock = MERMAID_ENV_LOCK.lock().expect("lock mermaid env");
 6586|       |            let previous = env::var(key).ok();
 6587|       |            env::set_var(key, value);
 6588|       |            Self {
 6589|       |                key,
 6590|       |                previous,
 6591|       |                _lock: lock,
 6592|       |            }
 6593|       |        }
 6594|       |    }
 6595|       |
 6596|       |    impl Drop for EnvVarGuard {
 6597|       |        fn drop(&mut self) {
 6598|       |            if let Some(prev) = &self.previous {
 6599|       |                env::set_var(self.key, prev);
 6600|       |            } else {
 6601|       |                env::remove_var(self.key);
 6602|       |            }
 6603|       |        }
 6604|       |    }
 6605|       |
 6606|       |    #[test]
 6607|       |    fn test_spawn_image_loader_handles_error_path() {
 6608|       |        MarkdownRenderer::force_thread_spawn_error_for_test();
 6609|       |        let (_job_tx, job_rx) = crossbeam_channel::unbounded();
 6610|       |        let (result_tx, _result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::unbounded();
 6611|       |        MarkdownRenderer::spawn_image_loader(job_rx, result_tx);
 6612|       |    }
 6613|       |
 6614|       |    struct ForcedRenderActions {
 6615|       |        actions: Vec<&'static str>,
 6616|       |    }
 6617|       |
 6618|       |    impl ForcedRenderActions {
 6619|       |        fn new(actions: &[&'static str]) -> Self {
 6620|       |            FORCED_RENDER_ACTIONS.with(|set| {
 6621|       |                let mut set = set.borrow_mut();
 6622|       |                for action in actions {
 6623|       |                    set.insert(*action);
 6624|       |                }
 6625|       |            });
 6626|       |            Self {
 6627|       |                actions: actions.to_vec(),
 6628|       |            }
 6629|       |        }
 6630|       |    }
 6631|       |
 6632|       |    impl Drop for ForcedRenderActions {
 6633|       |        fn drop(&mut self) {
 6634|       |            FORCED_RENDER_ACTIONS.with(|set| {
 6635|       |                let mut set = set.borrow_mut();
 6636|       |                for action in &self.actions {
 6637|       |                    set.remove(action);
 6638|       |                }
 6639|       |            });
 6640|       |        }
 6641|       |    }
 6642|       |
 6643|       |    struct ForcedTablePolicies;
 6644|       |
 6645|       |    impl ForcedTablePolicies {
 6646|       |        fn new(policies: Vec<ColumnPolicy>) -> Self {
 6647|       |            FORCED_TABLE_POLICIES.with(|slot| {
 6648|       |                *slot.borrow_mut() = Some(policies);
 6649|       |            });
 6650|       |            Self
 6651|       |        }
 6652|       |    }
 6653|       |
 6654|       |    impl Drop for ForcedTablePolicies {
 6655|       |        fn drop(&mut self) {
 6656|       |            FORCED_TABLE_POLICIES.with(|slot| {
 6657|       |                slot.borrow_mut().take();
 6658|       |            });
 6659|       |        }
 6660|       |    }

 6663|       |        if let MarkdownElement::Paragraph(spans) = element {
 6664|       |            Some(spans)
 6665|       |        } else {
 6666|       |            None
 6667|       |        }
 6668|       |    }

 6670|       |    fn image_span_fields(span: &InlineSpan) -> Option<(&str, &str, Option<&str>)> {
 6671|       |        if let InlineSpan::Image { src, alt, title } = span {
 6672|       |            Some((src.as_str(), alt.as_str(), title.as_deref()))
 6673|       |        } else {
 6674|       |            None
 6675|       |        }
 6676|       |    }

 6680|       |            Some(text.as_str())
 6681|       |        } else {
 6682|       |            None
 6683|       |        }
 6684|       |    }

 6686|       |    fn emoji_fragment_key<'a>(fragment: &'a CellFragment<'a>) -> Option<&'a str> {
 6687|       |        if let CellFragment::Emoji(key) = fragment {
 6688|       |            Some(key.as_str())
 6689|       |        } else {
 6690|       |            None
 6691|       |        }
 6692|       |    }

 6694|       |    fn with_test_ui<F>(f: F)
 6695|       |    where
 6696|       |        F: FnOnce(&egui::Context, &mut egui::Ui),
 6697|       |    {
 6698|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 6699|       |        ctx.begin_frame(screen_input(1024.0, 768.0));
 6700|       |        egui::CentralPanel::default().show(&ctx, |ui| {
 6701|       |            f(&ctx, ui);
 6702|       |        });
 6703|       |        let _ = ctx.end_frame();
 6704|       |    }
 6705|       |
 6706|       |    fn wait_for_image(
 6707|       |        renderer: &MarkdownRenderer,
 6708|       |        ctx: &egui::Context,
 6709|       |        ui: &egui::Ui,
 6710|       |        path: &str,
 6711|       |    ) -> Option<(egui::TextureHandle, u32, u32)> {
 6712|       |        // Allow extra time for async image loading during tests (up to 10s total).
 6713|       |        wait_for_image_with_limit(renderer, ctx, ui, path, 200)
 6714|       |    }
 6715|       |
 6716|       |    fn wait_for_image_with_limit(
 6717|       |        renderer: &MarkdownRenderer,
 6718|       |        ctx: &egui::Context,
 6719|       |        ui: &egui::Ui,
 6720|       |        path: &str,
 6721|       |        max_iters: usize,
 6722|       |    ) -> Option<(egui::TextureHandle, u32, u32)> {
 6723|       |        for _ in 0..max_iters {
 6724|       |            if let Some(loaded) = renderer.get_or_load_image_texture(ui, path) {
 6725|       |                return Some(loaded);
 6726|       |            }
 6727|       |            renderer.poll_image_results(ctx);
 6728|       |            std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(50));
 6729|       |        }
 6730|       |        renderer.get_or_load_image_texture(ui, path)
 6731|       |    }
 6732|       |
 6733|       |    fn run_frame_with_input<F>(ctx: &egui::Context, input: egui::RawInput, f: F)
 6734|       |    where
 6735|       |        F: FnOnce(&egui::Context, &mut egui::Ui),
 6736|       |    {
 6737|       |        ctx.begin_frame(input);
 6738|       |        egui::CentralPanel::default().show(ctx, |ui| {
 6739|       |            f(ctx, ui);
 6740|       |        });
 6741|       |        let _ = ctx.end_frame();
 6742|       |    }
 6743|       |
 6744|       |    fn input_with_click(pos: egui::Pos2, button: egui::PointerButton) -> egui::RawInput {
 6745|       |        let mut input = screen_input(320.0, 240.0);
 6746|       |        input.events.push(egui::Event::PointerMoved(pos));
 6747|       |        input.events.push(egui::Event::PointerButton {
 6748|       |            pos,
 6749|       |            button,
 6750|       |            pressed: true,
 6751|       |            modifiers: egui::Modifiers::default(),
 6752|       |        });
 6753|       |        input.events.push(egui::Event::PointerButton {
 6754|       |            pos,
 6755|       |            button,
 6756|       |            pressed: false,
 6757|       |            modifiers: egui::Modifiers::default(),
 6758|       |        });
 6759|       |        input
 6760|       |    }
 6761|       |
 6762|       |    /// Create a solid-color PNG of the given dimensions.  Solid PNGs compress
 6763|       |    /// extremely well (a few hundred bytes on disk) but decode to
 6764|       |    /// `width * height * 4` bytes of RGBA — ideal for cache-pressure tests.
 6765|       |    fn solid_png_bytes(width: u32, height: u32) -> Vec<u8> {
 6766|       |        let pixels = vec![255u8; (width * height * 4) as usize];
 6767|       |        let mut out = Vec::new();
 6768|       |        let encoder = PngEncoder::new(&mut out);
 6769|       |        encoder
 6770|       |            .write_image(&pixels, width, height, ColorType::Rgba8)
 6771|       |            .expect("encode png");
 6772|       |        out
 6773|       |    }
 6774|       |
 6775|       |    fn tiny_png_bytes() -> Vec<u8> {
 6776|       |        let width = 2u32;
 6777|       |        let height = 2u32;
 6778|       |        let pixels = vec![255u8; (width * height * 4) as usize];
 6779|       |        let mut out = Vec::new();
 6780|       |        let encoder = PngEncoder::new(&mut out);
 6781|       |        encoder
 6782|       |            .write_image(&pixels, width, height, ColorType::Rgba8)
 6783|       |            .expect("encode png");
 6784|       |        out
 6785|       |    }
 6786|       |
 6787|       |    /// Creates a test ImageCacheEntry with the given pixel size inside a with_test_ui context.
 6788|       |    fn make_test_cache_entry(size: [usize; 2]) -> ImageCacheEntry {
 6789|       |        let mut entry_slot = None;
 6790|       |        with_test_ui(|ctx, _ui| {
 6791|       |            let tex = ctx.load_texture(
 6792|       |                "test_entry",
 6793|       |                egui::ColorImage::new(size, Color32::WHITE),
 6794|       |                egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR,
 6795|       |            );
 6796|       |            entry_slot = Some(ImageCacheEntry {
 6797|       |                texture: tex,
 6798|       |                size: [size[0] as u32, size[1] as u32],
 6799|       |                modified: None,
 6800|       |                byte_size: ImageCacheEntry::estimate_bytes([size[0] as u32, size[1] as u32]),
 6801|       |                degraded: false,
 6802|       |            });
 6803|       |        });
 6804|       |        entry_slot.expect("texture")
 6805|       |    }
 6806|       |
 6807|       |    /// Creates an egui::RawInput with the specified screen dimensions.
 6808|       |    fn screen_input(width: f32, height: f32) -> egui::RawInput {
 6809|       |        egui::RawInput {
 6810|       |            screen_rect: Some(egui::Rect::from_min_size(
 6811|       |                egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0),
 6812|       |                egui::vec2(width, height),
 6813|       |            )),
 6814|       |            ..Default::default()
 6815|       |        }
 6816|       |    }

 6822|       |            .expect("rocket shortcode")
 6823|       |    }

 6825|       |    /// Asserts that inline code pipes were escaped (sentinel is present).
 6826|       |    fn assert_pipes_escaped(prepared: &str) {
 6827|       |        let expected = format!("`a{}b{}c`", PIPE_SENTINEL, PIPE_SENTINEL);
 6828|       |        assert!(
 6829|       |            prepared.contains(&expected),
 6830|       |            "expected pipe sentinel in: {}",
 6831|       |            prepared
 6832|       |        );
 6833|       |    }
 6834|       |
 6835|       |    /// Asserts that inline code pipes were NOT escaped (original backtick content preserved).
 6836|       |    fn assert_pipes_preserved(prepared: &str) {
 6837|       |        assert!(
 6838|       |            prepared.contains("`a|b|c`"),
 6839|       |            "expected preserved pipes in: {}",
 6840|       |            prepared
 6841|       |        );
 6842|       |        assert!(
 6843|       |            !prepared.contains(PIPE_SENTINEL),
 6844|       |            "unexpected pipe sentinel in: {}",
 6845|       |            prepared
 6846|       |        );
 6847|       |    }

 6881|       |    fn test_image_cache_insert_existing_key_updates_entry() {
 6882|       |        let mut cache = ImageCache::new(2);
 6883|       |        let entry = make_test_cache_entry([1, 1]);
 6884|       |        cache.insert("a".to_string(), entry);
 6885|       |
 6886|       |        let entry2 = make_test_cache_entry([2, 2]);
 6887|       |        cache.insert("a".to_string(), entry2);
 6888|       |
 6889|       |        let stored = cache.get("a").expect("stored");
 6890|       |        assert_eq!(stored.size, [2, 2]);
 6891|       |    }
