 6893|       |    #[test]
 6894|       |    fn test_image_cache_evicts_oldest_entry() {
 6895|       |        let mut cache = ImageCache::new(1);
 6896|       |        let entry = make_test_cache_entry([1, 1]);
 6897|       |        cache.insert("a".to_string(), entry.clone());
 6898|       |        cache.insert("b".to_string(), entry);
 6899|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key("b"));
 6900|       |        assert!(!cache.contains_key("a"));
 6901|       |    }
 6902|       |
 6903|       |    #[test]
 6904|       |    fn test_image_cache_tracks_memory_without_byte_eviction() {
 6905|       |        // insert() no longer evicts based on bytes — only enforce_budget() does.
 6906|       |        let mut cache = ImageCache::new(100);
 6907|       |        cache.max_bytes = 32; // 8 pixels worth (2x2 RGBA = 16 bytes each)
 6908|       |
 6909|       |        let entry = make_test_cache_entry([2, 2]);
 6910|       |
 6911|       |        // Insert first entry (16 bytes)
 6912|       |        cache.insert("a".to_string(), entry.clone());
 6913|       |        assert_eq!(cache.current_bytes(), 16);
 6914|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key("a"));
 6915|       |
 6916|       |        // Insert second entry (16 bytes) — fits within 32 byte limit
 6917|       |        cache.insert("b".to_string(), entry.clone());
 6918|       |        assert_eq!(cache.current_bytes(), 32);
 6919|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key("a"));
 6920|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key("b"));
 6921|       |
 6922|       |        // Insert third entry — does NOT evict despite exceeding max_bytes
 6923|       |        cache.insert("c".to_string(), entry);
 6924|       |        assert_eq!(cache.current_bytes(), 48);
 6925|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key("a"));
 6926|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key("b"));
 6927|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key("c"));
 6928|       |    }
 6929|       |
 6930|       |    #[test]
 6931|       |    fn test_image_cache_remove_decrements_bytes() {
 6932|       |        let mut cache = ImageCache::new(10);
 6933|       |        let entry = make_test_cache_entry([4, 4]);
 6934|       |        cache.insert("x".to_string(), entry);
 6935|       |        assert_eq!(cache.current_bytes(), 64);
 6936|       |
 6937|       |        cache.remove("x");
 6938|       |        assert_eq!(cache.current_bytes(), 0);
 6939|       |        assert!(!cache.contains_key("x"));
 6940|       |    }
 6941|       |
 6942|       |    #[test]
 6943|       |    fn test_image_cache_clear() {
 6944|       |        let mut cache = ImageCache::new(10);
 6945|       |        let entry1 = make_test_cache_entry([4, 4]);
 6946|       |        let entry2 = make_test_cache_entry([8, 8]);
 6947|       |        let entry3 = make_test_cache_entry([16, 16]);
 6948|       |
 6949|       |        cache.insert("a".to_string(), entry1);
 6950|       |        cache.insert("b".to_string(), entry2);
 6951|       |        cache.insert("c".to_string(), entry3);
 6952|       |
 6953|       |        // Verify entries exist and bytes are tracked
 6954|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key("a"));
 6955|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key("b"));
 6956|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key("c"));
 6957|       |        assert_eq!(cache.current_bytes(), 64 + 256 + 1024); // 4*4*4 + 8*8*4 + 16*16*4
 6958|       |
 6959|       |        cache.clear();
 6960|       |
 6961|       |        // Verify all entries removed and bytes zeroed
 6962|       |        assert!(!cache.contains_key("a"));
 6963|       |        assert!(!cache.contains_key("b"));
 6964|       |        assert!(!cache.contains_key("c"));
 6965|       |        assert_eq!(cache.current_bytes(), 0);
 6966|       |    }
 6967|       |
 6968|       |    // --- Viewport-aware degradation tests ---
 6969|       |
 6970|       |    #[test]
 6971|       |    fn test_image_cache_enforce_budget_degrades_furthest() {
 6972|       |        // Each 10x10 entry is 400 bytes. Budget fits 2 full entries (800 bytes).
 6973|       |        let mut cache = ImageCache::new(100);
 6974|       |        cache.max_bytes = 800;
 6975|       |
 6976|       |        with_test_ui(|ctx, _ui| {
 6977|       |            let make = |name: &str| {
 6978|       |                let tex = ctx.load_texture(
 6979|       |                    name,
 6980|       |                    egui::ColorImage::new([10, 10], Color32::WHITE),
 6981|       |                    egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR,
 6982|       |                );
 6983|       |                ImageCacheEntry {
 6984|       |                    texture: tex,
 6985|       |                    size: [10, 10],
 6986|       |                    modified: None,
 6987|       |                    byte_size: ImageCacheEntry::estimate_bytes([10, 10]), // 400
 6988|       |                    degraded: false,
 6989|       |                }
 6990|       |            };
 6991|       |
 6992|       |            cache.insert("near".to_string(), make("near"));
 6993|       |            cache.insert("mid".to_string(), make("mid"));
 6994|       |            cache.insert("far".to_string(), make("far"));
 6995|       |            assert_eq!(cache.current_bytes(), 1200); // over budget of 800
 6996|       |
 6997|       |            let mut distances = HashMap::new();
 6998|       |            distances.insert("near".to_string(), 100.0);
 6999|       |            distances.insert("mid".to_string(), 500.0);
 7000|       |            distances.insert("far".to_string(), 5000.0);
 7001|       |            cache.update_viewport_distances(distances);
 7002|       |
 7003|       |            cache.enforce_budget(ctx, 0.0);
 7004|       |
 7005|       |            // "far" should be degraded (furthest), others kept
 7006|       |            assert!(
 7007|       |                cache.entries.get("far").unwrap().degraded,
 7008|       |                "far should be degraded"
 7009|       |            );
 7010|       |            assert!(
 7011|       |                !cache.entries.get("near").unwrap().degraded,
 7012|       |                "near should survive"
 7013|       |            );
 7014|       |            // total_bytes should be back under budget (800 - 400 + 16 = 416 ≤ 800)
 7015|       |            assert!(cache.current_bytes() <= 800, "should be under budget");
 7016|       |        });
 7017|       |    }
 7018|       |
 7019|       |    #[test]
 7020|       |    fn test_image_cache_enforce_budget_noop_under_budget() {
 7021|       |        let mut cache = ImageCache::new(100);
 7022|       |        cache.max_bytes = 500;
 7023|       |
 7024|       |        with_test_ui(|ctx, _ui| {
 7025|       |            let tex = ctx.load_texture(
 7026|       |                "small",
 7027|       |                egui::ColorImage::new([10, 10], Color32::WHITE),
 7028|       |                egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR,
 7029|       |            );
 7030|       |            cache.insert(
 7031|       |                "a".to_string(),
 7032|       |                ImageCacheEntry {
 7033|       |                    texture: tex,
 7034|       |                    size: [10, 10],
 7035|       |                    modified: None,
 7036|       |                    byte_size: ImageCacheEntry::estimate_bytes([10, 10]), // 400
 7037|       |                    degraded: false,
 7038|       |                },
 7039|       |            );
 7040|       |
 7041|       |            let mut distances = HashMap::new();
 7042|       |            distances.insert("a".to_string(), 100.0);
 7043|       |            cache.update_viewport_distances(distances);
 7044|       |
 7045|       |            cache.enforce_budget(ctx, 0.0);
 7046|       |            assert!(!cache.entries.get("a").unwrap().degraded);
 7047|       |        });
 7048|       |    }
 7049|       |
 7050|       |    #[test]
 7051|       |    fn test_image_cache_enforce_budget_stops_when_under_budget() {
 7052|       |        // With 3 entries over budget, only enough should be degraded to get under.
 7053|       |        let mut cache = ImageCache::new(100);
 7054|       |        // Each 10x10 entry is 400 bytes. Budget fits 2 full entries (800 bytes).
 7055|       |        cache.max_bytes = 800;
 7056|       |
 7057|       |        with_test_ui(|ctx, _ui| {
 7058|       |            let make = |name: &str| {
 7059|       |                let tex = ctx.load_texture(
 7060|       |                    name,
 7061|       |                    egui::ColorImage::new([10, 10], Color32::WHITE),
 7062|       |                    egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR,
 7063|       |                );
 7064|       |                ImageCacheEntry {
 7065|       |                    texture: tex,
 7066|       |                    size: [10, 10],
 7067|       |                    modified: None,
 7068|       |                    byte_size: ImageCacheEntry::estimate_bytes([10, 10]), // 400
 7069|       |                    degraded: false,
 7070|       |                }
 7071|       |            };
 7072|       |
 7073|       |            cache.insert("a".to_string(), make("a"));
 7074|       |            cache.insert("b".to_string(), make("b"));
 7075|       |            cache.insert("c".to_string(), make("c"));
 7076|       |            assert_eq!(cache.current_bytes(), 1200);
 7077|       |
 7078|       |            let mut distances = HashMap::new();
 7079|       |            distances.insert("a".to_string(), 100.0); // nearest
 7080|       |            distances.insert("b".to_string(), 300.0);
 7081|       |            distances.insert("c".to_string(), 500.0); // furthest
 7082|       |            cache.update_viewport_distances(distances);
 7083|       |
 7084|       |            cache.enforce_budget(ctx, 0.0);
 7085|       |
 7086|       |            // 1200 - 400(c) + 16 = 816 > 800 → "c" degraded, still over budget.
 7087|       |            // 816 - 400(b) + 16 = 432 ≤ 800 → "b" also degraded, now under budget.
 7088|       |            assert!(
 7089|       |                cache.entries.get("c").unwrap().degraded,
 7090|       |                "c (furthest) should be degraded"
 7091|       |            );
 7092|       |            assert!(
 7093|       |                cache.entries.get("b").unwrap().degraded,
 7094|       |                "b (mid) should also be degraded"
 7095|       |            );
 7096|       |            assert!(
 7097|       |                !cache.entries.get("a").unwrap().degraded,
 7098|       |                "a (nearest) should survive"
 7099|       |            );
 7100|       |            assert!(cache.current_bytes() <= 800);
 7101|       |        });
 7102|       |    }
 7103|       |
 7104|       |    #[test]
 7105|       |    fn test_enforce_budget_degrades_orphan_entries_without_distances() {
 7106|       |        // Entries from a previous document have no viewport distances.
 7107|       |        // enforce_budget should degrade them as a fallback when still over budget.
 7108|       |        let mut cache = ImageCache::new(100);
 7109|       |        cache.max_bytes = 600; // fits 1 full entry (400 bytes) + margin
 7110|       |
 7111|       |        with_test_ui(|ctx, _ui| {
 7112|       |            let make = |name: &str| {
 7113|       |                let tex = ctx.load_texture(
 7114|       |                    name,
 7115|       |                    egui::ColorImage::new([10, 10], Color32::WHITE),
 7116|       |                    egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR,
 7117|       |                );
 7118|       |                ImageCacheEntry {
 7119|       |                    texture: tex,
 7120|       |                    size: [10, 10],
 7121|       |                    modified: None,
 7122|       |                    byte_size: ImageCacheEntry::estimate_bytes([10, 10]),
 7123|       |                    degraded: false,
 7124|       |                }
 7125|       |            };
 7126|       |
 7127|       |            // "old_a" and "old_b" are from a previous document (no viewport distances).
 7128|       |            cache.insert("old_a".to_string(), make("old_a"));
 7129|       |            cache.insert("old_b".to_string(), make("old_b"));
 7130|       |            // "current" is from the active document.
 7131|       |            cache.insert("current".to_string(), make("current"));
 7132|       |            assert_eq!(cache.current_bytes(), 1200);
 7133|       |
 7134|       |            // Only "current" has a viewport distance.
 7135|       |            let mut distances = HashMap::new();
 7136|       |            distances.insert("current".to_string(), 50.0);
 7137|       |            cache.update_viewport_distances(distances);
 7138|       |
 7139|       |            cache.enforce_budget(ctx, 0.0);
 7140|       |
 7141|       |            // "current" should survive (it's the only entry with a distance and
 7142|       |            // we keep at least one non-degraded). The orphans should be degraded.
 7143|       |            assert!(
 7144|       |                !cache.entries.get("current").unwrap().degraded,
 7145|       |                "current doc entry should survive"
 7146|       |            );
 7147|       |            // At least one orphan must be degraded to get under budget.
 7148|       |            let orphan_degraded = cache.entries.get("old_a").unwrap().degraded
 7149|       |                || cache.entries.get("old_b").unwrap().degraded;
 7150|       |            assert!(orphan_degraded, "at least one orphan should be degraded");
 7151|       |            assert!(cache.current_bytes() <= 600);
 7152|       |        });
 7153|       |    }
 7154|       |
 7155|       |    #[test]
 7156|       |    fn test_enforce_budget_phase3_degrades_far_to_make_room_for_near_degraded() {
 7157|       |        // Scenario: cache is under budget, but near-viewport degraded entries
 7158|       |        // can't restore because far non-degraded entries use the space.
 7159|       |        // Phase 3 should degrade the far entries to create headroom.
 7160|       |        let mut cache = ImageCache::new(256);
 7161|       |        cache.max_bytes = 1000;
 7162|       |
 7163|       |        with_test_ui(|ctx, _ui| {
 7164|       |            let make = |name: &str| {
 7165|       |                let tex = ctx.load_texture(
 7166|       |                    name,
 7167|       |                    egui::ColorImage::new([10, 10], Color32::WHITE),
 7168|       |                    egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR,
 7169|       |                );
 7170|       |                ImageCacheEntry {
 7171|       |                    texture: tex,
 7172|       |                    size: [10, 10],
 7173|       |                    modified: None,
 7174|       |                    byte_size: ImageCacheEntry::estimate_bytes([10, 10]), // 400
 7175|       |                    degraded: false,
 7176|       |                }
 7177|       |            };
 7178|       |
 7179|       |            // "near": degraded entry near viewport (distance=50, in reload zone).
 7180|       |            let mut near_entry = make("near");
 7181|       |            near_entry.byte_size = 16;
 7182|       |            near_entry.degraded = true;
 7183|       |            cache.insert("near".to_string(), near_entry);
 7184|       |
 7185|       |            // "far1" and "far2": full-res entries far from viewport.
 7186|       |            cache.insert("far1".to_string(), make("far1"));
 7187|       |            cache.insert("far2".to_string(), make("far2"));
 7188|       |
 7189|       |            // total_bytes = 16 + 400 + 400 = 816. Under max_bytes (1000).
 7190|       |            // But restoring "near" would need 816 - 16 + 400 = 1200 > 1000.
 7191|       |            assert!(
 7192|       |                !cache.would_restore_fit("near"),
 7193|       |                "near shouldn't fit before phase 3"
 7194|       |            );
 7195|       |
 7196|       |            let mut distances = HashMap::new();
 7197|       |            distances.insert("near".to_string(), 50.0);
 7198|       |            distances.insert("far1".to_string(), 5000.0);
 7199|       |            distances.insert("far2".to_string(), 6000.0);
 7200|       |            cache.update_viewport_distances(distances);
 7201|       |
 7202|       |            // viewport_height=600 → reload_zone=1200. "near" (50) in zone.
 7203|       |            // "far1" (5000) and "far2" (6000) outside zone.
 7204|       |            cache.enforce_budget(ctx, 600.0);
 7205|       |
 7206|       |            // Phase 3 should degrade far entries to make room for "near".
 7207|       |            assert!(
 7208|       |                cache.entries.get("far1").unwrap().degraded
 7209|       |                    || cache.entries.get("far2").unwrap().degraded,
 7210|       |                "at least one far entry should be degraded to make room"
 7211|       |            );
 7212|       |            assert!(
 7213|       |                cache.would_restore_fit("near"),
 7214|       |                "near should fit after phase 3"
 7215|       |            );
 7216|       |        });
 7217|       |    }
 7218|       |

 7220|       |    fn test_image_cache_clear_resets_viewport_distances() {
 7221|       |        let mut cache = ImageCache::new(10);

 7223|       |        cache.insert("a".to_string(), entry);
 7224|       |

 7231|       |    }

 7249|       |    #[test]
 7250|       |    fn test_degrade_reduces_bytes() {
 7251|       |        let mut cache = ImageCache::new(100);
 7252|       |        with_test_ui(|ctx, _ui| {
 7253|       |            let tex = ctx.load_texture(
 7254|       |                "big",
 7255|       |                egui::ColorImage::new([10, 10], Color32::WHITE),
 7256|       |                egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR,
 7257|       |            );
 7258|       |            cache.insert(
 7259|       |                "img".to_string(),
 7260|       |                ImageCacheEntry {
 7261|       |                    texture: tex,
 7262|       |                    size: [10, 10],
 7263|       |                    modified: None,
 7264|       |                    byte_size: ImageCacheEntry::estimate_bytes([10, 10]), // 400
 7265|       |                    degraded: false,
 7266|       |                },
 7267|       |            );
 7268|       |            assert_eq!(cache.current_bytes(), 400);
 7269|       |
 7270|       |            cache.degrade("img", ctx);
 7271|       |
 7272|       |            let entry = cache.entries.get("img").unwrap();
 7273|       |            assert!(entry.degraded);
 7274|       |            assert_eq!(entry.byte_size, 16); // 2*2*4
 7275|       |            assert_eq!(cache.current_bytes(), 16);
 7276|       |        });
 7277|       |    }

 7280|       |    fn test_degrade_preserves_size() {
 7281|       |        let mut cache = ImageCache::new(100);
 7282|       |        with_test_ui(|ctx, _ui| {
 7283|       |            let tex = ctx.load_texture(
 7284|       |                "sized",

 7295|       |                    degraded: false,
 7296|       |                },
 7297|       |            );

 7351|       |    #[test]
 7352|       |    fn test_insert_no_byte_eviction() {
 7353|       |        // Verify that inserting when over max_bytes does NOT evict.
 7354|       |        let mut cache = ImageCache::new(100);
 7355|       |        cache.max_bytes = 16;
 7356|       |
 7357|       |        let entry = make_test_cache_entry([2, 2]); // 16 bytes
 7358|       |        cache.insert("a".to_string(), entry.clone());
 7359|       |        cache.insert("b".to_string(), entry);
 7360|       |        // Both entries should coexist even though total (32) > max_bytes (16)
 7361|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key("a"));
 7362|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key("b"));
 7363|       |        assert_eq!(cache.current_bytes(), 32);
 7364|       |    }

 7366|       |    #[test]
 7367|       |    fn test_insert_count_limit_still_works() {
 7368|       |        let mut cache = ImageCache::new(2);
 7369|       |        let entry = make_test_cache_entry([2, 2]);
 7370|       |
 7371|       |        cache.insert("a".to_string(), entry.clone());
 7372|       |        cache.insert("b".to_string(), entry.clone());
 7373|       |        cache.insert("c".to_string(), entry);
 7374|       |
 7375|       |        // "a" should be evicted (oldest by insertion order)
 7376|       |        assert!(!cache.contains_key("a"));
 7377|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key("b"));
 7378|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key("c"));
 7379|       |        assert_eq!(cache.entries.len(), 2);
 7380|       |    }
 7381|       |
 7382|       |    #[test]
 7383|       |    fn test_would_restore_fit() {
 7384|       |        let mut cache = ImageCache::new(100);
 7385|       |        // Budget of 400 bytes
 7386|       |        cache.max_bytes = 400;
 7387|       |
 7388|       |        with_test_ui(|ctx, _ui| {
 7389|       |            let tex = ctx.load_texture(
 7390|       |                "restorable",
 7391|       |                egui::ColorImage::new([10, 10], Color32::WHITE),
 7392|       |                egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR,
 7393|       |            );
 7394|       |            cache.insert(
 7395|       |                "img".to_string(),
 7396|       |                ImageCacheEntry {
 7397|       |                    texture: tex,
 7398|       |                    size: [10, 10],
 7399|       |                    modified: None,
 7400|       |                    byte_size: ImageCacheEntry::estimate_bytes([10, 10]), // 400
 7401|       |                    degraded: false,
 7402|       |                },
 7403|       |            );
 7404|       |            cache.degrade("img", ctx);
 7405|       |            // Now 16 bytes used. Restoring would use 400. Budget is 400. Should fit.
 7406|       |            assert!(cache.would_restore_fit("img"));
 7407|       |
 7408|       |            // Shrink budget so restore wouldn't fit
 7409|       |            cache.max_bytes = 200;
 7410|       |            assert!(!cache.would_restore_fit("img"));
 7411|       |
 7412|       |            // Non-degraded entry returns false
 7413|       |            let tex2 = ctx.load_texture(
 7414|       |                "full",
 7415|       |                egui::ColorImage::new([2, 2], Color32::WHITE),
 7416|       |                egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR,
 7417|       |            );
 7418|       |            cache.insert(
 7419|       |                "full".to_string(),
 7420|       |                ImageCacheEntry {
 7421|       |                    texture: tex2,
 7422|       |                    size: [2, 2],
 7423|       |                    modified: None,
 7424|       |                    byte_size: 16,
 7425|       |                    degraded: false,
 7426|       |                },
 7427|       |            );
 7428|       |            assert!(!cache.would_restore_fit("full"));
 7429|       |
 7430|       |            // Missing key returns false
 7431|       |            assert!(!cache.would_restore_fit("missing"));
 7432|       |        });
 7433|       |    }
 7434|       |
 7435|       |    #[test]
 7436|       |    fn test_would_restore_fit_accounts_for_pending_bytes() {
 7437|       |        let mut cache = ImageCache::new(100);
 7438|       |        // Budget of 800 bytes. Two 10x10 images (400 bytes each).
 7439|       |        cache.max_bytes = 800;
 7440|       |
 7441|       |        with_test_ui(|ctx, _ui| {
 7442|       |            // Insert and degrade two images.
 7443|       |            for name in &["img_a", "img_b"] {
 7444|       |                let tex = ctx.load_texture(
 7445|       |                    *name,
 7446|       |                    egui::ColorImage::new([10, 10], Color32::WHITE),
 7447|       |                    egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR,
 7448|       |                );
 7449|       |                cache.insert(
 7450|       |                    name.to_string(),
 7451|       |                    ImageCacheEntry {
 7452|       |                        texture: tex,
 7453|       |                        size: [10, 10],
 7454|       |                        modified: None,
 7455|       |                        byte_size: ImageCacheEntry::estimate_bytes([10, 10]), // 400
 7456|       |                        degraded: false,
 7457|       |                    },
 7458|       |                );
 7459|       |                cache.degrade(name, ctx);
 7460|       |            }
 7461|       |            // Both degraded: total_bytes = 32 (two 2x2 placeholders).
 7462|       |            // Restoring either one: 32 - 16 + 400 = 416 ≤ 800. Fits.
 7463|       |            assert!(cache.would_restore_fit("img_a"));
 7464|       |            assert!(cache.would_restore_fit("img_b"));
 7465|       |
 7466|       |            // Simulate enqueuing img_a's restore: pending_restore_bytes = 400.
 7467|       |            cache.pending_restore_bytes = ImageCacheEntry::estimate_bytes([10, 10]);
 7468|       |            // Now img_b: 32 - 16 + 400 + 400 = 816 > 800. Doesn't fit.
 7469|       |            assert!(!cache.would_restore_fit("img_b"));
 7470|       |            // img_a still individually fits (its own pending is already counted,
 7471|       |            // but that's correct — the check is conservative).
 7472|       |            // 32 - 16 + 400 + 400 = 816 > 800. Also blocked.
 7473|       |            assert!(!cache.would_restore_fit("img_a"));
 7474|       |
 7475|       |            // Increase budget so both fit even with pending.
 7476|       |            cache.max_bytes = 900;
 7477|       |            // 32 - 16 + 400 + 400 = 816 ≤ 900. Fits.
 7478|       |            assert!(cache.would_restore_fit("img_b"));
 7479|       |        });
 7480|       |    }
 7481|       |

 7483|       |    fn test_pending_restore_bytes_cleared_by_clear() {

 7485|       |        cache.pending_restore_bytes = 12345;
 7486|       |        cache.clear();
 7487|       |        assert_eq!(cache.pending_restore_bytes, 0);
 7488|       |    }

 7494|       |        renderer.image_textures.borrow_mut().pending_restore_bytes = 99999;

 7500|       |    fn test_pending_restore_bytes_initialized_to_zero() {
 7501|       |        let cache = ImageCache::new(100);
 7502|       |        assert_eq!(cache.pending_restore_bytes, 0);
 7503|       |    }
 7504|       |
 7505|       |    #[test]
 7506|       |    fn test_get_no_touch() {
 7507|       |        // Verify get() doesn't reorder `order` — it's now a pure lookup.
 7508|       |        let mut cache = ImageCache::new(3);
 7509|       |        let entry = make_test_cache_entry([2, 2]);
 7510|       |
 7511|       |        cache.insert("a".to_string(), entry.clone());
 7512|       |        cache.insert("b".to_string(), entry.clone());
 7513|       |        cache.insert("c".to_string(), entry.clone());
 7514|       |
 7515|       |        // Order should be [a, b, c]
 7516|       |        assert_eq!(cache.order[0], "a");
 7517|       |        assert_eq!(cache.order[1], "b");
 7518|       |        assert_eq!(cache.order[2], "c");
 7519|       |
 7520|       |        // get("a") should NOT move "a" to the back
 7521|       |        let _ = cache.get("a");
 7522|       |        assert_eq!(cache.order[0], "a", "get() must not reorder");
 7523|       |        assert_eq!(cache.order[1], "b");
 7524|       |        assert_eq!(cache.order[2], "c");
 7525|       |
 7526|       |        // Insert "d" — should evict "a" (front of order), proving order wasn't touched
 7527|       |        cache.insert("d".to_string(), entry);
 7528|       |        assert!(!cache.contains_key("a"), "a should be evicted (oldest)");
 7529|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key("b"));
 7530|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key("c"));
 7531|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key("d"));
 7532|       |    }
 7533|       |
 7534|       |    // --- Degraded get_or_load tests (LOD Stage 2) ---
 7535|       |
 7536|       |    #[test]
 7537|       |    fn test_get_or_load_returns_degraded_texture_with_original_size() {
 7538|       |        // A degraded entry should still return Some with the original display
 7539|       |        // dimensions, even though the actual texture is 2x2.
 7540|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 7541|       |        with_test_ui(|ctx, ui| {
 7542|       |            let tex = ctx.load_texture(
 7543|       |                "full-res",
 7544|       |                egui::ColorImage::new([100, 80], Color32::WHITE),
 7545|       |                egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR,
 7546|       |            );
 7547|       |            renderer.image_textures.borrow_mut().insert(
 7548|       |                "assets/emoji/1f600.png".to_string(),
 7549|       |                ImageCacheEntry {
 7550|       |                    texture: tex,
 7551|       |                    size: [100, 80],
 7552|       |                    modified: None,
 7553|       |                    byte_size: ImageCacheEntry::estimate_bytes([100, 80]),
 7554|       |                    degraded: false,
 7555|       |                },
 7556|       |            );
 7557|       |            // Degrade the entry — texture becomes 2x2 but size stays [100, 80].
 7558|       |            renderer
 7559|       |                .image_textures
 7560|       |                .borrow_mut()
 7561|       |                .degrade("assets/emoji/1f600.png", ctx);
 7562|       |
 7563|       |            let result = renderer.get_or_load_image_texture(ui, "assets/emoji/1f600.png");
 7564|       |            assert!(result.is_some(), "degraded entry should still return Some");
 7565|       |            let (_tex, w, h) = result.unwrap();
 7566|       |            assert_eq!(w, 100, "width should be original display width");
 7567|       |            assert_eq!(h, 80, "height should be original display height");
 7568|       |        });
 7569|       |    }
 7570|       |
 7571|       |    #[test]
 7572|       |    fn test_degraded_entry_not_removed_when_stale() -> Result<()> {
 7573|       |        // When a degraded entry's source file changes on disk (stale), the
 7574|       |        // entry must NOT be removed — it stays in the cache as a layout
 7575|       |        // placeholder while a reload is enqueued.
 7576|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 7577|       |        let temp = tempdir()?;
 7578|       |        let image_path = temp.path().join("image.png");
 7579|       |        std::fs::write(&image_path, tiny_png_bytes())?;
 7580|       |        let resolved = image_path.to_string_lossy().to_string();
 7581|       |
 7582|       |        with_test_ui(|ctx, ui| {
 7583|       |            let tex = ctx.load_texture(
 7584|       |                "degraded-stale",
 7585|       |                egui::ColorImage::new([2, 2], Color32::from_rgb(128, 128, 128)),
 7586|       |                egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR,
 7587|       |            );
 7588|       |            renderer.image_textures.borrow_mut().insert(
 7589|       |                resolved.clone(),
 7590|       |                ImageCacheEntry {
 7591|       |                    texture: tex,
 7592|       |                    size: [50, 40],
 7593|       |                    // Use UNIX_EPOCH to ensure it's detected as stale
 7594|       |                    modified: Some(SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH),
 7595|       |                    byte_size: 2 * 2 * 4,
 7596|       |                    degraded: true,
 7597|       |                },
 7598|       |            );
 7599|       |
 7600|       |            let result = renderer.get_or_load_image_texture(ui, &resolved);
 7601|       |            // Should still return the degraded texture (layout preservation).
 7602|       |            assert!(result.is_some(), "degraded stale entry should return Some");
 7603|       |            let (_tex, w, h) = result.unwrap();
 7604|       |            assert_eq!(w, 50);
 7605|       |            assert_eq!(h, 40);
 7606|       |            // Entry must NOT have been removed from the cache.
 7607|       |            assert!(
 7608|       |                renderer.image_textures.borrow().contains_key(&resolved),
 7609|       |                "degraded stale entry must not be removed from cache"
 7610|       |            );
 7611|       |        });
 7612|       |        Ok(())
 7613|       |    }
 7614|       |
 7615|       |    // --- Generation token tests ---
 7616|       |
 7617|       |    #[test]
 7618|       |    fn test_stale_generation_result_is_discarded() {
 7619|       |        let mut renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 7620|       |        let (result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::unbounded::<ImageLoadResult>();
 7621|       |        renderer.image_result_rx = result_rx;
 7622|       |
 7623|       |        // Simulate: gen 0 enqueued an image, then document was switched (gen → 1)
 7624|       |        renderer
 7625|       |            .image_pending
 7626|       |            .borrow_mut()
 7627|       |            .insert("old.png".to_string());
 7628|       |        renderer.reset_image_state(); // bumps to gen 1
 7629|       |
 7630|       |        // Stale result from gen 0 arrives
 7631|       |        result_tx
 7632|       |            .send(ImageLoadResult::Failed {
 7633|       |                key: "old.png".to_string(),
 7634|       |                generation: 0,
 7635|       |            })
 7636|       |            .expect("send");
 7637|       |
 7638|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 7639|       |        renderer.poll_image_results(&ctx);
 7640|       |
 7641|       |        // The stale result should NOT register as a failure in the current gen
 7642|       |        assert!(
 7643|       |            !renderer
 7644|       |                .image_failures
 7645|       |                .borrow()
 7646|       |                .contains_key(&"old.png".to_string()),
 7647|       |            "stale generation result should be discarded"
 7648|       |        );
 7649|       |    }
 7650|       |
 7651|       |    #[test]
 7652|       |    fn test_current_generation_result_is_accepted() {
 7653|       |        let mut renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 7654|       |        let (result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::unbounded::<ImageLoadResult>();
 7655|       |        renderer.image_result_rx = result_rx;
 7656|       |
 7657|       |        renderer
 7658|       |            .image_pending
 7659|       |            .borrow_mut()
 7660|       |            .insert("current.png".to_string());
 7661|       |
 7662|       |        // Result from current gen (0)
 7663|       |        result_tx
 7664|       |            .send(ImageLoadResult::Failed {
 7665|       |                key: "current.png".to_string(),
 7666|       |                generation: 0,
 7667|       |            })
 7668|       |            .expect("send");
 7669|       |
 7670|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 7671|       |        renderer.poll_image_results(&ctx);
 7672|       |
 7673|       |        assert!(
 7674|       |            renderer
 7675|       |                .image_failures
 7676|       |                .borrow()
 7677|       |                .contains_key(&"current.png".to_string()),
 7678|       |            "current generation result should be processed"
 7679|       |        );
 7680|       |    }

 7683|       |
 7684|       |    #[test]
 7685|       |    fn test_downscale_color_image_no_op_when_within_limits() {
 7686|       |        let img = egui::ColorImage::new([100, 80], egui::Color32::RED);
 7687|       |        let (out, w, h) = downscale_color_image(img, 200, 200);
 7688|       |        assert_eq!(w, 100);
 7689|       |        assert_eq!(h, 80);
 7690|       |        assert_eq!(out.size, [100, 80]);
 7691|       |    }
 7692|       |
 7693|       |    #[test]
 7694|       |    fn test_downscale_color_image_shrinks_oversized() {
 7695|       |        let img = egui::ColorImage::new([1000, 500], egui::Color32::BLUE);
 7696|       |        let (out, w, h) = downscale_color_image(img, 200, 200);
 7697|       |        // Scale factor: min(200/1000, 200/500) = 0.2
 7698|       |        // 1000 * 0.2 = 200, 500 * 0.2 = 100
 7699|       |        assert_eq!(w, 200);
 7700|       |        assert_eq!(h, 100);
 7701|       |        assert_eq!(out.size, [200, 100]);
 7702|       |    }
 7703|       |
 7704|       |    #[test]
 7705|       |    fn test_downscale_color_image_preserves_aspect_ratio() {
 7706|       |        let img = egui::ColorImage::new([800, 200], egui::Color32::GREEN);
 7707|       |        let (_, w, h) = downscale_color_image(img, 400, 400);
 7708|       |        // Scale = min(400/800, 400/200) = 0.5
 7709|       |        assert_eq!(w, 400);
 7710|       |        assert_eq!(h, 100);
 7711|       |    }
 7712|       |
 7713|       |    #[test]
 7714|       |    fn test_downscale_color_image_exact_boundary() {

 7716|       |        let (_, w, h) = downscale_color_image(img, 200, 200);
 7717|       |        assert_eq!(w, 200);
 7718|       |        assert_eq!(h, 200);
 7719|       |    }

 7721|       |    // --- LruCache Tests ---
 7722|       |
 7723|       |    #[test]
 7724|       |    fn test_lru_cache_basic_insert_and_get() {
 7725|       |        let mut cache: LruCache<String, i32> = LruCache::new(10);
 7726|       |        cache.insert("a".to_string(), 1);
 7727|       |        cache.insert("b".to_string(), 2);
 7728|       |        cache.insert("c".to_string(), 3);
 7729|       |
 7730|       |        assert_eq!(cache.get(&"a".to_string()), Some(1));
 7731|       |        assert_eq!(cache.get(&"b".to_string()), Some(2));
 7732|       |        assert_eq!(cache.get(&"c".to_string()), Some(3));
 7733|       |        assert_eq!(cache.get(&"d".to_string()), None);
 7734|       |        assert_eq!(cache.len(), 3);
 7735|       |    }
 7736|       |
 7737|       |    #[test]
 7738|       |    fn test_lru_cache_evicts_oldest_entry() {
 7739|       |        let mut cache: LruCache<String, i32> = LruCache::new(2);
 7740|       |        cache.insert("a".to_string(), 1);
 7741|       |        cache.insert("b".to_string(), 2);
 7742|       |        // Cache is now full at capacity 2
 7743|       |
 7744|       |        // Insert third entry, should evict "a" (oldest)
 7745|       |        cache.insert("c".to_string(), 3);
 7746|       |
 7747|       |        assert!(!cache.contains_key(&"a".to_string())); // "a" evicted
 7748|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key(&"b".to_string()));
 7749|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key(&"c".to_string()));
 7750|       |        assert_eq!(cache.len(), 2);
 7751|       |    }
 7752|       |
 7753|       |    #[test]
 7754|       |    fn test_lru_cache_touch_updates_order() {
 7755|       |        let mut cache: LruCache<String, i32> = LruCache::new(2);
 7756|       |        cache.insert("a".to_string(), 1);
 7757|       |        cache.insert("b".to_string(), 2);
 7758|       |
 7759|       |        // Touch "a" to make it recently used
 7760|       |        cache.touch(&"a".to_string());
 7761|       |
 7762|       |        // Insert third entry, should evict "b" now (oldest after touch)
 7763|       |        cache.insert("c".to_string(), 3);
 7764|       |
 7765|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key(&"a".to_string())); // "a" preserved by touch
 7766|       |        assert!(!cache.contains_key(&"b".to_string())); // "b" evicted
 7767|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key(&"c".to_string()));
 7768|       |    }
 7769|       |
 7770|       |    #[test]
 7771|       |    fn test_lru_cache_insert_existing_updates_value_and_order() {
 7772|       |        let mut cache: LruCache<String, i32> = LruCache::new(2);
 7773|       |        cache.insert("a".to_string(), 1);
 7774|       |        cache.insert("b".to_string(), 2);
 7775|       |
 7776|       |        // Re-insert "a" with new value
 7777|       |        cache.insert("a".to_string(), 100);
 7778|       |
 7779|       |        assert_eq!(cache.get(&"a".to_string()), Some(100));
 7780|       |
 7781|       |        // Insert third entry, should evict "b" (oldest after "a" was updated)
 7782|       |        cache.insert("c".to_string(), 3);
 7783|       |
 7784|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key(&"a".to_string()));
 7785|       |        assert!(!cache.contains_key(&"b".to_string())); // "b" evicted
 7786|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key(&"c".to_string()));
 7787|       |    }
 7788|       |
 7789|       |    #[test]
 7790|       |    fn test_lru_cache_remove() {
 7791|       |        let mut cache: LruCache<String, i32> = LruCache::new(10);
 7792|       |        cache.insert("a".to_string(), 1);
 7793|       |        cache.insert("b".to_string(), 2);
 7794|       |
 7795|       |        cache.remove(&"a".to_string());
 7796|       |
 7797|       |        assert!(!cache.contains_key(&"a".to_string()));
 7798|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key(&"b".to_string()));
 7799|       |        assert_eq!(cache.len(), 1);
 7800|       |
 7801|       |        // Removing non-existent key should be safe
 7802|       |        cache.remove(&"nonexistent".to_string());
 7803|       |        assert_eq!(cache.len(), 1);
 7804|       |    }
 7805|       |
 7806|       |    #[test]
 7807|       |    fn test_lru_cache_clear() {
 7808|       |        let mut cache: LruCache<String, i32> = LruCache::new(10);
 7809|       |        cache.insert("a".to_string(), 1);
 7810|       |        cache.insert("b".to_string(), 2);
 7811|       |        cache.insert("c".to_string(), 3);
 7812|       |
 7813|       |        cache.clear();
 7814|       |
 7815|       |        assert_eq!(cache.len(), 0);
 7816|       |        assert!(!cache.contains_key(&"a".to_string()));
 7817|       |        assert!(!cache.contains_key(&"b".to_string()));
 7818|       |        assert!(!cache.contains_key(&"c".to_string()));
 7819|       |    }
 7820|       |
 7821|       |    #[test]
 7822|       |    fn test_lru_cache_capacity_enforcement() {
 7823|       |        let mut cache: LruCache<String, i32> = LruCache::new(3);
 7824|       |        for i in 0..10 {
 7825|       |            cache.insert(format!("key{}", i), i);
 7826|       |        }
 7827|       |
 7828|       |        // Only last 3 entries should remain
 7829|       |        assert_eq!(cache.len(), 3);
 7830|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key(&"key7".to_string()));
 7831|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key(&"key8".to_string()));
 7832|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key(&"key9".to_string()));
 7833|       |        assert!(!cache.contains_key(&"key0".to_string()));
 7834|       |        assert!(!cache.contains_key(&"key6".to_string()));
 7835|       |    }
 7836|       |
 7837|       |    #[test]
 7838|       |    fn test_lru_cache_touch_nonexistent_key() {
 7839|       |        let mut cache: LruCache<String, i32> = LruCache::new(10);
 7840|       |        cache.insert("a".to_string(), 1);
 7841|       |
 7842|       |        // Touching non-existent key should be safe (no-op)
 7843|       |        cache.touch(&"nonexistent".to_string());
 7844|       |
 7845|       |        assert_eq!(cache.len(), 1);
 7846|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key(&"a".to_string()));
 7847|       |    }
 7848|       |
 7849|       |    #[test]
 7850|       |    fn test_lru_cache_zero_capacity() {
 7851|       |        let mut cache: LruCache<String, i32> = LruCache::new(0);
 7852|       |        cache.insert("a".to_string(), 1);
 7853|       |
 7854|       |        // With zero capacity, nothing should be stored
 7855|       |        assert_eq!(cache.len(), 0);
 7856|       |        assert!(!cache.contains_key(&"a".to_string()));
 7857|       |    }

 7932|       |    #[test]
 7933|       |    fn test_extend_table_rect_handles_nan() {
 7934|       |        let mut target = Some(egui::Rect::from_min_size(
 7935|       |            egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0),
 7936|       |            egui::vec2(10.0, 10.0),
 7937|       |        ));
 7938|       |        let nan_rect = egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(f32::NAN, 0.0), egui::vec2(1.0, 1.0));
 7939|       |        MarkdownRenderer::extend_table_rect(&mut target, nan_rect);
 7940|       |        let stored = target.expect("target");
 7941|       |        assert!(!stored.min.x.is_nan());
 7942|       |
 7943|       |        let mut target_none = None;
 7944|       |        MarkdownRenderer::extend_table_rect(
 7945|       |            &mut target_none,
 7946|       |            egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(5.0, 6.0), egui::vec2(2.0, 2.0)),
 7947|       |        );
 7948|       |        assert!(target_none.is_some());
 7949|       |    }

 7952|       |    fn test_extend_table_rect_rejects_nan_components() {
 7953|       |        let base = egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(1.0, 2.0), egui::vec2(3.0, 4.0));
 7954|       |        let mut target = Some(base);
 7955|       |        let nan_min_y = egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(0.0, f32::NAN), egui::vec2(1.0, 1.0));
 7956|       |        MarkdownRenderer::extend_table_rect(&mut target, nan_min_y);
 7957|       |        assert_eq!(target, Some(base));
 7958|       |
 7959|       |        let mut target = Some(base);
 7960|       |        let nan_max_x = egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0), egui::vec2(f32::NAN, 1.0));
 7961|       |        MarkdownRenderer::extend_table_rect(&mut target, nan_max_x);
 7962|       |        assert_eq!(target, Some(base));
 7963|       |
 7964|       |        let mut target = Some(base);
 7965|       |        let nan_max_y = egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0), egui::vec2(1.0, f32::NAN));
 7966|       |        MarkdownRenderer::extend_table_rect(&mut target, nan_max_y);

 7986|       |    #[test]
 7987|       |    fn test_link_at_pointer_outside_rect_returns_none() {
 7988|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 7989|       |        let spans = vec![InlineSpan::Link {
 7990|       |            text: "Link".to_string(),
 7991|       |            url: "https://example.com".to_string(),
 7992|       |        }];
 7993|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 7994|       |        let mut input = screen_input(320.0, 240.0);
 7995|       |        input
 7996|       |            .events
 7997|       |            .push(egui::Event::PointerMoved(egui::pos2(200.0, 200.0)));
 7998|       |        ctx.begin_frame(input);
 7999|       |        let mut found = false;
 8000|       |        egui::CentralPanel::default().show(&ctx, |ui| {
 8001|       |            let response = ui.label("anchor");
 8002|       |            let build = renderer.build_layout_job(ui.style(), &spans, 200.0, false, Align::LEFT);
 8003|       |            let galley = ui.fonts(|f| f.layout_job(build.job.clone()));
 8004|       |            found = renderer
 8005|       |                .link_at_pointer(&response, &galley, &build, egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0))
 8006|       |                .is_some();
 8007|       |        });
 8008|       |        let _ = ctx.end_frame();
 8009|       |        assert!(!found);
 8010|       |    }

 8012|       |    #[test]
 8013|       |    fn test_spawn_image_loader_missing_file_reports_failed() {
 8014|       |        let (job_tx, job_rx) = bounded(1);
 8015|       |        let (result_tx, result_rx) = bounded(1);
 8016|       |        MarkdownRenderer::spawn_image_loader(job_rx, result_tx);
 8017|       |
 8018|       |        let dir = tempdir().expect("temp dir");
 8019|       |        let path = dir.path().join("missing.png");
 8020|       |        job_tx
 8021|       |            .send(ImageLoadRequest {
 8022|       |                key: "missing".to_string(),
 8023|       |                source: ImageLoadSource::File(path),
 8024|       |                generation: 0,
 8025|       |            })
 8026|       |            .expect("send");
 8027|       |        let result = result_rx
 8028|       |            .recv_timeout(Duration::from_secs(3))
 8029|       |            .expect("result");
 8030|       |        assert!(matches!(
 8031|       |            result,
 8032|       |            ImageLoadResult::Failed { ref key, .. } if key == "missing"
 8033|       |        ));
 8034|       |    }
 8035|       |

 8130|       |    fn test_fence_helpers_cover_branches() {
 8131|       |        assert_eq!(MarkdownRenderer::fence_start("```"), Some(('`', 3)));
 8132|       |        assert_eq!(MarkdownRenderer::fence_start("``"), None);
 8133|       |        assert_eq!(MarkdownRenderer::fence_start("~~~"), Some(('~', 3)));
 8134|       |        assert!(MarkdownRenderer::is_fence_end("```", '`', 3));
 8135|       |        assert!(!MarkdownRenderer::is_fence_end("``", '`', 3));
 8136|       |        assert!(MarkdownRenderer::is_fence_end("   ```", '`', 3));
 8137|       |    }

 8185|       |        renderer.image_failures.borrow_mut().insert(
 8186|       |            "recent".to_string(),
 8187|       |            ImageFailure {
 8188|       |                last_attempt: std::time::Instant::now(),
 8189|       |            },
 8190|       |        );
 8191|       |        assert!(!renderer.should_retry_image("recent"));
 8192|       |
 8193|       |        renderer.image_failures.borrow_mut().insert(
 8194|       |            "old".to_string(),
 8195|       |            ImageFailure {
 8196|       |                last_attempt: std::time::Instant::now()
 8197|       |                    - IMAGE_FAILURE_BACKOFF
 8198|       |                    - Duration::from_millis(1),
 8199|       |            },
 8200|       |        );

 8202|       |    }

 8205|       |    fn test_extend_table_rect_accepts_valid_rect() {
 8206|       |        let mut target = None;
 8207|       |        let rect = egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(1.0, 2.0), egui::vec2(3.0, 4.0));
 8208|       |        MarkdownRenderer::extend_table_rect(&mut target, rect);
 8209|       |        assert_eq!(target, Some(rect));
 8210|       |
 8211|       |        let rect2 = egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0), egui::vec2(10.0, 10.0));
 8212|       |        MarkdownRenderer::extend_table_rect(&mut target, rect2);
 8213|       |        let merged = target.expect("merged rect");
 8214|       |        assert!(merged.contains(rect.min));
 8215|       |        assert!(merged.contains(rect2.max));
 8216|       |    }

 8263|       |    #[test]
 8264|       |    fn test_superscript_expansion_single_caret() {
 8265|       |        // Test that single carets (not paired) are left as-is
 8266|       |        let s = "2^32 = 4,294,967,296";
 8267|       |        let out = MarkdownRenderer::expand_superscripts(s);
 8268|       |        assert_eq!(out, "2^32 = 4,294,967,296");
 8269|       |
 8270|       |        // Test the problematic line from the bug report
 8271|       |        let problematic = "A 32-bit address bus would provide 2^32 = 4,294,967,296 bytes (4 GB) of addressable memory.";
 8272|       |        let fixed = MarkdownRenderer::expand_superscripts(problematic);
 8273|       |        assert_eq!(fixed, problematic);
 8274|       |
 8275|       |        // Test mixed cases
 8276|       |        let mixed = "Use 2^32 for math and 5^th^ for ordinal";
 8277|       |        let result = MarkdownRenderer::expand_superscripts(mixed);
 8278|       |        assert!(result.contains("2^32"));
 8279|       |        assert!(result.contains("5ᵗʰ"));
 8280|       |    }

 8283|       |    fn test_superscript_expansion_rejects_invalid_sequences() {

 8286|       |        assert_eq!(out, s);
 8287|       |        let no_close = "10^abc";
 8288|       |        let out = MarkdownRenderer::expand_superscripts(no_close);
 8289|       |        assert_eq!(out, no_close);
 8290|       |    }

 8301|       |    fn test_zoom_functionality() {
 8302|       |        let mut renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 8303|       |        let original_body = renderer.font_sizes.body;
 8304|       |
 8305|       |        renderer.zoom_in();
 8306|       |        assert!(renderer.font_sizes.body > original_body);
 8307|       |
 8308|       |        renderer.zoom_out();
 8309|       |        assert!(renderer.font_sizes.body < original_body * 1.1);
 8310|       |
 8311|       |        renderer.reset_zoom();
 8312|       |        assert_eq!(renderer.font_sizes.body, original_body);
 8313|       |    }

 8316|       |    fn test_set_zoom_scale() {

 8320|       |        assert!((renderer.font_sizes.body - 21.0).abs() < 0.1);
 8321|       |
 8322|       |        renderer.set_zoom_scale(10.0);
 8323|       |        assert_eq!(renderer.font_sizes.body, 32.0);

 8329|       |    #[test]
 8330|       |    fn font_size_change_does_not_repersist_stale_widths() {
 8331|       |        let mut renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 8332|       |        let table_id = 7u64;
 8333|       |        let specs = vec![resizable_spec(0, "A", 20.0, 100.0)];
 8334|       |        let widths = vec![100.0f32];
 8335|       |
 8336|       |        // Initial persist at default font size.
 8337|       |        renderer.persist_resizable_widths(table_id, &specs, &widths);
 8338|       |        let policy_hash = specs[0].policy_hash;
 8339|       |        {
 8340|       |            let metrics = renderer.table_metrics.borrow();
 8341|       |            let entry = metrics.entry(table_id).expect("entry created");
 8342|       |            assert_eq!(entry.persisted_width(policy_hash), Some(100.0));
 8343|       |            assert_eq!(entry.persisted_font_size, Some(renderer.font_sizes.body));
 8344|       |        }
 8345|       |
 8346|       |        // Simulate zoom: font size changes but widths captured are still the old ones.
 8347|       |        renderer.font_sizes.body = 16.0;
 8348|       |        renderer.persist_resizable_widths(table_id, &specs, &widths);
 8349|       |        {
 8350|       |            let metrics = renderer.table_metrics.borrow();
 8351|       |            let entry = metrics.entry(table_id).expect("entry exists");
 8352|       |            // Cleared and NOT re-saved in the same frame.
 8353|       |            assert_eq!(entry.persisted_width(policy_hash), None);
 8354|       |            assert_eq!(entry.persisted_font_size, Some(16.0));
 8355|       |        }
 8356|       |
 8357|       |        // Next frame with new layout widths should persist again.
 8358|       |        let new_widths = vec![80.0f32];
 8359|       |        renderer.persist_resizable_widths(table_id, &specs, &new_widths);
 8360|       |        let metrics = renderer.table_metrics.borrow();
 8361|       |        let entry = metrics.entry(table_id).expect("entry exists");
 8362|       |        assert_eq!(entry.persisted_width(policy_hash), Some(80.0));
 8363|       |        assert_eq!(entry.persisted_font_size, Some(16.0));
 8364|       |    }
 8365|       |
 8366|       |    #[test]
 8367|       |    fn highlight_phrase_keeps_persisted_table_widths() {
 8368|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 8369|       |        let table_id = 11u64;
 8370|       |        let specs = vec![resizable_spec(0, "A", 20.0, 100.0)];
 8371|       |        let widths = vec![120.0f32];
 8372|       |        let policy_hash = specs[0].policy_hash;
 8373|       |
 8374|       |        renderer.persist_resizable_widths(table_id, &specs, &widths);
 8375|       |        renderer.set_highlight_phrase(Some("alpha"));
 8376|       |        renderer.set_highlight_phrase(Some("beta"));
 8377|       |
 8378|       |        let metrics = renderer.table_metrics.borrow();
 8379|       |        let entry = metrics.entry(table_id).expect("entry exists");
 8380|       |        assert_eq!(entry.persisted_width(policy_hash), Some(120.0));
 8381|       |    }
 8382|       |
 8383|       |    #[test]
 8384|       |    fn test_tight_list_inline_code_and_styles() {
 8385|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 8386|       |        let md = "Intro\n\n- Use `code` and **bold** and *italic* and ~~strike~~\n";
 8387|       |        let parsed = renderer.parse(md).expect("parse ok");
 8388|       |        let (ordered, items) = parsed
 8389|       |            .iter()
 8390|       |            .find_map(|el| {
 8391|       |                if let MarkdownElement::List { ordered, items } = el {
 8392|       |                    Some((*ordered, items))
 8393|       |                } else {
 8394|       |                    None
 8395|       |                }
 8396|       |            })
 8397|       |            .expect("list element");
 8398|       |        assert!(!ordered);
 8399|       |        assert_eq!(items.len(), 1);
 8400|       |        let mut blocks = Vec::new();
 8401|       |        blocks.push(MarkdownElement::HorizontalRule);
 8402|       |        blocks.extend(items[0].blocks.clone());
 8403|       |        let spans = blocks
 8404|       |            .iter()
 8405|       |            .find_map(|block| {
 8406|       |                if let MarkdownElement::Paragraph(spans) = block {
 8407|       |                    Some(spans)
 8408|       |                } else {
 8409|       |                    None
 8410|       |                }
 8411|       |            })
 8412|       |            .expect("paragraph block");
 8413|       |        let mut saw_code = false;
 8414|       |        let mut saw_strong = false;
 8415|       |        let mut saw_emphasis = false;
 8416|       |        let mut saw_strike = false;
 8417|       |        let mut saw_other = false;
 8418|       |        for span in spans {
 8419|       |            match span {
 8420|       |                InlineSpan::Code(_) => saw_code = true,
 8421|       |                InlineSpan::Strong(_) => saw_strong = true,
 8422|       |                InlineSpan::Emphasis(_) => saw_emphasis = true,
 8423|       |                InlineSpan::Strikethrough(_) => saw_strike = true,
 8424|       |                _ => saw_other = true,
 8425|       |            }
 8426|       |        }
 8427|       |        assert!(saw_code);
 8428|       |        assert!(saw_strong);
 8429|       |        assert!(saw_emphasis);
 8430|       |        assert!(saw_strike);
 8431|       |        assert!(saw_other);
 8432|       |    }
 8433|       |

 8435|       |    fn test_expand_shortcodes_basic() {
 8436|       |        let rocket = MarkdownRenderer::expand_shortcodes(":rocket:");
 8437|       |        assert_ne!(rocket, ":rocket:");
 8438|       |        assert!(crate::emoji_catalog::image_bytes_for(&rocket).is_some());
 8439|       |

 8441|       |        assert_ne!(tada, ":tada:");
 8442|       |        assert!(crate::emoji_catalog::image_bytes_for(&tada).is_some());
 8443|       |
 8444|       |        assert_eq!(
 8445|       |            MarkdownRenderer::expand_shortcodes("Hello :tada:!"),
 8446|       |            format!("Hello {}!", tada)
 8447|       |        );
 8448|       |    }

 8472|       |    fn test_elements_to_plain_text_basic() {
 8473|       |        let elements = vec![
 8474|       |            MarkdownElement::Header {
 8475|       |                level: 1,
 8476|       |                spans: vec![InlineSpan::Text("Test Header".to_string())],
 8477|       |                id: "test-header".to_string(),
 8478|       |            },
 8479|       |            MarkdownElement::Paragraph(vec![
 8480|       |                InlineSpan::Text("This is a ".to_string()),
 8481|       |                InlineSpan::Strong("bold".to_string()),
 8482|       |                InlineSpan::Text(" paragraph.".to_string()),
 8483|       |            ]),
 8484|       |        ];

 8491|       |    #[test]
 8492|       |    fn test_elements_to_plain_text_code_block() {
 8493|       |        let elements = vec![MarkdownElement::CodeBlock {
 8494|       |            language: Some("rust".to_string()),
 8495|       |            text: "fn main() {\n    println!(\"Hello\");\n}".to_string(),
 8496|       |            highlighted: None,
 8497|       |        }];
 8498|       |
 8499|       |        let plain_text = MarkdownRenderer::elements_to_plain_text(&elements);
 8500|       |        assert_eq!(plain_text, "fn main() {\n    println!(\"Hello\");\n}");
 8501|       |    }

 8531|       |            MarkdownElement::Paragraph(vec![InlineSpan::Text("First".to_string())]),
 8532|       |            MarkdownElement::List {
 8533|       |                ordered: false,
 8534|       |                items: vec![ListItem {
 8535|       |                    blocks: vec![MarkdownElement::Paragraph(vec![InlineSpan::Text(
 8536|       |                        "List".to_string(),
 8537|       |                    )])],
 8538|       |                }],
 8539|       |            },
 8540|       |            MarkdownElement::Quote {
 8541|       |                depth: 1,
 8542|       |                blocks: vec![MarkdownElement::Paragraph(vec![InlineSpan::Text(
 8543|       |                    "Quote".to_string(),
 8544|       |                )])],
 8545|       |            },

 8547|       |                headers: vec![
 8548|       |                    vec![InlineSpan::Text("H1".to_string())],
 8549|       |                    vec![InlineSpan::Text("H2".to_string())],
 8550|       |                ],
 8551|       |                rows: vec![vec![vec![InlineSpan::Text("R1".to_string())]]],
 8552|       |                alignments: vec![Alignment::Left, Alignment::Left],
 8553|       |            },
 8554|       |            MarkdownElement::HorizontalRule,
 8555|       |        ];
 8556|       |        let plain_text = MarkdownRenderer::elements_to_plain_text(&elements);
 8557|       |        assert_eq!(plain_text, "First\nList\nQuote\nH1\nH2\nR1\n---\n");
 8558|       |    }

 8640|       |
 8641|       |    #[test]
 8642|       |    fn test_image_source_stale_detects_file_changes() {
 8643|       |        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().expect("temp dir");
 8644|       |        let file_path = dir.path().join("image.bin");
 8645|       |        std::fs::write(&file_path, [1u8, 2, 3, 4]).expect("write image");
 8646|       |        let initial = MarkdownRenderer::disk_image_timestamp(&file_path);
 8647|       |
 8648|       |        // Unchanged file → not stale
 8649|       |        assert!(!MarkdownRenderer::image_source_stale(initial, &file_path));
 8650|       |
 8651|       |        // Deleted file → NOT stale (keep cached texture; avoids flashing when
 8652|       |        // antivirus or filesystem transiently locks the file)
 8653|       |        std::fs::remove_file(&file_path).expect("remove image");
 8654|       |        assert!(!MarkdownRenderer::image_source_stale(initial, &file_path));
 8655|       |
 8656|       |        // File without cached timestamp appears → stale (pick it up)
 8657|       |        std::fs::write(&file_path, [4u8, 3, 2, 1]).expect("rewrite image");
 8658|       |        assert!(MarkdownRenderer::image_source_stale(None, &file_path));
 8659|       |
 8660|       |        // A timestamp far in the past → stale (beyond tolerance)
 8661|       |        let old = Some(SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH);
 8662|       |        assert!(MarkdownRenderer::image_source_stale(old, &file_path));
 8663|       |    }
 8664|       |
 8665|       |    #[test]
 8666|       |    fn test_disk_image_timestamp_missing_returns_none() {
 8667|       |        let missing = std::path::Path::new("missing_image_timestamp.bin");
 8668|       |        assert!(MarkdownRenderer::disk_image_timestamp(missing).is_none());
 8669|       |    }
 8670|       |
 8671|       |    #[test]
 8672|       |    fn test_image_source_stale_with_timestamp_combinations() {
 8673|       |        let stamp = SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH;
 8674|       |        // Had cache, metadata now fails → NOT stale (transient failure)
 8675|       |        assert!(!MarkdownRenderer::image_source_stale_with_timestamp(
 8676|       |            Some(stamp),
 8677|       |            None
 8678|       |        ));
 8679|       |        // No cache, file appeared → stale (pick it up)
 8680|       |        assert!(MarkdownRenderer::image_source_stale_with_timestamp(
 8681|       |            None,
 8682|       |            Some(stamp)
 8683|       |        ));
 8684|       |        // Both None → not stale
 8685|       |        assert!(!MarkdownRenderer::image_source_stale_with_timestamp(
 8686|       |            None, None
 8687|       |        ));
 8688|       |        // Same timestamp → not stale
 8689|       |        assert!(!MarkdownRenderer::image_source_stale_with_timestamp(
 8690|       |            Some(stamp),
 8691|       |            Some(stamp)
 8692|       |        ));
 8693|       |        // Within tolerance → not stale
 8694|       |        let nearby = stamp + Duration::from_secs(5);
 8695|       |        assert!(!MarkdownRenderer::image_source_stale_with_timestamp(
 8696|       |            Some(stamp),
 8697|       |            Some(nearby)
 8698|       |        ));
 8699|       |        assert!(!MarkdownRenderer::image_source_stale_with_timestamp(
 8700|       |            Some(nearby),
 8701|       |            Some(stamp)
 8702|       |        ));
 8703|       |        // Beyond tolerance → stale
 8704|       |        let far = stamp + IMAGE_TIMESTAMP_TOLERANCE + Duration::from_secs(1);
 8705|       |        assert!(MarkdownRenderer::image_source_stale_with_timestamp(
 8706|       |            Some(stamp),
 8707|       |            Some(far)
 8708|       |        ));
 8709|       |        assert!(MarkdownRenderer::image_source_stale_with_timestamp(
 8710|       |            Some(far),
 8711|       |            Some(stamp)
 8712|       |        ));
 8713|       |    }
 8714|       |
 8715|       |    #[test]
 8716|       |    fn test_inline_code_preserves_whitespace() {
 8717|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 8718|       |        let md = "Start `code` end";
 8719|       |        let parsed = renderer.parse(md).expect("parse ok");
 8720|       |        let non_paragraph = MarkdownElement::HorizontalRule;
 8721|       |        let mut saw_paragraph = false;
 8722|       |        for element in [&parsed[0], &non_paragraph] {
 8723|       |            if let MarkdownElement::Paragraph(spans) = element {
 8724|       |                let code_span = spans.iter().find_map(|span| match span {
 8725|       |                    InlineSpan::Code(t) => Some(t),
 8726|       |                    _ => None,
 8727|       |                });
 8728|       |                assert_eq!(code_span, Some(&"code".to_string()));
 8729|       |                saw_paragraph = true;
 8730|       |            }
 8731|       |        }
 8732|       |        assert!(saw_paragraph);
 8733|       |    }

 8748|       |    fn test_parse_headers_assign_ids_and_dedupe() {
 8749|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 8750|       |        let md = "# Getting Started\n\nParagraph text.\n\n## Getting Started\n\n### API & Usage\n\n## API & Usage\n";
 8751|       |        let parsed = renderer.parse(md).expect("parse ok");
 8752|       |
 8753|       |        let mut ids = vec![];
 8754|       |        for el in parsed {
 8755|       |            if let MarkdownElement::Header { id, .. } = el {
 8756|       |                ids.push(id);
 8757|       |            }
 8758|       |        }
 8759|       |        assert_eq!(ids.len(), 4);
 8760|       |        assert_eq!(ids[0], "getting-started");
 8761|       |        assert_eq!(ids[1], "getting-started-1");
 8762|       |        assert_eq!(ids[2], "api-usage");
 8763|       |        assert_eq!(ids[3], "api-usage-1");
 8764|       |    }

 8766|       |    #[test]
 8767|       |    fn test_formatting_sample_contains_expected_header_ids() {
 8768|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 8769|       |        let formatting = SAMPLE_FILES
 8770|       |            .iter()
 8771|       |            .find(|f| f.name == "formatting.md")
 8772|       |            .expect("formatting sample present");
 8773|       |        let parsed = renderer.parse(formatting.content).expect("parse ok");
 8774|       |        let ids: Vec<String> = parsed
 8775|       |            .into_iter()
 8776|       |            .filter_map(|el| match el {
 8777|       |                MarkdownElement::Header { id, .. } => Some(id),
 8778|       |                _ => None,
 8779|       |            })
 8780|       |            .collect();
 8781|       |
 8782|       |        for expected in [
 8783|       |            "markdown-formatting-guide",
 8784|       |            "table-of-contents",
 8785|       |            "text-formatting",
 8786|       |            "headers",
 8787|       |            "lists",
 8788|       |            "links-and-images",
 8789|       |            "emojis",
 8790|       |            "blockquotes",
 8791|       |            "horizontal-rules",
 8792|       |            "tables",
 8793|       |        ] {
 8794|       |            assert!(ids.iter().any(|id| id == expected));
 8795|       |        }
 8796|       |    }

 8798|       |    #[test]
 8799|       |    fn test_inline_image_parsing() {
 8800|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 8801|       |        let md = "Here is an image: ![Alt text](images/pic.webp \"Title\") end.";
 8802|       |        let parsed = renderer.parse(md).expect("parse ok");
 8803|       |        assert_eq!(parsed.len(), 1);
 8804|       |        assert!(matches!(&parsed[0], MarkdownElement::Paragraph(_)));
 8805|       |        let non_paragraph = MarkdownElement::HorizontalRule;
 8806|       |        let mut saw_paragraph = false;
 8807|       |        for element in [&parsed[0], &non_paragraph] {
 8808|       |            if let MarkdownElement::Paragraph(spans) = element {
 8809|       |                let img = spans.iter().find(|s| matches!(s, InlineSpan::Image { .. }));
 8810|       |                assert!(img.is_some());
 8811|       |                let (src, alt, title) = image_span_fields(img.unwrap()).expect("image span");
 8812|       |                assert_eq!(src, "images/pic.webp");
 8813|       |                assert_eq!(alt, "Alt text");
 8814|       |                assert_eq!(title, Some("Title"));
 8815|       |                saw_paragraph = true;
 8816|       |            }
 8817|       |        }
 8818|       |        assert!(saw_paragraph);
 8819|       |        assert!(image_span_fields(&InlineSpan::Text("nope".into())).is_none());
 8820|       |    }

 8854|       |    #[test]
 8855|       |    fn test_cell_fragments_split_text_and_images() {
 8856|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 8857|       |        let spans = vec![
 8858|       |            InlineSpan::Text("alpha".into()),
 8859|       |            InlineSpan::Strong("beta".into()),
 8860|       |            InlineSpan::Image {
 8861|       |                src: "img.png".into(),
 8862|       |                alt: "img".into(),
 8863|       |                title: None,
 8864|       |            },
 8865|       |            InlineSpan::Text("gamma".into()),
 8866|       |        ];
 8867|       |        let fragments = renderer.cell_fragments(&spans);
 8868|       |        assert_eq!(fragments.len(), 3);
 8869|       |        assert!(matches!(fragments[0], CellFragment::Text(_)));
 8870|       |        let mut saw_first_text = false;
 8871|       |        for fragment in [&fragments[0], &fragments[1]] {
 8872|       |            if let CellFragment::Text(slice) = fragment {
 8873|       |                assert_eq!(slice.len(), 2);
 8874|       |                saw_first_text = true;
 8875|       |            }
 8876|       |        }
 8877|       |        assert!(saw_first_text);
 8878|       |        assert!(matches!(fragments[1], CellFragment::Image(_)));
 8879|       |        let mut saw_image = false;
 8880|       |        for fragment in [&fragments[1], &fragments[0]] {
 8881|       |            if let CellFragment::Image(span) = fragment {
 8882|       |                let (src, _alt, _title) = image_span_fields(span).expect("image span");
 8883|       |                assert_eq!(src, "img.png");
 8884|       |                saw_image = true;
 8885|       |            }
 8886|       |        }
 8887|       |        assert!(saw_image);
 8888|       |        assert!(matches!(fragments[2], CellFragment::Text(_)));
 8889|       |        let mut saw_last_text = false;
 8890|       |        for fragment in [&fragments[2], &fragments[1]] {
 8891|       |            if let CellFragment::Text(slice) = fragment {
 8892|       |                assert_eq!(slice.len(), 1);
 8893|       |                let content = text_span_content(&slice[0]).expect("text span");
 8894|       |                assert_eq!(content, "gamma");
 8895|       |                saw_last_text = true;
 8896|       |            }
 8897|       |        }
 8898|       |        assert!(saw_last_text);
 8899|       |        assert!(text_span_content(&InlineSpan::Image {
 8900|       |            src: "img.png".into(),
 8901|       |            alt: "img".into(),
 8902|       |            title: None,
 8903|       |        })
 8904|       |        .is_none());
 8905|       |    }

 8975|       |    #[test]
 8976|       |    fn test_get_or_make_emoji_texture_falls_back_on_decode_error() {
 8977|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 8978|       |        let rocket = rocket_emoji();
 8979|       |        force_emoji_decode_error_once();
 8980|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
 8981|       |            let _ = renderer.get_or_make_emoji_texture(ui, rocket);
 8982|       |        });
 8983|       |    }
 8984|       |
 8985|       |    #[test]
 8986|       |    fn test_get_or_make_emoji_texture_generates_on_decode_error() {
 8987|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 8988|       |        let fallback = [
 8989|       |            "\u{2705}",
 8990|       |            "\u{1f389}",
 8991|       |            "\u{1f680}",
 8992|       |            "\u{2764}",
 8993|       |            "\u{1f496}",
 8994|       |            "\u{2b50}",
 8995|       |            "\u{1f525}",
 8996|       |        ];
 8997|       |        let emoji = crate::emoji_catalog::shortcode_map()
 8998|       |            .values()
 8999|       |            .find(|emoji| {
 9000|       |                !fallback.contains(emoji) && emoji_catalog::image_bytes_for(emoji).is_some()
 9001|       |            })
 9002|       |            .expect("emoji outside fallback");
 9003|       |        assert!(emoji_assets::make_image(emoji, 64).is_none());
 9004|       |        force_emoji_decode_error_once();
 9005|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
 9006|       |            let _ = renderer.get_or_make_emoji_texture(ui, emoji);
 9007|       |        });
 9008|       |    }

 9010|       |    #[test]
 9011|       |    fn test_cell_fragments_inline_emoji_stays_text() {
 9012|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 9013|       |        let rocket = rocket_emoji();
 9014|       |        let spans = vec![
 9015|       |            InlineSpan::Text((*rocket).to_string()),
 9016|       |            InlineSpan::Text("tail".into()),
 9017|       |        ];
 9018|       |        let fragments = renderer.cell_fragments(&spans);
 9019|       |        assert_eq!(fragments.len(), 1);
 9020|       |        assert!(matches!(fragments[0], CellFragment::Text(_)));
 9021|       |        let dummy_span = InlineSpan::Image {
 9022|       |            src: "dummy.png".into(),
 9023|       |            alt: "dummy".into(),
 9024|       |            title: None,
 9025|       |        };
 9026|       |        let dummy_fragment = CellFragment::Image(&dummy_span);
 9027|       |        let mut saw_text = false;
 9028|       |        for fragment in [&fragments[0], &dummy_fragment] {
 9029|       |            if let CellFragment::Text(slice) = fragment {
 9030|       |                assert_eq!(slice.len(), 2);
 9031|       |                saw_text = true;
 9032|       |            }
 9033|       |        }
 9034|       |        assert!(saw_text);
 9035|       |    }

 9090|       |    fn test_layout_job_builder_highlights_matches() {
 9091|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 9092|       |        renderer.set_highlight_phrase(Some("wrap"));
 9093|       |        let spans = vec![InlineSpan::Text("wrap me, wrap me again".into())];
 9094|       |        let style = egui::Style::default();
 9095|       |        let build = renderer.build_layout_job(&style, &spans, 200.0, false, Align::LEFT);
 9096|       |        let highlight_bg = style.visuals.selection.bg_fill;
 9097|       |        assert!(build
 9098|       |            .job

 9104|       |    #[test]
 9105|       |    fn test_layout_job_builder_tracks_link_ranges() {
 9106|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 9107|       |        let spans = vec![InlineSpan::Link {
 9108|       |            text: "Docs".into(),
 9109|       |            url: "https://example.org/docs".into(),
 9110|       |        }];
 9111|       |        let style = egui::Style::default();
 9112|       |        let build = renderer.build_layout_job(&style, &spans, 220.0, false, Align::LEFT);
 9113|       |        assert_eq!(build.link_ranges.len(), 1);
 9114|       |        let link = &build.link_ranges[0];
 9115|       |        assert_eq!(link.url, "https://example.org/docs");
 9116|       |        let char_len = link.char_range.end - link.char_range.start;
 9117|       |        let linked_text: String = build
 9118|       |            .job
 9119|       |            .text
 9120|       |            .chars()
 9121|       |            .skip(link.char_range.start)
 9122|       |            .take(char_len)
 9123|       |            .collect();
 9124|       |        assert_eq!(linked_text, "Docs");
 9125|       |    }

 9154|       |    fn test_append_spans_empty_and_code_light() {
 9155|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 9156|       |        let style = egui::Style::default();
 9157|       |        let mut job = LayoutJob::default();
 9158|       |        let mut plain_text = String::new();
 9159|       |
 9160|       |        assert_eq!(
 9161|       |            renderer.append_plain_span(
 9162|       |                &style,
 9163|       |                &mut job,
 9164|       |                &mut plain_text,
 9165|       |                "",
 9166|       |                InlineStyle::default(),
 9167|       |                None
 9168|       |            ),
 9169|       |            0
 9170|       |        );
 9171|       |        assert_eq!(
 9172|       |            renderer.append_code_span(&style, &mut job, &mut plain_text, ""),
 9173|       |            0
 9174|       |        );
 9175|       |
 9176|       |        let mut light_style = style.clone();
 9177|       |        light_style.visuals.dark_mode = false;
 9178|       |        assert!(renderer.append_code_span(&light_style, &mut job, &mut plain_text, "code") > 0);
 9179|       |
 9180|       |        let mut empty_job = LayoutJob::default();
 9181|       |        let mut empty_plain = String::new();
 9182|       |        assert_eq!(
 9183|       |            renderer.append_text_sections(
 9184|       |                &style,
 9185|       |                &mut empty_job,
 9186|       |                &mut empty_plain,
 9187|       |                "",
 9188|       |                12.0,
 9189|       |                InlineStyle::default(),
 9190|       |                None
 9191|       |            ),
 9192|       |            0
 9193|       |        );
 9194|       |    }
