 9249|       |    #[test]
 9250|       |    fn test_table_width_solver_keeps_short_columns_readable() {
 9251|       |        let mins = vec![90.0, 90.0, 90.0, 90.0];
 9252|       |        let desired = vec![120.0, 120.0, 360.0, 160.0];
 9253|       |        let widths = MarkdownRenderer::resolve_table_widths(420.0, &mins, &desired);
 9254|       |        assert_eq!(widths.len(), 4);
 9255|       |        // Narrow columns should stay near their minimums even when a wide column exists
 9256|       |        assert!(widths[0] >= 85.0);
 9257|       |        assert!(widths[2] > widths[0]);
 9258|       |        let sum: f32 = widths.iter().sum();
 9259|       |        assert!((sum - 420.0).abs() < 0.5);
 9260|       |    }
 9261|       |
 9262|       |    #[test]
 9263|       |    fn test_table_width_solver_handles_constrained_space() {
 9264|       |        let mins = vec![100.0, 100.0, 100.0, 100.0];
 9265|       |        let desired = vec![200.0, 240.0, 360.0, 160.0];
 9266|       |        let widths = MarkdownRenderer::resolve_table_widths(260.0, &mins, &desired);
 9267|       |        assert_eq!(widths.len(), 4);
 9268|       |        assert!(widths.iter().all(|w| *w > 0.0));
 9269|       |        let sum: f32 = widths.iter().sum();
 9270|       |        assert!((sum - 260.0).abs() < 0.5);
 9271|       |    }
 9272|       |
 9273|       |    #[test]
 9274|       |    fn test_resolve_table_rects_clip_rect_fallbacks() {
 9275|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 9276|       |        let widths = vec![12.0];
 9277|       |        let body_clip = egui::Rect::from_min_max(egui::pos2(1.0, 2.0), egui::pos2(3.0, 4.0));
 9278|       |        let (rect, clip) =
 9279|       |            renderer.resolve_table_rects(None, None, None, None, Some(body_clip), &widths, 4.0);
 9280|       |        assert_eq!(clip, body_clip);
 9281|       |
 9282|       |        let (rect2, clip2) =
 9283|       |            renderer.resolve_table_rects(None, None, None, None, None, &widths, 4.0);
 9284|       |        assert_eq!(clip2, rect2);
 9285|       |        assert_eq!(rect2.min, egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0));
 9286|       |        assert_eq!(rect2.max, egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0));
 9287|       |        assert_eq!(rect.min, egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0));
 9288|       |    }
 9289|       |
 9290|       |    #[test]
 9291|       |    fn test_resolve_table_rects_prefers_layout_then_header_then_body() {
 9292|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 9293|       |        let widths = vec![10.0, 20.0];
 9294|       |        let layout = egui::Rect::from_min_max(egui::pos2(5.0, 6.0), egui::pos2(25.0, 26.0));
 9295|       |        let header = egui::Rect::from_min_max(egui::pos2(1.0, 2.0), egui::pos2(9.0, 12.0));
 9296|       |        let body = egui::Rect::from_min_max(egui::pos2(2.0, 3.0), egui::pos2(14.0, 18.0));
 9297|       |
 9298|       |        let (rect, _) = renderer.resolve_table_rects(
 9299|       |            Some(header),
 9300|       |            Some(body),
 9301|       |            Some(layout),
 9302|       |            None,
 9303|       |            None,
 9304|       |            &widths,
 9305|       |            4.0,
 9306|       |        );
 9307|       |        assert_eq!(rect.min.x, layout.min.x);
 9308|       |
 9309|       |        let (rect, _) =
 9310|       |            renderer.resolve_table_rects(Some(header), None, None, None, None, &widths, 4.0);
 9311|       |        assert_eq!(rect.min.x, header.min.x);
 9312|       |
 9313|       |        let (rect, _) =
 9314|       |            renderer.resolve_table_rects(None, Some(body), None, None, None, &widths, 4.0);
 9315|       |        assert_eq!(rect.min.x, body.min.x);
 9316|       |    }

 9319|       |    fn test_resolve_table_rects_layout_only_fallbacks() {

 9324|       |            renderer.resolve_table_rects(None, None, Some(layout), None, None, &widths, 4.0);

 9329|       |    #[test]
 9330|       |    fn table_cells_keep_images_and_formatting() {
 9331|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 9332|       |        let md = "\
 9333|       |Intro
 9334|       |
 9335|       || H1 | H2 |
 9336|       || --- | --- |
 9337|       || text **extra** ![Alt](img.png) | **bold** and [link](https://example.com) |";
 9338|       |        let elements = renderer.parse(md).expect("parse ok");
 9339|       |        let table = elements
 9340|       |            .iter()
 9341|       |            .find_map(|el| match el {
 9342|       |                MarkdownElement::Table {
 9343|       |                    headers: _,
 9344|       |                    rows,
 9345|       |                    alignments: _,
 9346|       |                } => Some(rows),
 9347|       |                _ => None,
 9348|       |            })
 9349|       |            .expect("table present");
 9350|       |        assert_eq!(table.len(), 1);
 9351|       |        let row = &table[0];
 9352|       |        let mut saw_image = false;
 9353|       |        let mut saw_text = false;
 9354|       |        for span in &row[0] {
 9355|       |            match span {
 9356|       |                InlineSpan::Image { .. } => saw_image = true,
 9357|       |                InlineSpan::Text(_) => saw_text = true,
 9358|       |                _ => {}
 9359|       |            }
 9360|       |        }
 9361|       |        assert!(saw_image);
 9362|       |        assert!(saw_text);
 9363|       |        let mut saw_strong = false;
 9364|       |        let mut saw_link = false;
 9365|       |        let mut saw_other = false;
 9366|       |        for span in &row[1] {
 9367|       |            match span {
 9368|       |                InlineSpan::Strong(_) => saw_strong = true,
 9369|       |                InlineSpan::Link { .. } => saw_link = true,
 9370|       |                _ => saw_other = true,
 9371|       |            }
 9372|       |        }
 9373|       |        assert!(saw_strong);
 9374|       |        assert!(saw_link);
 9375|       |        assert!(saw_other);
 9376|       |    }
 9377|       |
 9378|       |    #[test]
 9379|       |    fn table_inline_code_keeps_pipes() {
 9380|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 9381|       |        let md = "\
 9382|       |Intro
 9383|       |
 9384|       || Col | Notes |
 9385|       || --- | --- |
 9386|       || code | see `a|b|c` |";
 9387|       |        let elements = renderer.parse(md).expect("parse ok");
 9388|       |        let rows = elements
 9389|       |            .iter()
 9390|       |            .find_map(|el| match el {
 9391|       |                MarkdownElement::Table {
 9392|       |                    headers: _,
 9393|       |                    rows,
 9394|       |                    alignments: _,
 9395|       |                } => Some(rows),
 9396|       |                _ => None,
 9397|       |            })
 9398|       |            .expect("table present");
 9399|       |        let code_text = rows[0][1]
 9400|       |            .iter()
 9401|       |            .find_map(|span| match span {
 9402|       |                InlineSpan::Code(text) => Some(text.as_str()),
 9403|       |                _ => None,
 9404|       |            })
 9405|       |            .expect("code span");
 9406|       |        assert_eq!(code_text, "a|b|c");
 9407|       |    }

 9436|       |    #[test]
 9437|       |    fn blockquote_table_after_paragraph_parses() -> Result<()> {
 9438|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 9439|       |        let md = "\
 9440|       |Prelude
 9441|       |
 9442|       |> Intro line
 9443|       |> | Col | Notes |
 9444|       |> | --- | --- |
 9445|       |> | A | B |";
 9446|       |        let elements = renderer.parse(md)?;
 9447|       |        let blocks = elements
 9448|       |            .iter()
 9449|       |            .find_map(|el| match el {
 9450|       |                MarkdownElement::Quote { blocks, .. } => Some(blocks),
 9451|       |                _ => None,
 9452|       |            })
 9453|       |            .expect("quote block");
 9454|       |        assert!(blocks
 9455|       |            .iter()
 9456|       |            .any(|block| matches!(block, MarkdownElement::Table { .. })));
 9457|       |        Ok(())
 9458|       |    }
 9459|       |
 9460|       |    #[test]
 9461|       |    fn blockquote_list_table_after_paragraph_parses() -> Result<()> {
 9462|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 9463|       |        let md = "\
 9464|       |Prelude
 9465|       |
 9466|       |> Note before list
 9467|       |> - Item:
 9468|       |>   | Key | Val |
 9469|       |>   | --- | --- |
 9470|       |>   | A | 1 |";
 9471|       |        let elements = renderer.parse(md)?;
 9472|       |        let blocks = elements
 9473|       |            .iter()
 9474|       |            .find_map(|el| match el {
 9475|       |                MarkdownElement::Quote { blocks, .. } => Some(blocks),
 9476|       |                _ => None,
 9477|       |            })
 9478|       |            .expect("quote block");
 9479|       |        let list = blocks
 9480|       |            .iter()
 9481|       |            .find_map(|block| match block {
 9482|       |                MarkdownElement::List { items, .. } => Some(items),
 9483|       |                _ => None,
 9484|       |            })
 9485|       |            .expect("list block");
 9486|       |        let first = list.first().expect("list item");
 9487|       |        assert!(first
 9488|       |            .blocks
 9489|       |            .iter()
 9490|       |            .any(|block| matches!(block, MarkdownElement::Table { .. })));
 9491|       |        Ok(())
 9492|       |    }
 9493|       |

 9851|       |    #[test]
 9852|       |    fn table_ids_are_unique_per_position() {
 9853|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 9854|       |        let md = "\
 9855|       |Intro
 9856|       |
 9857|       || H |
 9858|       || - |
 9859|       || a |
 9860|       |
 9861|       || H |
 9862|       || - |
 9863|       || a |";
 9864|       |        let elements = renderer.parse(md).expect("parse ok");
 9865|       |        let tables: Vec<_> = elements
 9866|       |            .iter()
 9867|       |            .enumerate()
 9868|       |            .filter_map(|(idx, el)| match el {
 9869|       |                MarkdownElement::Table {
 9870|       |                    headers,
 9871|       |                    rows,
 9872|       |                    alignments,
 9873|       |                } => Some(renderer.compute_table_id(headers, rows, alignments, idx as u64)),
 9874|       |                _ => None,
 9875|       |            })
 9876|       |            .collect();
 9877|       |        assert_eq!(tables.len(), 2);
 9878|       |        assert_ne!(tables[0], tables[1]);
 9879|       |    }
 9880|       |
 9881|       |    #[test]
 9882|       |    fn test_render_to_ui_rich_document_populates_rects_and_table_stats() {
 9883|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 9884|       |        let md = "\
 9885|       |# Coverage Demo
 9886|       |
 9887|       |Paragraph with :tada: emoji and **bold** text and a [link](#target).
 9888|       |
 9889|       |> Quote line one
 9890|       |> Quote line two
 9891|       |
 9892|       |- item one
 9893|       |- item two
 9894|       |
 9895|       |---
 9896|       |
 9897|       |```rust
 9898|       |fn main() {}
 9899|       |```
 9900|       |
 9901|       || Col A | Col B |
 9902|       || --- | --- |
 9903|       || A1 | B1 |
 9904|       || A2 | B2 |
 9905|       |
 9906|       |![Logo](assets/samples/logo.svg \"Logo\")
 9907|       |![Sample](assets/samples/webp_sample.webp \"Sample\")
 9908|       |![Missing](missing_test_image.png \"Missing\")
 9909|       |![Remote](https://example.com/image.png \"Remote\")
 9910|       |";
 9911|       |
 9912|       |        let elements = renderer.parse(md).expect("parse ok");
 9913|       |        renderer.set_highlight_phrase(Some("item"));
 9914|       |
 9915|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
 9916|       |            renderer.render_to_ui(ui, &elements);
 9917|       |        });
 9918|       |
 9919|       |        assert!(renderer.element_rect_at(0).is_some());
 9920|       |        assert!(renderer.header_rect_for("coverage-demo").is_some());
 9921|       |        let (rendered, total) = renderer.table_render_stats();
 9922|       |        assert!(total > 0);
 9923|       |        assert!(rendered > 0);
 9924|       |        let (_hits, misses) = renderer.table_layout_cache_stats();
 9925|       |        assert!(misses > 0);
 9926|       |    }
 9927|       |
 9928|       |    #[test]
 9929|       |    fn test_table_layout_cache_records_hits() {
 9930|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 9931|       |        let spans = vec![InlineSpan::Text("alpha".to_string())];
 9932|       |        let style = egui::Style::default();
 9933|       |
 9934|       |        let _ = renderer.cached_layout_job(&style, Some(0), 0, &spans, 120.0, false, Align::LEFT);
 9935|       |        let _ = renderer.cached_layout_job(&style, Some(0), 0, &spans, 120.0, false, Align::LEFT);
 9936|       |
 9937|       |        let (hits, misses) = renderer.table_layout_cache_stats();
 9938|       |        assert!(hits >= 1);
 9939|       |        assert!(misses >= 1);
 9940|       |
 9941|       |        renderer.clear_table_layout_cache();
 9942|       |        assert_eq!(renderer.table_layout_cache_stats(), (0, 0));
 9943|       |    }
 9944|       |
 9945|       |    #[test]
 9946|       |    fn test_cell_layout_cache_eviction() {
 9947|       |        let mut cache = CellLayoutCache::new(2);
 9948|       |        let build = LayoutJobBuild {
 9949|       |            job: LayoutJob::default(),
 9950|       |            plain_text: String::new(),
 9951|       |            link_ranges: Vec::new(),
 9952|       |        };
 9953|       |        let key = |row, col| CellLayoutKey {
 9954|       |            row: Some(row),
 9955|       |            col,
 9956|       |            width: 120,
 9957|       |            align: 0,
 9958|       |            strong: false,
 9959|       |            text_color: [0, 0, 0, 255],
 9960|       |            highlight_hash: 0,
 9961|       |            content_hash: row as u64,
 9962|       |        };
 9963|       |
 9964|       |        let key_a = key(0, 0);
 9965|       |        let key_b = key(1, 0);
 9966|       |        let key_c = key(2, 0);
 9967|       |
 9968|       |        cache.insert(key_a.clone(), build.clone());
 9969|       |        cache.insert(key_b.clone(), build.clone());
 9970|       |        cache.insert(key_c.clone(), build);
 9971|       |
 9972|       |        assert!(cache.get(&key_a).is_none());
 9973|       |        assert!(cache.get(&key_b).is_some());
 9974|       |        assert!(cache.get(&key_c).is_some());
 9975|       |    }
 9976|       |
 9977|       |    #[test]
 9978|       |    fn test_cell_layout_cache_handles_empty_order() {
 9979|       |        let mut cache = CellLayoutCache::new(1);
 9980|       |        let build = LayoutJobBuild {
 9981|       |            job: LayoutJob::default(),
 9982|       |            plain_text: String::new(),
 9983|       |            link_ranges: Vec::new(),
 9984|       |        };
 9985|       |        let key_a = CellLayoutKey {
 9986|       |            row: Some(0),
 9987|       |            col: 0,
 9988|       |            width: 120,
 9989|       |            align: 0,
 9990|       |            strong: false,
 9991|       |            text_color: [0, 0, 0, 255],
 9992|       |            highlight_hash: 0,
 9993|       |            content_hash: 1,
 9994|       |        };
 9995|       |        let key_b = CellLayoutKey {
 9996|       |            row: Some(1),
 9997|       |            col: 0,
 9998|       |            width: 120,
 9999|       |            align: 0,
10000|       |            strong: false,
10001|       |            text_color: [0, 0, 0, 255],
10002|       |            highlight_hash: 0,
10003|       |            content_hash: 2,
10004|       |        };
10005|       |
10006|       |        cache.insert(key_a, build.clone());
10007|       |        cache.order.clear();
10008|       |        cache.insert(key_b.clone(), build);
10009|       |
10010|       |        assert!(cache.get(&key_b).is_some());
10011|       |    }
10012|       |

10014|       |    fn test_cell_layout_cache_reinsert_existing_key() {
10015|       |        let mut cache = CellLayoutCache::new(1);
10016|       |        let build = LayoutJobBuild {
10017|       |            job: LayoutJob::default(),
10018|       |            plain_text: String::new(),
10019|       |            link_ranges: Vec::new(),
10020|       |        };
10021|       |        let key = CellLayoutKey {
10022|       |            row: Some(0),
10023|       |            col: 0,
10024|       |            width: 120,
10025|       |            align: 0,
10026|       |            strong: false,
10027|       |            text_color: [0, 0, 0, 255],
10028|       |            highlight_hash: 0,
10029|       |            content_hash: 42,
10030|       |        };
10031|       |        cache.insert(key.clone(), build.clone());
10032|       |        cache.insert(key.clone(), build);
10033|       |        assert_eq!(cache.entries.len(), 1);
10034|       |        assert_eq!(cache.order.len(), 1);

10041|       |        let mut light_style = egui::Style::default();

10043|       |        light_style.visuals.override_text_color = Some(Color32::WHITE);

10045|       |

10049|       |            renderer.cached_layout_job(&dark_style, Some(0), 0, &spans, 120.0, false, Align::LEFT);

10062|       |            .iter()

10080|       |            .iter()
10081|       |            .find_map(|el| match el {

10084|       |            })

10091|       |    #[test]
10092|       |    fn test_table_first_column_alignment_parses_three_columns() -> Result<()> {
10093|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
10094|       |        let md = fs::read_to_string("tests/regression/cases/026-table-first-column-align.md")?;
10095|       |        let elements = renderer.parse(&md)?;
10096|       |        let tables: Vec<_> = elements
10097|       |            .iter()
10098|       |            .filter_map(|el| match el {
10099|       |                MarkdownElement::Table {
10100|       |                    headers,
10101|       |                    rows,
10102|       |                    alignments,
10103|       |                } => Some((headers, rows, alignments)),
10104|       |                _ => None,
10105|       |            })
10106|       |            .collect();
10107|       |        assert!(tables.len() >= 3);
10108|       |        let (first_headers, first_rows, first_alignments) = tables[0];
10109|       |        for (headers, rows, alignments) in tables.iter() {
10110|       |            assert_eq!(headers.len(), 3);
10111|       |            assert_eq!(alignments.len(), 3);
10112|       |            assert!(rows.iter().all(|row| row.len() == 3));
10113|       |        }
10114|       |        let table_id = renderer.compute_table_id(first_headers, first_rows, first_alignments, 0);
10115|       |        let available = Cell::new(0.0f32);
10116|       |        let spacing = Cell::new(0.0f32);
10117|       |        let _estimated_before = {
10118|       |            let column_stats = renderer.column_stats_for_table(
10119|       |                table_id,
10120|       |                first_headers,
10121|       |                first_rows,
10122|       |                first_alignments,
10123|       |            );
10124|       |            let ctx = TableColumnContext::new(
10125|       |                first_headers,
10126|       |                first_rows,
10127|       |                &column_stats,
10128|       |                renderer.font_sizes.body,
10129|       |                table_id,
10130|       |            );
10131|       |            let column_specs = derive_column_specs(&ctx);
10132|       |            let column_spacing = 6.0f32.max(egui::Style::default().spacing.item_spacing.x);
10133|       |            renderer.estimate_table_total_width(table_id, &column_specs, column_spacing)
10134|       |        };
10135|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
10136|       |            available.set(ui.available_width());
10137|       |            spacing.set(ui.spacing().item_spacing.x.max(6.0));
10138|       |            renderer.render_table_tablebuilder(
10139|       |                ui,
10140|       |                first_headers,
10141|       |                first_rows,
10142|       |                first_alignments,
10143|       |                table_id,
10144|       |            );
10145|       |        });
10146|       |        let widths = renderer
10147|       |            .table_metrics
10148|       |            .borrow()
10149|       |            .entry(table_id)
10150|       |            .map(|entry| entry.current_widths().to_vec())
10151|       |            .unwrap_or_default();
10152|       |        assert_eq!(widths.len(), 3);
10153|       |        assert!(widths.iter().all(|w| *w > 8.0));
10154|       |        Ok(())
10155|       |    }

10158|       |    fn test_trigger_link_handles_fragments_and_schemes() {
10159|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
10160|       |
10161|       |        renderer.trigger_link("#Section-One");
10162|       |        assert_eq!(
10163|       |            renderer.take_pending_anchor(),
10164|       |            Some("section-one".to_string())
10165|       |        );
10166|       |        assert!(renderer.take_pending_anchor().is_none());
10167|       |
10168|       |        renderer.trigger_link("ftp://example.com");
10169|       |        assert!(renderer.take_pending_anchor().is_none());
10170|       |
10171|       |        renderer.trigger_link("https://example.com");
10172|       |        assert!(renderer.take_pending_anchor().is_none());
10173|       |
10174|       |        renderer.trigger_link("mailto:hello@example.com");
10175|       |        assert!(renderer.take_pending_anchor().is_none());
10176|       |    }

10179|       |    fn test_resolve_image_path_with_base_dir() {
10180|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
10181|       |        let temp = tempdir().expect("temp dir");
10182|       |        renderer.set_base_dir(Some(temp.path()));
10183|       |
10184|       |        let resolved = renderer.resolve_image_path("sample.png");
10185|       |        assert!(resolved.contains("sample.png"));
10186|       |
10187|       |        let abs = temp.path().join("abs.png");
10188|       |        let abs_str = abs.to_string_lossy().into_owned();
10189|       |        assert_eq!(renderer.resolve_image_path(&abs_str), abs_str);
10190|       |
10191|       |        renderer.set_base_dir(None);
10192|       |        assert_eq!(renderer.resolve_image_path("relative.png"), "relative.png");
10193|       |    }

10196|       |    fn test_disk_image_loads_and_caches() {
10197|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
10198|       |        let temp = tempdir().expect("temp dir");
10199|       |        let image_path = temp.path().join("disk.png");
10200|       |
10201|       |        let mut img = image::RgbaImage::new(2, 2);
10202|       |        for pixel in img.pixels_mut() {
10203|       |            *pixel = image::Rgba([10, 20, 30, 255]);
10204|       |        }
10205|       |        img.save(&image_path).expect("save png");
10206|       |
10207|       |        renderer.set_base_dir(Some(temp.path()));
10208|       |        let resolved = renderer.resolve_image_path("disk.png");
10209|       |        with_test_ui(|ctx, ui| {
10210|       |            // Use wait_for_image to handle async image loading
10211|       |            let loaded = wait_for_image(&renderer, ctx, ui, &resolved);
10212|       |            assert!(loaded.is_some());
10213|       |        });
10214|       |
10215|       |        assert!(renderer.image_textures.borrow().contains_key(&resolved));
10216|       |    }

10267|       |    fn test_estimate_table_row_widths_distributes_remainder() {
10268|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
10269|       |        let min_floor = renderer.table_min_column_width();
10270|       |        let specs = vec![
10271|       |            fixed_spec(0, "Ord", 80.0),
10272|       |            ColumnSpec::new(1, "Params", ColumnPolicy::Remainder { clip: false }, None),
10273|       |        ];
10274|       |        let resolved = vec![80.0, min_floor];
10275|       |        let adjusted = resolved.clone();
10276|       |        let widths = renderer.estimate_table_row_widths(
10277|       |            &specs, &resolved, &adjusted, 320.0, min_floor, false, false,
10278|       |        );
10279|       |        assert!((widths[0] - 80.0).abs() < 0.1);
10280|       |        assert!(widths[1] > min_floor + 40.0);
10281|       |    }

10355|       |        };

10382|       |                ui,

10384|       |                None,
10385|       |                None,
10386|       |            );

10388|       |                ui,
10389|       |                &InlineSpan::Link {
10390|       |                    text: "ext".to_string(),
10391|       |                    url: "https://example.com".to_string(),
10392|       |                },
10393|       |                None,
10394|       |                None,
10395|       |            );

10397|       |                ui,

10399|       |                    text: "local".to_string(),

10401|       |                },
10402|       |                None,
10403|       |                None,
10404|       |            );

10406|       |            let _loaded = wait_for_image(&renderer, ctx, ui, &resolved);
10407|       |            renderer.render_inline_span(ui, &image_span, None, None);
10408|       |            renderer.render_inline_span(ui, &missing_span, None, None);

10426|       |    fn test_has_pending_renders_with_mermaid_pending() {
10427|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
10428|       |        // Give worker threads time to start up under heavy parallel test execution.
10429|       |        // Workers need to initialize QuickJS runtime which can be slow under load.
10430|       |        std::thread::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(200));
10431|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
10432|       |            renderer.mermaid.begin_frame();
10433|       |            renderer
10434|       |                .mermaid
10435|       |                .render_block(ui, "graph TD; A-->B;", 1.0, 14.0, MermaidTheme::Default);
10436|       |        });
10437|       |        assert!(renderer.has_pending_renders());
10438|       |    }

10440|       |    #[test]
10441|       |    fn test_parse_lists_and_blockquotes() {
10442|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
10443|       |        let md = "\
10444|       |- Item 1
10445|       |- Item 2
10446|       |  - Nested
10447|       |
10448|       |1. First
10449|       |2. Second
10450|       |
10451|       |> Quote line
10452|       |> > Nested quote
10453|       |
10454|       |---";
10455|       |
10456|       |        let elements = renderer.parse(md).expect("parse ok");
10457|       |        assert!(elements
10458|       |            .iter()
10459|       |            .any(|el| matches!(el, MarkdownElement::List { ordered: false, .. })));
10460|       |        assert!(elements
10461|       |            .iter()
10462|       |            .any(|el| matches!(el, MarkdownElement::List { ordered: true, .. })));
10463|       |        assert!(elements
10464|       |            .iter()
10465|       |            .any(|el| matches!(el, MarkdownElement::Quote { .. })));
10466|       |        assert!(elements
10467|       |            .iter()
10468|       |            .any(|el| matches!(el, MarkdownElement::HorizontalRule)));
10469|       |    }

10471|       |    #[test]
10472|       |    fn test_elements_to_plain_text_variants() {

10562|       |    fn test_measure_inline_spans_and_emoji_texture() {
10563|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
10564|       |        let spans = vec![
10565|       |            InlineSpan::Text("Hello".to_string()),
10566|       |            InlineSpan::Text("🙂".to_string()),
10567|       |        ];
10568|       |
10569|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
10570|       |            let width = renderer.measure_inline_spans(ui, &spans);
10571|       |            assert!(width > 0.0);
10572|       |            let tex = renderer.get_or_make_emoji_texture(ui, "👾");
10573|       |            assert!(tex.size()[0] > 0);
10574|       |        });

10581|       |    fn test_measure_inline_spans_respects_line_breaks() {
10582|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
10583|       |        let single_line = vec![InlineSpan::Text(
10584|       |            "Short This is a much longer line".to_string(),
10585|       |        )];
10586|       |        let multi_line = vec![InlineSpan::Text(
10587|       |            "Short\nThis is a much longer line".to_string(),
10588|       |        )];
10589|       |
10590|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
10591|       |            let single = renderer.measure_inline_spans(ui, &single_line);
10592|       |            let multi = renderer.measure_inline_spans(ui, &multi_line);
10593|       |            assert!(multi < single);
10594|       |        });
10595|       |    }

10597|       |    #[test]
10598|       |    fn test_measure_inline_spans_normalizes_unicode() {
10599|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
10600|       |        let unicode = vec![InlineSpan::Text("A\u{2192}B".to_string())];
10601|       |        let ascii = vec![InlineSpan::Text("A->B".to_string())];
10602|       |
10603|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
10604|       |            let unicode_width = renderer.measure_inline_spans(ui, &unicode);
10605|       |            let ascii_width = renderer.measure_inline_spans(ui, &ascii);
10606|       |            assert!((unicode_width - ascii_width).abs() < 0.5);
10607|       |        });
10608|       |    }

10611|       |    fn test_to_superscript_full_mapping() {
10612|       |        let input = "0123456789+-=()abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyzABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ#";
10613|       |        let out = MarkdownRenderer::to_superscript(input);
10614|       |        assert_eq!(out.chars().count(), input.chars().count());
10615|       |        assert!(out.contains('\u{2070}'));
10616|       |        assert!(out.contains('\u{00b9}'));
10617|       |        assert!(out.contains('\u{1d43}'));
10618|       |        assert!(out.contains('#'));
10619|       |    }

10625|       |            items: vec![ListItem {
10626|       |                blocks: vec![MarkdownElement::Paragraph(vec![InlineSpan::Text(
10627|       |                    "Item".to_string(),
10628|       |                )])],
10629|       |            }],
10630|       |        };

10632|       |            depth: 1,
10633|       |            blocks: vec![MarkdownElement::Paragraph(vec![InlineSpan::Text(

10635|       |            )])],
10636|       |        };
10637|       |        let table = MarkdownElement::Table {
10638|       |            headers: vec![vec![InlineSpan::Text("Header".to_string())]],

10640|       |            alignments: Vec::new(),
10641|       |        };
10642|       |

10648|       |        );

10651|       |    }

10654|       |    fn test_parse_element_image_outside_paragraph() {
10655|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
10656|       |        let events = vec![
10657|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Image(
10658|       |                LinkType::Inline,
10659|       |                "img.png".into(),
10660|       |                "Title".into(),
10661|       |            )),
10662|       |            Event::Text("Alt".into()),
10663|       |            Event::End(Tag::Image(LinkType::Inline, "".into(), "".into())),
10664|       |        ];
10665|       |        let mut elements = Vec::new();
10666|       |        let mut slugs = std::collections::HashMap::new();
10667|       |        let next = renderer.parse_element(&events, 0, &mut elements, &mut slugs);
10668|       |        assert_eq!(next, events.len());
10669|       |        assert!(matches!(
10670|       |            elements.first(),
10671|       |            Some(MarkdownElement::Paragraph(_))
10672|       |        ));
10673|       |        let spans = paragraph_spans(elements.first().expect("element")).expect("paragraph spans");
10674|       |        let (_src, _alt, title) = image_span_fields(&spans[0]).expect("image span");
10675|       |        assert_eq!(title, Some("Title"));
10676|       |        assert!(paragraph_spans(&MarkdownElement::HorizontalRule).is_none());
10677|       |    }

10706|       |    #[test]
10707|       |    fn test_parse_list_event_variants() -> Result<()> {
10708|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
10709|       |        let events = vec![
10710|       |            Event::Start(Tag::List(None)),
10711|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Item),
10712|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Emphasis),
10713|       |            Event::Text("em".into()),
10714|       |            Event::End(Tag::Emphasis),
10715|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Strong),
10716|       |            Event::Text("strong".into()),
10717|       |            Event::End(Tag::Strong),
10718|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Strikethrough),
10719|       |            Event::Text("strike".into()),
10720|       |            Event::End(Tag::Strikethrough),
10721|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Link(
10722|       |                LinkType::Inline,
10723|       |                "https://example.com".into(),
10724|       |                "".into(),
10725|       |            )),
10726|       |            Event::Text("link".into()),
10727|       |            Event::End(Tag::Link(LinkType::Inline, "".into(), "".into())),
10728|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Image(
10729|       |                LinkType::Inline,
10730|       |                "img.png".into(),
10731|       |                "Title".into(),
10732|       |            )),
10733|       |            Event::Text("alt".into()),
10734|       |            Event::End(Tag::Image(LinkType::Inline, "".into(), "".into())),
10735|       |            Event::Code("code".into()),
10736|       |            Event::Text("text".into()),
10737|       |            Event::SoftBreak,
10738|       |            Event::Start(Tag::List(Some(1))),
10739|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Item),
10740|       |            Event::Text("nested".into()),
10741|       |            Event::End(Tag::Item),
10742|       |            Event::End(Tag::List(Some(1))),
10743|       |            Event::End(Tag::Item),
10744|       |            Event::End(Tag::List(None)),
10745|       |        ];
10746|       |
10747|       |        let mut slugs = std::collections::HashMap::new();
10748|       |        let (items, next) = renderer.parse_list(&events, 1, &mut slugs);
10749|       |        assert_eq!(next, events.len());
10750|       |        assert_eq!(items.len(), 1);
10751|       |        let item = &items[0];
10752|       |        let mut blocks = Vec::new();
10753|       |        blocks.push(MarkdownElement::HorizontalRule);
10754|       |        blocks.extend(item.blocks.clone());
10755|       |        let spans = blocks
10756|       |            .iter()
10757|       |            .find_map(|block| match block {
10758|       |                MarkdownElement::Paragraph(spans) => Some(spans),
10759|       |                _ => None,
10760|       |            })
10761|       |            .expect("paragraph block");
10762|       |        assert!(spans.iter().any(|s| matches!(s, InlineSpan::Emphasis(_))));
10763|       |        assert!(spans.iter().any(|s| matches!(s, InlineSpan::Strong(_))));
10764|       |        assert!(spans
10765|       |            .iter()
10766|       |            .any(|s| matches!(s, InlineSpan::Strikethrough(_))));
10767|       |        assert!(spans.iter().any(|s| matches!(s, InlineSpan::Link { .. })));
10768|       |        assert!(spans.iter().any(|s| matches!(s, InlineSpan::Image { .. })));
10769|       |        assert!(spans.iter().any(|s| matches!(s, InlineSpan::Code(_))));
10770|       |        assert!(item
10771|       |            .blocks
10772|       |            .iter()
10773|       |            .any(|block| matches!(block, MarkdownElement::List { ordered: true, .. })));
10774|       |        Ok(())
10775|       |    }

10777|       |    #[test]
10778|       |    fn test_parse_inline_spans_with_breaks_variants() {
10779|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
10780|       |        let events = vec![
10781|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Paragraph),
10782|       |            Event::Text("Hello".into()),
10783|       |            Event::SoftBreak,
10784|       |            Event::Code("code".into()),
10785|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Strong),
10786|       |            Event::Text("bold".into()),
10787|       |            Event::End(Tag::Strong),
10788|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Emphasis),
10789|       |            Event::Text("em".into()),
10790|       |            Event::End(Tag::Emphasis),
10791|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Strikethrough),
10792|       |            Event::Text("strike".into()),
10793|       |            Event::End(Tag::Strikethrough),
10794|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Link(
10795|       |                LinkType::Inline,
10796|       |                "https://example.com".into(),
10797|       |                "".into(),
10798|       |            )),
10799|       |            Event::Text("link".into()),
10800|       |            Event::End(Tag::Link(LinkType::Inline, "".into(), "".into())),
10801|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Image(
10802|       |                LinkType::Inline,
10803|       |                "img.png".into(),
10804|       |                "Title".into(),
10805|       |            )),
10806|       |            Event::Text("alt".into()),
10807|       |            Event::End(Tag::Image(LinkType::Inline, "".into(), "".into())),
10808|       |            Event::End(Tag::Paragraph),
10809|       |        ];
10810|       |
10811|       |        let (spans, next) =
10812|       |            renderer.parse_inline_spans_with_breaks(&events, 1, Tag::Paragraph, true);
10813|       |        assert_eq!(next, events.len());
10814|       |        let mut saw_code = false;
10815|       |        let mut saw_strong = false;
10816|       |        let mut saw_emphasis = false;
10817|       |        let mut saw_strike = false;
10818|       |        let mut saw_link = false;
10819|       |        let mut saw_image = false;
10820|       |        let mut saw_break_text = false;
10821|       |        for span in &spans {
10822|       |            match span {
10823|       |                InlineSpan::Code(_) => saw_code = true,
10824|       |                InlineSpan::Strong(_) => saw_strong = true,
10825|       |                InlineSpan::Emphasis(_) => saw_emphasis = true,
10826|       |                InlineSpan::Strikethrough(_) => saw_strike = true,
10827|       |                InlineSpan::Link { .. } => saw_link = true,
10828|       |                InlineSpan::Image { .. } => saw_image = true,
10829|       |                InlineSpan::Text(t) if t.contains('\n') => saw_break_text = true,
10830|       |                _ => {}
10831|       |            }
10832|       |        }
10833|       |        assert!(saw_code);
10834|       |        assert!(saw_strong);
10835|       |        assert!(saw_emphasis);
10836|       |        assert!(saw_strike);
10837|       |        assert!(saw_link);
10838|       |        assert!(saw_image);
10839|       |        assert!(saw_break_text);
10840|       |    }

10842|       |    #[test]
10843|       |    fn test_parse_inline_spans_html_breaks() {
10844|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
10845|       |        let events = vec![
10846|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Paragraph),
10847|       |            Event::Text("one".into()),
10848|       |            Event::Html("<br>".into()),
10849|       |            Event::Text("two".into()),
10850|       |            Event::End(Tag::Paragraph),
10851|       |        ];
10852|       |
10853|       |        let (spans, next) =
10854|       |            renderer.parse_inline_spans_with_breaks(&events, 1, Tag::Paragraph, true);
10855|       |        assert_eq!(next, events.len());
10856|       |        assert!(spans
10857|       |            .iter()
10858|       |            .any(|s| matches!(s, InlineSpan::Text(t) if t == "\n")));
10859|       |
10860|       |        let (spans_no_break, _) = renderer.parse_inline_spans(&events, 1, Tag::Paragraph);
10861|       |        let text = MarkdownRenderer::spans_plain_text(&spans_no_break);
10862|       |        assert!(text.contains("one two"));
10863|       |
10864|       |        let attr_events = vec![
10865|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Paragraph),
10866|       |            Event::Text("alpha".into()),
10867|       |            Event::Html("<br class=\"tight\">".into()),
10868|       |            Event::Text("beta".into()),
10869|       |            Event::End(Tag::Paragraph),
10870|       |        ];
10871|       |        let (spans, next) =
10872|       |            renderer.parse_inline_spans_with_breaks(&attr_events, 1, Tag::Paragraph, true);
10873|       |        assert_eq!(next, attr_events.len());
10874|       |        assert!(spans
10875|       |            .iter()
10876|       |            .any(|s| matches!(s, InlineSpan::Text(t) if t == "\n")));
10877|       |
10878|       |        let strong_events = vec![
10879|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Paragraph),
10880|       |            Event::Text("lead ".into()),
10881|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Strong),
10882|       |            Event::Text("alpha".into()),
10883|       |            Event::Html("<br>".into()),
10884|       |            Event::Text("beta".into()),
10885|       |            Event::End(Tag::Strong),
10886|       |            Event::End(Tag::Paragraph),
10887|       |        ];
10888|       |        let (_spans, next) =
10889|       |            renderer.parse_inline_spans_with_breaks(&strong_events, 1, Tag::Paragraph, true);
10890|       |        assert_eq!(next, strong_events.len());
10891|       |
10892|       |        let (spans_no_break, _) = renderer.parse_inline_spans(&strong_events, 1, Tag::Paragraph);
10893|       |        let mut saw_non_strong = false;
10894|       |        let mut strong_text = None;
10895|       |        for span in &spans_no_break {
10896|       |            if let InlineSpan::Strong(text) = span {
10897|       |                strong_text = Some(text.as_str());
10898|       |            } else {
10899|       |                saw_non_strong = true;
10900|       |            }
10901|       |        }
10902|       |        assert!(saw_non_strong);
10903|       |        let mut saw_match = false;
10904|       |        let mut saw_miss = false;
10905|       |        let mut saw_none = false;
10906|       |        for candidate in [strong_text, Some("nope"), None] {
10907|       |            if let Some(text) = candidate {
10908|       |                if text.contains("alpha beta") {
10909|       |                    saw_match = true;
10910|       |                } else {
10911|       |                    saw_miss = true;
10912|       |                }
10913|       |            } else {
10914|       |                saw_none = true;
10915|       |            }
10916|       |        }
10917|       |        assert!(saw_match);
10918|       |        assert!(saw_miss);
10919|       |        assert!(saw_none);
10920|       |    }
10921|       |

10927|       |            Event::Text("alpha".into()),

10930|       |            Event::End(Tag::Paragraph),

10938|       |    #[test]
10939|       |    fn test_collect_blockquotes_nested_and_breaks() {
10940|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
10941|       |        let events = vec![
10942|       |            Event::Start(Tag::BlockQuote),
10943|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Paragraph),
10944|       |            Event::Text("Line1\nLine2".into()),
10945|       |            Event::End(Tag::Paragraph),
10946|       |            Event::SoftBreak,
10947|       |            Event::Start(Tag::BlockQuote),
10948|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Paragraph),
10949|       |            Event::Text("Nested".into()),
10950|       |            Event::End(Tag::Paragraph),
10951|       |            Event::End(Tag::BlockQuote),
10952|       |            Event::End(Tag::BlockQuote),
10953|       |        ];
10954|       |        let mut slugs = HashMap::new();
10955|       |        let (quotes, next) = renderer.collect_blockquotes(&events, 1, 1, &mut slugs);
10956|       |        assert_eq!(next, events.len());
10957|       |        assert!(quotes.iter().any(|(depth, _)| *depth == 1));
10958|       |        assert!(quotes.iter().any(|(depth, _)| *depth == 2));
10959|       |    }
10960|       |

11121|       |    fn test_parse_element_variants() {
11122|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
11123|       |        let mut slugs = HashMap::new();
11124|       |        let mut elements = Vec::new();
11125|       |
11126|       |        let paragraph = vec![
11127|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Paragraph),
11128|       |            Event::Text("para".into()),
11129|       |            Event::End(Tag::Paragraph),
11130|       |        ];
11131|       |        let next = renderer.parse_element(&paragraph, 0, &mut elements, &mut slugs);
11132|       |        assert_eq!(next, paragraph.len());
11133|       |
11134|       |        let heading = vec![
11135|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Heading(pulldown_cmark::HeadingLevel::H2, None, vec![])),
11136|       |            Event::Text("Heading".into()),
11137|       |            Event::End(Tag::Heading(pulldown_cmark::HeadingLevel::H2, None, vec![])),
11138|       |        ];
11139|       |        let next = renderer.parse_element(&heading, 0, &mut elements, &mut slugs);
11140|       |        assert_eq!(next, heading.len());
11141|       |
11142|       |        let code_block = vec![
11143|       |            Event::Start(Tag::CodeBlock(pulldown_cmark::CodeBlockKind::Fenced(
11144|       |                "rust".into(),
11145|       |            ))),
11146|       |            Event::Text("fn main() {}\n".into()),
11147|       |            Event::End(Tag::CodeBlock(pulldown_cmark::CodeBlockKind::Fenced(
11148|       |                "rust".into(),
11149|       |            ))),
11150|       |        ];
11151|       |        let next = renderer.parse_element(&code_block, 0, &mut elements, &mut slugs);
11152|       |        assert_eq!(next, code_block.len());
11153|       |
11154|       |        let list = vec![
11155|       |            Event::Start(Tag::List(None)),
11156|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Item),
11157|       |            Event::Text("item".into()),
11158|       |            Event::End(Tag::Item),
11159|       |            Event::End(Tag::List(None)),
11160|       |        ];
11161|       |        let next = renderer.parse_element(&list, 0, &mut elements, &mut slugs);
11162|       |        assert_eq!(next, list.len());
11163|       |
11164|       |        let quote = vec![
11165|       |            Event::Start(Tag::BlockQuote),
11166|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Paragraph),
11167|       |            Event::Text("quote".into()),
11168|       |            Event::End(Tag::Paragraph),
11169|       |            Event::End(Tag::BlockQuote),
11170|       |        ];
11171|       |        let next = renderer.parse_element(&quote, 0, &mut elements, &mut slugs);
11172|       |        assert_eq!(next, quote.len());
11173|       |
11174|       |        let table = vec![
11175|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Table(vec![Alignment::Left])),
11176|       |            Event::Start(Tag::TableHead),
11177|       |            Event::Start(Tag::TableRow),
11178|       |            Event::Start(Tag::TableCell),
11179|       |            Event::Text("H".into()),
11180|       |            Event::End(Tag::TableCell),

11182|       |            Event::End(Tag::TableHead),
11183|       |            Event::Start(Tag::TableRow),

11187|       |            Event::End(Tag::TableRow),
11188|       |            Event::End(Tag::Table(vec![Alignment::Left])),
11189|       |        ];

11191|       |        assert_eq!(next, table.len());
11192|       |

11194|       |        let next = renderer.parse_element(&rule, 0, &mut elements, &mut slugs);
11195|       |        assert_eq!(next, rule.len());
11196|       |    }

11199|       |    fn test_parse_code_block_with_language() -> Result<()> {
11200|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
11201|       |        let elements = renderer.parse("Intro\n\n```rust\nfn main() {}\n```")?;
11202|       |        let mut rust_lang = None;
11203|       |        for element in &elements {
11204|       |            if let MarkdownElement::CodeBlock { language, .. } = element {
11205|       |                rust_lang = language.as_deref();
11206|       |            }
11207|       |        }
11208|       |        assert_eq!(rust_lang, Some("rust"));
11209|       |        Ok(())
11210|       |    }

11259|       |            InlineSpan::Code("c".to_string()),
11260|       |            InlineSpan::Strong("s".to_string()),

11265|       |                url: "https://example.com".to_string(),

11268|       |                src: "img.png".to_string(),
11269|       |                alt: "alt".to_string(),
11270|       |                title: Some("title".to_string()),
11271|       |            },
11272|       |        ];
11273|       |        let hash = MarkdownRenderer::hash_inline_spans(&spans);
11274|       |        let hash2 = MarkdownRenderer::hash_inline_spans(&[InlineSpan::Text("other".to_string())]);
11275|       |        assert_ne!(hash, hash2);
11276|       |    }

11279|       |    fn test_highlight_segments_edge_cases() {
11280|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
11281|       |        assert!(renderer.highlight_segments("", Some("a")).is_empty());
11282|       |        assert_eq!(
11283|       |            renderer.highlight_segments("abc", None),
11284|       |            vec![(0..3, false)]
11285|       |        );
11286|       |        assert_eq!(
11287|       |            renderer.highlight_segments("abc", Some("")),
11288|       |            vec![(0..3, false)]
11289|       |        );
11290|       |        let segments = renderer.highlight_segments("Hello", Some("ell"));
11291|       |        assert!(segments.iter().any(|(_, highlighted)| *highlighted));
11292|       |    }

11294|       |    #[test]
11295|       |    fn test_resolve_table_widths_branches() {
11296|       |        assert!(MarkdownRenderer::resolve_table_widths(50.0, &[], &[]).is_empty());
11297|       |        assert_eq!(
11298|       |            MarkdownRenderer::resolve_table_widths(100.0, &[10.0, 10.0], &[20.0, 30.0]),
11299|       |            vec![20.0, 30.0]
11300|       |        );
11301|       |
11302|       |        assert_eq!(
11303|       |            MarkdownRenderer::resolve_table_widths(10.0, &[0.0, 0.0], &[0.0, 0.0]),
11304|       |            vec![5.0, 5.0]
11305|       |        );
11306|       |
11307|       |        assert_eq!(
11308|       |            MarkdownRenderer::resolve_table_widths(10.0, &[10.0, 10.0], &[20.0, 20.0]),
11309|       |            vec![5.0, 5.0]
11310|       |        );
11311|       |
11312|       |        assert_eq!(
11313|       |            MarkdownRenderer::resolve_table_widths(30.0, &[10.0, 10.0], &[10.0, 10.0]),
11314|       |            vec![15.0, 15.0]
11315|       |        );
11316|       |
11317|       |        let widths = MarkdownRenderer::resolve_table_widths(30.0, &[10.0, 10.0], &[20.0, 30.0]);
11318|       |        assert_eq!(widths.len(), 2);
11319|       |        assert!((widths[0] + widths[1] - 30.0).abs() < 0.1);
11320|       |    }

11359|       |        let mut tables = elements.iter().filter_map(|el| match el {
11360|       |            MarkdownElement::Table {
11361|       |                headers,

11363|       |                alignments,
11364|       |            } => Some((headers, rows, alignments)),
11365|       |            _ => None,

11385|       |    fn test_handle_width_change_skips_duplicate_frame() {
11386|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
11387|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
11388|       |        renderer.handle_width_change(&ctx, 42, WidthChange::Large);
11389|       |        renderer.handle_width_change(&ctx, 42, WidthChange::Large);
11390|       |        let metrics = renderer.table_metrics.borrow();
11391|       |        let entry = metrics.entry(42).expect("metrics entry");
11392|       |        assert_eq!(entry.last_discard_frame, Some(ctx.frame_nr()));

11486|       |                None,

11618|       |            ui.allocate_ui_at_rect(
11619|       |                egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0), egui::vec2(200.0, 80.0)),

11622|       |                        renderer.build_layout_job(ui.style(), &spans, 30.0, false, Align::LEFT);

11625|       |            );

11630|       |            ui.allocate_ui_at_rect(
11631|       |                egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0), egui::vec2(200.0, 80.0)),

11634|       |                        renderer.build_layout_job(ui.style(), &spans, 30.0, false, Align::LEFT);

11641|       |    #[test]
11642|       |    fn test_aligned_text_origin_anchors_to_rect() {
11643|       |        let rect = egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(10.0, 20.0), egui::vec2(100.0, 20.0));
11644|       |        let galley = Arc::new(Galley {
11645|       |            job: Arc::new(LayoutJob::default()),
11646|       |            rows: Vec::new(),
11647|       |            elided: false,
11648|       |            rect: egui::Rect::from_min_max(egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0), egui::pos2(20.0, 10.0)),
11649|       |            mesh_bounds: egui::Rect::NOTHING,
11650|       |            num_vertices: 0,
11651|       |            num_indices: 0,
11652|       |            pixels_per_point: 1.0,
11653|       |        });
11654|       |        let left_origin = MarkdownRenderer::aligned_text_origin(rect, &galley, Align::LEFT);
11655|       |        let center_origin = MarkdownRenderer::aligned_text_origin(rect, &galley, Align::Center);
11656|       |        let right_origin = MarkdownRenderer::aligned_text_origin(rect, &galley, Align::RIGHT);
11657|       |
11658|       |        assert_eq!(left_origin.x, rect.left());
11659|       |        assert_eq!(center_origin.x, rect.center().x - galley.rect.center().x);
11660|       |        assert_eq!(right_origin.x, rect.right() - galley.rect.right());
11661|       |        assert_eq!(left_origin.y, rect.top());
11662|       |    }
11663|       |

11665|       |    fn test_layout_job_center_rect_is_origin() {
11666|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
11667|       |        let spans = vec![InlineSpan::Text("Item".to_string())];
11668|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
11669|       |            let build = renderer.build_layout_job(ui.style(), &spans, 200.0, true, Align::Center);
11670|       |            let galley = ui.fonts(|f| f.layout_job(build.job.clone()));
11671|       |            assert!(galley.rect.center().x.abs() <= 0.01);
11672|       |        });
11673|       |    }

11723|       |    #[test]
11724|       |    fn test_find_syntax_for_language_direct_name_match() -> Result<()> {
11725|       |        let mut renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
11726|       |        let syntax = SyntaxDefinition::load_from_str(
11727|       |            r#"
11728|       |name: testlang
11729|       |scope: source.testlang
11730|       |file_extensions: [tst]
11731|       |contexts:
11732|       |  main:
11733|       |    - match: '.'
11734|       |      scope: source.testlang
11735|       |"#,
11736|       |            true,
11737|       |            None,
11738|       |        )
11739|       |        .expect("syntax");
11740|       |        let mut builder = renderer.syntax_set.clone().into_builder();
11741|       |        builder.add(syntax);
11742|       |        renderer.syntax_set = builder.build();
11743|       |        assert!(renderer.find_syntax_for_language("testlang").is_some());
11744|       |        Ok(())
11745|       |    }

11869|       |    fn test_parse_blockquote_with_nested_blocks() {
11870|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
11871|       |        let md = r#"> # Quote Heading
11872|       |> Paragraph **bold** *em* ~~strike~~ [link](https://example.com)
11873|       |> ![alt](assets/samples/logo.svg "Logo")
11874|       |> - Item one
11875|       |>   - Nested item
11876|       |> 
11877|       |> ```rust
11878|       |> let x = 1;
11879|       |> ```
11880|       |> 
11881|       |> | H1 | H2 |
11882|       |> | --- | --- |
11883|       |> | 🎉 | ![a](assets/samples/logo.svg "Img") |
11884|       |"#;
11885|       |        let elements = renderer.parse(md).expect("parse");
11886|       |        assert!(elements
11887|       |            .iter()
11888|       |            .any(|e| matches!(e, MarkdownElement::Quote { .. })));
11889|       |    }

11891|       |    #[test]
11892|       |    fn test_parse_list_with_heading_and_table() {
11893|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
11894|       |        let md = r#"- # Item heading
11895|       |  Paragraph text with `code` and **strong**
11896|       |  - Nested child
11897|       |- | A | B |
11898|       |  | - | - |
11899|       |  | 1 | 2 |
11900|       |- ```rust
11901|       |  fn main() {}
11902|       |  ```
11903|       |- > Quote in list
11904|       |- ---
11905|       |"#;
11906|       |        let elements = renderer.parse(md).expect("parse");
11907|       |        assert!(elements
11908|       |            .iter()
11909|       |            .any(|e| matches!(e, MarkdownElement::List { .. })));
11910|       |    }

12054|       |    fn test_get_or_load_image_texture_stale_cache_evicted() -> Result<()> {
12055|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
12056|       |        let temp = tempdir()?;
12057|       |        let image_path = temp.path().join("image.png");
12058|       |        std::fs::write(&image_path, tiny_png_bytes())?;
12059|       |        let resolved = image_path.to_string_lossy().to_string();
12060|       |
12061|       |        with_test_ui(|ctx, ui| {
12062|       |            let tex = ctx.load_texture(
12063|       |                "stale-image",
12064|       |                egui::ColorImage::new([2, 2], Color32::WHITE),
12065|       |                egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR,
12066|       |            );
12067|       |            renderer.image_textures.borrow_mut().insert(
12068|       |                resolved.clone(),
12069|       |                ImageCacheEntry {
12070|       |                    texture: tex,
12071|       |                    size: [2, 2],
12072|       |                    modified: Some(SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH),
12073|       |                    byte_size: ImageCacheEntry::estimate_bytes([2, 2]),
12074|       |                    degraded: false,
12075|       |                },
12076|       |            );
12077|       |
12078|       |            let result = renderer.get_or_load_image_texture(ui, &resolved);
12079|       |            assert!(result.is_none());

12081|       |            assert!(renderer.image_pending.borrow().contains(&resolved));
12082|       |        });
12083|       |
12084|       |        Ok(())
12085|       |    }

12109|       |    fn test_poll_image_results_records_failure() {
12110|       |        let mut renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
12111|       |        let (result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::unbounded::<ImageLoadResult>();
12112|       |        renderer.image_result_rx = result_rx;
12113|       |        renderer
12114|       |            .image_pending
12115|       |            .borrow_mut()
12116|       |            .insert("failed.png".to_string());
12117|       |
12118|       |        result_tx
12119|       |            .send(ImageLoadResult::Failed {
12120|       |                key: "failed.png".to_string(),
12121|       |                generation: 0,
12122|       |            })
12123|       |            .expect("send");
12124|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
12125|       |        renderer.poll_image_results(&ctx);
12126|       |        assert!(renderer
12127|       |            .image_failures
12128|       |            .borrow()
12129|       |            .contains_key(&"failed.png".to_string()));
12130|       |        assert!(!renderer.image_pending.borrow().contains("failed.png"));
12131|       |    }

12133|       |    #[test]
12134|       |    fn test_enqueue_image_job_full_returns_err() {
12135|       |        let mut renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
12136|       |        let (job_tx, job_rx) = bounded(1);
12137|       |        renderer.image_job_tx = job_tx;
12138|       |        let request = ImageLoadRequest {
12139|       |            key: "full".to_string(),
12140|       |            source: ImageLoadSource::Embedded(b"png"),
12141|       |            generation: 0,
12142|       |        };
12143|       |        renderer.image_job_tx.send(request).expect("fill queue");
12144|       |        let request = ImageLoadRequest {
12145|       |            key: "full-2".to_string(),
12146|       |            source: ImageLoadSource::Embedded(b"png"),
12147|       |            generation: 0,
12148|       |        };
12149|       |        assert!(renderer.enqueue_image_job(request).is_err());
12150|       |        drop(job_rx);
12151|       |    }
12152|       |
12153|       |    /// Integration test: solid-color PNGs that are tiny on disk but large when
12154|       |    /// decoded.  Verifies that (a) insert() does not evict by bytes, and
12155|       |    /// (b) enforce_budget() degrades far entries to bring memory under budget.
12156|       |    #[test]
12157|       |    fn test_cache_pressure_with_large_decoded_images() {
12158|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
12159|       |
12160|       |        // Each 200x200 image decodes to 200*200*4 = 160,000 bytes.
12161|       |        // Set cache limit to hold exactly 2 images worth (320,000 bytes).
12162|       |        renderer.image_textures.borrow_mut().max_bytes = 160_000 * 2;
12163|       |
12164|       |        let temp = tempdir().expect("temp dir");
12165|       |        let mut keys = Vec::new();
12166|       |        for i in 0..4 {
12167|       |            let name = format!("img_{i}.png");
12168|       |            let path = temp.path().join(&name);
12169|       |            std::fs::write(&path, solid_png_bytes(200, 200)).expect("write png");
12170|       |            keys.push(path.to_string_lossy().to_string());
12171|       |        }
12172|       |
12173|       |        // Verify the PNGs are tiny on disk
12174|       |        let disk_size = std::fs::metadata(temp.path().join("img_0.png"))
12175|       |            .unwrap()
12176|       |            .len();
12177|       |        assert!(
12178|       |            disk_size < 2000,
12179|       |            "solid PNG should be tiny on disk, got {disk_size} bytes"
12180|       |        );
12181|       |
12182|       |        with_test_ui(|ctx, _ui| {
12183|       |            // Simulate the background loader completing all 4 images.
12184|       |            // insert() no longer evicts by bytes — all 4 should be present.
12185|       |            for key in keys.iter() {
12186|       |                let image = egui::ColorImage::new([200, 200], Color32::WHITE);
12187|       |                let tex =
12188|       |                    ctx.load_texture(format!("img:{key}"), image, egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR);
12189|       |                renderer.store_image_texture(key, tex, [200, 200], None);
12190|       |            }
12191|       |
12192|       |            {
12193|       |                let cache = renderer.image_textures.borrow();
12194|       |                assert_eq!(
12195|       |                    cache.entries.len(),
12196|       |                    4,
12197|       |                    "all 4 images should be present (no byte eviction on insert)"
12198|       |                );
12199|       |                assert_eq!(cache.current_bytes(), 160_000 * 4);
12200|       |            }
12201|       |
12202|       |            // Set viewport distances: keys[0] near, keys[3] far
12203|       |            {
12204|       |                let mut cache = renderer.image_textures.borrow_mut();
12205|       |                let mut distances = HashMap::new();
12206|       |                for (i, key) in keys.iter().enumerate() {
12207|       |                    distances.insert(key.clone(), (i as f32 + 1.0) * 1000.0);
12208|       |                }
12209|       |                cache.update_viewport_distances(distances);
12210|       |            }
12211|       |
12212|       |            // enforce_budget should degrade the furthest entries
12213|       |            {
12214|       |                let mut cache = renderer.image_textures.borrow_mut();
12215|       |                cache.enforce_budget(ctx, 0.0);
12216|       |            }
12217|       |
12218|       |            let cache = renderer.image_textures.borrow();
12219|       |            // All entries should still exist (degraded, not evicted)
12220|       |            for key in &keys {
12221|       |                assert!(cache.contains_key(key), "entry should still exist: {key}");
12222|       |            }
12223|       |            // Memory should be at or under budget
12224|       |            assert!(
12225|       |                cache.current_bytes() <= 160_000 * 2,
12226|       |                "total_bytes should be under budget, got {}",
12227|       |                cache.current_bytes()
12228|       |            );
12229|       |            // Near entry should not be degraded
12230|       |            assert!(
12231|       |                !cache.entries.get(&keys[0]).unwrap().degraded,
12232|       |                "nearest image should not be degraded"
12233|       |            );
12234|       |        });
12235|       |    }
12236|       |
12237|       |    /// Verify that solid PNGs produce the expected compression ratio:
12238|       |    /// small on disk, large decoded.
12239|       |    #[test]
12240|       |    fn test_solid_png_compression_ratio() {
12241|       |        let bytes = solid_png_bytes(500, 500);
12242|       |        // 500x500 decodes to 1,000,000 bytes (1 MB), but the PNG should be
12243|       |        // well under 10 KB on disk due to solid-color compression.
12244|       |        assert!(
12245|       |            bytes.len() < 10_000,
12246|       |            "solid 500x500 PNG should compress well, got {} bytes",
12247|       |            bytes.len()
12248|       |        );
12249|       |        // Verify it decodes to the expected dimensions
12250|       |        let (img, w, h) =
12251|       |            image_decode::bytes_to_color_image_guess(&bytes, None).expect("should decode");
12252|       |        assert_eq!(w, 500);
12253|       |        assert_eq!(h, 500);
12254|       |        assert_eq!(img.pixels.len(), 500 * 500);
12255|       |    }

12370|       |    #[test]
12371|       |    fn test_render_table_tablebuilder_fixed_columns_no_clip() {
12372|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
12373|       |        let _forced = ForcedTablePolicies::new(vec![
12374|       |            ColumnPolicy::Fixed {
12375|       |                width: 80.0,
12376|       |                clip: false,
12377|       |            },
12378|       |            ColumnPolicy::Fixed {
12379|       |                width: 100.0,
12380|       |                clip: false,
12381|       |            },
12382|       |        ]);
12383|       |        let headers = vec![
12384|       |            vec![InlineSpan::Text("Left".to_string())],
12385|       |            vec![InlineSpan::Text("Right".to_string())],
12386|       |        ];
12387|       |        let rows = vec![vec![
12388|       |            vec![InlineSpan::Text("A".to_string())],
12389|       |            vec![InlineSpan::Text("B".to_string())],
12390|       |        ]];
12391|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
12392|       |            let layout = *ui.layout();
12393|       |            ui.allocate_ui_with_layout(Vec2::new(400.0, 0.0), layout, |ui| {
12394|       |                ui.spacing_mut().item_spacing.x = 6.0;
12395|       |                ui.set_width(400.0);
12396|       |                let table_id = renderer.compute_table_id(&headers, &rows, &[], 16);
12397|       |                renderer.render_table_tablebuilder(ui, &headers, &rows, &[], table_id);
12398|       |            });
12399|       |        });

12457|       |    #[test]
12458|       |    fn test_render_table_estimates_wrapped_row_height() {
12459|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
12460|       |        let _forced = ForcedTablePolicies::new(vec![ColumnPolicy::Fixed {
12461|       |            width: 80.0,
12462|       |            clip: false,
12463|       |        }]);
12464|       |        let headers = vec![vec![InlineSpan::Text("Params".to_string())]];
12465|       |        let rows = vec![vec![vec![InlineSpan::Text(
12466|       |            "word word word word word word word word".to_string(),
12467|       |        )]]];
12468|       |        let table_id = renderer.compute_table_id(&headers, &rows, &[], 18);
12469|       |
12470|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
12471|       |            let layout = *ui.layout();
12472|       |            ui.allocate_ui_with_layout(Vec2::new(120.0, 0.0), layout, |ui| {
12473|       |                ui.spacing_mut().item_spacing.x = 6.0;
12474|       |                ui.set_width(120.0);
12475|       |                renderer.render_table_tablebuilder(ui, &headers, &rows, &[], table_id);
12476|       |            });
12477|       |        });
12478|       |
12479|       |        // Check stored row height in metrics
12480|       |        let height = renderer
12481|       |            .table_metrics
12482|       |            .borrow()
12483|       |            .entry(table_id)
12484|       |            .and_then(|e| e.row(0))
12485|       |            .map(|r| r.max_height)
12486|       |            .unwrap_or(0.0);
12487|       |        assert!(height > renderer.row_height_fallback() + 0.5);
12488|       |    }

12598|       |    #[test]
12599|       |    fn test_list_marker_info_any_indent_variants() {
12600|       |        assert!(MarkdownRenderer::list_marker_info_any_indent("").is_none());
12601|       |        assert!(MarkdownRenderer::list_marker_info_any_indent("   ").is_none());
12602|       |
12603|       |        let (rest, indent, content_indent, leading_cols) =
12604|       |            MarkdownRenderer::list_marker_info_any_indent(" 1. item").expect("marker");
12605|       |        assert_eq!(rest, "item");
12606|       |        assert_eq!(indent, 1);
12607|       |        assert_eq!(content_indent, 4);
12608|       |        assert_eq!(leading_cols, 1);
12609|       |
12610|       |        let (rest, indent, content_indent, leading_cols) =
12611|       |            MarkdownRenderer::list_marker_info_any_indent("2)\titem").expect("marker");
12612|       |        assert_eq!(rest, "item");
12613|       |        assert_eq!(indent, 2);
12614|       |        assert_eq!(content_indent, 4);
12615|       |        assert_eq!(leading_cols, 0);
12616|       |    }

12631|       |    #[test]
12632|       |    fn test_collect_until_tag_end_breaks_and_code() {
12633|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
12634|       |        let events = vec![
12635|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Emphasis),
12636|       |            Event::Text("alpha".into()),
12637|       |            Event::Code("beta".into()),
12638|       |            Event::SoftBreak,
12639|       |            Event::Html("<br>".into()),
12640|       |            Event::HardBreak,
12641|       |            Event::Html("<span>".into()),
12642|       |            Event::End(Tag::Emphasis),
12643|       |        ];
12644|       |        let (text, next) = renderer.collect_until_tag_end(&events, 1, Tag::Emphasis, true);
12645|       |        assert_eq!(next, events.len());
12646|       |        assert_eq!(text, "alphabeta\n\n\n");
12647|       |
12648|       |        let (text, next) = renderer.collect_until_tag_end(&events, 1, Tag::Emphasis, false);
12649|       |        assert_eq!(next, events.len());
12650|       |        assert_eq!(text, "alphabeta   ");
12651|       |
12652|       |        let unclosed = vec![Event::Text("tail".into()), Event::SoftBreak];
12653|       |        let (text, next) = renderer.collect_until_tag_end(&unclosed, 0, Tag::Emphasis, true);
12654|       |        assert_eq!(next, unclosed.len());
12655|       |        assert_eq!(text, "tail\n");
12656|       |    }

12658|       |    #[test]
12659|       |    fn test_parse_inline_spans_with_breaks_link_image_and_unclosed() {
12660|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
12661|       |        let events = vec![
12662|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Paragraph),
12663|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Link(
12664|       |                LinkType::Inline,
12665|       |                "https://example.com".into(),
12666|       |                "".into(),
12667|       |            )),
12668|       |            Event::Text("link".into()),
12669|       |            Event::End(Tag::Link(
12670|       |                LinkType::Inline,
12671|       |                "https://example.com".into(),
12672|       |                "".into(),
12673|       |            )),
12674|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Image(
12675|       |                LinkType::Inline,
12676|       |                "img.png".into(),
12677|       |                "Title".into(),
12678|       |            )),
12679|       |            Event::Text("alt".into()),
12680|       |            Event::End(Tag::Image(
12681|       |                LinkType::Inline,
12682|       |                "img.png".into(),
12683|       |                "Title".into(),
12684|       |            )),
12685|       |            Event::FootnoteReference("note".into()),
12686|       |            Event::End(Tag::Paragraph),
12687|       |        ];
12688|       |        let (spans, next) =
12689|       |            renderer.parse_inline_spans_with_breaks(&events, 1, Tag::Paragraph, true);
12690|       |        assert_eq!(next, events.len());
12691|       |        assert!(spans
12692|       |            .iter()
12693|       |            .any(|span| matches!(span, InlineSpan::Link { .. })));
12694|       |        assert!(spans
12695|       |            .iter()
12696|       |            .any(|span| matches!(span, InlineSpan::Image { .. })));
12697|       |
12698|       |        let unclosed = vec![Event::Text("tail".into())];
12699|       |        let (spans, next) =
12700|       |            renderer.parse_inline_spans_with_breaks(&unclosed, 0, Tag::Paragraph, true);
12701|       |        assert_eq!(next, unclosed.len());
12702|       |        assert_eq!(MarkdownRenderer::spans_plain_text(&spans), "tail");
12703|       |    }

12737|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Paragraph),

12759|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Heading(pulldown_cmark::HeadingLevel::H2, None, vec![])),
12760|       |            Event::Text("Dup".into()),
12761|       |            Event::End(Tag::Heading(pulldown_cmark::HeadingLevel::H2, None, vec![])),
12762|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Heading(pulldown_cmark::HeadingLevel::H2, None, vec![])),
12763|       |            Event::Text("Dup".into()),
12764|       |            Event::End(Tag::Heading(pulldown_cmark::HeadingLevel::H2, None, vec![])),
12765|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Emphasis),
12766|       |            Event::Text("em".into()),
12767|       |            Event::End(Tag::Emphasis),
12768|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Strong),
12769|       |            Event::Text("strong".into()),
12770|       |            Event::End(Tag::Strong),
12771|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Strikethrough),
12772|       |            Event::Text("strike".into()),
12773|       |            Event::End(Tag::Strikethrough),
12774|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Link(
12775|       |                LinkType::Inline,
12776|       |                "https://example.com".into(),
12777|       |                "".into(),
12778|       |            )),
12779|       |            Event::Text("link".into()),
12780|       |            Event::End(Tag::Link(
12781|       |                LinkType::Inline,
12782|       |                "https://example.com".into(),
12783|       |                "".into(),
12784|       |            )),

12786|       |            Event::Text("alt".into()),

12790|       |            Event::FootnoteReference("note".into()),
12791|       |        ];

12795|       |        assert_eq!(items.len(), 1);
