12818|       |    #[test]
12819|       |    fn test_collect_blockquotes_unclosed_variants() -> Result<()> {
12820|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
12821|       |        let events = vec![
12822|       |            Event::Start(Tag::BlockQuote),
12823|       |            Event::Text("plain".into()),
12824|       |            Event::Code("code".into()),
12825|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Heading(pulldown_cmark::HeadingLevel::H2, None, vec![])),
12826|       |            Event::Text("Dup".into()),
12827|       |            Event::End(Tag::Heading(pulldown_cmark::HeadingLevel::H2, None, vec![])),
12828|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Heading(pulldown_cmark::HeadingLevel::H2, None, vec![])),
12829|       |            Event::Text("Dup".into()),
12830|       |            Event::End(Tag::Heading(pulldown_cmark::HeadingLevel::H2, None, vec![])),
12831|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Link(
12832|       |                LinkType::Inline,
12833|       |                "https://example.com".into(),
12834|       |                "".into(),
12835|       |            )),
12836|       |            Event::Text("link".into()),
12837|       |            Event::End(Tag::Link(
12838|       |                LinkType::Inline,
12839|       |                "https://example.com".into(),
12840|       |                "".into(),
12841|       |            )),
12842|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Image(LinkType::Inline, "img.png".into(), "".into())),
12843|       |            Event::Text("alt".into()),
12844|       |            Event::End(Tag::Image(LinkType::Inline, "img.png".into(), "".into())),
12845|       |            Event::FootnoteReference("note".into()),
12846|       |        ];
12847|       |        let mut slugs = HashMap::new();
12848|       |        let (quotes, next) = renderer.collect_blockquotes(&events, 1, 1, &mut slugs);
12849|       |        assert_eq!(next, events.len());
12850|       |        let mut ids = Vec::new();
12851|       |        for (_, blocks) in &quotes {
12852|       |            for block in blocks {
12853|       |                if let MarkdownElement::Header { id, .. } = block {
12854|       |                    ids.push(id.clone());
12855|       |                }
12856|       |            }
12857|       |        }
12858|       |        assert_eq!(ids.len(), 2);
12859|       |        assert_ne!(ids[0], ids[1]);
12860|       |        Ok(())
12861|       |    }

12921|       |    #[test]
12922|       |    fn test_paint_table_text_job_triggers_anchor_on_click() {
12923|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
12924|       |        let spans = vec![InlineSpan::Link {
12925|       |            text: "Jump".to_string(),
12926|       |            url: "#Section-One".to_string(),
12927|       |        }];
12928|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
12929|       |        let click_pos = std::cell::Cell::new(egui::pos2(1.0, 1.0));
12930|       |        let layout_input = screen_input(220.0, 120.0);
12931|       |        run_frame_with_input(&ctx, layout_input, |_, ui| {
12932|       |            ui.allocate_ui_at_rect(
12933|       |                egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0), egui::vec2(200.0, 80.0)),
12934|       |                |ui| {
12935|       |                    let build =
12936|       |                        renderer.build_layout_job(ui.style(), &spans, 60.0, false, Align::LEFT);
12937|       |                    let galley = ui.fonts(|f| f.layout_job(build.job.clone()));
12938|       |                    let (rect, _response) = ui.allocate_exact_size(
12939|       |                        egui::vec2(120.0, galley.size().y),
12940|       |                        egui::Sense::click(),
12941|       |                    );
12942|       |                    let text_origin =
12943|       |                        MarkdownRenderer::aligned_text_origin(rect, &galley, build.job.halign);
12944|       |                    click_pos.set(text_origin + egui::vec2(2.0, 2.0));
12945|       |                },
12946|       |            );
12947|       |        });
12948|       |
12949|       |        let input = input_with_click(click_pos.get(), egui::PointerButton::Primary);
12950|       |        run_frame_with_input(&ctx, input, |_, ui| {
12951|       |            ui.allocate_ui_at_rect(
12952|       |                egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0), egui::vec2(200.0, 80.0)),
12953|       |                |ui| {
12954|       |                    let build =
12955|       |                        renderer.build_layout_job(ui.style(), &spans, 60.0, false, Align::LEFT);
12956|       |                    renderer.paint_table_text_job(ui, 120.0, build);
12957|       |                },
12958|       |            );
12959|       |        });
12960|       |
12961|       |        assert_eq!(
12962|       |            renderer.take_pending_anchor(),
12963|       |            Some("section-one".to_string())
12964|       |        );
12965|       |    }

12976|       |        let layout_input = screen_input(220.0, 120.0);
12977|       |        run_frame_with_input(&ctx, layout_input, |_, ui| {
12978|       |            ui.allocate_ui_at_rect(
12979|       |                egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0), egui::vec2(200.0, 80.0)),

12985|       |                        egui::vec2(120.0, galley.size().y),

12996|       |        input
12997|       |            .events
12998|       |            .push(egui::Event::PointerMoved(hover_pos.get()));

13118|       |    // ── Stage 1: content-driven natural width tests ──────────────────────
13119|       |
13120|       |    #[test]
13121|       |    fn test_natural_widths_two_short_columns() {
13122|       |        // Two columns with content wider than policy → content drives widths
13123|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
13124|       |        let headers = vec![
13125|       |            vec![InlineSpan::Text("Name".to_string())],
13126|       |            vec![InlineSpan::Text("Value".to_string())],
13127|       |        ];
13128|       |        let stats = vec![
13129|       |            ColumnStat {
13130|       |                max_graphemes: 20,
13131|       |                ..Default::default()
13132|       |            },
13133|       |            ColumnStat {
13134|       |                max_graphemes: 18,
13135|       |                ..Default::default()
13136|       |            },
13137|       |        ];
13138|       |        // Small resolved widths so content should dominate
13139|       |        let resolved = vec![60.0, 60.0];
13140|       |        let widths = renderer.estimate_natural_column_widths(&stats, &headers, &resolved, 2);
13141|       |        assert_eq!(widths.len(), 2);
13142|       |        // body=14.0, char_width_factor=14*0.62=8.68, cell_padding=14*1.8=25.2
13143|       |        // col0: max(4,20)=20 → 20*8.68+25.2 = 198.8 → clamp(198.8, 56, 490) = 198.8
13144|       |        // col1: max(5,18)=18 → 18*8.68+25.2 = 181.44 → clamp(181.44, 56, 490) = 181.44
13145|       |        assert!(widths[0] > 150.0, "col 0 = {} should be > 150", widths[0]);
13146|       |        assert!(widths[1] > 150.0, "col 1 = {} should be > 150", widths[1]);
13147|       |    }
13148|       |
13149|       |    #[test]
13150|       |    fn test_natural_widths_uses_max_of_content_and_policy() {
13151|       |        // Short content (3 graphemes), policy width 98 should win
13152|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
13153|       |        let headers = vec![vec![InlineSpan::Text("ID".to_string())]];
13154|       |        let stats = vec![ColumnStat {
13155|       |            max_graphemes: 3,
13156|       |            ..Default::default()
13157|       |        }];
13158|       |        // body=14.0, char_width_factor=8.68, cell_padding=25.2
13159|       |        // header "ID" → width 2; max(2,3)=3 → 3*8.68+25.2 = 51.24
13160|       |        // policy = 98.0 → max(51.24, 98.0) = 98.0
13161|       |        let resolved = vec![98.0];
13162|       |        let widths = renderer.estimate_natural_column_widths(&stats, &headers, &resolved, 1);
13163|       |        assert_eq!(widths.len(), 1);
13164|       |        assert!(
13165|       |            (widths[0] - 98.0).abs() < 0.1,
13166|       |            "policy should win: got {}",
13167|       |            widths[0]
13168|       |        );
13169|       |    }
13170|       |
13171|       |    #[test]
13172|       |    fn test_natural_widths_clamped_to_bounds() {
13173|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
13174|       |        // body=14.0 → min_col_width=56.0, max_col_width=490.0
13175|       |        let min_col = 14.0 * 4.0; // 56
13176|       |        let max_col = 14.0 * 35.0; // 490
13177|       |
13178|       |        // Tiny content: 1 grapheme → 1*8.68+25.2 = 33.88, policy=10 → clamp to min
13179|       |        let headers_tiny = vec![vec![InlineSpan::Text("X".to_string())]];
13180|       |        let stats_tiny = vec![ColumnStat {
13181|       |            max_graphemes: 1,
13182|       |            ..Default::default()
13183|       |        }];
13184|       |        let resolved_tiny = vec![10.0];
13185|       |        let widths_tiny =
13186|       |            renderer.estimate_natural_column_widths(&stats_tiny, &headers_tiny, &resolved_tiny, 1);
13187|       |        assert!(
13188|       |            (widths_tiny[0] - min_col).abs() < 0.1,
13189|       |            "tiny should clamp to min {}: got {}",
13190|       |            min_col,
13191|       |            widths_tiny[0]
13192|       |        );
13193|       |
13194|       |        // Huge content: 200 graphemes → 200*8.68+25.2 = 1761.2 → clamp to max
13195|       |        let headers_huge = vec![vec![InlineSpan::Text("Description".to_string())]];
13196|       |        let stats_huge = vec![ColumnStat {
13197|       |            max_graphemes: 200,
13198|       |            ..Default::default()
13199|       |        }];
13200|       |        let resolved_huge = vec![10.0];
13201|       |        let widths_huge =
13202|       |            renderer.estimate_natural_column_widths(&stats_huge, &headers_huge, &resolved_huge, 1);
13203|       |        assert!(
13204|       |            (widths_huge[0] - max_col).abs() < 0.1,
13205|       |            "huge should clamp to max {}: got {}",
13206|       |            max_col,
13207|       |            widths_huge[0]
13208|       |        );
13209|       |    }
13210|       |
13211|       |    #[test]
13212|       |    fn test_natural_table_width_sums_correctly() {
13213|       |        // Empty input
13214|       |        let empty = MarkdownRenderer::natural_table_width(&[], 10.0);
13215|       |        assert!((empty - 0.0).abs() < f32::EPSILON);
13216|       |
13217|       |        // Single column → no spacing
13218|       |        let single = MarkdownRenderer::natural_table_width(&[120.0], 10.0);
13219|       |        assert!((single - 120.0).abs() < f32::EPSILON);
13220|       |
13221|       |        // Three columns: 100+200+150 = 450, spacing = 10*2 = 20, total = 470
13222|       |        let three = MarkdownRenderer::natural_table_width(&[100.0, 200.0, 150.0], 10.0);
13223|       |        assert!((three - 470.0).abs() < 0.1, "expected 470, got {}", three);
13224|       |    }

13229|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
13230|       |        let headers = vec![
13231|       |            vec![InlineSpan::Text("Key".to_string())],
13232|       |            vec![InlineSpan::Text("Value".to_string())],
13233|       |        ];
13234|       |        let stats = vec![
13235|       |            ColumnStat {
13236|       |                max_graphemes: 10,
13237|       |                ..Default::default()
13238|       |            },

13240|       |                max_graphemes: 12,
13241|       |                ..Default::default()
13242|       |            },
13243|       |        ];
13244|       |        let resolved = vec![60.0, 60.0];

13276|       |
13277|       |    // Test: column_count exceeds headers/stats/resolved_widths length
13278|       |    #[test]
13279|       |    fn test_natural_widths_handles_mismatched_lengths() {
13280|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
13281|       |        let headers = vec![vec![InlineSpan::Text("Name".into())]]; // only 1 header
13282|       |        let stats = vec![ColumnStat {
13283|       |            max_graphemes: 10,
13284|       |            longest_word: 5,
13285|       |            ..Default::default()
13286|       |        }]; // only 1 stat
13287|       |        let resolved = vec![98.0]; // only 1 width
13288|       |                                   // column_count = 3, exceeds all inputs
13289|       |        let widths = renderer.estimate_natural_column_widths(&stats, &headers, &resolved, 3);
13290|       |        assert_eq!(widths.len(), 3);
13291|       |        // First column should be content/policy-driven
13292|       |        assert!(widths[0] > 50.0);
13293|       |        // Remaining columns should be clamped to MIN (body * 4.0 = 56.0)
13294|       |        let min_col = renderer.font_sizes.body * 4.0;
13295|       |        assert!(
13296|       |            (widths[1] - min_col).abs() < 1.0,
13297|       |            "extra col should be MIN, got {}",
13298|       |            widths[1]
13299|       |        );
13300|       |        assert!(
13301|       |            (widths[2] - min_col).abs() < 1.0,
13302|       |            "extra col should be MIN, got {}",
13303|       |            widths[2]
13304|       |        );
13305|       |    }
13306|       |
13307|       |    // Test: CJK characters in header get double-width estimate
13308|       |    #[test]
13309|       |    fn test_natural_widths_cjk_header_gets_double_width() {
13310|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
13311|       |        let headers_ascii = vec![vec![InlineSpan::Text("Name".into())]]; // 4 display width
13312|       |        let headers_cjk = vec![vec![InlineSpan::Text(
13313|       |            "\u{540D}\u{524D}\u{5217}\u{5E45}".into(),
13314|       |        )]]; // 4 CJK chars = 8 display width
13315|       |        let stats = vec![ColumnStat::default()]; // no content
13316|       |        let resolved = vec![0.0]; // no policy
13317|       |        let widths_ascii =
13318|       |            renderer.estimate_natural_column_widths(&stats, &headers_ascii, &resolved, 1);
13319|       |        let widths_cjk =
13320|       |            renderer.estimate_natural_column_widths(&stats, &headers_cjk, &resolved, 1);
13321|       |        // CJK header should produce a wider natural width than ASCII header
13322|       |        assert!(
13323|       |            widths_cjk[0] > widths_ascii[0],
13324|       |            "CJK width {} should exceed ASCII width {}",
13325|       |            widths_cjk[0],
13326|       |            widths_ascii[0]
13327|       |        );
13328|       |    }

13335|       |  | --- | --- |
13336|       |  | code | `a|b` |
13337|       |";
13338|       |        let escaped_parent = MarkdownRenderer::escape_table_pipes_in_inline_code(md_parent);
13339|       |        assert!(escaped_parent.contains(PIPE_SENTINEL));
13340|       |

13344|       |      | --- | --- |
13345|       |      | code | `a|b` |
13346|       |";
13347|       |        let escaped_nested = MarkdownRenderer::escape_table_pipes_in_inline_code(md_nested);

13375|       |    fn test_escape_table_pipes_parent_indent_marker_within_range() {
13376|       |        let md = "\
13377|       |- Parent
13378|       |    - | H | I |
13379|       |      | --- | --- |
13380|       |      | `a|b` | c |
13381|       |";
13382|       |        let lines: Vec<&str> = md.split_inclusive('\n').collect();
13383|       |        let (level, rest) = MarkdownRenderer::table_line_info(lines[1]);
13384|       |        let _parent_indent =
13385|       |            MarkdownRenderer::parent_list_indent_for_line(&lines, 1, level, rest).unwrap();
13386|       |        let _leading_spaces =
13387|       |            MarkdownRenderer::list_marker_info_any_indent(rest).map(|(_, _, _, spaces)| spaces);
13388|       |        let escaped = MarkdownRenderer::escape_table_pipes_in_inline_code(md);
13389|       |        assert!(escaped.contains(PIPE_SENTINEL));
13390|       |    }

13393|       |    fn test_escape_table_pipes_parent_indent_strip_without_code_indent() {
13394|       |        let md = "\
13395|       |- Parent
13396|       |  continuation
13397|       |  | H | I |
13398|       |  | --- | --- |
13399|       |  | `a|b` | c |
13400|       |";
13401|       |        let lines: Vec<&str> = md.split_inclusive('\n').collect();
13402|       |        let (level, rest) = MarkdownRenderer::table_line_info(lines[1]);
13403|       |        let parent_indent =
13404|       |            MarkdownRenderer::parent_list_indent_for_line(&lines, 1, level, rest).unwrap();
13405|       |        let strip = MarkdownRenderer::strip_indent_columns(rest, parent_indent);
13406|       |        let code_strip = MarkdownRenderer::strip_indent_columns(rest, parent_indent + 4);
13407|       |        assert!(strip.is_some());
13408|       |        assert!(code_strip.is_none());
13409|       |        let escaped = MarkdownRenderer::escape_table_pipes_in_inline_code(md);
13410|       |        assert!(escaped.contains(PIPE_SENTINEL));
13411|       |    }

13531|       |    #[test]
13532|       |    fn test_parse_table_unclosed_and_noise() -> Result<()> {
13533|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
13534|       |        let events = vec![
13535|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Table(vec![Alignment::Left])),
13536|       |            Event::Start(Tag::TableHead),
13537|       |            Event::Start(Tag::TableRow),
13538|       |            Event::Start(Tag::TableCell),
13539|       |            Event::Text("H".into()),
13540|       |            Event::End(Tag::TableCell),
13541|       |            Event::End(Tag::TableRow),
13542|       |            Event::End(Tag::TableHead),
13543|       |            Event::Start(Tag::TableRow),
13544|       |            Event::Text("noise".into()),
13545|       |            Event::Start(Tag::TableCell),
13546|       |            Event::Text("R".into()),
13547|       |            Event::End(Tag::TableCell),
13548|       |            Event::End(Tag::TableRow),
13549|       |            Event::Text("after".into()),
13550|       |        ];
13551|       |        let (headers, rows, next) = renderer.parse_table(&events, 1);
13552|       |        assert_eq!(headers.len(), 1);
13553|       |        assert_eq!(rows.len(), 1);
13554|       |        assert_eq!(next, events.len());
13555|       |        Ok(())
13556|       |    }

13631|       |    fn test_parse_code_block_unclosed() -> Result<()> {
13632|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
13633|       |        let events = vec![
13634|       |            Event::Start(Tag::CodeBlock(pulldown_cmark::CodeBlockKind::Fenced(
13635|       |                "rust".into(),
13636|       |            ))),
13637|       |            Event::Text("fn main() {}".into()),
13638|       |            Event::Html("<span>".into()),
13639|       |        ];
13640|       |        let (code, language, next) = renderer.parse_code_block(&events, 0);
13641|       |        assert_eq!(language.as_deref(), Some("rust"));
13642|       |        assert_eq!(code, "fn main() {}");
13643|       |        assert_eq!(next, events.len());
13644|       |        Ok(())
13645|       |    }

13678|       |    fn test_render_table_tablebuilder_scaled_down_adjustments() {
13679|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
13680|       |        let headers = vec![
13681|       |            vec![InlineSpan::Text("A".to_string())],
13682|       |            vec![InlineSpan::Text("B".to_string())],
13683|       |            vec![InlineSpan::Text("C".to_string())],
13684|       |        ];
13685|       |        let rows = vec![vec![
13686|       |            vec![InlineSpan::Text("row1".to_string())],
13687|       |            vec![InlineSpan::Text("row2".to_string())],
13688|       |            vec![InlineSpan::Text("row3".to_string())],
13689|       |        ]];
13690|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
13691|       |        let widths = [220.0, 240.0];
13692|       |        for (idx, width) in widths.iter().enumerate() {
13693|       |            let _forced = ForcedTablePolicies::new(vec![
13694|       |                ColumnPolicy::Resizable {
13695|       |                    min: 40.0,
13696|       |                    preferred: 200.0,
13697|       |                    clip: false,
13698|       |                },
13699|       |                ColumnPolicy::Auto,
13700|       |                ColumnPolicy::Remainder { clip: false },
13701|       |            ]);
13702|       |            let input = screen_input(*width, 120.0);
13703|       |            run_frame_with_input(&ctx, input, |_, ui| {
13704|       |                ui.spacing_mut().item_spacing.x = 2.0;
13705|       |                let table_id = renderer.compute_table_id(&headers, &rows, &[], 90 + idx as u64);
13706|       |                renderer.render_table_tablebuilder(ui, &headers, &rows, &[], table_id);
13707|       |            });
13708|       |        }
13709|       |    }

13776|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
13777|       |        let spans = vec![InlineSpan::Text("   ".to_string())];
13778|       |        assert!(renderer.cell_single_emoji(&spans).is_none());

13803|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Image(LinkType::Inline, "img.png".into(), "".into())),

13805|       |            Event::End(Tag::Image(LinkType::Inline, "img.png".into(), "".into())),
13806|       |        ];

13808|       |        let mut slugs = HashMap::new();

13869|       |        let headers = vec![vec![InlineSpan::Text("H".to_string())]];
13870|       |        let rows: Vec<Vec<Vec<InlineSpan>>> = (0..(COLUMN_STATS_SAMPLE_ROWS + 1))
13871|       |            .map(|idx| vec![vec![InlineSpan::Text(format!("R{idx}"))]])
13872|       |            .collect();

13877|       |    #[test]
13878|       |    fn test_table_width_helpers_empty_inputs() {
13879|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
13880|       |        let table_id = 100u64;
13881|       |        let mut specs: Vec<ColumnSpec> = Vec::new();
13882|       |        renderer.apply_persisted_widths(table_id, &mut specs);
13883|       |        renderer.persist_resizable_widths(table_id, &[], &[]);
13884|       |        let change = renderer.record_resolved_widths(table_id, 0, &[]);
13885|       |        assert!(matches!(change, WidthChange::None));
13886|       |        assert!(renderer
13887|       |            .resolve_table_column_widths(table_id, &[], 10.0)
13888|       |            .is_empty());
13889|       |        assert_eq!(renderer.estimate_table_total_width(table_id, &[], 6.0), 0.0);
13890|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
13891|       |            let style = ui.style().clone();
13892|       |            let row = vec![vec![]];
13893|       |            let height =
13894|       |                renderer.estimate_table_row_height(ui, &style, &row, &[Align::LEFT], &[40.0], 12.0);
13895|       |            assert_eq!(height, 12.0);
13896|       |            let row = vec![
13897|       |                vec![InlineSpan::Text("a".to_string())],
13898|       |                vec![InlineSpan::Text("b".to_string())],
13899|       |            ];
13900|       |            let height =
13901|       |                renderer.estimate_table_row_height(ui, &style, &row, &[Align::LEFT], &[40.0], 12.0);
13902|       |            assert!(height >= 12.0);
13903|       |            let rect = egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0), egui::vec2(10.0, 10.0));
13904|       |            renderer.paint_table_dividers(
13905|       |                ui.painter(),
13906|       |                ui.visuals(),
13907|       |                rect,
13908|       |                rect,
13909|       |                &[],
13910|       |                0.0,
13911|       |                0.0,
13912|       |                0.0,
13913|       |                true,
13914|       |            );
13915|       |        });
13916|       |    }

13918|       |    #[test]
13919|       |    fn test_resolve_table_column_widths_ignores_invalid_stored_widths() {
13920|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
13921|       |        let table_id = 101u64;
13922|       |        {
13923|       |            let mut metrics = renderer.table_metrics.borrow_mut();
13924|       |            let entry = metrics.entry_mut(table_id);
13925|       |            entry.resolved_widths = vec![f32::NAN, -5.0];
13926|       |        }
13927|       |        let specs = vec![
13928|       |            resizable_spec(0, "A", 20.0, 40.0),
13929|       |            ColumnSpec::new(1, "B", ColumnPolicy::Auto, None),
13930|       |        ];
13931|       |        let widths = renderer.resolve_table_column_widths(table_id, &specs, 30.0);
13932|       |        assert_eq!(widths, vec![40.0, 30.0]);
13933|       |    }

13935|       |    #[test]
13936|       |    fn test_persist_resizable_widths_stores_new_width() {
13937|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
13938|       |        let table_id = 200u64;
13939|       |        let specs = vec![resizable_spec(0, "A", 20.0, 100.0)];
13940|       |        let widths = vec![140.0f32];
13941|       |        renderer.persist_resizable_widths(table_id, &specs, &widths);
13942|       |        let metrics = renderer.table_metrics.borrow();
13943|       |        let entry = metrics.entry(table_id).expect("entry created");
13944|       |        assert_eq!(entry.persisted_width(specs[0].policy_hash), Some(140.0));
13945|       |    }
13946|       |
13947|       |    #[test]
13948|       |    fn test_persist_resizable_widths_skips_small_delta() {
13949|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
13950|       |        let table_id = 201u64;
13951|       |        let spec = resizable_spec(0, "A", 20.0, 100.0);
13952|       |        let hash = spec.policy_hash;
13953|       |        let specs = vec![spec];
13954|       |        renderer.persist_resizable_widths(table_id, &specs, &[120.0]);
13955|       |        renderer.persist_resizable_widths(table_id, &specs, &[120.2]);
13956|       |        let metrics = renderer.table_metrics.borrow();
13957|       |        let entry = metrics.entry(table_id).expect("entry created");
13958|       |        let stored = entry.persisted_width(hash).unwrap_or_default();
13959|       |        assert!((stored - 120.0).abs() < 0.01);
13960|       |    }
13961|       |
13962|       |    #[test]
13963|       |    fn test_persist_resizable_widths_updates_on_large_delta() {
13964|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
13965|       |        let table_id = 202u64;
13966|       |        let spec = resizable_spec(0, "A", 20.0, 100.0);
13967|       |        let hash = spec.policy_hash;
13968|       |        let specs = vec![spec];
13969|       |        renderer.persist_resizable_widths(table_id, &specs, &[120.0]);
13970|       |        renderer.persist_resizable_widths(table_id, &specs, &[130.0]);
13971|       |        let metrics = renderer.table_metrics.borrow();
13972|       |        let entry = metrics.entry(table_id).expect("entry created");
13973|       |        assert_eq!(entry.persisted_width(hash), Some(130.0));
13974|       |    }

14124|       |    #[test]
14125|       |    fn test_link_at_pointer_outside_text_rect() {
14126|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
14127|       |        let spans = vec![InlineSpan::Link {
14128|       |            text: "link".to_string(),
14129|       |            url: "https://example.com".to_string(),
14130|       |        }];
14131|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
14132|       |        let outside_pos = std::cell::Cell::new(egui::pos2(1.0, 1.0));
14133|       |        let layout_input = screen_input(220.0, 120.0);
14134|       |        run_frame_with_input(&ctx, layout_input, |_, ui| {
14135|       |            let build = renderer.build_layout_job(ui.style(), &spans, 40.0, false, Align::LEFT);
14136|       |            let galley = ui.fonts(|f| f.layout_job(build.job.clone()));
14137|       |            let (rect, _response) =
14138|       |                ui.allocate_exact_size(egui::vec2(200.0, galley.size().y), egui::Sense::hover());
14139|       |            let text_origin =
14140|       |                MarkdownRenderer::aligned_text_origin(rect, &galley, build.job.halign);
14141|       |            let text_rect = galley.rect.translate(text_origin.to_vec2());
14142|       |            let pos = egui::pos2(rect.right() - 2.0, text_rect.center().y);
14143|       |            assert!(pos.x > text_rect.right());
14144|       |            outside_pos.set(pos);
14145|       |        });
14146|       |
14147|       |        let mut input = screen_input(220.0, 120.0);
14148|       |        input
14149|       |            .events
14150|       |            .push(egui::Event::PointerMoved(outside_pos.get()));
14151|       |        run_frame_with_input(&ctx, input, |_, ui| {
14152|       |            let build = renderer.build_layout_job(ui.style(), &spans, 40.0, false, Align::LEFT);
14153|       |            let galley = ui.fonts(|f| f.layout_job(build.job.clone()));
14154|       |            let (rect, response) =
14155|       |                ui.allocate_exact_size(egui::vec2(200.0, galley.size().y), egui::Sense::hover());
14156|       |            let text_origin =
14157|       |                MarkdownRenderer::aligned_text_origin(rect, &galley, build.job.halign);
14158|       |            let link = renderer.link_at_pointer(&response, &galley, &build, text_origin);
14159|       |            assert!(link.is_none());
14160|       |        });
14161|       |    }
14162|       |
14163|       |    #[test]
14164|       |    fn test_link_at_pointer_inside_text_rect_returns_link() {
14165|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
14166|       |        let spans = vec![InlineSpan::Link {
14167|       |            text: "link".to_string(),
14168|       |            url: "#anchor".to_string(),
14169|       |        }];
14170|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
14171|       |        let click_pos = std::cell::Cell::new(egui::pos2(1.0, 1.0));
14172|       |        let layout_input = screen_input(220.0, 120.0);
14173|       |        run_frame_with_input(&ctx, layout_input, |_, ui| {
14174|       |            let build = renderer.build_layout_job(ui.style(), &spans, 40.0, false, Align::LEFT);
14175|       |            let galley = ui.fonts(|f| f.layout_job(build.job.clone()));
14176|       |            let (rect, _response) =
14177|       |                ui.allocate_exact_size(egui::vec2(200.0, galley.size().y), egui::Sense::hover());
14178|       |            let text_origin =
14179|       |                MarkdownRenderer::aligned_text_origin(rect, &galley, build.job.halign);
14180|       |            click_pos.set(text_origin + egui::vec2(2.0, 2.0));
14181|       |        });
14182|       |
14183|       |        let mut input = screen_input(220.0, 120.0);
14184|       |        input
14185|       |            .events
14186|       |            .push(egui::Event::PointerMoved(click_pos.get()));
14187|       |        run_frame_with_input(&ctx, input, |_, ui| {
14188|       |            let build = renderer.build_layout_job(ui.style(), &spans, 40.0, false, Align::LEFT);
14189|       |            let galley = ui.fonts(|f| f.layout_job(build.job.clone()));
14190|       |            let (rect, response) =
14191|       |                ui.allocate_exact_size(egui::vec2(200.0, galley.size().y), egui::Sense::hover());
14192|       |            let text_origin =
14193|       |                MarkdownRenderer::aligned_text_origin(rect, &galley, build.job.halign);
14194|       |            let link = renderer.link_at_pointer(&response, &galley, &build, text_origin);
14195|       |            assert!(link.is_some());
14196|       |        });
14197|       |    }
14198|       |
14199|       |    #[test]
14200|       |    fn test_element_plain_text_list_quote_table_spacing() {
14201|       |        let list = MarkdownElement::List {
14202|       |            ordered: false,
14203|       |            items: vec![ListItem {
14204|       |                blocks: vec![
14205|       |                    MarkdownElement::Paragraph(vec![InlineSpan::Text("One".to_string())]),
14206|       |                    MarkdownElement::Paragraph(vec![InlineSpan::Text("Two".to_string())]),
14207|       |                ],
14208|       |            }],
14209|       |        };
14210|       |        let list_text = MarkdownRenderer::element_plain_text(&list);
14211|       |        assert!(list_text.contains('\n'));
14212|       |
14213|       |        let quote = MarkdownElement::Quote {
14214|       |            depth: 1,
14215|       |            blocks: vec![
14216|       |                MarkdownElement::Paragraph(vec![InlineSpan::Text("Alpha".to_string())]),
14217|       |                MarkdownElement::Paragraph(vec![InlineSpan::Text("Beta".to_string())]),
14218|       |            ],
14219|       |        };
14220|       |        let quote_text = MarkdownRenderer::element_plain_text(&quote);
14221|       |        assert!(quote_text.contains('\n'));
14222|       |
14223|       |        let table = MarkdownElement::Table {
14224|       |            headers: vec![
14225|       |                vec![InlineSpan::Text("H1".to_string())],
14226|       |                vec![InlineSpan::Text("H2".to_string())],
14227|       |            ],
14228|       |            rows: vec![vec![
14229|       |                vec![InlineSpan::Text("A".to_string())],
14230|       |                vec![InlineSpan::Text("B".to_string())],
14231|       |            ]],
14232|       |            alignments: vec![],
14233|       |        };
14234|       |        let table_text = MarkdownRenderer::element_plain_text(&table);
14235|       |        assert_eq!(table_text, "H1 H2 A B");
14236|       |    }
14237|       |
14238|       |    #[test]
14239|       |    fn test_resolve_image_path_data_uri_decodes_and_remote_rejects() {
14240|       |        use base64::Engine;
14241|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
14242|       |
14243|       |        // resolve_image_path still passes data URIs through unchanged
14244|       |        let data_uri_raw = "data:image/png;base64,AAAA";
14245|       |        assert_eq!(renderer.resolve_image_path(data_uri_raw), data_uri_raw);
14246|       |
14247|       |        // Build a valid 1x1 PNG data URI for the texture-loading test
14248|       |        let png_bytes = make_1x1_png();
14249|       |        let b64 = base64::engine::general_purpose::STANDARD.encode(&png_bytes);
14250|       |        let valid_data_uri = format!("data:image/png;base64,{b64}");
14251|       |
14252|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
14253|       |            // A valid data URI should now decode and return a texture
14254|       |            let loaded = renderer.get_or_load_image_texture(ui, &valid_data_uri);
14255|       |            assert!(loaded.is_some(), "valid data URI should produce a texture");
14256|       |            let (_, w, h) = loaded.unwrap();
14257|       |            assert_eq!((w, h), (1, 1), "expected 1x1 pixel image");
14258|       |
14259|       |            // Remote images without allow_remote should still be rejected
14260|       |            assert!(renderer
14261|       |                .get_or_load_image_texture(ui, "https://example.com/image.png")
14262|       |                .is_none());
14263|       |            assert!(renderer
14264|       |                .get_or_load_image_texture(ui, "http://example.com/image.png")
14265|       |                .is_none());
14266|       |        });
14267|       |    }

14288|       |

14290|       |        assert_eq!(level, 1);
14291|       |        assert_eq!(rest, "abc");
14292|       |

14411|       |    fn test_parse_inline_spans_link_and_image() {
14412|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
14413|       |        let events = vec![
14414|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Paragraph),
14415|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Link(
14416|       |                LinkType::Inline,
14417|       |                "https://example.com".into(),
14418|       |                "".into(),
14419|       |            )),
14420|       |            Event::Text("Link".into()),
14421|       |            Event::End(Tag::Link(LinkType::Inline, "".into(), "".into())),
14422|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Image(
14423|       |                LinkType::Inline,
14424|       |                "img.png".into(),
14425|       |                "Title".into(),
14426|       |            )),
14427|       |            Event::Text("Alt".into()),
14428|       |            Event::End(Tag::Image(LinkType::Inline, "".into(), "".into())),
14429|       |            Event::End(Tag::Paragraph),
14430|       |        ];
14431|       |        let (spans, next) = renderer.parse_inline_spans(&events, 1, Tag::Paragraph);
14432|       |        assert_eq!(next, events.len());
14433|       |        assert!(spans
14434|       |            .iter()
14435|       |            .any(|span| matches!(span, InlineSpan::Link { .. })));
14436|       |        assert!(spans
14437|       |            .iter()
14438|       |            .any(|span| matches!(span, InlineSpan::Image { .. })));
14439|       |    }

14492|       |    #[test]
14493|       |    fn test_parse_list_inline_formats_without_paragraph() -> Result<()> {
14494|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
14495|       |        let events = vec![
14496|       |            Event::Start(Tag::List(None)),
14497|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Item),
14498|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Emphasis),
14499|       |            Event::Text("em".into()),
14500|       |            Event::End(Tag::Emphasis),
14501|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Strikethrough),
14502|       |            Event::Text("strike".into()),
14503|       |            Event::End(Tag::Strikethrough),
14504|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Link(
14505|       |                LinkType::Inline,
14506|       |                "https://example.com".into(),
14507|       |                "".into(),
14508|       |            )),
14509|       |            Event::Text("link".into()),
14510|       |            Event::End(Tag::Link(LinkType::Inline, "".into(), "".into())),
14511|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Image(
14512|       |                LinkType::Inline,
14513|       |                "img.png".into(),
14514|       |                "Title".into(),
14515|       |            )),
14516|       |            Event::Text("Alt".into()),
14517|       |            Event::End(Tag::Image(LinkType::Inline, "".into(), "".into())),
14518|       |            Event::End(Tag::Item),
14519|       |            Event::End(Tag::List(None)),
14520|       |        ];
14521|       |        let mut slugs = std::collections::HashMap::new();
14522|       |        let (items, next) = renderer.parse_list(&events, 1, &mut slugs);
14523|       |        assert_eq!(next, events.len());
14524|       |        assert!(matches!(items[0].blocks[0], MarkdownElement::Paragraph(_)));
14525|       |        let dummy = MarkdownElement::HorizontalRule;
14526|       |        let mut saw_paragraph = false;
14527|       |        for element in [&items[0].blocks[0], &dummy] {
14528|       |            if let MarkdownElement::Paragraph(spans) = element {
14529|       |                assert!(spans
14530|       |                    .iter()
14531|       |                    .any(|span| matches!(span, InlineSpan::Emphasis(_))));
14532|       |                assert!(spans
14533|       |                    .iter()
14534|       |                    .any(|span| matches!(span, InlineSpan::Strikethrough(_))));
14535|       |                assert!(spans
14536|       |                    .iter()
14537|       |                    .any(|span| matches!(span, InlineSpan::Link { .. })));
14538|       |                assert!(spans
14539|       |                    .iter()
14540|       |                    .any(|span| matches!(span, InlineSpan::Image { .. })));
14541|       |                saw_paragraph = true;
14542|       |            }
14543|       |        }
14544|       |        assert!(saw_paragraph);
14545|       |        Ok(())
14546|       |    }

14628|       |    #[test]
14629|       |    fn test_render_text_with_emojis_and_styles() {
14630|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
14631|       |        let md = "Text with 🚀 emoji and **bold** and *italic* and `code` inline.\n";
14632|       |        let elements = renderer.parse(md).expect("parse ok");
14633|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
14634|       |            renderer.render_to_ui(ui, &elements);
14635|       |        });
14636|       |    }
14637|       |
14638|       |    /// Verify that U+FE0F (Variation Selector 16) is stripped from non-catalog
14639|       |    /// emoji before they reach the text buffer. U+1F6E0 (HAMMER AND WRENCH) +
14640|       |    /// U+FE0F is NOT in the 80-emoji Twemoji catalog, so it falls through to
14641|       |    /// text rendering. Without the fix, FE0F would appear as tofu in egui.
14642|       |    #[test]
14643|       |    fn test_render_text_with_emojis_strips_vs16() {
14644|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
14645|       |        // U+1F6E0 U+FE0F = 🛠️  — not in catalog, should strip FE0F
14646|       |        let input = "before \u{1F6E0}\u{FE0F} after";
14647|       |
14648|       |        // Confirm the emoji is NOT in our catalog (prerequisite for the fix)
14649|       |        assert!(
14650|       |            renderer
14651|       |                .emoji_key_for_grapheme("\u{1F6E0}\u{FE0F}")
14652|       |                .is_none(),
14653|       |            "test assumes U+1F6E0+FE0F is not in emoji catalog"
14654|       |        );
14655|       |
14656|       |        // The stripping logic is inline in render_text_with_emojis, so we
14657|       |        // verify it directly: for a non-catalog grapheme containing FE0F,
14658|       |        // the chars().filter() path should remove it.
14659|       |        let g = "\u{1F6E0}\u{FE0F}";
14660|       |        assert!(g.contains('\u{FE0F}'));
14661|       |        let clean: String = g.chars().filter(|&c| c != '\u{FE0F}').collect();
14662|       |        assert!(!clean.contains('\u{FE0F}'), "FE0F should be stripped");
14663|       |        assert!(clean.contains('\u{1F6E0}'), "base emoji should remain");
14664|       |
14665|       |        // Smoke test: render_text_with_emojis should not panic with VS16 input
14666|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
14667|       |            renderer.render_text_with_emojis(ui, input, 14.0, InlineStyle::default());
14668|       |        });
14669|       |    }
14670|       |
14671|       |    #[test]
14672|       |    fn test_render_link_context_menu_in_test_mode() {
14673|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
14674|       |        let md = "[Click here](https://example.com)\n";
14675|       |        let elements = renderer.parse(md).expect("parse ok");
14676|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
14677|       |            renderer.render_to_ui(ui, &elements);
14678|       |        });
14679|       |    }

14685|       |        renderer.set_dark_mode(false);

14691|       |    #[test]
14692|       |    fn test_highlight_code_light_theme() {
14693|       |        let mut renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
14694|       |
14695|       |        // Parse with dark theme
14696|       |        let code = "let x = 42;";
14697|       |        let dark_tokens = renderer
14698|       |            .highlight_code(Some("rust"), code)
14699|       |            .expect("dark highlight");
14700|       |
14701|       |        // Switch to light theme and re-highlight
14702|       |        renderer.set_dark_mode(false);
14703|       |        let light_tokens = renderer
14704|       |            .highlight_code(Some("rust"), code)
14705|       |            .expect("light highlight");
14706|       |
14707|       |        // The two themes should produce different token colors
14708|       |        assert!(!dark_tokens.is_empty());
14709|       |        assert!(!light_tokens.is_empty());
14710|       |        // At least one token color should differ between themes
14711|       |        let dark_first_color = dark_tokens[0][0].color;
14712|       |        let light_first_color = light_tokens[0][0].color;
14713|       |        assert_ne!(dark_first_color, light_first_color);
14714|       |    }

14716|       |    #[test]
14717|       |    fn test_release_gpu_textures_clears_all_caches() {
14718|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
14719|       |        with_test_ui(|ctx, _ui| {
14720|       |            // Populate emoji_textures cache
14721|       |            let emoji_tex = ctx.load_texture(
14722|       |                "test-emoji",
14723|       |                egui::ColorImage::new([2, 2], Color32::RED),
14724|       |                egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR,
14725|       |            );
14726|       |            renderer
14727|       |                .emoji_textures
14728|       |                .borrow_mut()
14729|       |                .insert("rocket".to_string(), emoji_tex);
14730|       |
14731|       |            // Populate image_textures cache
14732|       |            let img_tex = ctx.load_texture(
14733|       |                "test-image",
14734|       |                egui::ColorImage::new([2, 2], Color32::BLUE),
14735|       |                egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR,
14736|       |            );
14737|       |            renderer.image_textures.borrow_mut().insert(
14738|       |                "test.png".to_string(),
14739|       |                ImageCacheEntry {
14740|       |                    texture: img_tex,
14741|       |                    size: [64, 32],
14742|       |                    modified: None,
14743|       |                    byte_size: ImageCacheEntry::estimate_bytes([64, 32]),
14744|       |                    degraded: false,
14745|       |                },
14746|       |            );
14747|       |
14748|       |            // Verify caches are populated
14749|       |            assert!(!renderer.emoji_textures.borrow().entries.is_empty());
14750|       |            assert!(!renderer.image_textures.borrow().entries.is_empty());
14751|       |
14752|       |            // Call release_gpu_textures
14753|       |            renderer.release_gpu_textures();
14754|       |
14755|       |            // Assert all texture caches are empty
14756|       |            assert_eq!(renderer.emoji_textures.borrow().len(), 0);
14757|       |            assert_eq!(renderer.image_textures.borrow().entries.len(), 0);
14758|       |        });
14759|       |    }

14761|       |    // ---------------------------------------------------------------
14762|       |    // decode_data_uri() tests
14763|       |    // ---------------------------------------------------------------
14764|       |
14765|       |    /// Helper: create a minimal 1x1 PNG in memory and return the raw bytes.
14766|       |    fn make_1x1_png() -> Vec<u8> {
14767|       |        let mut buf = Vec::new();
14768|       |        let encoder = PngEncoder::new(&mut buf);
14769|       |        // 1x1 white pixel, RGBA
14770|       |        encoder
14771|       |            .write_image(&[255, 255, 255, 255], 1, 1, ColorType::Rgba8)
14772|       |            .expect("encode 1x1 PNG");
14773|       |        buf
14774|       |    }
14775|       |
14776|       |    /// Helper: create a minimal JPEG in memory using the `image` crate.
14777|       |    fn make_1x1_jpeg() -> Vec<u8> {
14778|       |        use image::codecs::jpeg::JpegEncoder;
14779|       |        let mut buf = Vec::new();
14780|       |        let encoder = JpegEncoder::new(&mut buf);
14781|       |        // 1x1 white pixel, RGB (JPEG doesn't support alpha)
14782|       |        encoder
14783|       |            .write_image(&[255, 255, 255], 1, 1, ColorType::Rgb8)
14784|       |            .expect("encode 1x1 JPEG");
14785|       |        buf
14786|       |    }
14787|       |
14788|       |    #[test]
14789|       |    fn test_decode_data_uri_valid_png() {
14790|       |        use base64::Engine;
14791|       |        let png_bytes = make_1x1_png();
14792|       |        let b64 = base64::engine::general_purpose::STANDARD.encode(&png_bytes);
14793|       |        let uri = format!("data:image/png;base64,{b64}");
14794|       |        let decoded = decode_data_uri(&uri);
14795|       |        assert!(decoded.is_some(), "valid PNG data URI should decode");
14796|       |        assert_eq!(decoded.unwrap(), png_bytes);
14797|       |    }

14878|       |    fn test_decode_data_uri_charset_parameter_present() {
14879|       |        use base64::Engine;
14880|       |        let png_bytes = make_1x1_png();
14881|       |        let b64 = base64::engine::general_purpose::STANDARD.encode(&png_bytes);
14882|       |        let uri = format!("data:image/png;charset=utf-8;base64,{b64}");
14883|       |        let decoded = decode_data_uri(&uri);
14884|       |        assert!(
14885|       |            decoded.is_some(),
14886|       |            "charset parameter before ;base64 should still decode"
14887|       |        );
14888|       |        assert_eq!(decoded.unwrap(), png_bytes);
14889|       |    }

14891|       |    #[test]
14892|       |    fn test_decode_data_uri_whitespace_in_payload() {
14893|       |        use base64::Engine;
14894|       |        let png_bytes = make_1x1_png();
14895|       |        let b64 = base64::engine::general_purpose::STANDARD.encode(&png_bytes);
14896|       |        // Inject newlines into the base64 payload
14897|       |        let b64_with_newlines: String = b64
14898|       |            .chars()
14899|       |            .enumerate()
14900|       |            .flat_map(|(i, c)| {
14901|       |                if i > 0 && i % 10 == 0 {
14902|       |                    vec!['\n', c]
14903|       |                } else {
14904|       |                    vec![c]
14905|       |                }
14906|       |            })
14907|       |            .collect();
14908|       |        let uri = format!("data:image/png;base64,{b64_with_newlines}");
14909|       |        let decoded = decode_data_uri(&uri);
14910|       |        assert!(
14911|       |            decoded.is_some(),
14912|       |            "whitespace in payload should be stripped before decoding"
14913|       |        );
14914|       |        assert_eq!(decoded.unwrap(), png_bytes);
14915|       |    }
14916|       |
14917|       |    #[test]
14918|       |    fn test_decode_data_uri_tabs_in_payload() {
14919|       |        use base64::Engine;
14920|       |        let png_bytes = make_1x1_png();
14921|       |        let b64 = base64::engine::general_purpose::STANDARD.encode(&png_bytes);
14922|       |        // Inject tabs and spaces into the base64 payload
14923|       |        let b64_with_tabs: String = b64
14924|       |            .chars()
14925|       |            .enumerate()
14926|       |            .flat_map(|(i, c)| {
14927|       |                if i > 0 && i % 8 == 0 {
14928|       |                    vec!['\t', ' ', c]
14929|       |                } else {
14930|       |                    vec![c]
14931|       |                }
14932|       |            })
14933|       |            .collect();
14934|       |        let uri = format!("data:image/png;base64,{b64_with_tabs}");
14935|       |        let decoded = decode_data_uri(&uri);
14936|       |        assert!(
14937|       |            decoded.is_some(),
14938|       |            "tabs and spaces in payload should be stripped before decoding"
14939|       |        );
14940|       |        assert_eq!(decoded.unwrap(), png_bytes);
14941|       |    }

14948|       |    fn test_get_or_load_image_texture_data_uri_produces_texture() {
14949|       |        use base64::Engine;
14950|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
14951|       |        let png_bytes = make_1x1_png();
14952|       |        let b64 = base64::engine::general_purpose::STANDARD.encode(&png_bytes);
14953|       |        let data_uri = format!("data:image/png;base64,{b64}");
14954|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
14955|       |            let loaded = renderer.get_or_load_image_texture(ui, &data_uri);
14956|       |            assert!(loaded.is_some(), "valid data URI should produce a texture");
14957|       |            let (_, w, h) = loaded.unwrap();
14958|       |            assert_eq!((w, h), (1, 1), "expected 1x1 pixel image");
14959|       |        });
14960|       |    }

14963|       |    fn test_get_or_load_image_texture_data_uri_invalid_returns_none() {
14964|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
14965|       |        // "dGVzdA==" decodes to "test" — valid base64 but not a valid image
14966|       |        let data_uri = "data:image/png;base64,dGVzdA==";
14967|       |        let expected_key = data_uri_cache_key(data_uri);
14968|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
14969|       |            let loaded = renderer.get_or_load_image_texture(ui, data_uri);
14970|       |            assert!(loaded.is_none(), "garbage image bytes should return None");
14971|       |            // Failure should be cached under the hash key
14972|       |            assert!(
14973|       |                renderer.image_failures.borrow().contains_key(&expected_key),
14974|       |                "failure should be cached under the hash key"
14975|       |            );
14976|       |        });
14977|       |    }
14978|       |
14979|       |    #[test]
14980|       |    fn test_get_or_load_image_texture_data_uri_cached_on_second_call() {
14981|       |        use base64::Engine;
14982|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
14983|       |        let png_bytes = make_1x1_png();
14984|       |        let b64 = base64::engine::general_purpose::STANDARD.encode(&png_bytes);
14985|       |        let data_uri = format!("data:image/png;base64,{b64}");
14986|       |        let expected_key = data_uri_cache_key(&data_uri);
14987|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
14988|       |            // First call: decodes and caches
14989|       |            let first = renderer.get_or_load_image_texture(ui, &data_uri);
14990|       |            assert!(first.is_some(), "first call should produce a texture");
14991|       |
14992|       |            // Verify the hash key exists in image_textures
14993|       |            assert!(
14994|       |                renderer.image_textures.borrow().contains_key(&expected_key),
14995|       |                "hash key should exist in image_textures after first call"
14996|       |            );
14997|       |
14998|       |            // Second call: should hit cache and still return Some
14999|       |            let second = renderer.get_or_load_image_texture(ui, &data_uri);
15000|       |            assert!(second.is_some(), "second call should return cached texture");
15001|       |            let (_, w1, h1) = first.unwrap();
15002|       |            let (_, w2, h2) = second.unwrap();
15003|       |            assert_eq!((w1, h1), (w2, h2), "dimensions should match between calls");
15004|       |        });
15005|       |    }
15006|       |

15018|       |    #[test]
15019|       |    fn test_data_uri_not_stale_after_cache() {
15020|       |        use base64::Engine;
15021|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
15022|       |        let png_bytes = make_1x1_png();
15023|       |        let b64 = base64::engine::general_purpose::STANDARD.encode(&png_bytes);
15024|       |        let data_uri = format!("data:image/png;base64,{b64}");
15025|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
15026|       |            // Load into cache
15027|       |            let first = renderer.get_or_load_image_texture(ui, &data_uri);
15028|       |            assert!(first.is_some(), "initial load should succeed");
15029|       |            let (_, w1, h1) = first.unwrap();
15030|       |
15031|       |            // Second call: served from cache (not re-decoded), same dimensions
15032|       |            let second = renderer.get_or_load_image_texture(ui, &data_uri);
15033|       |            assert!(second.is_some(), "cached lookup should succeed");
15034|       |            let (_, w2, h2) = second.unwrap();
15035|       |            assert_eq!(
15036|       |                (w1, h1),
15037|       |                (w2, h2),
15038|       |                "cached image should have same dimensions"
15039|       |            );
15040|       |        });
15041|       |    }

15043|       |    #[test]
15044|       |    fn test_placeholder_label_data_uri_truncated() {
15045|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
15046|       |        // Use a data URI with invalid image bytes so the placeholder path is taken
15047|       |        let long_data_uri = format!("data:image/png;base64,{}", "A".repeat(1000));
15048|       |        let span = InlineSpan::Image {
15049|       |            src: long_data_uri.clone(),
15050|       |            alt: "".to_string(),
15051|       |            title: None,
15052|       |        };
15053|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
15054|       |            // Ensure the data URI fails to load (garbage bytes)
15055|       |            let loaded = renderer.get_or_load_image_texture(ui, &long_data_uri);
15056|       |            assert!(
15057|       |                loaded.is_none(),
15058|       |                "garbage data URI should not produce a texture"
15059|       |            );
15060|       |            // Render the span — this exercises the placeholder path
15061|       |            // The placeholder should show "Embedded image (data URI)" instead of the raw base64
15062|       |            renderer.render_inline_span(ui, &span, None, None);
15063|       |            // If we got here without hanging or crashing, the truncation is working.
15064|       |            // The placeholder text is rendered internally via ui.label() — we verify
15065|       |            // the code path is exercised rather than inspecting the rendered text.
15066|       |        });
15067|       |    }
15068|       |
15069|       |    #[test]
15070|       |    fn test_placeholder_label_data_uri_uses_alt_text() {
15071|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
15072|       |        let data_uri = "data:image/png;base64,dGVzdA==";
15073|       |        let span = InlineSpan::Image {
15074|       |            src: data_uri.to_string(),
15075|       |            alt: "My alt text".to_string(),
15076|       |            title: None,
15077|       |        };
15078|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
15079|       |            // Ensure the data URI fails (invalid image bytes)
15080|       |            let _ = renderer.get_or_load_image_texture(ui, data_uri);
15081|       |            // Render — should use alt text, not "Embedded image (data URI)"
15082|       |            renderer.render_inline_span(ui, &span, None, None);
15083|       |        });
15084|       |    }

15090|       |            src: "missing_file.png".to_string(),

15092|       |            title: None,
15093|       |        };

15096|       |            renderer.render_inline_span(ui, &span, None, None);
15097|       |            // The placeholder for file paths should show the file path itself,

15100|       |        });

15107|       |    #[test]
15108|       |    fn test_data_uri_cache_key_deterministic() {
15109|       |        let uri = "data:image/png;base64,iVBORw0KGgo=";
15110|       |        let key1 = data_uri_cache_key(uri);
15111|       |        let key2 = data_uri_cache_key(uri);
15112|       |        assert_eq!(key1, key2, "same URI must produce same cache key");
15113|       |    }
15114|       |
15115|       |    #[test]
15116|       |    fn test_data_uri_cache_key_different_uris_differ() {
15117|       |        let key1 = data_uri_cache_key("data:image/png;base64,AAAA");
15118|       |        let key2 = data_uri_cache_key("data:image/png;base64,BBBB");
15119|       |        assert_ne!(
15120|       |            key1, key2,
15121|       |            "different URIs must produce different cache keys"
15122|       |        );
15123|       |    }
15124|       |
15125|       |    #[test]
15126|       |    fn test_data_uri_cache_key_starts_with_prefix() {
15127|       |        let key = data_uri_cache_key("data:image/png;base64,test");
15128|       |        assert!(
15129|       |            key.starts_with("data-uri:"),
15130|       |            "cache key must start with 'data-uri:' prefix, got: {key}"
15131|       |        );
15132|       |    }
15133|       |
15134|       |    #[test]
15135|       |    fn test_data_uri_cache_key_fixed_length() {
15136|       |        let short_uri = "data:image/png;base64,AA==";
15137|       |        let long_uri = format!("data:image/png;base64,{}", "A".repeat(1_000_000));
15138|       |        let short_key = data_uri_cache_key(short_uri);
15139|       |        let long_key = data_uri_cache_key(&long_uri);
15140|       |        assert_eq!(
15141|       |            short_key.len(),
15142|       |            long_key.len(),
15143|       |            "cache key length must be fixed regardless of input size (short={}, long={})",
15144|       |            short_key.len(),
15145|       |            long_key.len()
15146|       |        );
15147|       |        // "data-uri:" (9 chars) + 16 hex digits = 25 chars
15148|       |        assert_eq!(
15149|       |            short_key.len(),
15150|       |            25,
15151|       |            "expected 25-char cache key, got {}",
15152|       |            short_key.len()
15153|       |        );

  396|       |/// LRU cache for Mermaid textures with both entry count and size limits.
  397|       |/// Prevents excessive GPU memory usage from large rendered diagrams.
  398|       |#[cfg(feature = "mermaid-quickjs")]
  399|       |struct TextureCache {
  400|       |    entries: HashMap<String, MermaidTextureEntry>,
  401|       |    order: VecDeque<String>,
  402|       |    capacity: usize,
  403|       |    max_bytes: usize,
  404|       |    current_bytes: usize,
  405|       |}

  410|    932|        Self {
  411|    932|            entries: HashMap::new(),
  412|    932|            order: VecDeque::new(),
  413|    932|            capacity: capacity.max(1),

  415|    932|            current_bytes: 0,
  416|    932|        }
  417|    932|    }
  418|       |
  419|       |    /// Calculate memory size of a texture entry in bytes (width * height * 4 for RGBA)
  420|     54|    fn texture_bytes(entry: &MermaidTextureEntry) -> usize {
  421|     54|        entry.size[0] as usize * entry.size[1] as usize * 4
  422|     54|    }

  524|       |
  525|       |/// Per-diagram debounce state for resize-aware rendering.
  526|       |/// Tracks the last-enqueued width bucket and the time when the width bucket last changed.
  527|       |#[cfg(feature = "mermaid-quickjs")]
  528|       |struct WidthDebounce {
  529|       |    /// The width bucket for which a render was last successfully enqueued.
  530|       |    last_enqueued_bucket: u32,
  531|       |    /// The width bucket observed on the most recent frame.
  532|       |    /// Used to detect continuing resize (trailing-edge timer reset).
  533|       |    last_seen_bucket: u32,
  534|       |    /// When the width bucket last changed (i.e., last_seen_bucket was updated).
  535|       |    /// None if the current bucket matches last_enqueued_bucket (no resize in progress).
  536|       |    bucket_changed_at: Option<Instant>,
  537|       |}
  538|       |
  539|       |/// How long to wait after the last width bucket change before
  540|       |/// enqueueing a re-render. Prevents flooding workers during resize.
  541|       |#[cfg(feature = "mermaid-quickjs")]
  542|       |const MERMAID_RESIZE_DEBOUNCE_MS: u64 = 150;
  543|       |
  544|       |pub(crate) struct MermaidRenderer {
  545|       |    #[cfg(feature = "mermaid-quickjs")]
  546|       |    mermaid_textures: RefCell<TextureCache>,
  547|       |    #[cfg(feature = "mermaid-quickjs")]
  548|       |    mermaid_pending: RefCell<HashSet<String>>,
  549|       |    #[cfg(feature = "mermaid-quickjs")]
  550|       |    mermaid_frame_pending: Cell<bool>,
  551|       |    #[cfg(feature = "mermaid-quickjs")]
  552|       |    mermaid_svg_cache: RefCell<SvgCache>,
  553|       |    #[cfg(feature = "mermaid-quickjs")]
  554|       |    mermaid_errors: RefCell<LruCache<u64, String>>,
  555|       |    #[cfg(feature = "mermaid-quickjs")]
  556|       |    mermaid_texture_errors: RefCell<LruCache<String, String>>,
  557|       |    /// Job sender wrapped in Option so Drop can take and drop it first,
  558|       |    /// closing the channel before joining worker threads.
  559|       |    #[cfg(feature = "mermaid-quickjs")]
  560|       |    mermaid_job_tx: Option<MermaidJobSender>,
  561|       |    #[cfg(feature = "mermaid-quickjs")]
  562|       |    mermaid_result_rx: MermaidResultReceiver,
  563|       |    /// Worker thread handles for cleanup on drop.
  564|       |    /// When MermaidRenderer is dropped, we join all worker threads to ensure
  565|       |    /// they exit cleanly and release memory (font database, QuickJS runtime).
  566|       |    #[cfg(feature = "mermaid-quickjs")]
  567|       |    worker_handles: RefCell<Vec<JoinHandle<()>>>,
  568|       |    /// Deferred worker spawn: holds channel ends until the first diagram is requested.
  569|       |    /// Avoids loading QuickJS + mermaid.min.js when no Mermaid diagrams are used.
  570|       |    #[cfg(feature = "mermaid-quickjs")]
  571|       |    deferred_spawn: RefCell<Option<(MermaidJobReceiver, MermaidResultSender)>>,
  572|       |    /// Per-diagram debounce state for resize-aware rendering.
  573|       |    /// Key: svg_key (u64). Tracks the last-enqueued width bucket
  574|       |    /// and the time when the width bucket last changed.
  575|       |    #[cfg(feature = "mermaid-quickjs")]
  576|       |    mermaid_width_debounce: RefCell<HashMap<u64, WidthDebounce>>,
  577|       |    /// Maps svg_key → the texture_key of the most recently stored texture.
  578|       |    /// Used during resize debounce to find a stale-but-displayable texture.
  579|       |    #[cfg(feature = "mermaid-quickjs")]
  580|       |    mermaid_latest_texture: RefCell<HashMap<u64, String>>,
  581|       |    /// The width bucket that was most recently *requested* for each svg_key.
  582|       |    /// Updated every frame in render_block before poll_mermaid_results.
  583|       |    /// Used by poll_mermaid_results to detect stale results.
  584|       |    #[cfg(feature = "mermaid-quickjs")]
  585|       |    mermaid_wanted_bucket: RefCell<HashMap<u64, u32>>,
  586|       |}
  587|       |
  588|       |impl MermaidRenderer {
  589|       |    #[cfg(feature = "mermaid-quickjs")]
  590|       |    const MERMAID_TEXTURE_CACHE_CAPACITY: usize = 128;
  591|       |    /// Maximum total bytes for texture cache (100MB). Textures at 4096x4096 are 64MB each,
  592|       |    /// so this comfortably holds several full-res diagrams. Size limit ensures bounded memory.
  593|       |    #[cfg(feature = "mermaid-quickjs")]
  594|       |    const MERMAID_TEXTURE_CACHE_MAX_BYTES: usize = 100 * 1024 * 1024;
  595|       |    #[cfg(any(test, feature = "mermaid-quickjs"))]

  600|       |    const MAX_MERMAID_JOBS: usize = 4;
  601|       |    #[cfg(feature = "mermaid-quickjs")]
  602|       |    const MERMAID_SVG_CACHE_CAPACITY: usize = 64;
  603|       |    /// Maximum total bytes for SVG cache (100MB). Large SVGs (100KB-10MB each)

  619|       |

  723|       |
  724|     54|    pub(crate) fn render_block(
  725|     54|        &self,
  726|     54|        ui: &mut egui::Ui,
  727|     54|        code: &str,
  728|     54|        ui_scale: f32,
  729|     54|        code_font_size: f32,
  730|     54|        theme: MermaidTheme,
  731|     54|    ) -> bool {
  732|       |        #[cfg(not(feature = "mermaid-quickjs"))]
  733|       |        {
  734|       |            let _ = code;
  735|       |            let _ = ui_scale;
  736|       |            let _ = theme;
  737|       |            egui::Frame::none()
  738|       |                .fill(ThemeColors::current(ui.visuals().dark_mode).box_bg)
  739|       |                .stroke(Stroke::new(
  740|       |                    1.0,
  741|       |                    ThemeColors::current(ui.visuals().dark_mode).box_border,
  742|       |                ))
  743|       |                .inner_margin(8.0)
  744|       |                .show(ui, |ui| {
  745|       |                    ui.label(
  746|       |                        RichText::new(
  747|       |                            "Mermaid rendering requires the mermaid-embedded feature. Build with default features to enable.",
  748|       |                        )
  749|       |                        .color(ThemeColors::current(ui.visuals().dark_mode).box_title)

                                   ^18

  |  Branch (1152:19): [True: 18, False: 68]
  ------------------

 1238|       |    #[cfg(feature = "mermaid-quickjs")]
 1239|     16|    fn mermaid_worker_count() -> usize {

 1241|       |        // typical documents have few diagrams. Each worker loads a QuickJS runtime
 1242|       |        // with mermaid.min.js (~30-50 MB), so multiple workers waste memory.

 1254|     14|        worker_rx: MermaidJobReceiver,
 1255|     14|        worker_tx: MermaidResultSender,
 1256|     14|        worker_fontdb: Arc<usvg::fontdb::Database>,
 1257|     14|    ) -> std::io::Result<JoinHandle<()>> {
 1258|       |        #[cfg(test)]
 1259|     14|        if FORCE_MERMAID_THREAD_SPAWN_ERROR.swap(false, Ordering::Relaxed) {
  ------------------

  ------------------
 1260|      2|            return Err(std::io::Error::other("forced mermaid spawn error"));
 1261|     12|        }
 1262|     12|        std::thread::Builder::new()
 1263|     12|            .name(format!("mdmdview-mermaid-{worker_idx}"))
 1264|     12|            .spawn(move || {
 1265|     12|                let mut worker = MermaidWorker::new(worker_idx, worker_fontdb).map_err(|e| {
                                                                                                         ^2
 1266|      2|                    eprintln!("Mermaid worker init failed: {}", e);
 1267|      2|                    e
 1268|      2|                });
 1269|     12|                for job in worker_rx.iter() {
 1270|     12|                    let payload = match &mut worker {
 1271|     10|                        Ok(state) => state.process_job(job),
 1272|      2|                        Err(err) => {
 1273|       |                            let MermaidRequest {
 1274|      2|                                svg_key,
 1275|      2|                                texture_key,
 1276|      2|                                svg,
 1277|       |                                ..
 1278|      2|                            } = job;

 1280|      2|                                svg_key,
 1281|      2|                                texture_key,
 1282|      2|                                svg,
 1283|      2|                                rgba: None,
 1284|      2|                                size: None,
 1285|      2|                                error: Some(err.clone()),
 1286|      2|                            }
 1287|       |                        }
 1288|       |                    };
 1289|       |                    // Use try_send to avoid blocking if result channel is full.
 1290|       |                    // This prevents deadlock during shutdown: if Drop joins workers
 1291|       |                    // while they're blocked on send (because result_rx isn't being
 1292|       |                    // polled), we'd hang forever. Dropping results is acceptable
 1293|       |                    // since we're shutting down anyway.
 1294|     12|                    let _ = worker_tx.try_send(payload);
 1295|       |                }
 1296|     12|            })
 1297|     14|    }

 1299|       |    #[cfg(feature = "mermaid-quickjs")]
 1300|     14|    fn spawn_mermaid_workers(
 1301|     14|        job_rx: MermaidJobReceiver,
 1302|     14|        result_tx: MermaidResultSender,
 1303|     14|    ) -> Vec<JoinHandle<()>> {
 1304|     14|        let worker_count = Self::mermaid_worker_count();
 1305|     14|        let mut fontdb = usvg::fontdb::Database::new();
 1306|     14|        fontdb.load_system_fonts();

 1308|     14|        let mut handles = Vec::with_capacity(worker_count);
 1309|     14|        for worker_idx in 0..worker_count {
 1310|     14|            let worker_rx = job_rx.clone();
 1311|     14|            let worker_tx = result_tx.clone();
 1312|     14|            let worker_fontdb = Arc::clone(&fontdb);
 1313|     14|            match Self::spawn_mermaid_worker_thread(worker_idx, worker_rx, worker_tx, worker_fontdb)
 1314|       |            {
 1315|     12|                Ok(handle) => handles.push(handle),
 1316|      2|                Err(err) => eprintln!("Failed to start Mermaid worker thread: {}", err),
 1317|       |            }
 1318|       |        }
 1319|     14|        drop(job_rx);
 1320|     14|        handles
 1321|     14|    }

 1572|      2|                            prev = None;
 1573|      2|                        }
 1574|       |                    }
 1575|    102|                    max_width_units = max_width_units.max(width_units);
 1576|       |                }
 1577|       |
 1578|    102|                let ascender = face.ascender() as f32;
 1579|    102|                let descender = face.descender() as f32;
 1580|    102|                let line_gap = face.line_gap() as f32;
 1581|    102|                let line_height_units = (ascender - descender + line_gap).max(units_per_em);
 1582|    102|                let line_height = line_height_units * scale * Self::LINE_HEIGHT_SCALE;
 1583|    102|                let width = max_width_units * scale;
 1584|    102|                let height = line_height * line_count as f32;

 1586|    104|            })

 1941|      2|            None => return svg.to_string(),

 2310|       |    /// Converts escaped HTML formatting tags in SVG text elements to tspan equivalents.
 2311|       |    ///
 2312|       |    /// Transforms tags like `&lt;i&gt;text&lt;/i&gt;` to `<tspan font-style="italic">text</tspan>`.
 2313|       |    ///
 2314|       |    /// Supported tags: `<i>`, `<em>`, `<b>`, `<strong>`, `<u>`, `<s>`, `<del>`, `<strike>`
 2315|     64|    fn convert_html_tags_to_tspan(svg: &str) -> String {

 2870|       |  if (hit) { return hit; }
 2871|       |  if (typeof __mdmdview_measure_text_native === 'function') {

 2874|       |      if (native && typeof native.width === 'number' && typeof native.height === 'number') {
 2875|       |        __mdmdview_text_cache[key] = native;
 2876|       |        return native;
 2877|       |      }

 2900|       |function __mdmdview_parse_font_size(value, baseSize) {
 2901|       |  if (value === null || value === undefined) { return baseSize; }
 2902|       |  var raw = String(value).trim();
 2903|       |  if (!raw) { return baseSize; }
 2904|       |  var num = parseFloat(raw);
 2905|       |  if (isNaN(num)) { return baseSize; }
 2906|       |  if (raw.indexOf('em') >= 0) {
 2907|       |    var base = baseSize || 16;
 2908|       |    return num * base;
 2909|       |  }
 2910|       |  if (raw.indexOf('ex') >= 0) {
 2911|       |    var baseEx = baseSize || 16;
 2912|       |    return num * baseEx * 0.5;
 2913|       |  }
 2914|       |  return num;
 2915|       |}
 2916|       |function __mdmdview_parse_font_weight_value(value) {
 2917|       |  if (value === null || value === undefined) { return null; }
 2918|       |  var raw = String(value).trim().toLowerCase();
 2919|       |  if (!raw) { return null; }
 2920|       |  if (raw === 'normal') { return 400; }
 2921|       |  if (raw === 'bold' || raw === 'bolder') { return 700; }
 2922|       |  if (raw === 'lighter') { return 300; }
 2923|       |  var num = parseFloat(raw);
 2924|       |  if (isNaN(num)) { return null; }
 2925|       |  return num;

 2927|       |function __mdmdview_parse_css_font_sizes(cssText, cache) {
 2928|       |  if (!cssText || !cache) { return; }
 2929|       |  var chunks = String(cssText).split('}');
 2930|       |  for (var i = 0; i < chunks.length; i++) {
 2931|       |    var rule = chunks[i];
 2932|       |    var parts = rule.split('{');
 2933|       |    if (parts.length < 2) { continue; }
 2934|       |    var selector = parts[0];
 2935|       |    var body = parts.slice(1).join('{');
 2936|       |    if (!selector || !body) { continue; }
 2937|       |    var sizeMatch = body.match(/font-size\s*:\s*([^;]+)/i);
 2938|       |    if (!sizeMatch) { continue; }

 2940|       |    var size = __mdmdview_parse_font_size(sizeValue, 16);
 2941|       |    if (!size || isNaN(size)) { continue; }
 2942|       |    var selectors = selector.split(',');
 2943|       |    for (var s = 0; s < selectors.length; s++) {
 2944|       |      var sel = selectors[s].trim();
 2945|       |      if (!sel) { continue; }
 2946|       |      if (sel.indexOf(':') >= 0) { continue; }
 2947|       |      var classMatches = sel.match(/\\.([A-Za-z0-9_-]+)/g);
 2948|       |      if (!classMatches || classMatches.length !== 1) { continue; }
 2949|       |      var cls = classMatches[0].slice(1);
 2950|       |      if (!cls) { continue; }
 2951|       |      if (!cache[cls] || cache[cls] < size) {
 2952|       |        cache[cls] = size;
 2953|       |      }
 2954|       |    }
 2955|       |  }

 3204|       |function __mdmdview_parse_num(value) {

 3208|       |function __mdmdview_parse_length(value, fontSize) {
 3209|       |  if (value === null || value === undefined) { return 0; }
 3210|       |  var raw = String(value).trim();
 3211|       |  if (!raw) { return 0; }
 3212|       |  var num = parseFloat(raw);
 3213|       |  if (isNaN(num)) { return 0; }
 3214|       |  if (raw.indexOf('em') >= 0) {
 3215|       |    return num * (fontSize || 16);
 3216|       |  }
 3217|       |  if (raw.indexOf('ex') >= 0) {
 3218|       |    return num * (fontSize || 16) * 0.5;
 3219|       |  }
 3220|       |  return num;

 3222|       |function __mdmdview_transform_point(el, x, y) {
