  323|       |
  324|       |    /// Rasterize an SVG string to RGBA pixels. Pure function -- no GPU access,
  325|       |    /// no egui context needed. Runs on the worker thread.
  326|       |    ///
  327|       |    /// Injects font-family into Pikchr SVGs, parses via usvg (using the shared
  328|       |    /// fontdb with system fonts), and rasterizes via resvg/tiny-skia.
  329|       |    /// Rasterizes at `PIKCHR_SUPERSAMPLE` x the logical display resolution
  330|       |    /// for crisp text and lines. Returns display dimensions in logical pixels
  331|       |    /// and raster dimensions in actual pixels.
  332|      8|    fn rasterize_svg(
  333|      8|        fontdb: &Arc<usvg::fontdb::Database>,
  334|      8|        svg: &str,
  335|      8|        width_bucket: u32,
  336|      8|        scale_bucket: u32,
  337|      8|    ) -> Result<RasterOutput, String> {
  338|       |        // Inject a font-family into the SVG so usvg picks a known good font.
  339|       |        // Pikchr outputs <svg style='font-size:initial;'> with no font-family
  340|       |        // on <text> elements, so usvg falls back to whatever thin default it finds.
  341|      8|        let svg = Self::inject_svg_font_family(svg);
  342|       |
  343|       |        // Parse SVG using the shared fontdb with system fonts loaded.
  344|      8|        let opt = usvg::Options {
  345|      8|            fontdb: Arc::clone(fontdb),
  346|      8|            ..Default::default()
  347|      8|        };
  348|      8|        let tree = usvg::Tree::from_data(svg.as_bytes(), &opt)
  349|      8|            .map_err(|e| format!("SVG parse error: {}", e))?;
                                       ^0                                ^0
  350|       |
  351|       |        // Compute dimensions from the parsed SVG tree
  352|      8|        let size = tree.size().to_int_size();
  353|      8|        let (w, h) = (size.width().max(1), size.height().max(1));
  354|       |
  355|       |        // Compute logical scale factor (user zoom, constrained by available width)
  356|      8|        let base_scale = scale_bucket as f32 / 100.0;
  357|      8|        let width_scale = if width_bucket > 0 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (357:30): [True: 8, False: 0]
  ------------------
  358|      8|            width_bucket as f32 / w.max(1) as f32
  359|       |        } else {
  360|      0|            base_scale
  361|       |        };
  362|      8|        let scale = base_scale.min(width_scale).clamp(0.1, 4.0);
  363|       |
  364|       |        // Display dimensions in logical pixels (what render_texture uses)
  365|      8|        let display_w = (w as f32 * scale).round().max(1.0) as u32;
  366|      8|        let display_h = (h as f32 * scale).round().max(1.0) as u32;
  367|       |
  368|       |        // Raster dimensions: supersample for crisp text and thin lines
  369|      8|        let raster_scale = scale * PIKCHR_SUPERSAMPLE;
  370|      8|        let mut raster_w = (w as f32 * raster_scale).round().max(1.0) as u32;
  371|      8|        let mut raster_h = (h as f32 * raster_scale).round().max(1.0) as u32;
  372|       |
  373|       |        // Clamp to reasonable maximum
  374|      8|        if raster_w > MAX_RASTER_SIDE || raster_h > MAX_RASTER_SIDE {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (374:12): [True: 0, False: 8]
  |  Branch (374:42): [True: 0, False: 8]
  ------------------
  375|      0|            let clamp_scale = (MAX_RASTER_SIDE as f32 / raster_w as f32)
  376|      0|                .min(MAX_RASTER_SIDE as f32 / raster_h as f32);
  377|      0|            raster_w = (raster_w as f32 * clamp_scale).round().max(1.0) as u32;
  378|      0|            raster_h = (raster_h as f32 * clamp_scale).round().max(1.0) as u32;
  379|      8|        }
  380|       |
  381|       |        // Rasterize: Pikchr SVGs have no background fill, so the pixmap starts
  382|       |        // transparent and the diagram draws on top.
  383|      8|        let mut pixmap = tiny_skia::Pixmap::new(raster_w, raster_h)
  384|      8|            .ok_or_else(|| "Pixmap allocation failed".to_string())?;
                                         ^0                         ^0          ^0
  385|      8|        let transform = tiny_skia::Transform::from_scale(raster_scale, raster_scale);
  386|       |        // Bind PixmapMut to a local -- can't take &mut of a temporary rvalue.
  387|      8|        let mut pmut = pixmap.as_mut();
  388|      8|        resvg::render(&tree, transform, &mut pmut);
  389|       |
  390|      8|        Ok(RasterOutput {
  391|      8|            rgba: pixmap.data().to_vec(),
  392|      8|            raster_size: [raster_w as usize, raster_h as usize],
  393|      8|            display_size: [display_w, display_h],
  394|      8|        })
  395|      8|    }
  396|       |
  397|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  398|       |    // Main-thread polling
  399|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  400|       |
  401|       |    /// Drain completed results from the worker thread. Called once per frame
  402|       |    /// from `begin_frame`, NOT from `render_block`. This is critical:
  403|       |    /// `render_block` only runs for visible diagrams, so if diagrams scroll
  404|       |    /// off-screen while jobs are in-flight, results would pile up in the
  405|       |    /// bounded channel and eventually block the worker thread (deadlock).
  406|       |    /// Polling from `begin_frame` ensures results are always drained.
  407|     62|    fn poll_results(&self, ctx: &egui::Context) -> bool {
  408|     62|        let mut any_processed = false;
  409|       |
  410|       |        loop {
  411|     62|            match self.result_rx.try_recv() {
  412|      0|                Ok(result) => {
  413|      0|                    any_processed = true;
  414|       |
  415|       |                    // Remove from pending set
  416|      0|                    self.pending.borrow_mut().remove(&result.code_hash);
  417|       |
  418|       |                    // Always cache SVG (content-addressed, width-independent)
  419|      0|                    if let Some(ref svg) = result.svg {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (419:28): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  420|      0|                        let sk = svg_cache_key(result.code_hash, result.dark_mode);
  421|      0|                        self.svg_cache.borrow_mut().insert(sk, svg.clone());
  422|      0|                    }
  423|       |
  424|       |                    // Always cache errors (prevents infinite retry loops)
  425|      0|                    if let Some(ref err) = result.error {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (425:28): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  426|      0|                        self.errors
  427|      0|                            .borrow_mut()
  428|      0|                            .insert(result.code_hash, err.clone());
  429|      0|                        continue; // No RGBA to upload
  430|      0|                    }
  431|       |
  432|       |                    // Stale result check: skip GPU upload if width bucket changed since enqueue
  433|      0|                    if self.is_stale_result(&result.texture_key, result.code_hash) {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (433:24): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  434|      0|                        continue;
  435|      0|                    }
  436|       |
  437|       |                    // Upload RGBA to GPU texture
  438|      0|                    if let (Some(rgba), Some(raster_size), Some(display_size)) =
  ------------------
  |  Branch (438:28): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  439|      0|                        (result.rgba, result.raster_size, result.display_size)
  440|       |                    {
  441|      0|                        let image = egui::ColorImage::from_rgba_unmultiplied(raster_size, &rgba);
  442|      0|                        let texture = ctx.load_texture(
  443|      0|                            result.texture_key.clone(),
  444|      0|                            image,
  445|       |                            egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR,
  446|       |                        );
  447|      0|                        let entry = PikchrTextureEntry {
  448|      0|                            texture,
  449|      0|                            display_size,
  450|      0|                        };
  451|      0|                        self.textures
  452|      0|                            .borrow_mut()
  453|      0|                            .insert(result.texture_key.clone(), entry);
  454|      0|                        self.latest_texture_key
  455|      0|                            .borrow_mut()
  456|      0|                            .insert(result.code_hash, result.texture_key.clone());
  457|       |                        // Update debounce state
  458|      0|                        if let Some(width_bucket) =
  ------------------
  |  Branch (458:32): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  459|      0|                            Self::parse_width_from_texture_key(&result.texture_key)
  460|      0|                        {
  461|      0|                            self.debounce.borrow_mut().insert(
  462|      0|                                result.code_hash,
  463|      0|                                DiagramDebounce {
  464|      0|                                    last_rasterized_bucket: width_bucket,
  465|      0|                                    last_seen_bucket: width_bucket,
  466|      0|                                    bucket_changed_at: None,
  467|      0|                                },
  468|      0|                            );
  469|      0|                        }
  470|      0|                    }
  471|       |                }
  472|     62|                Err(crossbeam_channel::TryRecvError::Empty) => break,
  473|       |                Err(crossbeam_channel::TryRecvError::Disconnected) => {
  474|       |                    // Worker thread has died (panic or unexpected exit).
  475|       |                    // Clear all pending entries so render_block can detect the dead
  476|       |                    // worker via try_send(Disconnected) and show an error instead of
  477|       |                    // showing "Rendering..." forever.
  478|      0|                    self.pending.borrow_mut().clear();
  479|      0|                    break;
  480|       |                }
  481|       |            }
  482|       |        }
  483|       |
  484|     62|        if any_processed {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (484:12): [True: 0, False: 62]
  ------------------
  485|      0|            ctx.request_repaint();
  486|     62|        }
  487|     62|        any_processed
  488|     62|    }
  489|       |
  490|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  491|       |    // Public API
  492|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  493|       |
  494|       |    /// Called once at the start of each egui frame. Drains completed results
  495|       |    /// from the worker thread and requests repaint if jobs are still pending.
  496|     62|    pub(crate) fn begin_frame(&self, ctx: &egui::Context) {
  497|       |        // Drain completed results from worker thread. Must happen here (not in
  498|       |        // render_block) because render_block only runs for visible diagrams --
  499|       |        // off-screen results would pile up and block the worker.
  500|     62|        self.poll_results(ctx);
  501|       |
  502|       |        // If jobs are still in-flight, ensure we get another frame to poll again.
  503|     62|        if self.has_pending() {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (503:12): [True: 0, False: 62]
  ------------------
  504|      0|            ctx.request_repaint_after(Duration::from_millis(16));
  505|     62|        }
  506|     62|    }
  507|       |
  508|       |    /// Returns true if any rasterization jobs are in-flight on the worker thread.
  509|     90|    pub(crate) fn has_pending(&self) -> bool {
  510|     90|        !self.pending.borrow().is_empty()
  511|     90|    }
  512|       |
  513|       |    /// Release GPU textures to reduce idle GPU usage (e.g., when minimized).
  514|       |    /// SVG and error caches are retained for fast texture rebuilds on next render.
  515|       |    /// Also clears pending, wanted_bucket, and debounce to allow clean re-enqueue
  516|       |    /// on restore. In-flight results will be detected as stale (wanted_bucket is
  517|       |    /// empty) and discarded in poll_results.
  518|      4|    pub(crate) fn release_gpu_textures(&self) {
  519|      4|        self.textures.borrow_mut().clear();
  520|      4|        self.debounce.borrow_mut().clear();
  521|      4|        self.latest_texture_key.borrow_mut().clear();
  522|      4|        self.wanted_bucket.borrow_mut().clear();
  523|      4|        self.pending.borrow_mut().clear();
  524|      4|    }
  525|       |
  526|       |    /// Main entry point for rendering a Pikchr code block.
  527|       |    ///
  528|       |    /// Checks caches (error, texture, SVG) before enqueuing. On cache miss,
  529|       |    /// enqueues a job to the worker thread and shows a stale texture or
  530|       |    /// placeholder while waiting.
  531|       |    ///
  532|       |    /// Returns `true` when the block has produced UI output (always true).
  533|      0|    pub(crate) fn render_block(
  534|      0|        &self,
  535|      0|        ui: &mut egui::Ui,
  536|      0|        code: &str,
  537|      0|        ui_scale: f32,
  538|      0|        code_font_size: f32,
  539|      0|    ) -> bool {
  540|      0|        let code_hash = hash_str(code);
  541|      0|        let dark_mode = ui.visuals().dark_mode;
  542|      0|        let svg_key = svg_cache_key(code_hash, dark_mode);
  543|       |
  544|       |        // 1. Check error cache (don't re-render known failures).
  545|       |        //    Errors depend only on syntax, not dark_mode, so keyed by code_hash.
  546|       |        //    Clone the error and drop the RefMut before calling render_error_block,
  547|       |        //    to avoid holding a borrow on self.errors across a &self method call.
  548|      0|        let cached_err = self.errors.borrow_mut().get(&code_hash);
  549|      0|        if let Some(err) = cached_err {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (549:16): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  550|      0|            self.render_error_block(ui, &err, code, code_font_size);
  551|      0|            return true;
  552|      0|        }
  553|       |
  554|       |        // 2. Compute texture cache key (incorporates width bucket + scale + theme)
  555|      0|        let available_width = ui.available_width().max(1.0);
  556|      0|        let width_bucket = bucket_width(available_width);
  557|      0|        let scale_bucket = bucket_scale(ui_scale);
  558|      0|        let texture_key = format!(
  559|       |            "pikchr:{:016x}:w{}:s{}:dm{}",
  560|      0|            code_hash, width_bucket, scale_bucket, dark_mode as u8
  561|       |        );
  562|       |
  563|       |        // 2b. Update wanted_bucket (for stale discard in poll_results)
  564|      0|        self.wanted_bucket
  565|      0|            .borrow_mut()
  566|      0|            .insert(code_hash, width_bucket);
  567|       |
  568|       |        // 3. Check texture cache.
  569|       |        {
  570|      0|            let mut cache = self.textures.borrow_mut();
  571|      0|            if let Some(entry) = cache.get(&texture_key) {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (571:20): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  572|      0|                Self::render_texture(ui, &entry, available_width);
  573|      0|                return true;
  574|      0|            }
  575|       |        }
  576|       |
  577|       |        // 3b. Debounce gate: suppress rasterization enqueue during resize.
  578|       |        //     Show the last rendered texture scaled to fit. Only enqueue
  579|       |        //     once the width stabilizes for RESIZE_DEBOUNCE_MS.
  580|      0|        let db_entry = self.debounce.borrow().get(&code_hash).map(|d| {
  581|      0|            (
  582|      0|                d.last_rasterized_bucket,
  583|      0|                d.last_seen_bucket,
  584|      0|                d.bucket_changed_at,
  585|      0|            )
  586|      0|        });
  587|      0|        if let Some((last_rasterized, last_seen, _changed_at)) = db_entry {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (587:16): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  588|      0|            if width_bucket != last_rasterized {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (588:16): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  589|       |                // Width changed since last rasterize -- apply trailing-edge debounce
  590|      0|                if width_bucket != last_seen {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (590:20): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  591|       |                    // New bucket since last frame -- reset timer
  592|      0|                    if let Some(d) = self.debounce.borrow_mut().get_mut(&code_hash) {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (592:28): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  593|      0|                        d.last_seen_bucket = width_bucket;
  594|      0|                        d.bucket_changed_at = Some(Instant::now());
  595|      0|                    }
  596|      0|                }
  597|      0|                let changed_at = self
  598|      0|                    .debounce
  599|      0|                    .borrow()
  600|      0|                    .get(&code_hash)
  601|      0|                    .and_then(|d| d.bucket_changed_at)
  602|      0|                    .unwrap_or_else(Instant::now);
  603|      0|                let elapsed = changed_at.elapsed();
  604|      0|                let debounce_dur = Duration::from_millis(RESIZE_DEBOUNCE_MS);
  605|      0|                if elapsed < debounce_dur {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (605:20): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  606|       |                    // Still in cooldown -- show stale texture scaled to fit
  607|      0|                    if let Some(stale_key) =
  ------------------
  |  Branch (607:28): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  608|      0|                        self.latest_texture_key.borrow().get(&code_hash).cloned()
  609|       |                    {
  610|      0|                        if let Some(entry) = self.textures.borrow_mut().get(&stale_key) {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (610:32): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  611|      0|                            Self::render_texture(ui, &entry, available_width);
  612|      0|                            ui.ctx().request_repaint_after(debounce_dur - elapsed);
  613|      0|                            return true;
  614|      0|                        }
  615|      0|                    }
  616|       |                    // No stale texture available (first render) -- fall through to enqueue
  617|      0|                }
  618|       |                // Debounce expired -- fall through to enqueue
  619|      0|            }
  620|       |            // width == last_rasterized: texture was evicted -- fall through to enqueue
  621|      0|        }
  622|       |        // No debounce entry: first render -- fall through to enqueue
  623|       |
  624|       |        // 4. Check if already pending for this diagram (by code_hash, not texture_key,
  625|       |        //    to prevent redundant enqueues when the width changes mid-flight)
  626|      0|        if self.pending.borrow().contains(&code_hash) {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (626:12): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  627|      0|            return self.show_stale_or_placeholder(ui, code_hash, available_width);
  628|      0|        }
  629|       |
  630|       |        // 5. Enqueue job
  631|      0|        let cached_svg = self.svg_cache.borrow_mut().get(&svg_key);
  632|      0|        let job = RasterJob {
  633|      0|            code_hash,
  634|      0|            texture_key: texture_key.clone(),
  635|      0|            code: if cached_svg.is_none() {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (635:22): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  636|      0|                Some(code.to_string())
  637|       |            } else {
  638|      0|                None
  639|       |            },
  640|      0|            svg: cached_svg,
  641|      0|            dark_mode,
  642|      0|            width_bucket,
  643|      0|            scale_bucket,
  644|       |        };
  645|       |
  646|      0|        match self.job_tx.as_ref().unwrap().try_send(job) {
  647|      0|            Ok(()) => {
  648|      0|                self.pending.borrow_mut().insert(code_hash);
  649|      0|            }
  650|      0|            Err(TrySendError::Full(_)) => {
  651|      0|                // Queue full -- will retry next frame
  652|      0|            }
  653|       |            Err(TrySendError::Disconnected(_)) => {
  654|       |                // Worker died -- show error
  655|      0|                self.errors
  656|      0|                    .borrow_mut()
  657|      0|                    .insert(code_hash, "Raster worker unavailable".to_string());
  658|      0|                self.render_error_block(ui, "Raster worker unavailable", code, code_font_size);
  659|      0|                return true;
  660|       |            }
  661|       |        }
  662|       |
  663|       |        // 6. Show stale texture or placeholder while waiting
  664|      0|        self.show_stale_or_placeholder(ui, code_hash, available_width)
  665|      0|    }
  666|       |
  667|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  668|       |    // Helpers
  669|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  670|       |
  671|       |    /// Show a stale texture (from a previous width) scaled to fit, or a
  672|       |    /// minimal-height placeholder if no stale texture is available.
  673|       |    /// Returns true (block always produces UI output).
  674|      0|    fn show_stale_or_placeholder(
  675|      0|        &self,
  676|      0|        ui: &mut egui::Ui,
  677|      0|        code_hash: u64,
  678|      0|        available_width: f32,
  679|      0|    ) -> bool {
  680|       |        // Try stale texture first (gives a good visual approximation)
  681|      0|        if let Some(stale_key) = self.latest_texture_key.borrow().get(&code_hash).cloned() {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (681:16): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  682|      0|            if let Some(entry) = self.textures.borrow_mut().get(&stale_key) {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (682:20): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  683|      0|                Self::render_texture(ui, &entry, available_width);
  684|      0|                return true;
  685|      0|            }
  686|      0|        }
  687|       |        // No stale texture -- show minimal placeholder (first render).
  688|       |        // Use a small fixed height to reduce layout shift when the real texture arrives.
  689|      0|        let placeholder_height = 60.0;
  690|      0|        let (rect, _) = ui.allocate_exact_size(
  691|      0|            egui::vec2(available_width, placeholder_height),
  692|      0|            egui::Sense::hover(),
  693|      0|        );
  694|      0|        ui.painter().text(
  695|      0|            rect.center(),
  696|       |            egui::Align2::CENTER_CENTER,
  697|       |            "Rendering...",
  698|      0|            egui::FontId::proportional(12.0),
  699|      0|            ui.visuals().weak_text_color(),
  700|       |        );
  701|      0|        true
  702|      0|    }
  703|       |
  704|       |    /// Parse the width bucket value from a texture cache key.
  705|       |    /// Key format: `pikchr:<hex>:w<width>:s<scale>:dm<0|1>`
  706|     14|    fn parse_width_from_texture_key(key: &str) -> Option<u32> {
  707|     14|        let w_start = key.find(":w")? + 2;
                          ^12                     ^2
  708|     12|        let w_end = key[w_start..].find(':')? + w_start;
                                                          ^0
  709|     12|        key[w_start..w_end].parse().ok()
  710|     14|    }
  711|       |
  712|       |    /// Check whether a result's width bucket no longer matches what the main
  713|       |    /// thread currently wants for this diagram. Stale results have their SVG
  714|       |    /// cached but skip the expensive GPU upload.
  715|      6|    fn is_stale_result(&self, texture_key: &str, code_hash: u64) -> bool {
  716|      6|        let result_bucket = Self::parse_width_from_texture_key(texture_key);
  717|      6|        let wanted = self.wanted_bucket.borrow().get(&code_hash).copied();
  718|      6|        match (result_bucket, wanted) {
  719|      4|            (Some(rb), Some(wb)) => rb != wb, // Width mismatch -> stale
  720|      2|            (Some(_), None) => true,          // No wanted bucket (e.g. after
  721|       |            // release_gpu_textures) -> stale
  722|      0|            _ => false, // Can't parse -> assume fresh
  723|       |        }
  724|      6|    }
  725|       |
  726|       |    /// Render Pikchr source code to an SVG string.
  727|       |    ///
  728|       |    /// Static associated function (no `&self`) -- pure input/output.
  729|       |    /// Uses the pikchr crate's builder-pattern API for flags.
  730|     90|    fn render_pikchr_to_svg(code: &str, dark_mode: bool) -> Result<String, String> {
  731|       |        use pikchr::{Pikchr, PikchrFlags};
  732|       |
  733|     90|        let mut flags = PikchrFlags::default();
  734|     90|        flags.generate_plain_errors();
  735|     90|        if dark_mode {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (735:12): [True: 8, False: 82]
  ------------------
  736|      8|            flags.use_dark_mode();
  737|     82|        }
  738|       |
  739|     90|        Pikchr::render(code, None, flags).map(|svg| svg.to_string())
                                                                  ^76 ^76
  740|       |        // Pikchr::render already returns Err(String); no map_err needed.
  741|     90|    }
  742|       |
  743|       |    /// Inject a `font-family` declaration into a Pikchr SVG string.
  744|       |    ///
  745|       |    /// Pikchr outputs `<text>` elements with no `font-family` attribute,
  746|       |    /// causing usvg to fall back to a potentially thin default font.
  747|       |    /// We prepend a `font-family` to the existing `<svg style='...'>`
  748|       |    /// so all text inherits a known good sans-serif font.
  749|     12|    fn inject_svg_font_family(svg: &str) -> String {
  750|       |        // Pikchr outputs: <svg ... style='font-size:initial;' ...>
  751|       |        // Inject font-family before the existing font-size declaration.
  752|     12|        if let Some(pos) = svg.find("style='font-size:") {
                                  ^8
  ------------------
  |  Branch (752:16): [True: 8, False: 4]
  ------------------
  753|      8|            let insert_at = pos + "style='".len();
  754|      8|            let mut result = String::with_capacity(svg.len() + 60);
  755|      8|            result.push_str(&svg[..insert_at]);
  756|      8|            result.push_str("font-family:\"Segoe UI\",\"Helvetica Neue\",Arial,sans-serif;");
  757|      8|            result.push_str(&svg[insert_at..]);
  758|      8|            result
  759|       |        } else {
  760|      4|            svg.to_string()
  761|       |        }
  762|     12|    }
  763|       |
  764|       |    /// Display a cached texture in the UI, scaling down if it exceeds available width.
  765|      0|    fn render_texture(ui: &mut egui::Ui, entry: &PikchrTextureEntry, available_width: f32) {
  766|      0|        let (tw, th) = (entry.display_size[0] as f32, entry.display_size[1] as f32);
  767|      0|        let scale = if tw > available_width {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (767:24): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  768|      0|            (available_width / tw).clamp(0.01, 4.0)
  769|       |        } else {
  770|      0|            1.0
  771|       |        };
  772|      0|        let size = egui::vec2((tw * scale).round(), (th * scale).round());
  773|      0|        ui.add(egui::Image::new(&entry.texture).fit_to_exact_size(size));
  774|      0|    }
  775|       |
  776|       |    /// Display a Pikchr error block with the error message and original source code.
  777|      0|    fn render_error_block(&self, ui: &mut egui::Ui, error: &str, code: &str, code_font_size: f32) {
  778|      0|        let tc = ThemeColors::current(ui.visuals().dark_mode);
  779|      0|        egui::Frame::none()

  781|      0|            .stroke(egui::Stroke::new(1.0, egui::Color32::from_rgb(200, 80, 80)))
  782|      0|            .inner_margin(8.0)
  783|      0|            .show(ui, |ui| {
  784|      0|                ui.label(
  785|      0|                    egui::RichText::new("Pikchr rendering error:")
  786|      0|                        .color(egui::Color32::from_rgb(200, 80, 80))
  787|      0|                        .size(code_font_size),
  788|       |                );
  789|      0|                ui.label(
  790|      0|                    egui::RichText::new(error)

  793|      0|                        .family(egui::FontFamily::Monospace),
  794|       |                );
  795|      0|                ui.add_space(4.0);
  796|      0|                ui.label(
  797|      0|                    egui::RichText::new(code)
  798|      0|                        .size(code_font_size)

  806|       |impl Drop for PikchrRenderer {
  807|    880|    fn drop(&mut self) {
  808|       |        // Close job channel -- worker will see Disconnected on recv() and exit.
  809|       |        // The worker uses try_send for results (never blocks), so it will
  810|       |        // drain remaining jobs and exit promptly once job_rx disconnects.
  811|    880|        self.job_tx.take();
  812|       |        // Join worker thread for clean shutdown
  813|    880|        if let Some(handle) = self.worker_handle.take() {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (813:16): [True: 880, False: 0]
  ------------------
  814|    880|            let _ = handle.join();
  815|    880|        }
                      ^0
  816|    880|    }

  898|       |    #[test]
  899|      2|    fn test_width_bucketing() {
  900|       |        // Same bucket for nearby widths (round() gives +/-16px half-buckets)
  901|      2|        assert_eq!(bucket_width(640.0), bucket_width(641.0));
  902|      2|        assert_eq!(bucket_width(640.0), bucket_width(655.0)); // 655/32=20.47 -> rounds to 20
  903|       |                                                              // Different buckets once we cross the half-bucket boundary
  904|      2|        assert_ne!(bucket_width(640.0), bucket_width(656.0)); // 656/32=20.5 -> rounds to 21
  905|      2|        assert_ne!(bucket_width(640.0), bucket_width(672.0)); // clearly different bucket
  906|       |                                                              // Verify actual bucket values
  907|      2|        assert_eq!(bucket_width(640.0), 640); // 640/32=20.0 -> 20*32=640
  908|      2|        assert_eq!(bucket_width(672.0), 672); // 672/32=21.0 -> 21*32=672
  909|      2|    }

  959|       |    #[test]
  960|      2|    fn test_scale_bucketing() {
  961|      2|        assert_eq!(bucket_scale(1.0), 100);
  962|      2|        assert_eq!(bucket_scale(1.25), 125);
  963|      2|        assert_eq!(bucket_scale(1.5), 150);
  964|      2|        assert_eq!(bucket_scale(2.0), 200);
  965|      2|        assert_eq!(bucket_scale(0.5), 50);
  966|       |        // Rounding: 1.124 * 100 = 112.4 -> rounds to 112
  967|      2|        assert_eq!(bucket_scale(1.124), 112);
  968|       |        // Rounding: 1.126 * 100 = 112.6 -> rounds to 113
  969|      2|        assert_eq!(bucket_scale(1.126), 113);
  970|       |        // Values within rounding distance bucket together
  971|      2|        assert_eq!(bucket_scale(1.001), bucket_scale(1.004)); // both round to 100
  972|      2|    }

 1094|      2|    fn test_svg_dimensions_are_reasonable() {
 1095|      2|        let svg = PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg("box \"Test\" fit", false).unwrap();
 1096|       |        // Pikchr uses viewBox="x y width height" for dimensions
 1097|      2|        let (w, h) =
 1098|      2|            extract_viewbox_size(&svg).expect("SVG should have a parseable viewBox attribute");
 1099|      2|        assert!(w > 0.0, "viewBox width must be > 0, got {}", w);
 1100|      2|        assert!(h > 0.0, "viewBox height must be > 0, got {}", h);
 1101|      2|        assert!(w < 10000.0, "viewBox width must be < 10000, got {}", w);

 1103|      2|    }

 1105|       |    #[test]
 1106|      2|    fn test_different_diagrams_produce_different_dimensions() {
 1107|      2|        let small = PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg("box", false).unwrap();
 1108|      2|        let large = PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg(
 1109|      2|            "box; arrow right; box; arrow right; box; arrow right; box; arrow right; box",
 1110|       |            false,
 1111|       |        )
 1112|      2|        .unwrap();
 1113|      2|        let (w_small, _) = extract_viewbox_size(&small).expect("small SVG should have viewBox");
 1114|      2|        let (w_large, _) = extract_viewbox_size(&large).expect("large SVG should have viewBox");
 1115|      2|        assert!(
 1116|      2|            w_large > w_small,
 1117|       |            "Chain of boxes should be wider than single box: {} vs {}",
 1118|       |            w_large,
 1119|       |            w_small
 1120|       |        );
 1121|      2|    }
 1122|       |
 1123|       |    /// Helper: extract width and height from an SVG `viewBox="x y w h"` attribute.
 1124|      6|    fn extract_viewbox_size(svg: &str) -> Option<(f64, f64)> {
 1125|      6|        let marker = "viewBox=\"";
 1126|      6|        let start = svg.find(marker)? + marker.len();
                                                  ^0
 1127|      6|        let rest = &svg[start..];
 1128|      6|        let end = rest.find('"')?;
                                              ^0
 1129|      6|        let parts: Vec<f64> = rest[..end]
 1130|      6|            .split_whitespace()
 1131|     24|            .filter_map(|s| s.parse().ok())
                           ^6
 1132|      6|            .collect();
 1133|      6|        if parts.len() >= 4 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (1133:12): [True: 6, False: 0]
  ------------------
 1134|      6|            Some((parts[2], parts[3]))
 1135|       |        } else {
 1136|      0|            None
 1137|       |        }
 1138|      6|    }

 1170|       |            svg

 1186|       |    #[test]
 1187|      2|    fn test_error_message_has_content() {
 1188|      2|        let err = PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg("this is not valid pikchr }{}{", false)
 1189|      2|            .unwrap_err();
 1190|      2|        assert!(
 1191|      2|            !err.is_empty(),
 1192|       |            "Error message should not be empty for invalid syntax"
 1193|       |        );
 1194|       |        // Error should contain some useful diagnostic info
 1195|      2|        assert!(
 1196|      2|            err.len() > 5,
 1197|       |            "Error message should be descriptive, got: '{}'",
 1198|       |            err
 1199|       |        );
 1200|      2|    }
 1201|       |
 1202|       |    #[test]
 1203|      2|    fn test_error_cache_theme_independent() {
 1204|       |        // Errors are keyed by code_hash, not theme -- same syntax error in
 1205|       |        // light and dark mode should produce the same error message.
 1206|      2|        let invalid = "totally broken pikchr syntax }{}{";
 1207|      2|        let err_light = PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg(invalid, false).unwrap_err();
 1208|      2|        let err_dark = PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg(invalid, true).unwrap_err();
 1209|      2|        assert_eq!(
 1210|       |            err_light, err_dark,
 1211|       |            "Same syntax error should produce same message regardless of theme"
 1212|       |        );
 1213|      2|    }
 1214|       |
 1215|       |    #[test]
 1216|      2|    fn test_multiple_errors_are_distinct() {
 1217|      2|        let err1 =
 1218|      2|            PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg("invalid syntax aaa }{}{", false).unwrap_err();
 1219|      2|        let err2 =
 1220|      2|            PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg("different invalid bbb }{}{", false).unwrap_err();
 1221|       |        // Different invalid inputs may produce different error messages
 1222|       |        // (at minimum they should both be errors, which we've already asserted
 1223|       |        // by unwrap_err). If they happen to be the same generic error, that's
 1224|       |        // still valid -- but let's verify they're both non-empty.
 1225|      2|        assert!(!err1.is_empty(), "Error 1 should not be empty");
 1226|      2|        assert!(!err2.is_empty(), "Error 2 should not be empty");
 1227|      2|    }

 1233|       |    #[test]
 1234|      2|    fn test_dark_mode_inverts_stroke_colors() {
 1235|      2|        let light = PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg("box \"X\" fit", false).unwrap();
 1236|      2|        let dark = PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg("box \"X\" fit", true).unwrap();
 1237|       |
 1238|       |        // Pikchr uses PIKCHR_DARK_MODE flag to invert colors.
 1239|       |        // Light mode should contain dark strokes (e.g., "black" or rgb(0,0,0))
 1240|       |        // Dark mode should contain light strokes (e.g., "white" or rgb(255,255,255))
 1241|      2|        let light_has_black =
 1242|      2|            light.contains("black") || light.contains("rgb(0,0,0)") || light.contains("#000");
                                                                                     ^0
  ------------------
  |  Branch (1242:13): [True: 0, False: 2]
  |  Branch (1242:40): [True: 2, False: 0]
  ------------------
 1243|      2|        let dark_has_white =
 1244|      2|            dark.contains("white") || dark.contains("rgb(255,255,255)") || dark.contains("#fff");
                                                                                         ^0
  ------------------
  |  Branch (1244:13): [True: 0, False: 2]
  |  Branch (1244:39): [True: 2, False: 0]
  ------------------
 1245|       |
 1246|      2|        assert!(
 1247|      2|            light_has_black,
 1248|       |            "Light mode SVG should reference dark colors:\n{}",
 1249|       |            light
 1250|       |        );
 1251|      2|        assert!(
 1252|      2|            dark_has_white,
 1253|       |            "Dark mode SVG should reference light colors:\n{}",
 1254|       |            dark
 1255|       |        );
 1256|      2|    }
 1257|       |

 1259|      2|    fn test_dark_mode_preserves_labels() {

 1281|      2|    fn test_large_diagram_renders() {
 1282|       |        // Build a chain of 50+ boxes connected by arrows
 1283|      2|        let mut code = String::from("box \"B0\" fit\n");
 1284|    100|        for i in 1..=50 {
                               ^2
 1285|    100|            code.push_str(&format!("arrow right\nbox \"B{}\" fit\n", i));
 1286|    100|        }
 1287|      2|        let result = PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg(&code, false);
 1288|      2|        assert!(
 1289|      2|            result.is_ok(),

 1291|      0|            result.err()
 1292|       |        );
 1293|      2|        let svg = result.unwrap();
 1294|      2|        assert!(svg.contains("B0"), "First box label missing");
 1295|      2|        assert!(svg.contains("B50"), "Last box label missing");
 1296|      2|    }

 1334|       |    #[test]
 1335|      2|    fn test_svg_output_snapshot() {
 1336|       |        // Render a known diagram and validate specific SVG structural fragments.
 1337|       |        // This acts as a characterization test documenting what the output looks like.
 1338|      2|        let code = r#"arrow right 200% "Input" above
 1339|      2|box "Process" fit rad 10px
 1340|      2|arrow right 200% "Output" above"#;
 1341|      2|        let svg = PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg(code, false).unwrap();
 1342|       |
 1343|       |        // Must have proper SVG wrapper
 1344|      2|        assert!(svg.starts_with("<svg"), "SVG must start with <svg tag");
 1345|      2|        assert!(
 1346|      2|            svg.trim_end().ends_with("</svg>"),
 1347|       |            "SVG must end with </svg>"
 1348|       |        );
 1349|       |
 1350|       |        // Must have viewBox for proper scaling
 1351|      2|        assert!(svg.contains("viewBox"), "SVG should have viewBox attribute");
 1352|       |
 1353|       |        // Arrow produces polygon elements (arrowheads)
 1354|      2|        assert!(
 1355|      2|            svg.contains("<polygon"),
 1356|       |            "Arrows should produce <polygon> arrowheads"
 1357|       |        );
 1358|       |
 1359|       |        // Labels appear as text elements
 1360|      2|        assert!(
 1361|      2|            svg.contains("<text"),
 1362|       |            "Labels should produce <text> elements"
 1363|       |        );
 1364|      2|        assert!(svg.contains("Input"), "Label 'Input' must be in SVG");
 1365|      2|        assert!(svg.contains("Process"), "Label 'Process' must be in SVG");
 1366|      2|        assert!(svg.contains("Output"), "Label 'Output' must be in SVG");
 1367|       |
 1368|       |        // Lines appear as path or line elements
 1369|      2|        assert!(
 1370|      2|            svg.contains("<line") || svg.contains("<path"),
  ------------------
  |  Branch (1370:13): [True: 0, False: 2]
  |  Branch (1370:38): [True: 2, False: 0]
  ------------------
 1371|       |            "Connectors should produce <line> or <path> elements"
 1372|       |        );
 1373|      2|    }

 1429|      2|    fn test_parse_width_from_texture_key() {

 1431|      2|            PikchrRenderer::parse_width_from_texture_key("pikchr:0000000000003039:w640:s100:dm0"),
 1432|       |            Some(640)
 1433|       |        );

 1435|      2|            PikchrRenderer::parse_width_from_texture_key("pikchr:abc:w1024:s150:dm1"),
 1436|       |            Some(1024)
 1437|       |        );

 1446|       |        );

 1449|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
 1450|       |    // Async worker and channel tests (V3 HLD tests #1-14)
 1451|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
 1452|       |
 1453|       |    /// Helper: create a fontdb with system fonts loaded (shared across worker tests).
 1454|     10|    fn make_fontdb() -> Arc<usvg::fontdb::Database> {
 1455|     10|        let mut db = usvg::fontdb::Database::new();
 1456|     10|        db.load_system_fonts();
 1457|     10|        Arc::new(db)
 1458|     10|    }
 1459|       |
 1460|       |    #[test]
 1461|      2|    fn test_worker_processes_job() {
 1462|      2|        let fontdb = make_fontdb();
 1463|      2|        let (job_tx, job_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded::<RasterJob>(4);
 1464|      2|        let (result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded::<RasterResult>(4);
 1465|       |
 1466|      2|        let code = "box \"Hello\" fit";
 1467|      2|        let code_hash = hash_str(code);
 1468|      2|        let texture_key = format!("pikchr:{:016x}:w640:s100:dm0", code_hash);
 1469|      2|        let job = RasterJob {
 1470|      2|            code_hash,
 1471|      2|            texture_key: texture_key.clone(),
 1472|      2|            code: Some(code.to_string()),
 1473|      2|            svg: None,
 1474|      2|            dark_mode: false,
 1475|      2|            width_bucket: 640,
 1476|      2|            scale_bucket: 100,
 1477|      2|        };
 1478|       |
 1479|      2|        job_tx.send(job).unwrap();
 1480|       |        // Drop sender so worker_loop exits after processing the one job.
 1481|      2|        drop(job_tx);
 1482|       |
 1483|      2|        PikchrRenderer::worker_loop(fontdb, job_rx, result_tx);
 1484|       |
 1485|      2|        let result = result_rx.recv_timeout(Duration::from_secs(5)).unwrap();
 1486|      2|        assert!(result.rgba.is_some(), "Worker should produce RGBA data");
 1487|      2|        assert!(
 1488|      2|            result.raster_size.is_some(),
 1489|       |            "Worker should produce raster_size"
 1490|       |        );
 1491|      2|        assert!(
 1492|      2|            result.display_size.is_some(),
 1493|       |            "Worker should produce display_size"
 1494|       |        );
 1495|      2|        assert!(result.error.is_none(), "Worker should not produce an error");
 1496|      2|        assert!(result.svg.is_some(), "Worker should produce SVG on success");
 1497|      2|    }
 1498|       |
 1499|       |    #[test]
 1500|      2|    fn test_worker_caches_svg_on_success() {
 1501|      2|        let fontdb = make_fontdb();
 1502|      2|        let (job_tx, job_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded::<RasterJob>(4);
 1503|      2|        let (result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded::<RasterResult>(4);
 1504|       |
 1505|      2|        let code = "box \"Hello\" fit";
 1506|      2|        let code_hash = hash_str(code);
 1507|      2|        let texture_key = format!("pikchr:{:016x}:w640:s100:dm0", code_hash);
 1508|      2|        let job = RasterJob {
 1509|      2|            code_hash,
 1510|      2|            texture_key,
 1511|      2|            code: Some(code.to_string()),
 1512|      2|            svg: None,
 1513|      2|            dark_mode: false,
 1514|      2|            width_bucket: 640,
 1515|      2|            scale_bucket: 100,
 1516|      2|        };
 1517|       |
 1518|      2|        job_tx.send(job).unwrap();
 1519|      2|        drop(job_tx);
 1520|       |
 1521|      2|        PikchrRenderer::worker_loop(fontdb, job_rx, result_tx);
 1522|       |
 1523|      2|        let result = result_rx.recv_timeout(Duration::from_secs(5)).unwrap();
 1524|      2|        assert!(result.svg.is_some(), "SVG should be cached on success");
 1525|      2|        let svg = result.svg.unwrap();
 1526|      2|        assert!(
 1527|      2|            svg.contains("<svg"),
 1528|       |            "Cached SVG should contain <svg tag, got: {}",
 1529|      0|            &svg[..svg.len().min(200)]
 1530|       |        );
 1531|      2|    }

 1533|       |    #[test]
 1534|      2|    fn test_worker_caches_error() {
 1535|      2|        let fontdb = make_fontdb();
 1536|      2|        let (job_tx, job_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded::<RasterJob>(4);
 1537|      2|        let (result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded::<RasterResult>(4);
 1538|       |
 1539|      2|        let code = "invalid syntax }{}{";
 1540|      2|        let code_hash = hash_str(code);
 1541|      2|        let texture_key = format!("pikchr:{:016x}:w640:s100:dm0", code_hash);
 1542|      2|        let job = RasterJob {
 1543|      2|            code_hash,
 1544|      2|            texture_key,
 1545|      2|            code: Some(code.to_string()),
 1546|      2|            svg: None,
 1547|      2|            dark_mode: false,
 1548|      2|            width_bucket: 640,
 1549|      2|            scale_bucket: 100,
 1550|      2|        };
 1551|       |
 1552|      2|        job_tx.send(job).unwrap();
 1553|      2|        drop(job_tx);
 1554|       |
 1555|      2|        PikchrRenderer::worker_loop(fontdb, job_rx, result_tx);
 1556|       |
 1557|      2|        let result = result_rx.recv_timeout(Duration::from_secs(5)).unwrap();
 1558|      2|        assert!(
 1559|      2|            result.error.is_some(),
 1560|       |            "Worker should produce an error for invalid code"

 1564|       |            "Worker should not produce RGBA for invalid code"
 1565|       |        );

 1567|      2|            result.svg.is_none(),
 1568|       |            "Worker should not produce SVG for invalid code"
 1569|       |        );

 1572|       |    #[test]
 1573|      2|    fn test_is_stale_result_width_mismatch() {
 1574|      2|        let renderer = PikchrRenderer::new();
 1575|      2|        let code_hash = 12345u64;
 1576|       |
 1577|       |        // Insert wanted_bucket: code_hash -> width 640
 1578|      2|        renderer.wanted_bucket.borrow_mut().insert(code_hash, 640);
 1579|       |
 1580|       |        // Texture key with w1024 should be stale (mismatch)
 1581|      2|        let stale_key = format!("pikchr:{:016x}:w1024:s100:dm0", code_hash);
 1582|      2|        assert!(
 1583|      2|            renderer.is_stale_result(&stale_key, code_hash),
 1584|       |            "Width 1024 vs wanted 640 should be stale"
 1585|       |        );
 1586|       |
 1587|       |        // Texture key with w640 should be fresh (match)
 1588|      2|        let fresh_key = format!("pikchr:{:016x}:w640:s100:dm0", code_hash);
 1589|      2|        assert!(
 1590|      2|            !renderer.is_stale_result(&fresh_key, code_hash),
 1591|       |            "Width 640 vs wanted 640 should be fresh"
 1592|       |        );
 1593|      2|    }

 1601|      2|            renderer.is_stale_result(&texture_key, 99999),

 1628|       |    #[test]
 1629|      2|    fn test_release_gpu_clears_pending_and_wanted() {
 1630|      2|        let renderer = PikchrRenderer::new();
 1631|       |
 1632|       |        // Populate all four collections
 1633|      2|        renderer.pending.borrow_mut().insert(1);
 1634|      2|        renderer.wanted_bucket.borrow_mut().insert(1, 640);
 1635|      2|        renderer.debounce.borrow_mut().insert(
 1636|      2|            1,
 1637|      2|            DiagramDebounce {
 1638|      2|                last_rasterized_bucket: 640,
 1639|      2|                last_seen_bucket: 640,
 1640|      2|                bucket_changed_at: None,
 1641|      2|            },
 1642|      2|        );
 1643|      2|        renderer
 1644|      2|            .latest_texture_key
 1645|      2|            .borrow_mut()
 1646|      2|            .insert(1, "pikchr:key".to_string());
 1647|       |
 1648|      2|        renderer.release_gpu_textures();
 1649|       |
 1650|      2|        assert!(
 1651|      2|            renderer.pending.borrow().is_empty(),
 1652|       |            "pending should be empty after release_gpu_textures"
 1653|       |        );
 1654|      2|        assert!(
 1655|      2|            renderer.wanted_bucket.borrow().is_empty(),
 1656|       |            "wanted_bucket should be empty after release_gpu_textures"
 1657|       |        );
 1658|      2|        assert!(
 1659|      2|            renderer.debounce.borrow().is_empty(),
 1660|       |            "debounce should be empty after release_gpu_textures"
 1661|       |        );
 1662|      2|        assert!(
 1663|      2|            renderer.latest_texture_key.borrow().is_empty(),
 1664|       |            "latest_texture_key should be empty after release_gpu_textures"
 1665|       |        );
 1666|      2|    }
 1667|       |
 1668|       |    #[test]
 1669|      2|    fn test_queue_full_does_not_block() {
 1670|      2|        let (tx, _rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded::<RasterJob>(2);
 1671|       |
 1672|       |        // Fill the channel with 2 dummy jobs
 1673|      4|        for i in 0..2 {
                               ^2
 1674|      4|            tx.send(RasterJob {
 1675|      4|                code_hash: i,
 1676|      4|                texture_key: format!("pikchr:dummy:w640:s100:dm0:{}", i),
 1677|      4|                code: Some("box".to_string()),
 1678|      4|                svg: None,
 1679|      4|                dark_mode: false,

    1|       |// Shell integration utilities for OS-level file operations.
    2|       |//
    3|       |// Provides cross-platform functions for interacting with the operating system's
    4|       |// file manager and clipboard at the file level:
    5|       |//
    6|       |// - `clipboard_copy_file`: Places a file path on the system clipboard as a
    7|       |//   file copy operation (CF_HDROP on Windows, osascript on macOS, xclip on
    8|       |//   Linux). This allows the user to paste the file into Explorer/Finder/etc.
    9|       |//
   10|       |// - `reveal_in_file_manager`: Opens the native file manager (Explorer, Finder,
   11|       |//   or xdg-open) with the specified file selected/highlighted.
   12|       |//
   13|       |// These functions are used by the application's File menu to provide
   14|       |// shell operations on the currently open file. Each function uses platform-specific
   15|       |// implementations gated by `#[cfg(target_os = "...")]` attributes and returns
   16|       |// `anyhow::Result` for consistent error handling.
   17|       |
   18|       |use std::path::Path;
   19|       |

   22|       |///
   23|       |/// After calling this, the user can paste the file in their file manager to

   49|       |/// Place a file on the system clipboard as a file copy (macOS via osascript).
   50|       |#[cfg(target_os = "macos")]
   51|       |pub fn clipboard_copy_file(path: &Path) -> anyhow::Result<()> {
   52|       |    let abs_path = path.canonicalize()?;
   53|       |    // Escape backslashes and double-quotes for AppleScript string literal
   54|       |    let escaped = abs_path
   55|       |        .display()
   56|       |        .to_string()
   57|       |        .replace('\\', "\\\\")
   58|       |        .replace('"', "\\\"");
   59|       |    let script = format!("set the clipboard to (POSIX file \"{}\")", escaped);
   60|       |    let output = std::process::Command::new("osascript")
   61|       |        .arg("-e")
   62|       |        .arg(&script)
   63|       |        .output()?;
   64|       |    if !output.status.success() {
   65|       |        anyhow::bail!(
   66|       |            "osascript failed: {}",
   67|       |            String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr)
   68|       |        );
   69|       |    }
   70|       |    Ok(())
   71|       |}
   72|       |
   73|       |/// Place a file on the system clipboard as a file copy (Linux via xclip).
   74|       |#[cfg(not(any(target_os = "windows", target_os = "macos")))]
   75|       |pub fn clipboard_copy_file(path: &Path) -> anyhow::Result<()> {
   76|       |    let abs_path = path.canonicalize()?;
   77|       |    let uri = format!("file://{}\n", abs_path.display());
   78|       |    let mut child = std::process::Command::new("xclip")
   79|       |        .args(["-selection", "clipboard", "-t", "text/uri-list"])
   80|       |        .stdin(std::process::Stdio::piped())
   81|       |        .spawn()
   82|       |        .map_err(|_| anyhow::anyhow!("xclip not found. Install with: sudo apt install xclip"))?;
   83|       |    if let Some(stdin) = child.stdin.as_mut() {
   84|       |        use std::io::Write;
   85|       |        stdin.write_all(uri.as_bytes())?;
   86|       |    }
   87|       |    let status = child.wait()?;
   88|       |    if !status.success() {
   89|       |        anyhow::bail!("xclip exited with status {}", status);
   90|       |    }
   91|       |    Ok(())
   92|       |}

  558|      8|                if !text.is_empty() {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (558:20): [True: 2, False: 6]
  ------------------
  559|      2|                    text.push(' ');
  560|      6|                }
  561|      8|                text.push_str(t);
  562|       |            }
  563|      6|            InlineSpan::Image { alt, .. } => {
  564|      6|                if !text.is_empty() {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (564:20): [True: 2, False: 4]
  ------------------
  565|      2|                    text.push(' ');
  566|      4|                }
  567|      6|                text.push_str(alt);
  568|       |            }
  569|       |        }
  570|       |    }
  571|    500|    text.trim().to_string()
  572|    500|}
  573|       |

  591|       |        assert!(matches!(specs[1].policy, ColumnPolicy::Remainder { .. }));

  653|       |            vec![span("Right")],
  654|       |        ];

  656|       |            vec![span("Centered label with enough words to raise score")],
  657|       |            vec![span("L1")],

  659|       |        ]];
  660|       |        let stats = compute_column_stats(&headers, &rows, 32);

  664|       |        assert!(specs[1..]
  665|       |            .iter()

  759|       |    fn multiple_remainder_columns_allowed() {
  760|       |        let headers = vec![
  761|       |            vec![span("Summary")],
  762|       |            vec![span("Details")],
  763|       |            vec![span("Examples")],
  764|       |        ];
  765|       |        let rows = vec![vec![
  766|       |            vec![span("short")],
  767|       |            vec![span(
  768|       |                "Long content that should trigger a remainder column due to its width and words.",
  769|       |            )],
  770|       |            vec![span(
  771|       |                "Another large column with links https://example.com and more text.",
  772|       |            )],
  773|       |        ]];
  774|       |        let stats = compute_column_stats(&headers, &rows, 32);
  775|       |        let ctx = TableColumnContext::new(&headers, &rows, &stats, 14.0, 0);
  776|       |        let specs = derive_column_specs(&ctx);
  777|       |        let remainder_count = specs
  778|       |            .iter()
  779|       |            .filter(|s| matches!(s.policy, ColumnPolicy::Remainder { .. }))
  780|       |            .count();
  781|       |        assert!(remainder_count >= 2);
  782|       |    }

  793|       |    fn test_header_text_and_spans_to_text_variants() {
  794|       |        let spans = vec![
  795|       |            InlineSpan::Text(" Alpha ".to_string()),
  796|       |            InlineSpan::Strong("Beta".to_string()),
  797|       |            InlineSpan::Emphasis("Gamma".to_string()),
  798|       |            InlineSpan::Strikethrough("Delta".to_string()),
  799|       |            InlineSpan::Code("Epsilon".to_string()),
  800|       |            InlineSpan::Link {
  801|       |                text: "Zeta".to_string(),
  802|       |                url: "https://example.invalid".to_string(),
  803|       |            },
  804|       |            InlineSpan::Image {
  805|       |                src: "img.png".to_string(),
  806|       |                alt: "Eta".to_string(),
  807|       |                title: None,
  808|       |            },
  809|       |        ];
  810|       |
  811|       |        let header = header_text(&spans);
  812|       |        assert!(header.contains("Alpha"));
  813|       |        assert!(header.contains("Beta"));
  814|       |        assert!(header.contains("Zeta"));
  815|       |        assert!(header.contains("Eta"));
  816|       |
  817|       |        let text = spans_to_text(&spans);
  818|       |        assert!(text.contains("Alpha"));
  819|       |        assert!(text.contains("Zeta"));
  820|       |        assert!(text.contains("Eta"));
  821|       |
  822|       |        assert_eq!(header_text(&[]), "Column");
  823|       |        assert_eq!(
  824|       |            header_text(&[InlineSpan::Text("   ".to_string())]),
  825|       |            "Column"
  826|       |        );
  827|       |    }

  834|       |                width: 120.0,
  835|       |                clip: true,
  836|       |            },
  837|       |            ColumnPolicy::Remainder { clip: false },
  838|       |            ColumnPolicy::Resizable {
  839|       |                min: 50.0,
  840|       |                preferred: 140.0,
  841|       |                clip: true,
  842|       |            },
  843|       |        ];
  844|       |
  845|       |        for policy in policies {
  846|       |            let _col = policy.to_column();
  847|       |            let tooltip = column_tooltip("Header", &policy).expect("tooltip");
  848|       |            assert!(tooltip.contains("Header"));
  849|       |        }
  850|       |    }

  852|       |    #[test]
  853|       |    fn test_apply_preferred_width_clamps_and_hash_stable_for_resizable() {
  854|       |        let mut spec = ColumnSpec::new(
  855|       |            0,
  856|       |            "body",
  857|       |            ColumnPolicy::Resizable {
  858|       |                min: 50.0,
  859|       |                preferred: 120.0,
  860|       |                clip: false,
  861|       |            },
  862|       |            None,
  863|       |        );
  864|       |        let original_hash = spec.policy_hash;
  865|       |        spec.apply_preferred_width(10.0);
  866|       |        assert!(matches!(spec.policy, ColumnPolicy::Resizable { .. }));
  867|       |        assert_eq!(spec.policy_hash, original_hash);
  868|       |
  869|       |        let mut fixed = ColumnSpec::new(
  870|       |            1,
  871|       |            "fixed",
  872|       |            ColumnPolicy::Fixed {
  873|       |                width: 40.0,
  874|       |                clip: false,
  875|       |            },
  876|       |            None,
  877|       |        );
  878|       |        let fixed_hash = fixed.policy_hash;
  879|       |        fixed.apply_preferred_width(100.0);
  880|       |        assert_eq!(fixed.policy_hash, fixed_hash);
  881|       |    }

  888|       |            clip: false,

  939|       |            width: 120.0,
  940|       |            clip: false,
  941|       |        };
  942|       |        let remainder = ColumnPolicy::Remainder { clip: true };
  943|       |

  945|       |        assert!(!fixed_tooltip.contains("clipped"));

  954|       |        let original = spec.policy_hash;
  955|       |        spec.set_policy(ColumnPolicy::Fixed {

  978|       |    #[test]
  979|       |    fn test_column_policy_hash_variants() {
  980|       |        let policies = [
  981|       |            ColumnPolicy::Auto,
  982|       |            ColumnPolicy::Fixed {
  983|       |                width: 120.0,
  984|       |                clip: true,
  985|       |            },
  986|       |            ColumnPolicy::Remainder { clip: true },
  987|       |            ColumnPolicy::Resizable {
  988|       |                min: 40.0,
  989|       |                preferred: 120.0,
  990|       |                clip: true,
  991|       |            },
  992|       |        ];
  993|       |        for policy in policies {
  994|       |            let mut hasher = std::collections::hash_map::DefaultHasher::new();
  995|       |            policy.hash(&mut hasher);
  996|       |            let _ = hasher.finish();
  997|       |        }
  998|       |    }

 1000|       |    #[test]
 1001|       |    fn test_column_policy_to_column_clipped_variants() {
 1002|       |        let fixed = ColumnPolicy::Fixed {
 1003|       |            width: 120.0,
 1004|       |            clip: true,
 1005|       |        };
 1006|       |        let fixed_unclipped = ColumnPolicy::Fixed {
 1007|       |            width: 120.0,
 1008|       |            clip: false,
 1009|       |        };
 1010|       |        let remainder = ColumnPolicy::Remainder { clip: true };
 1011|       |        let resizable = ColumnPolicy::Resizable {
 1012|       |            min: 40.0,
 1013|       |            preferred: 120.0,
 1014|       |            clip: true,
 1015|       |        };
 1016|       |        let _ = fixed.to_column();
 1017|       |        let _ = fixed_unclipped.to_column();
 1018|       |        let _ = remainder.to_column();
 1019|       |        let _ = resizable.to_column();
 1020|       |    }

 1039|       |    #[test]

 1183|       |        assert!(is_emoji_like('\u{1F600}')); // grinning face
 1184|       |        assert!(is_emoji_like('\u{1F64F}')); // person with folded hands
 1185|       |
 1186|       |        // Miscellaneous Symbols (0x2600-0x26FF)
 1187|       |        assert!(is_emoji_like('\u{2600}')); // sun
 1188|       |        assert!(is_emoji_like('\u{26FF}')); // end of block
 1189|       |
 1190|       |        // Dingbats (0x2700-0x27BF)
 1191|       |        assert!(is_emoji_like('\u{2700}')); // black scissors
 1192|       |        assert!(is_emoji_like('\u{27BF}')); // end of block
 1193|       |
 1194|       |        // Transport and Map (0x1F680-0x1F6FF)
 1195|       |        assert!(is_emoji_like('\u{1F680}')); // rocket
 1196|       |        assert!(is_emoji_like('\u{1F6FF}')); // end of block
 1197|       |
 1198|       |        // Supplemental Symbols (0x1F900-0x1F9FF)
 1199|       |        assert!(is_emoji_like('\u{1F900}')); // start of block
 1200|       |        assert!(is_emoji_like('\u{1F9FF}')); // end of block
 1201|       |
 1202|       |        // Extended-A (0x1FA00-0x1FAFF)
 1203|       |        assert!(is_emoji_like('\u{1FA00}')); // start of block
 1204|       |        assert!(is_emoji_like('\u{1FAFF}')); // end of block
 1205|       |
 1206|       |        // Non-emoji should return false
 1207|       |        assert!(!is_emoji_like('A'));
 1208|       |        assert!(!is_emoji_like('z'));
 1209|       |        assert!(!is_emoji_like('0'));
 1210|       |        assert!(!is_emoji_like(' '));

  188|       |#[cfg(test)]

  190|       |mod tests {
  191|       |    use super::*;
  192|       |
  193|       |    #[test]
  194|       |    fn update_widths_detects_large_change() {
  195|       |        let mut entry = TableMetricEntry::default();
  196|       |        assert_eq!(entry.update_widths(&[100.0, 120.0], 1), WidthChange::None);
  197|       |        assert_eq!(entry.update_widths(&[100.2, 120.6], 2), WidthChange::Small);
  198|       |        assert_eq!(entry.update_widths(&[140.0, 120.6], 3), WidthChange::Large);
  199|       |    }
  200|       |
  201|       |    #[test]
  202|       |    fn update_widths_preserves_rows_on_width_changes() {
  203|       |        let mut entry = TableMetricEntry::default();
  204|       |
  205|       |        // Set up some rows
  206|       |        entry.ensure_row(0).max_height = 20.0;
  207|       |        entry.ensure_row(1).max_height = 25.0;
  208|       |        assert_eq!(entry.rows.len(), 2);
  209|       |
  210|       |        // First update should preserve rows (uniform row heights approach)
  211|       |        assert_eq!(entry.update_widths(&[100.0, 120.0], 1), WidthChange::None);
  212|       |        assert_eq!(entry.rows.len(), 2, "first update should preserve rows");
  213|       |
  214|       |        // Small change should preserve rows
  215|       |        assert_eq!(entry.update_widths(&[100.2, 120.6], 2), WidthChange::Small);
  216|       |        assert_eq!(entry.rows.len(), 2, "small change should preserve rows");
  217|       |
  218|       |        // Large change should preserve rows
  219|       |        assert_eq!(entry.update_widths(&[140.0, 120.6], 3), WidthChange::Large);
  220|       |        assert_eq!(entry.rows.len(), 2, "large change should preserve rows");
  221|       |
  222|       |        // No change should preserve rows
  223|       |        assert_eq!(entry.update_widths(&[140.0, 120.6], 4), WidthChange::None);
  224|       |        assert_eq!(entry.rows.len(), 2, "no change should preserve rows");
  225|       |    }
  226|       |
  227|       |    #[test]
  228|       |    fn font_size_change_clears_persisted_widths() {
  229|       |        let mut entry = TableMetricEntry::default();
  230|       |
  231|       |        // Set some persisted widths at font size 14.0
  232|       |        entry.set_persisted_width(1, 100.0);
  233|       |        entry.set_persisted_width(2, 150.0);
  234|       |        assert!(!entry.check_font_size_change(14.0)); // First call sets baseline
  235|       |        assert_eq!(entry.persisted_width(1), Some(100.0));
  236|       |        assert_eq!(entry.persisted_width(2), Some(150.0));
  237|       |
  238|       |        // Small font size change (within epsilon) should NOT clear
  239|       |        assert!(!entry.check_font_size_change(14.3));
  240|       |        assert_eq!(entry.persisted_width(1), Some(100.0));
  241|       |
  242|       |        // Large font size change should clear all persisted widths
  243|       |        assert!(entry.check_font_size_change(16.0));
  244|       |        assert_eq!(entry.persisted_width(1), None);
  245|       |        assert_eq!(entry.persisted_width(2), None);
  246|       |        assert_eq!(entry.persisted_font_size, Some(16.0));
  247|       |    }
  248|       |
  249|       |    #[test]
  250|       |    fn row_management_tracks_rows_and_rendered_counts() {
  251|       |        let mut entry = TableMetricEntry::default();
  252|       |        entry.begin_pass(3);
  253|       |        assert_eq!(entry.total_rows, 3);
  254|       |        assert_eq!(entry.rendered_rows, 0);

  522|       |
  523|       |#[cfg(test)]
  524|       |#[cfg_attr(coverage_nightly, coverage(off))]
  525|       |mod tests {
  526|       |    use super::*;
  527|       |    use std::sync::{Mutex, OnceLock};
  528|       |    use tempfile::TempDir;
  529|       |
  530|       |    fn env_lock() -> std::sync::MutexGuard<'static, ()> {
  531|       |        static ENV_LOCK: OnceLock<Mutex<()>> = OnceLock::new();
  532|       |        ENV_LOCK
  533|       |            .get_or_init(|| Mutex::new(()))
  534|       |            .lock()
  535|       |            .expect("env lock")
  536|       |    }
  537|       |
  538|       |    struct EnvGuard {
  539|       |        key: &'static str,
  540|       |        original: Option<String>,
  541|       |    }
  542|       |
  543|       |    impl EnvGuard {
  544|       |        fn set(key: &'static str, value: &str) -> Self {
  545|       |            let original = std::env::var(key).ok();
  546|       |            std::env::set_var(key, value);
  547|       |            Self { key, original }
  548|       |        }
  549|       |
  550|       |        fn unset(key: &'static str) -> Self {
  551|       |            let original = std::env::var(key).ok();
  552|       |            std::env::remove_var(key);
  553|       |            Self { key, original }
  554|       |        }
  555|       |    }
  556|       |
  557|       |    impl Drop for EnvGuard {
  558|       |        fn drop(&mut self) {
  559|       |            if let Some(value) = &self.original {
  560|       |                std::env::set_var(self.key, value);
  561|       |            } else {
  562|       |                std::env::remove_var(self.key);
  563|       |            }
  564|       |        }
  565|       |    }
  566|       |
  567|       |    /// Helper to set platform-appropriate config env var and return expected config subdir.
  568|       |    /// On Windows: sets APPDATA, returns "MarkdownView"
  569|       |    /// On macOS: sets HOME, returns "Library/Application Support/MarkdownView"
  570|       |    /// On Linux: sets XDG_CONFIG_HOME, returns "mdmdview"
  571|       |    fn set_config_env(temp_path: &std::path::Path) -> (EnvGuard, PathBuf) {
  572|       |        #[cfg(target_os = "windows")]
  573|       |        {
  574|       |            let guard = EnvGuard::set("APPDATA", temp_path.to_string_lossy().as_ref());
  575|       |            let subdir = temp_path.join("MarkdownView");
  576|       |            (guard, subdir)
  577|       |        }
  578|       |        #[cfg(target_os = "macos")]
  579|       |        {
  580|       |            let guard = EnvGuard::set("HOME", temp_path.to_string_lossy().as_ref());
  581|       |            let subdir = temp_path.join("Library/Application Support/MarkdownView");
  582|       |            (guard, subdir)
  583|       |        }
  584|       |        #[cfg(not(any(target_os = "windows", target_os = "macos")))]
  585|       |        {
  586|       |            let guard = EnvGuard::set("XDG_CONFIG_HOME", temp_path.to_string_lossy().as_ref());
  587|       |            let subdir = temp_path.join("mdmdview");
  588|       |            (guard, subdir)
  589|       |        }
  590|       |    }
  591|       |
  592|       |    #[test]
  593|       |    fn test_sanitize_window_state_clamps_and_rejects_invalid() {
  594|       |        let invalid = WindowState {
  595|       |            pos: [f32::NAN, 10.0],
  596|       |            size: [800.0, 600.0],
  597|       |            maximized: false,
  598|       |        };
  599|       |        assert!(sanitize_window_state(invalid).is_none());
  600|       |
  601|       |        let input = WindowState {
  602|       |            pos: [-50.0, 25000.0],
  603|       |            size: [100.0, 200.0],
  604|       |            maximized: true,
  605|       |        };
  606|       |        let sanitized = sanitize_window_state(input).expect("expected sanitized state");
  607|       |        assert_eq!(sanitized.pos[0], 0.0);
  608|       |        assert_eq!(sanitized.pos[1], 20000.0);
  609|       |        assert_eq!(sanitized.size[0], 600.0);
  610|       |        assert_eq!(sanitized.size[1], 400.0);
  611|       |        assert!(sanitized.maximized);
  612|       |    }

  638|       |    #[test]
  639|       |    fn test_save_and_load_window_state_from_file() {
  640|       |        let _lock = env_lock();
  641|       |        let temp = TempDir::new().expect("temp dir");
  642|       |        let (_guard, _config_dir) = set_config_env(temp.path());
  643|       |
  644|       |        let state = WindowState {
  645|       |            pos: [120.0, 80.0],
  646|       |            size: [1024.0, 768.0],
  647|       |            maximized: false,
  648|       |        };
  649|       |        save_window_state(&state).expect("save");
  650|       |
  651|       |        let loaded = load_window_state().expect("load");
  652|       |        assert_eq!(loaded.pos, state.pos);
  653|       |        assert_eq!(loaded.size, state.size);
  654|       |        assert_eq!(loaded.maximized, state.maximized);
  655|       |    }
  656|       |
  657|       |    #[test]
  658|       |    fn test_save_window_state_creates_dir_and_writes_file() {
  659|       |        let _lock = env_lock();
  660|       |        let temp = TempDir::new().expect("temp dir");
  661|       |        let (_guard, config_dir) = set_config_env(temp.path());
  662|       |
  663|       |        let _ = fs::remove_dir_all(&config_dir);
  664|       |
  665|       |        let state = WindowState {
  666|       |            pos: [10.0, 20.0],
  667|       |            size: [800.0, 600.0],
  668|       |            maximized: true,
  669|       |        };
  670|       |        save_window_state(&state).expect("save");
  671|       |
  672|       |        let path = config_dir.join("window_state.txt");
  673|       |        let contents = fs::read_to_string(&path).expect("read");
  674|       |        let mut parts = contents.split_whitespace();
  675|       |        let pos_x: f32 = parts.next().expect("pos x").parse().expect("parse pos x");
  676|       |        let pos_y: f32 = parts.next().expect("pos y").parse().expect("parse pos y");
  677|       |        let size_x: f32 = parts.next().expect("size x").parse().expect("parse size x");
  678|       |        let size_y: f32 = parts.next().expect("size y").parse().expect("parse size y");
  679|       |        let max_flag = parts.next().expect("max flag");
  680|       |        assert_eq!(pos_x, state.pos[0]);
  681|       |        assert_eq!(pos_y, state.pos[1]);
  682|       |        assert_eq!(size_x, state.size[0]);
  683|       |        assert_eq!(size_y, state.size[1]);
  684|       |        assert_eq!(max_flag, "1");
  685|       |    }
  686|       |
  687|       |    #[test]
  688|       |    fn test_write_window_state_error_propagates() {
  689|       |        struct FailingWriter;
  690|       |
  691|       |        impl Write for FailingWriter {
  692|       |            fn write(&mut self, _buf: &[u8]) -> std::io::Result<usize> {
  693|       |                Err(std::io::Error::other("fail"))
  694|       |            }
  695|       |
  696|       |            fn flush(&mut self) -> std::io::Result<()> {
  697|       |                Ok(())
  698|       |            }
  699|       |        }
  700|       |
  701|       |        let state = WindowState {
  702|       |            pos: [1.0, 2.0],
  703|       |            size: [3.0, 4.0],
  704|       |            maximized: false,
  705|       |        };
  706|       |        let mut writer = FailingWriter;
  707|       |        let err = write_window_state(&mut writer, &state).expect_err("err");
  708|       |        assert_eq!(err.kind(), std::io::ErrorKind::Other);
  709|       |        writer.flush().expect("flush");
  710|       |    }
  711|       |
  712|       |    #[test]
  713|       |    fn test_save_window_state_errors_when_state_path_is_dir() {
  714|       |        let _lock = env_lock();
  715|       |        let temp = TempDir::new().expect("temp dir");
  716|       |        let (_guard, config_dir) = set_config_env(temp.path());
  717|       |
  718|       |        fs::create_dir_all(&config_dir).expect("create config dir");
  719|       |        let state_dir = config_dir.join("window_state.txt");
  720|       |        fs::create_dir_all(&state_dir).expect("create state dir");
  721|       |
  722|       |        let state = WindowState {
  723|       |            pos: [1.0, 2.0],
  724|       |            size: [3.0, 4.0],
  725|       |            maximized: false,
  726|       |        };
  727|       |        assert!(save_window_state(&state).is_err());
  728|       |    }
  729|       |

  766|       |        let _lock = env_lock();
  767|       |        let temp = TempDir::new().expect("temp dir");
  768|       |        let _guard = EnvGuard::set("APPDATA", temp.path().to_string_lossy().as_ref());
  769|       |
  770|       |        let state = WindowState {
  771|       |            pos: [42.0, 24.0],
  772|       |            size: [800.0, 600.0],
  773|       |            maximized: true,
  774|       |        };
  775|       |        force_registry_load_once(state);
  776|       |
  777|       |        let loaded = load_window_state().expect("load");
  778|       |        assert_eq!(loaded.pos, state.pos);
  779|       |        assert_eq!(loaded.size, state.size);

  781|       |    }
